### AfdxGetPacketData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetPacketData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetPacketData.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies the packet content (including protocol header and payload) to a local buffer.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetPacketData ( long packet, long offset, long length, byte buffer[] );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket or from within a callback. |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the Ethernet packet. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 frame (destination MAC address). |
| `length` | This is the number of bytes to be copied from Ethernet packet to the specified buffer. |
| `buffer` | Buffer in which the requested data are copied. Make sure that the size of the buffer is at least length bytes. |


**Return Value:** Number of copied data bytes. With AfdxGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
void OnAfdxRawPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { byte rawdata[8330]; long packetLength; long rxLength; long packetHandle; long msgId; if (afdxFlags & packetIsReassembled) { write( "<%NODE_NAME%> OnAfdxRawPacket: packet is JumboPacket --> skip" ); return; // don't transfer Jumbo-frames } if (afdxFlags & packetIsFragmented) { write( "<%NODE_NAME%> OnAfdxRawPacket: packet is fragmented" ); } rxLength = AfdxGetPacketData ( packet, 0 , elCount(rawdata), rawdata ); write( "<%NODE_NAME%> AfdxGetPacketData has length: %d", rxLength ); if (rxLength==0) { write ("<%NODE_NAME%> Error empty packet-length at timestamp %u", timestamp / 1000); } }
```


---

### AfdxGetTokenData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenData.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies a number of bytes from a token's data area to a destination buffer.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, byte buffer[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, char buffer[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long AfdxGetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, struct * buffer ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the number of bytes to be copied from the token's data area to the specified buffer. |
| `buffer` | This is the destination buffer for the copied data. Make sure that the size of the buffer is at least length bytes. |


**Return Value:** number of copied bytes or 0 With AfdxGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - preStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { byte data[8]; char error[100]; // get first 8 byte (FS) of payload of the receive packet AfdxGetTokenData ( packet, "afdx", "data", 0, 8, data ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { // do something with data } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


---

### AfdxGetTokenInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenInt.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the specified token's data as an integer value. With additional parameters (form 2) the user may specify the starting byte, length and byte ordering of the extracted integer value. Note The network byte order interpretation for form 1 is big-endian.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, long networkByteOrder ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "lpvSpeed". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the length of the integer value to be returned from the token's data area (range 1..4). |
| `networkByteOrder` | 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) |


**Return Value:** Integer value gathered from the token's data. With AfdxGetLastError you have to check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - preStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long status; char error[100]; // get status byte 1  of FDS1 (offset=4, length=1byte) status = AfdxGetTokenInt ( packet, "udp", "data", 4, 1, 1 ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { // do something with status } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


---

### AfdxGetTokenInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenInt64.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the specified token's data as an 64-bit integer value . With additional parameters (form 2) the user may specify the starting byte, length and byte ordering of the extracted integer value. Note The network byte order interpretation for form 1 is big-endian.


**Syntax:**

```c
int64 AfdxGetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] ); // form 1
```

```c
int64 AfdxGetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, long networkByteOrder ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "lpvSpeed". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the length of the integer value to be returned from the token's data (range 1..8). |
| `networkByteOrder` | 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) |


**Return Value:** Integer value gathered from the token's data. With AfdxGetLastError you have to check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - PreStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { int64 timeTS; char error[100]; // get a signed double length integer at FDS1 (offset = 8) with length 8: timeTS = AfdxGetTokenInt64 (packet, "afdx", "data", 8, 8, 1 ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { // do something with timeTS } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


---

### AfdxGetTokenLengthBit

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenLengthBit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenLengthBit.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the length of a token in bit.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetTokenLengthBit ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket |
| `protocolDesignator` | name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx" |
| `tokenDesignator` | name of the token, e.g. "header" |


**Return Value:** length of the token in bit With AfdxGetLastError you have to check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
long packet = 0; long len = 0; char error[100]; packet = AfdxInitPacket( "afdx" ); len = AfdxGetTokenLengthBit ( packet, "afdx", "data" ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { // do something with len } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetTokenReal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenReal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenReal.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the specified token's data as a float value.


**Syntax:**

```c
float AfdxGetTokenReal ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket or from within a callback. |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "udp". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | The values 4 (32-bit float value) and 8 (64-bit float value) are allowed here. This is the length of the value to be returned from the token's data area. |


**Return Value:** Integer value gathered from the token's data. With AfdxGetLastError you have to check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
void OnAfdxPacket(long channel, long dir, long flags, long bag, long packet ) { double temperature; char error[100]; // get temperature value from payload position 8 as a float (4byte length) temperature = AfdxGetTokenReal ( packet, "udp", "data", 8, 4 ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { // do something with temperature } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); } }
```


---

### AfdxGetTokenString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetTokenString.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies characters from the token and adds a terminating "\0".


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetTokenString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long bufSize, char buffer[] );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `bufSize` | Size of buffer in bytes. This function adds a terminating "\0". Thus the maximum number of copied characters is length-1. |
| `buffer` | The characters are copied to this buffer area. |


**Return Value:** Number of copied characters or 0. With AfdxGetLastError you have to check if the function has been processed successfully.


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - PreStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { char rx_str[100]; char error[100]; // get a string value located in FDS1 long offset = 8; // FDS1 + 2 byte length info long status  = 0; long len = 0; len = AfdxGetTokenInt( packet, "udp", "data", offset, 2, 1 ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { AfdxGetTokenString ( packet, "udp", "data", offset+2,  len, rx_str ); if (AfdxGetLastError() == 0) { write(rx_str); } } else { AfdxGetLastErrorText( elCount(error), error ); write("Error: %s", error ); } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


---

### AfdxInitPacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitPacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitPacket.htm)

**Description:**

Any access to an AFDX message requires a valid handle. This function returns such a handle and enables the user to manipulate or transmit the message in subsequent calls to AFDX CAPL functions . This function allocates internal buffer memory and initializes the corresponding protocol fields. The level of initialization depends on the selected function signature. Form 1 When an AFDX DBC is used this function may be called for a message in this DBC with either the message ID or the message name. Note that the message attributes from the DBC file are used for initialization too. Form 2 When the complete addressing information is available this function signature provides a convenient way to create the handle. Form 3 If you want to create another instance of an existing AFDX message (either previously created with AfdxInitPacket or signaled with the callback <OnAfdxPacket> ) this function signature should be used. Addressing information, attributes and payload contents are duplicated for the new handle. Form 4 This function signature returns a handle to an "empty" message (all fields are set to "0"). Form 5-7 With these function signatures it is possible to create a message based on either a raw byte stream or an initialized structure. The specified initialization data is copied to the target message "as is".


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long msgID, char msgName[], long initSignals ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long srcIP, long dstIP, long srcUdpPort, long dstUdpPort, long virtualLinkId, long payloadSize ); // form 2
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long copyFromPacket ); // form 3
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( ); // form 4
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, byte rawData[] ); // form 5
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, char rawData[] ); // form 6
```

```c
long AfdxInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, struct * rawData ); // form 7
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msgID` | 0: Instead of msgID the parameter msgName is used for referencing the message in the assigned AFDX DBC file. !0: This value references a message ID in the assigned AFDX DBC file. The corresponding message is used for initialization. |
| `msgName` | 0: Instead of msgName the parameter msgID is used for referencing the message in the assigned AFDX DBC file. <Message Name>: This value references a message name in the assigned AFDX DBC file. The corresponding message is used for initialization. |
| `initSignals` | 0: The signal area of the message is initialized with 0. !0: The value of an already initialized signal is used. |
| `srcIP` | Source IP address. |
| `dstIP` | Destination IP address. The IP address is given as an integer value (hexadecimal address notation 0xwwxxyyzz corresponds to WW.XX.YY.ZZ with decimal notation). For this function the value of the source IP address has to start with 10.XX.YY.ZZ. For the destination IP address the value has to start with either 10.XX.YY.ZZ or 224.XX.YY.ZZ.  Note, that you may enter the IP addresses later on with the function AfdxSetTokenInt . |
| `srcUdpPort` | 1..65535: source UDP port |
| `dstUdpPort` | 1..65535: destination UDP port |
| `virtualLinkId` | 1..65535: Virtual Link Identifier |
| `payloadSize` | 0..8192: payload size of AFDX message |
| `copyFromPacket` | The protocol header and the data of a message identified via copyFromPacket is used for initializing a new message. |
| `rawDataLength` | This is the number of bytes in an initialization array or structure. Note that the size may be gathered with elCount (for byte rawData[]), strlen (for char rawData[]) or __size_of (for struct * rawData). |
| `rawData` | Raw initialization data. |


**Return Value:** 0 Could not initialize the message. The error code may be retrieved with AfdxGetLastError . !0 Handle of the message.


**Example(s):**

```c
Form 1
variables { // create packet handle long gMSG1; }
// instantiate message MSG1 gMSG1 = AfdxInitPacket ( 0, "AFDX_MSG_OUT_1", 0 ); if (gMSG1 == 0) { write( "Creating packet failed, result %d", AfdxGetLastError() ); }
variables { // static configuration for message MSG1 const dword k_MSG1_SrcIP  = 0x0A0A0401; // source IP address = 10.10.4.1 const dword k_MSG1_DstIP  = 0xE0E03908; // destination IP address = 224.224.57.8 const dword k_MSG1_SrcUDP = 35420; // source UDP port const dword k_MSG1_DstUDP = 35420; // destination UDP port const dword k_VLID1       = 14600; // VL ID const dword k_MSG1_MxFrSz = 784; // maximum frame size // create packet handle long gMSG1; }
// instantiate packet MSG1 gMSG1 = AfdxInitPacket ( k_MSG1_SrcIP, k_MSG1_DstIP, k_MSG1_SrcUDP, k_MSG1_DstUDP, k_VLID1, k_MSG1_MxFrSz ); if (gMSG1 == 0) { write( "Creating packet failed, result %d", AfdxGetLastError() ); }
```

> Snippet placed in e.g. on preStart system function Form 2 Snippet placed in e.g. on preStart system function


---

### AfdxInitProtocol

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitProtocol.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitProtocol.htm)

**Description:**

This function initializes the protocol for a packet. If necessary further needed lower protocols are initialized, e.g. IPv4. Already initialized higher protocols are deleted.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxInitProtocol ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxInitProtocol ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char packetTypeDesignator[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol . |
| `packetTypeDesignator` | Type of the packet. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value See error code .


**Example(s):**

```c
testfunction Packet_InitProtocolICMP( long expectError, char expectErrorText[] ) { // // Create AFDX gPacket // gPacket_Result = AfdxInitProtocol ( gPacket[gPacketSelect], "afdx", "icmp" ); if (expectError == 0) { if (gPacket_Result != 0) { snprintf( gPacket_Text, elcount(gPacket_Text), "AfdxInitProtocol failed, result %d", gPacket_Result ); TestStepFail( gPacket_Text ); Packet_ReportLastError(); return; } else { TestStepPass(); } } else { if (Packet_CheckExpectError( "AfdxInitProtocol", expectError, expectErrorText )) { TestStepPass(); } } }
```


---

### AfdxInitSchedule

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitSchedule.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxInitSchedule.htm)

**Description:**

Initializes the scheduled transmission of the specified message during the start phase of the measurement. The behavior of the function call differs for different measurement phases .


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxInitSchedule ( long packet );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |


**Return Value:** 0 other value See error code .


**Example(s):**

```c
on preStart { long packet; char errTxt[256]; packet = AfdxInitPacket(0, "TESTMSG_ALLTYPES", 0 ); if (packet == 0) { AfdxGetLastErrorText(elCount(errTxt), errTxt); writeEx( -3, 0, "<%NODE_NAME%> AfdxInitPacket failed due to:%s", errTxt ); } if ( AfdxInitSchedule (packet) != 0) { AfdxGetLastErrorText(elCount(errTxt), errTxt); writeEx( -3, 0, "<%NODE_NAME%> AfdxInitSchedule failed due to:%s", errTxt ); } AfdxReleasePacket(packet); }
```

### AfdxIsPacketValid

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxIsPacketValid.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxIsPacketValid.htm)

**Description:**

This function checks, if a received packet has a protocol error. Packets with a protocol error are marked with an error icon in the Trace Window.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxIsPacketValid ( long packet );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that should be checked. |


**Return Value:** 0 Packet is valid. 1 Packet has protocol errors.


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - PreStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 time, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { if ( AfdxIsPacketValid (handle) != 0) { write( "Receive packet with protocol error" ); } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


### AfdxOutputPacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacket.htm)

**Description:**

The specified AFDX message is transmitted with this function call. Note that the user has complete control of all the transmission scenarios. If the message is defined in an AFDX database the corresponding message attributes (e.g. timing, redundancy) are used for the transmission. Your CANoe DE product internally uses an AFDX software stack for the message handling. In the following cases the message needs to be defined in a DBC file and the file needs to be assigned to the actual configuration:


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxOutputPacket ( long packet ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxOutputPacket ( long packet, long txMode, long frag, long vl, long seqNo, long rma ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message to send that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `txMode` | Set message's transmission behavior: 0: (CyclicOff) An active cyclic transmission is switched off. The corresponding message is transmitted at least once after this call. Note: The message needs to be defined in the assigned DBC file. 1: (CyclicOn) The message is transmitted cyclically and the cycle time is defined via the attribute AfdxMsgTxRate in the assigned DBC file. Note: The message needs to be defined in the assigned DBC file. 2: (SingleShot) The message is transmitted once. |
| `frag` | Activate or deactivate IP fragmentation: 0: (NoIPFragmentation) The message is transmitted without fragmentation. 1: (DoIPFragmentation) This parameter value activates the IP fragmentation for a message based on the attributes in the DBC file. The fragment's size corresponds to the value of the attribute AfdxVLmaxFrame. Furthermore the fragmentation for the message must be allowed (attribute AfdxVLipFrag = True). Note: The message needs to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `vl` | Activate/deactivate software controlled VL scheduling: 0: (NoAFDXVLScheduling) The corresponding virtual link of this message is not controlled by the AFDX software stack. 1: (DoAFDXVLScheduling) The corresponding virtual link of this message is controlled by the AFDX software stack. The BAG value is taken from the message's attribute AfdxVLbag. Note: The message needs to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `seqNo` | Activate/deactivate automatic sequence number handling: 0: (NoAFDXSeqNoManagement) The actual sequence number from message's payload is used. 1: (DoAFDXSeqNoManagement) The sequence number is automatically updated for the message. |
| `rma` | Activate a specific redundancy handling mode. There is a relation between the field Interface_ID in the MAC source address and the selected transmission line (A or B). As a result the MAC source address may be updated accordingly by the AFDX software stack. Furthermore the message may be duplicated for both lines A and B. Value Short Name Description Interface_ID updated? Message duplicated? 0 DBRelated The line assignment for the message is derived from the assigned DBC file X X 1 ForceA The message is transmitted on the specified line. X — 2 ForceB The message is transmitted on the specified line. X — 3 ForceAB The message is transmitted on both lines. X X 4 ForceAonB The message is transmitted on the other line. The assignment is inverted. X — 5 ForceBonA The message is transmitted on the other line. The assignment is inverted. X — 6 ForceABonBA The message is transmitted on both lines and the line assignment is inverted. X X 7 ForceLineA The message is transmitted on the specified line. — — 8 ForceLineB The message is transmitted on the specified line. — — 9 ForceLineAB The message is transmitted on both lines. — X 10 MACIFRelated The line assignment is derived from the given MAC source address. — X |
| `Value` | Short Name |
| `0` | DBRelated |
| `1` | ForceA |
| `2` | ForceB |
| `3` | ForceAB |
| `4` | ForceAonB |
| `5` | ForceBonA |
| `6` | ForceABonBA |
| `7` | ForceLineA |
| `8` | ForceLineB |
| `9` | ForceLineAB |
| `10` | MACIFRelated |


---

### AfdxOutputPacketRaw

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacketRaw.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacketRaw.htm)

**Description:**

The specified AFDX message is transmitted without considering the internal AFDX software stack. This call is equivalent to AfdxOutputPacket( packet, SingleShot, NoIPFragmentation, NoAFDXVLScheduling, NoAFDXSeqNoManagement, MACIFRelated ) .


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxOutputPacketRaw ( long packet );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | handle of the message to send that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
includes { #include "AFDX/Utils.cin" }
on preStart { long result; result=AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback("OnAfdxRawPacket", BothDirections, NotDropped, EveryFrameAndPacket); }
void OnAfdxRawPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long packetHandle; if (afdxFlags & packetIsReassembled) { write( "<%NODE_NAME%> OnAfdxRawPacket  packet is JumboPacket --> skip" ); return; // don't transfer Jumbo-frames } if (getBusContext() == gContextAfdx1) { setBusContext( gContextAfdx2 ); } else { setBusContext( gContextAfdx1 ); } packetHandle  = AfdxInitPacket( packet); if( packetHandle == 0) { write( "<%NODE_NAME%> AfdxInitPacket failed" ); return; } // modify signals only if not a fragment if (!(packetIsFragmented & afdxFlags )) { // TBD } else { // must use direct byte access to manipulate } AfdxOutputPacketRaw ( packetHandle ); AfdxReleasePacket( packetHandle ); }
```

> Node System - preStart in CAPL Browser Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser


---

### AfdxOutputPacketWithSignals

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacketWithSignals.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxOutputPacketWithSignals.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a message and automatically updates the payload with the current Tx-signal values of the SignalServer. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxOutputPacketWithSignals ( long packet );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `0` |  |
| `other value` | error code |


**Return Value:** error code


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { long packet; } on preStart { // initialize the frame header, don't care for the payload packet  = AfdxInitPacket(0, "TESTMSG_ALLTYPES", 0 ); if (packet == 0) { write( "<%NODE_NAME%> AfdxInitPacket failed due to:%s", errTxt ); } } on key '1' { // update payload with current tx-signal values from // signal server and then send the frame out AfdxOutputPacketWithSignals (packet); }
```

### AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxRegisterReceiveCallback.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxRegisterReceiveCallback.htm)

**Description:**

Use this function to register a CAPL callback to handle AFDX related events. Calling the function with form 1 is equivalent to calling it with form 2 with the additional parameters set to these values:


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( char callback[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( char callback[], long direction, long dropped, long type ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `callback` | Name of CAPL callback. |
| `direction` | Specifies the direction for the packet or fragment, the callback will be called. 0: RxDirection 1: TxDirection 2: BothDirections |
| `dropped` | Specifies if the callback shall be called for dropped packets or fragments. 0: Dropped 1: NotDropped 2: DroppedAndNotDropped |
| `type` | Specifies if the callback shall be called for every kind of frame or just for complete and error free IP fragments or packets. 0: ValidPackets 1: ValidFragments 2: EveryFrameAndPacket |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
Node System - PreStart in CAPL Browser
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ( "OnAfdxPacket"); }
void OnAfdxPacket( long dir, long line, int64 time, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { byte rawdata[2330]; long value; long rxLength; long packetHandle; long err; char msgName[256]; dword gContextAfdx1 = 0; dword gContextAfdx2 = 0; if (!((long)AFDXFlagFrameIsFragment & afdxFlags )) { // gets the message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName( packet, 256, msgName ); if (err > 0) { write("AfdxGetMessageName: Message name could not be read"); } if (strncmp ( msgName , "DEMOMSG_INT3" , strlen ("DEMOMSG_INT3") ) == 0) { // copies the packet content to a local buffer. rxLength = AfdxGetPacketData( packet, 0 , elCount(rawdata), rawdata ); // Returns the context of the specified bus. gContextAfdx1 = GetBusNameContext( "AFDX1" ); gContextAfdx2 = GetBusNameContext( "AFDX2" ); if ( 0 == gContextAfdx1) { writeex( 0, 3, "Error: Cannot determine context for bus: AFDX1"); } if ( 0 == gContextAfdx2) { writeex( 0, 3, "Error: Cannot determine context for bus: AFDX2"); } // Check the current bus context of the CAPL block. if (GetBusContext() == gContextAfdx1) { // Sets the bus context of the CAPL block. SetBusContext( gContextAfdx2 ); // Check the bus context of the CAPL block. if (GetBusContext() != gContextAfdx2) { return; } // creates a new AFDX packet. Other functions can access to the newly //created packet with the returned handle. packetHandle = AfdxInitPacket( rxLength, rawdata ); // check returned handle. if ( packetHandle == 0) { return; } // sets the length of a token. AfdxResizeToken(packetHandle, "eth", "data", (rxLength-14)*8); // gets the content of an AFDX integer signal (32 bit) within a packet, // with or without DBC information. value = AfdxGetSignalInt( packetHandle, "IOM_DEMO_VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_33" ); // sets the content of an AFDX integer signal (32 bit) within a packet, // with or without DBC information. AfdxSetSignalInt( packetHandle, "IOM_DEMO_VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_33", value *2); AfdxOutputPacketRaw( packetHandle ); AfdxReleasePacket( packetHandle ); } } } }
```

> Node Callback Function in CAPL Browser

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxReleasePacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxReleasePacket.htm)

```c
long AfdxReleasePacket ( long packet );
```

|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message to delete. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


| Parameter | Description |

### AfdxResizeToken

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxResizeToken.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxResizeToken.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the length of a token. This function sets the length of a token and can only be used with resizable tokens ("header" and "data"). If a token is not resizable the error code 46-0125 is returned. The main purpose for this function is to create AFDX message with or without sequence number field. Calling the function with (n = number of bytes) results in a "data" field size of n bytes. Additionally the sequence number field is located after the data field. Calling the function with (n = number of bytes) again results in a "data" field size of n bytes. In this case there is no sequence number field considered.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxResizeToken ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long newBitLength );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx" or "udp". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `newBitLength` | New length of the token in bits. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
long packet; long packetlen = 60; packet = AfdxInitPacket(0x0a022600, 0xe0e01a0f, 11121, 0x1a0f, 640 ); packetlen = sysGetVariableLong( "%NODE_NAME%", "OutputPacketLength" ); // adapt frame size with an additional seqNo byte AfdxResizeToken ( packet, "afdx", "data", packetlen*8  ); AfdxCompletePacket(packet); AfdxOutputPacket(packet); AfdxReleasePacket(packet);
```


---

### AfdxSetTokenData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenData.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies a number of bytes from source buffer to a token's data area. The token is not resized. Use AfdxResizeToken to change the token's length.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, byte buffer[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, char buffer[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long AfdxSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, struct * buffer ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "source". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the number of bytes to be copied from the specified buffer to the token's data area. Note that the token is not resized. Make sure that the token's size is at least length bytes. |
| `buffer` | This is the source buffer for the copied data. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


---

### AfdxSetTokenInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenInt.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the specified token's data to a new integer value. With additional parameters (form 2) the user may specify the starting byte, length and byte ordering of the integer value in the token. Note The network byte order interpretation for form 1 is big-endian.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long value ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, long networkByteOrder, long value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "lpvSpeed". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the length of the integer value to be copied to the token's data area (range 1..4). |
| `networkByteOrder` | 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) |
| `value` | New integer value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


---

### AfdxSetTokenInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenInt64.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the specified token's data to a new 64-bit integer value. With additional parameters (form 2) the user may specify the starting byte, length and byte ordering of the integer value in the token. Note The network byte order interpretation for form 1 is big-endian.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], int64 value ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, long networkByteOrder, int64 value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol v, e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "lpvSpeed". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | This is the length of the integer value to be copied to the token's data area (range 1..8). |
| `networkByteOrder` | 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) |
| `value` | New integer value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


---

### AfdxSetTokenReal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenReal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenReal.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the specified token's data as a float value.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetTokenReal ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long offset, long length, float value );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "udp". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `length` | The values 4 (32-bit float value) and 8 (64-bit float value) are allowed here. This is the length of the value to be copied to the token's data area. |
| `value` | New value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
{ // have  a packet handle <packet> created already long err = 0; err = AfdxSetTokenReal ( packet, "udp", "data", 8, 8, 1.234); }
```


---

### AfdxSetTokenString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetTokenString.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies the string value to the token without terminating "\0".


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetTokenString ( long packet, long protocolDesignator[], long tokenDesignator[], long offset, char data[] );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message that has been created with AfdxInitPacket . |
| `protocolDesignator` | Name of the protocol , e.g. "afdx". |
| `tokenDesignator` | Name of the token, e.g. "data". |
| `offset` | This is the offset from the beginning of the token's data area. If this is 0, data is copied starting at byte 0 of the token's data area. |
| `data` | Data as string. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
long packetHandle; // create packet packetHandle = AfdxInitPacket( "afdx" ); // set afdx payload AfdxResizeToken( packetHandle, "afdx", "data", 5*8 /*bits*/ ); AfdxSetTokenString ( packetHandle, "afdx", "data", "Hello" ); // Complete and send packet AfdxCompletePacket( packetHandle ); AfdxOutputPacket( packetHandle ); // release packet AfdxReleasePacket( packetHandle );
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalBool

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalBool.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalBool.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the value of a Boolean signal. Note A Boolean is represented by a single bit in a 32-bit field.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalBool ( long packet, char* signalName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalBool ( long packet, long offset, long bitPosition ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of a 32-bit field in the AFDX payload area. |
| `bitPosition` | Position [1..32] of a single bit in a 32-bit area pointed to by offset . |


**Return Value:** 0 or 1 other value See error codes .


**Example(s):**

```c
Analyze signals from an incoming AFDX packet using DBC information
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ("OnPacket"); }
void OnPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long lVal=0; // integer signal long eVal=0; // enumeration signal long bVal=0; // boolean signal double rVal = 0.0; // float signal char sVal[16]; // string signal byte oVal[16]; // opaque signal long len, status, err; long msgID; char msgName[256]; // get message ID of this message msgID = AfdxGetMessageId ( packet ); // get message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName ( packet, 256, msgName ); // get signal status status = AfdxGetSignalStatus ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get integer signal lVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get float-signal 2 rVal = AfdxGetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32" ); // get boolean signal 3 bVal = AfdxGetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON" ); // get enumeration signal 4 eVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3" ); // get string signal 6 len = AfdxGetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", 16, sVal ); // get opaque signal 7 len = AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 16, oVal ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalInt.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the value of a 32-bit integer signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalInt ( long packet, char* signalName ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalInt ( long packet, long offset ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the 32-bit integer value in the AFDX payload area. |


**Return Value:** Signal value Call AfdxGetLastErrror() to check for errors in the call.


**Example(s):**

```c
Analyze signals from an incoming AFDX packet using DBC information
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ("OnPacket"); }
void OnPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long lVal=0; // integer signal long eVal=0; // enumeration signal long bVal=0; // boolean signal double rVal = 0.0; // float signal char sVal[16]; // string signal byte oVal[16]; // opaque signal long len, status, err; long msgID; char msgName[256]; // get message ID of this message msgID = AfdxGetMessageId ( packet ); // get message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName ( packet, 256, msgName ); // get signal status status = AfdxGetSignalStatus ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get integer signal lVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get float-signal 2 rVal = AfdxGetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32" ); // get boolean signal 3 bVal = AfdxGetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON" ); // get enumeration signal 4 eVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3" ); // get string signal 6 len = AfdxGetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", 16, sVal ); // get opaque signal 7 len = AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 16, oVal ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalInt64.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the value of a 64-bit integer signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalInt64 ( long packet, char* signalName ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalInt64 ( long packet, long offset ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the 64-bit integer value in the AFDX payload area. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |


**Return Value:** Signal value Call AfdxGetLastErrror() to check for errors in the call.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### AfdxGetSignalOpaque

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalOpaque.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalOpaque.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies the content of an opaque data to the specified buffer. There are two different opaque data types available in AFDX: Since the opaque type is not detectable from the payload stream it must be described explicitly by the network designer.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( long packet, char signalName[], long bufSize, byte buffer[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( long packet, long offset, long bufSize, byte buffer[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( long packet, long offset, long length, long bufSize, byte buffer[] ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal, the signal's data type and the message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the opaque signal in the AFDX payload area. |
| `bufSize` | Size of available buffer area. |
| `length` | This is the number of bytes to be copied from the fixed size opaque signal to the specified buffer. |
| `buffer` | Buffer containing the read data. Make sure that the size of the buffer is at least length bytes. |


**Return Value:** Number of copied data bytes. If this value is > bufSize an error occurred.


**Example(s):**

```c
Analyze signals from an incoming AFDX packet using DBC information
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ("OnPacket"); }
void OnPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long lVal=0; // integer signal long eVal=0; // enumeration signal long bVal=0; // boolean signal double rVal = 0.0; // float signal char sVal[16]; // string signal byte oVal[16]; // opaque signal long len, status, err; long msgID; char msgName[256]; // get message ID of this message msgID = AfdxGetMessageId ( packet ); // get message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName ( packet, 256, msgName ); // get signal status status = AfdxGetSignalStatus ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get integer signal lVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get float-signal 2 rVal = AfdxGetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32" ); // get boolean signal 3 bVal = AfdxGetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON" ); // get enumeration signal 4 eVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3" ); // get string signal 6 len = AfdxGetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", 16, sVal ); // get opaque signal 7 len = AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 16, oVal ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalReal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalReal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalReal.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the value of a 32-bit or 64-bit float signal (IEEE 754) in a 64-bit float value.


**Syntax:**

```c
float AfdxGetSignalReal ( long packet, char signalName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
float AfdxGetSignalReal ( long packet, long offset, long isDouble ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the float value in the AFDX payload area. |
| `isDouble` | 0: get 32-bit float value 1: get 64-bit float value |


**Return Value:** Signal value (call AfdxGetLastErrror to check for errors in the call).


**Example(s):**

```c
Analyze signals from an incoming AFDX packet using DBC information
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ("OnPacket"); }
void OnPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long lVal=0; // integer signal long eVal=0; // enumeration signal long bVal=0; // boolean signal double rVal = 0.0; // float signal char sVal[16]; // string signal byte oVal[16]; // opaque signal long len, status, err; long msgID; char msgName[256]; // get message ID of this message msgID = AfdxGetMessageId ( packet ); // get message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName ( packet, 256, msgName ); // get signal status status = AfdxGetSignalStatus ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get integer signal lVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get float-signal 2 rVal = AfdxGetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32" ); // get boolean signal 3 bVal = AfdxGetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON" ); // get enumeration signal 4 eVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3" ); // get string signal 6 len = AfdxGetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", 16, sVal ); // get opaque signal 7 len = AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 16, oVal ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalStatus

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalStatus.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalStatus.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the functional status (FS) of a signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalStatus ( long packet, char signalName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalStatus ( long packet, long offset ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to a single byte in the 32-bit functional status area. |


**Return Value:** functional status value All other values indicate an error code .


**Example(s):**

```c
Analyze signals from an incoming AFDX packet using DBC information
on preStart { AfdxRegisterReceiveCallback ("OnPacket"); }
void OnPacket( long dir, long line, int64 timestamp, long bag, long afdxFlags, long packet ) { long lVal=0; // integer signal long eVal=0; // enumeration signal long bVal=0; // boolean signal double rVal = 0.0; // float signal char sVal[16]; // string signal byte oVal[16]; // opaque signal long len, status, err; long msgID; char msgName[256]; // get message ID of this message msgID = AfdxGetMessageId ( packet ); // get message name of this message err = AfdxGetMessageName ( packet, 256, msgName ); // get signal status status = AfdxGetSignalStatus ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get integer signal lVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34" ); // get float-signal 2 rVal = AfdxGetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32" ); // get boolean signal 3 bVal = AfdxGetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON" ); // get enumeration signal 4 eVal = AfdxGetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3" ); // get string signal 6 len = AfdxGetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", 16, sVal ); // get opaque signal 7 len = AfdxGetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 16, oVal ); }
```


---

### AfdxGetSignalString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxGetSignalString.htm)

**Description:**

This function copies up to bufSize characters of an AFDX string to the specified buffer. The AFDX string structure consists of a 16-bit length code n and a sequence of n ASCII characters.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxGetSignalString ( long packet, char signalName[], long bufSize, char buffer[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxGetSignalString ( long packet, long offset, long bufSize, char buffer[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and the message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the AFDX string. |
| `bufSize` | Size of available buffer area. |
| `buffer` | Buffer containing the read string value. |


**Return Value:** number of copied data bytes. If this value is > bufSize an error occurred.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalBool

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalBool.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalBool.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the value of a Boolean signal. Note A Boolean is represented by a single bit in a 32-bit field.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalBool ( long packet, char signalName[], long value, long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalBool ( long packet, long offset, long bitPosition, long value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `value` | One of the values 0 or 1 is allowed. |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of a 32-bit field in the AFDX payload area. |
| `bitPosition` | Position [1..32] of a single bit in a 32-bit area pointed to by offset . |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalInt.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the value of an integer signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalInt ( long packet, char signalName[], long value, long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalInt ( long packet, long offset, long value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the integer signal in the AFDX payload area. |
| `value` | New integer value. |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
long packet; long msgID = 0x1A112B73; // create a new AFDX packet as defined in the DBC with this msgID packet = AfdxInitPacket ( msgID, NULL, 0 ); // set integer signal 1 to 78 degrees, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34", 78 ); // set float-signal 2 to 52 degree, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32", 52, 3 ); // set boolean signal 3 to 1, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON", 1, 3 ); // set enumeration signal 4 to 5, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3", 5 ); // set string signal 6 to hello, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", "hello" ); // set opaque signal 7 to 0x31,0x32,0x33, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 3, "123" ); AfdxOutputPacket ( packet );
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalInt64.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the value of a 64-bit integer signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalInt64 ( long packet, char signalName[], int64 value, long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalInt64 ( long packet, long offset, int64 value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the 64-bit integer signal in the AFDX payload area. |
| `value` | New 64-bit integer value. |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### AfdxSetSignalOpaque

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalOpaque.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalOpaque.htm)

**Description:**

This function updates an opaque AFDX signal. There are two different opaque data types available in AFDX:


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalOpaque ( long packet, char signalName[], long length, byte value[], long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalOpaque ( long packet, long offset, long length, byte value[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal, the signal's data type and the message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the opaque signal in the AFDX payload area. |
| `length` | This is the number of bytes, the opaque signal is occupying in the value buffer. |
| `value` | For form 1 the bytes of the opaque data have to be provided. For form 2 the complete opaque signal is available in the buffer (for variable length the length value belongs to the opaque data). |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
long packet; long msgID = 0x1A112B73; // create a new AFDX packet as defined in the DBC with this msgID packet = AfdxInitPacket ( msgID, NULL, 0 ); // set integer signal 1 to 78 degrees, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34", 78 ); // set float-signal 2 to 52 degree, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32", 52, 3 ); // set boolean signal 3 to 1, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON", 1, 3 ); // set enumeration signal 4 to 5, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3", 5 ); // set string signal 6 to hello, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", "hello" ); // set opaque signal 7 to 0x31,0x32,0x33, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 3, "123" ); AfdxOutputPacket ( packet );
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalReal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalReal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalReal.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the value of a 32-bit or 64-bit float signal (IEEE 754).


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalReal ( long packet, char signalName[], float value, long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalReal ( long packet, long offset, long isDouble, float value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | handle of the message |
| `signalName` | name of the signal Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the float signal in the AFDX payload area. |
| `isDouble` | 0: set 32-bit float value 1: set 64-bit float value |
| `value` | new float value |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 or error code


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalStatus

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalStatus.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalStatus.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the functional status (FS) of a signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalStatus ( long packet, char signalName[], long value ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalStatus ( long packet, long offset, long value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to a single byte in the 32-bit functional status area. |
| `value` | Valid status enumeration : This functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
Create and output an AFDX packet with two signals without  DBC
long packet; long srcIP = 0x0a022600; long destIP = 0xe0e01a0f; long srcUdpPort = 0x2b71; long vlID = 0x1a0f; long len = 640; packet = AfdxInitPacket (srcIP, destIP, srcUdpPort, vlID, len ); // set oil temperature 21 to 78 degrees AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, 8, 78 ); // offset=8 // set corresponding status byte to <NO> AfdxSetSignalStatus ( packet, 4, 3 ); // set oil temp 22 to 52 degree AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, 12, 52 );  // offset=12 // set corresponding status byte to <NO> AfdxSetSignalStatus ( packet, 5, 3 ); AfdxOutputPacket ( packet );
```


---

### AfdxSetSignalString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/AFDX/Functions/CAPLfunctionAfdxSetSignalString.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the value of an AFDX string signal. The AFDX string structure consists of a 16-bit length code n and a sequence of n ASCII characters. The length code is updated too.


**Syntax:**

```c
long AfdxSetSignalString ( long packet, char signalName[], char value[], long fdsStatus ); // form 1
```

```c
long AfdxSetSignalString ( long packet, long offset, char value[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `packet` | Handle of the message. |
| `signalName` | Name of the signal. Note: The signal and its message need to be defined in the assigned DBC file. |
| `offset` | The offset value points to the starting byte of the AFDX string. |
| `value` | New ASCII string value. |
| `fdsStatus` | 255: The status is not updated valid status enumeration : The functional status is updated with this value. |


**Return Value:** 0 other value error code


**Example(s):**

```c
long packet; long msgID = 0x1A112B73; // create a new AFDX packet as defined in the DBC with this msgID packet = AfdxInitPacket ( msgID, NULL, 0 ); // set integer signal 1 to 78 degrees, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_34", 78 ); // set float-signal 2 to 52 degree, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalReal ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_AB_32", 52, 3 ); // set boolean signal 3 to 1, set corresponding status to <NO> AfdxSetSignalBool ( packet, "VFG_OIL_TEMP_A_ON", 1, 3 ); // set enumeration signal 4 to 5, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalInt ( packet, "FLIGHT_GROUND_COND_3", 5 ); // set string signal 6 to hello, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalString ( packet, "TEST_STRING14_CITY", "hello" ); // set opaque signal 7 to 0x31,0x32,0x33, don’t change status byte AfdxSetSignalOpaque ( packet, "TEST_OPAQUE14_IDENT", 3, "123" ); AfdxOutputPacket ( packet );
```

## CAN {#can}


---

### on message

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnMessage.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on message is called on the receipt of a valid CAN message. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on message procedure, to access the data of the message that has just been received. CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the measurement configuration will block the data flow to its right side. You must explicitly program the passing of messages in CAPL nodes in the evaluation branch. To make the CAPL node transparent to messages  you would write: on message * { output(this); }; However, please note that you are programming a recursive if you are using this code in CANoe's simulation configuration: For each message received, the reaction is to immediately resend the same message on the bus, causing the event procedure on message * to be called, etc. Note If for example a CAPL program contains the on message procedures on message 123 and on message * , the procedure on message 123 will be called up, when a message with the identifier 123 is received. For all other messages the procedure on message * will be called up. In contrast to bus events, value changes of environment variables are transparent to CAPL programs in the evaluation branch. Thus, a new environment variable is also available on the right side of CAPL nodes, and the user does not need to program this explicitly. valid for CANoe DE


**Example(s):**

*Gateway Example*

```c
on message CAN1.* { message CAN2.* msg; if(this.dir != rx) return; //important! msg = this; output(msg); } on message CAN2.* { message CAN1.* msg; if(this.dir != rx) return; //important! msg = this; output(msg); }
```

> The gateway should transmit all messages between Bus 1 and Bus 2 in both directions:

*Further Examples*

```c
on message 123
on message 123x
on message 0x123
on message 0x123x
on message EngineData
on message CAN1.123
on message CAN1.<symbolic name>
on message *
on message CAN2.*
on message CAN2.[*]
on message 0,1,10-20
```

> React to message 123 (dec, standard identifier), regardless of receiving chip React to message 123 (dec, extended identifier ), regardless of receiving chip React to message 123 (hex, standard identifier), regardless of receiving chip React to message 123 (hex, extended identifier), regardless of receiving chip React to message EngineData React to message 123 if it is received by CAN1 chip Resolution of an Ambiguous Name React to all messages, that are not used within another on message procedure in the same node. React to all messages received by CAN2 chip ( unboxed ) React to all messages received by CAN2 chip ( boxed ) React to messages 0, 1 and 10 through 20


---

### canEnableStatistics

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanEnableStatistics.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanEnableStatistics.htm)

**Description:**

Enables or disables CAN statistics on a specific (Form 1) or all (Form 2) CAN channels. CAN statistics for CAPL is enabled by default, but you can disable it to prevent performance issues when running a measurement. You can do this for example in an on start handler, see below.


**Syntax:**

```c
void canEnableStatistics(long channel, long flag); // Form 1
```

```c
void canEnableStatistics(long flag); // Form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |
| `flag` | 0 = disable 1 = enable |


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { canEnableStatistics(0); // Disable CAN statistics on all channels } on key 'e' { canEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable CAN statistics on channel 1 }
```


---

### canGetBurstsCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBurstsCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBurstsCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the total count of bursts of a CAN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetBurstsCount(long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |


**Return Value:** Total count of bursts, if CAN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
long value; value = canGetBurstsCount(1); // Do something with the value, e.g.: if (value > 100) { // Some action… }
```


---

### canGetBurstTime

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBurstTime.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBurstTime.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the burst time of a CAN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetBurstTime(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Burst time in microseconds, if CAN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
write("Current burst time CAN1: %i", canGetBurstTime(1, eCurrValue));
```


---

### canGetBusLoad

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBusLoad.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetBusLoad.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the busload of a CAN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetBusLoad(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Busload in percent, if CAN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
long value; value = canGetBusLoad(1, eAvgValue); // Channel 1, average // Do something with the value, e.g.: write("Average busload CAN1: %ld", value);
```

### canGetChipState

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetChipState.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetChipState.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the current chip state of a CAN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetChipState(long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |


**Return Value:** Chip state of a CAN channel. Returned values are: 0 Value not available 1 Simulated 2 Not used 3 Error Active 4 Warning Level 5 Error Passive 6 Bus Off You may find further information about the chip states also on the Bus Statistics Window page.


**Example(s):**

```c
long value = canGetChipState(1); // Now do something with the value: switch (value) { case 1: write("Simulated"); break; case 3: write("Error Active"); break; case 4: write("Warning Level"); break; case 5: write("Error Passive"); break; default: break; }
```


---

**Example(s):**

```c
write("Data frames on CAN2: %i [fr/s]", canGetStdDataRate(2, eCurrValue));
```

### canGetStdRemoteCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetStdRemoteCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetStdRemoteCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the total count of standard remote frames of a CAN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetStdRemoteCount(long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |


**Return Value:** Total count of standard remote frames, if CAN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
write("Count of remote frames on CAN2: %i", canGetStdRemoteCount(2));
```


---

### canGetStdRemoteRate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetStdRemoteRate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAN/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanGetStdRemoteRate.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the rate of standard remote frames of a CAN channel in frames per second [fr/s]. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canGetStdRemoteRate(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…32). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Rate of standard remote frames in frames per second, if CAN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
write("Remote frames on CAN2: %i [fr/s]", canGetStdRemoteRate(2, eCurrValue));
```
