### EthernetEAPConfiguration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionEthernetEAPConfiguration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionEthernetEAPConfiguration.htm)

**Description:**

Define a variable of type EthernetEapConfiguration . The variable must be initialized by accessing the eapConfig selector of a EthernetPortAccessEntity . Validity of a variable of type EthernetEapConfiguration can be verified using its selector isValid . All modifications of a EthernetEapConfiguration are temporary, that is, the assigned values will replace the values loaded from the security manager profile only while the measurement is running. The security profile in the security manager will stay untouched. Note Note that you can not Replace a PAE's EthernetEapConfiguration like this: pae.eapConfig = otherEapConfig; pae.eapConfig = otherPae.eapConfig; The selector pae.eapConfig as such is read-only. Write to selectors of a EthernetEapConfiguration while its PAE is active. Writing to EthernetEapConfiguration's selectors means reconfiguring the PAE, which can only be done while it is not running.


**Syntax:**

```c
EthernetEapConfiguration <variable name>;
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `nameme` | Variable name. |


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
EthernetEapConfiguration cfg; if(cfg.isValid) { write("PAE config name: %s", cfg.name); switch(cfg.EAPMethod) { case eMethodTLS: { write("PAE EAP Method: EAP-TLS"); break; } default: { write("Unknown EAP Method."); break; } } write("PAE EAP Method Config Name: %s", cfg.EAPMethodName); write("PAE EAP retransmit timeout [ms]: %d", cfg.EAPRetransmitTimeout); write("PAE EAP maximum retransmits: %d", cfg.EAPMaximumRetransmit); write("PAE EAP maximum restarts: %d", cfg.EAPMaximumRestarts); if(cfg.EAPIsServer) { write("PAE Quiet Period [ms]: %d", cfg.EAPQuietDuration); write("PAE Request Identity: %d", cfg.EAPRequestIdentity); } else { write("PAE Held Duration [ms]: %d", cfg.EAPHeldDuration); write("PAE Identity: %s", cfg.EAPGetIdentity); } }
```


---

### on ethernet10BASET1SStatus

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernet10BASET1SStatus.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernet10BASET1SStatus.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the change of the status of the 10Base link. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernet10BASET1SStatus procedure, to access the information of the link status.


**Syntax:**

```c
on ethernet10BASET1SStatus *; // form 1
```

```c
on ethernet10BASET1SStatus ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>; // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>` | Ethernet port qualification. |


**Example(s):**

```c
on ethernet10BASET1SStatus * { write("Transmit jabber: %d", this.transmitJabber); write("Receiptions in transmit opportunity: %d", this.receiptionInTransmitOpportunity); write("Unexpected beacon received: %d", this.unexpectedBeaconReceived); write("Beacon received before transmit opportunity: %d", this.beaconReceivedBeforeTransmitOpportunity); write("Unexpected PLCA symbols detected: %d", this.unexpectedPLCASymbolsDetected); write("ES delimiter error: %d", this.esDelimiterError); write("Decode 5B error: %d", this.decode5BError); }
```


---

### on someipMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnSomeIpMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnSomeIpMessage.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the receipt of a valid SOME/IP message. This allows CAPL programs, e.g. in test units or in the Measurement Setup, to evaluate SOME/IP messages that are simply received from the VN interface or read from the log file. The event procedure is called for every SOME/IP message that matches the specified qualifier. For example, only the service interface name can be specified and the handler is called for all messages with the corresponding service interface ID. The qualifier can be further restricted, for example by specifying an event name or message type. To access the control information, you can use selectors or methods. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
on someipMessage *; // form 1
```

```c
on someipMessage ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 2
```

```c
on someipMessage [<network-name|database>::][<packag-name::]<service-interface-name>[::major-version[::instance-id]]>::<event|field|method-Name>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error ][::TPSegment]; // form 3
```

```c
on someipMessage ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>::[<packag-name::]<service-interface-name[::major-version[::instance-id]]>::<event|field|method-Name>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error][::TPSegment]; // form 4
```

```c
on someipMessage <service-interface-id>[::major-version[::instance-id]]::<method-ID>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error][::TPSegment]; // form 5
```

```c
on someipMessage ServiceDiscovery; // form 6
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>` | Ethernet
 port qualification. |
| `[<network-name|database>::][<packag-name::]<service-interface-name>[::major-version[::instance-id]]>::<event|field|method-Name>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error|TPSegment]` | Service event/method/field qualification by database
 qualifier |
| `ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.[<packag-name::]<service-interface-name[::major-version[::instance-id]]>::<event|field|method-Name>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error|TPSegment]` | Ethernet port and database qualification (the dot
 after the portname ist important!). |
| `<service-interface-id>[::major-version[::instance-id]]::<method-ID>[::Notification|Request|Response|RequestNoReturn|Error|TPSegment]` | Service interface event/method qualification by ID. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on someipMessage * { write( "Service-ID=%d Method-ID=%d Session-ID=%X Message-Type=%d Protocol-Version=%d Interface-Version=%d Message-Type=%d Return-Code=%d", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.SessionID, this.MessageType, this.ProtocolVersion, this.InterfaceVersion, this.ReturnCode ); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on someipMessage ethernetPort::VehicleNetwork::Port1.* { write( "SOME/IP message %d:%d on port %s", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.hwPort.name ); }
```

*Example 3*

```c
// receive only SOME/IP message with Service ID 1000 and Method ID 0x8001 on someipMessage 1000::32769 { write( "SOME/IP message %d:%d", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID ); }
```

*Example 4*

```c
// receive only Service ID 1000, Method ID 0x8001 and Interface Version 1 on someipMessage 1000::1::32769 { write( "SOME/IP message %d:%d version %d", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.InterfaceVersion ); }
```

*Example 5*

```c
// receive SOME/IP message with message-type 'Error' (0x81) on someipMessage Error { write( "SOME/IP Error %d:%d return-code %d", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.ReturnCode ); }
```

*Example 6*

```c
// receive SOME/IP method-response with service ID 1000 and method ID 1 on someipMessage 1000::1::Response { write( "SOME/IP Method-Response %d:%d", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID ); }
```

*Example 7*

```c
// receive SOME/IP message with service/method ID of database definition of Lighting::HeadLight on someipMessage Lighting::HeadLight { write( "Received SOME/IP-message Lighting::HeadLight (%d:%d)", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID ); }
```

*Example 8*

```c
// receive SOME/IP message with service/method ID of database definition of Lighting::HeadLight on a specific Ethernet port on someipMessage ethernetPort::VehicleNetwork::Port1.Lighting::HeadLight { write( "Received SOME/IP-message Lighting::HeadLight (%d:%d) on Ethernet port %s", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.hwPort.name ); }
```

*Example 9*

```c
// receive SOME/IP method-response Lighting::CommingHome on someipMessage Lighting::CommingHome::Response { write( "Received SOME/IP method-response Lighting::CommingHome (%d:%d) message-type 0x%X", this.ServiceID, this.MethodID, this.MessageType ); }
```

*Example 10*

```c
on someipMessage 1000::1::Notification { write( "Assembled SOME/IP message - Length=%d", this.SerializedDataLength); } on someipMessage 1000::1::Notification::TPSegment { write( "TP Segment %d - Offset=%d, More=%d", this.IsTPSegment, this.TPOffset, this.TPMoreSegments ); }
```


---

### on someipSD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnSomeIpSD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnSomeIpSD.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the receipt of a valid SOME/IP Service Discovery Entry. A Service Discovery message (Service ID FFFFh, Method ID 8100h) can contain more than one Service Discovery entry. To access the control information, you would use selectors and methods. The key word this is available within an on someipSD procedure, to access the data of the Service Discovery entry that has just been received. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
on someipSD *;			// form 1
```

```c
on someipSD ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*;	// form 2
```

```c
on someipSD <service-id>[::major-version[::instance-id]];	// form 3
```

```c
on someipSD [<network-name|database>::][<packag-name::]<service-interface-name>[::major-version[::instance-id]];	// form 4
```

```c
on someipSD [<network-name|database>::][<package-name::][<service-interface-name|id>[::major-version[::instance-id]]] [::<OfferService|StopOfferService|FindService|StopFindService|Subscribe|StopSubscribe|SubscribeACK|StopSubscribeACK>];	// form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>` | Ethernet port qualification. |
| `<service-id[::major-version[::instance-id]]>` | Service qualification by ID. |
| `<service-name[::major-version[::instance-id]]>` | Service qualification by database qualifier. |
| `[::<OfferService|StopOfferService|FindService|StopFindService|Subscribe|StopSubscribe|SubscribeACK|StopSubscribeACK>]` | Service Discovery entry type. Can be appended to
 other qualifications. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on someipSD * { write( "<on someipSD> %.6f Eth%d %s %d %d Type=%d Service-ID=%d Instance-ID=%d, Version=%d.%d TTL=%d", this.time_ns/1e9, this.msgChannel, this.hwPort.name, this.dir, this.simulated, this.Type, this.ServiceID, this.InstanceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.TTL ); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
// Service ID 1000 on someipSD 1000 { write( "SD received service ID %d, type %d", this.ServiceID, this.Type ); }
```

*Example 3*

```c
// Service ID 1000, Version 2, Instance ID 1 on someipSD 1000::2::1 { write( "SD received service ID %d version %d.%d instance ID %d type %d", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.InstanceID, this.Type ); }
```

*Example 4*

```c
// Offer Service ID 1000, Version 2, Instance ID 1 on someipSD 1000::2::1::OfferService { write( "Offer Service received - ID %d version %d.%d instance ID %d", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.InstanceID ); }
```

*Example 5*

```c
// all Offer Service on someipSD OfferService { write( "Offer Service received - ID %d version %d.%d instance ID %d", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.InstanceID ); }
```

*Example 6*

```c
// receive Service Discovery entries for service Lighting on someipSD Lighting { write( "SD received for service Lighting ID %d, type %d", this.ServiceID, this.Type ); }
```

*Example 7*

```c
// receive Find-Service for service Lighting on someipSD Lighting::FindService { }
```

*Example 8*

```c
// receive Stop-Offer-Service for service Lighting, Version 1, Instance 2 on someipSD Lighting::1::2::StopOfferService { }
```


---

### someipMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionSomeipMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionSomeipMessage.htm)

**Description:**

In on someipMessage , this has this data type. The selectors and methods of this type can be used with this .


**Syntax:**

```c
someipMessage * <message var>;
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on someipMessage * { HandleMsg( this ); } void HandleMsg( someipMessage * msg ) { write( "HandleMsg: Service ID %d, Method ID %d", msg.ServiceID, msg.MethodID ); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on someipMessage 1000::1 { switch(this.MessageType) { case 0x00: // REQUEST write( "Method Request" ); break; case 0x01: // REQUEST_NO_RETURN write( "Request-no-Return Method" ); break; case 0x02: // NOTIFICATION write( "Notification" ); break; case 0x80: // RESPONSE write( "Method Response" ); break; case 0x81: // ERROR write( "Error" ); break; } }
```


---

### someipSD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionSomeipSD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Objects/CAPLfunctionSomeipSD.htm)

**Description:**

In on someipSD , this has this data type. The selectors and methods of this type can be used with this .


**Syntax:**

```c
someipSD * <packet var>;
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on someipSD * { switch(this.Type) { case 0x00: // Find Service write( "Find Service %d %d.%d #%d", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.InstanceID ); break; case 0x01: // Offer Service write( "Offer Service %d %d.%d #%d", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.MinorVersion, this.InstanceID ); break; case 0x06: // Subscribe Service write( "Subscribe Service %d v%d #%d TTL %ds", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.InstanceID, this.TTL, this.EventgroupID ); break; case 0x07: // Subscribe Acknowledge write( "Subscribe Acknowledge Service %d v%d #%d Event.Gropu", this.ServiceID, this.MajorVersion, this.InstanceID, this.EventgroupID ); break; } }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetData.htm)

**Description:**

Copies the serialized payload to a provided buffer.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword someipMessage ::GetData( dword byteOffset, byte buffer[], dword bufferLength); // form 1
```

```c
dword someipMessage ::GetData( dword byteOffset, char buffer[], dword bufferLength); // form 2
```

```c
dword someipMessage ::GetData( dword byteOffset, struct buffer, dword bufferLength); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteOffset` | Offset from which the data is copied from the serialized payload. Offset 0 is the first byte after the SOME/IP header. |
| `buffer` | The data is copied to this buffer, which must be provided as a variable in CAPL. |
| `bufferLength` | The specified number of bytes is copied. The buffer provided must be large enough. |


**Return Value:** Number of copied bytes


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on someipMessage * { byte serializedData[1000]; dword serializedDataLength; serializedDataLength = this.GetData( 0, serializedData, elcount(serializedData) ); write( "SOME/IP message data, length %d", serializedDataLength ); // ... do something with serializedData }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on someipMessage * { _align(1) struct MyMessage { dword arg1; word  arg2; byte  arg3; } myMessage; dword myMessageLength; myMessageLength = this.GetData( 0, myMessage ); write( "SOME/IP message: arg1=%X, arg2=%X, arg3=%X", myMessage.arg1, myMessage.arg2, myMessage.arg3 ); }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetDestinationAddress

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetDestinationAddress.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetDestinationAddress.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the IP address of the SOME/IP message.


**Syntax:**

```c
long someipMessage ::GetDestinationAddress(ip_Address address);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `address` | Address is copied to this ip_Address variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Failed


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
on someipMessage * { ip_Address  addr; ip_Endpoint ep; if (this.GetSourceIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetSourceIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetDestinationEndpoint

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetDestinationEndpoint.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetDestinationEndpoint.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the IP endpoint of the SOME/IP message.


**Syntax:**

```c
long someipMessage ::GetDestinationEndpoint(ip_Endpoint endpoint);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `endpoint` | IP endpoint is copied to this ip_Endpoint variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Failed


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
on someipMessage * { ip_Address  addr; ip_Endpoint ep; if (this.GetSourceIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetSourceIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetEthernetPacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetEthernetPacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetEthernetPacket.htm)

**Description:**

The method can be used to query the Ethernet packet that contains the SOME/IP message. If the transmission was segmented/fragmented, the last Ethernet packet is returned.


**Syntax:**

```c
long someipMessage ::GetEthernetPacket( ethernetPacket * pkt);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `pkt` | A variable of type ethernetPacket must be transferred. The data is copied to this variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Failed


**Example(s):**

```c
on someipMessage * { ethernetPacket pkt; if (this.GetEthernetPacket( pkt ) == 0) { char macAddrStr[100]; ethGetMacAddressAsString( pkt.source, macAddrStr, elcount(macAddrStr) ); write( "Source MAC Address %s", macAddrStr ); } }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetServiceInstanceID

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetServiceInstanceID.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetServiceInstanceID.htm)

**Description:**

The method can be used to query the service instance ID. The service instance ID is not directly contained in the SOME/IP message. CANoe can use the service discovery to track which IP address a service instance ID is assigned to. It may happen that no instance ID can be assigned to a SOME/IP message. Therefore, HasServiceInstanceID must first be used to check whether a service instance ID could be assigned before GetServiceInstanceID is used.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword someipMessage ::GetServiceInstanceID();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** Service Instance ID or 0.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
on someipMessage * { if (this.HasServiceInstanceID()) { write( "<%BASE_FILE_NAME%> SOME/IP message with instance ID", this.GetServiceInstanceID() ); } }
```


---

### someipMessage::GetSourceAddress

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetSourceAddress.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpMessageGetSourceAddress.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the IP address of the SOME/IP message.


**Syntax:**

```c
long someipMessage ::GetSourceAddress(ip_Address address);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `address` | Address is copied to this ip_Address variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Failed


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
on someipMessage * { ip_Address  addr; ip_Endpoint ep; if (this.GetSourceIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPAddress( addr ) == 0) { char str[100]; addr.PrintAddressToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Address %s", str ); } if (this.GetSourceIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Source IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } if (this.GetDestinationIPEndpoint( ep ) == 0) { char str[100]; ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Destination IP-Endpoint %s", str ); } }
```

### someipSD::GetOptionIPEndpoint

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpSDGetOptionIPEndpoint.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionSomeIpSDGetOptionIPEndpoint.htm)

**Description:**

The method can be used to query an IP endpoint of an SD option. The type of SD option is specified as a parameter. If this option exists and contains an IP endpoint, this is returned.


**Syntax:**

```c
long someipSD ::GetOptionIPEndpoint ( byte optionType, ip_endpoint & retIPEndpoint );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `optionType` | Type of SD option: 0x01 – Configuration option, does not contain an IP endpoint 0x02 – Load Balancing option, does not contain an IP endpoint 0x04 – IPv4 Endpoint option 0x06 – IPv6 Endpoint option 0x14 – IPv4 Multicast option 0x16 – IPv6 Multicast option 0x24 – IPv4 SD Endpoint option 0x26 – IPv6 SD Endpoint option |
| `retIPEndpoint` | Returns the IP Endpoint. If return value is not 0, retIPEndpoint is cleared. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Failed, option or IP endpoint not available.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
on someipSD * { ip_Endpoint ep; char        str[40]; // get IP endpoint of IPv4 endpoint option if (this.GetOptionIPEndpoint( 0x04, ep ) == 0) { ep.PrintEndpointToString( str ); write( "Endpoint option IP endpoint %s", str ); } }
```

### AvbReceive

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbReceive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbReceive.htm)

**Description:**

The function receives data into the specified buffer. If the receive operation completes immediately the function returns the number of received elements in the length parameter and the passed CAPL callback OnAvbReceive will not be called. If the receive operation does not complete immediately the operation is performed asynchronously and the function will return 460609. In this case the CAPL callback OnAvbReceive will be called on completion (successful or not). The type of the buffer parameter of the called overload of AvbReceive must match with the type of the buffer parameter of the supplied callback. Otherwise, the callback is not invoked. If a message object returned by form 4 was transmitted using the CAN_BRIEF format of IEEE 1722-2016, its time_ns field is set to -1.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, int buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 1
```

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, long buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 2
```

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, byte buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 3
```

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, message * buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 4
```

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, linFrame * buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 5
```

```c
dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, frFrame * buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 6
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `listenerHandle` | The Listener handle. |
| `buffer` | The buffer used to store the incoming data. |
| `length` | The length of the data buffer. Note that a reference must be supplied as this is an output parameter. |
| `onReceiveCallback` | The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbReceive). |


**Return Value:** 0 The function completed successfully. 460609 The operation is pending and will be completed later. Any other value Other Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
includes { } variables { dword gListenerHandle = 0; dword gStreamHandle = 0; const dword gLength = 1000; } // Callback for connection requests void OnAvbListen(dword listenerHandle, dword result) { message * buffer[1000]; dword length = gLength; dword res; // Accept stream gStreamHandle = AvbAccept(listenerHandle); // Receive data in buffer res = AvbReceive(gStreamHandle, buffer, length, "OnAvbReceive"); if (res != 460609) { OnAvbReceive(gStreamHandle, res, buffer, length); } } void OnAvbReceive(dword listenerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length) { dword res; dword len = gLength; // Do not reuse the length argument for the next call to AvbReceive write("Received %i CAN messages", length); // Wait for more data res = AvbReceive(gStreamHandle, buffer, len, "OnAvbReceive"); // Reuse buffer if (res != 460609) { OnAvbReceive(gStreamHandle, res, buffer, len); } } on start { // Open Listener gListenerHandle = AvbOpenListener(1); if (gListenerHandle == 0) { return; } // Configure Listener AvbSetProperty("StreamReservation", 0); // Wait for incoming connections AvbListen(gListenerHandle, "OnAvbListen"); } on preStop { if (gStreamHandle != 0) { AvbCloseListener(gStreamHandle); gStreamHandle = 0; } if (gListenerHandle != 0) { AvbCloseListener(gListenerHandle); gListenerHandle = 0; } }
```


---

### AvbSend

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbSend.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbSend.htm)

**Description:**

The function sends the data passed in the buffer.
If the send operation completes immediately, the function returns the number of sent elements in the length parameter and the passed CAPL callback OnAvbSend will not be called. If the send operation does not complete immediately the operation is performed asynchronously and the function will return 460609. In this case the CAPL callback OnAvbSend will be called on completion (successful or not). No data should be written to the buffer until the send operation is complete to avoid corruption of the sent data. The semantics of the raw data overloads (for int, long, qword, and byte buffers) depends on the configured media type of the respective Talker. When sending audio data, the supplied data buffer can contain any number of samples and sample frames. In contrast, when the data buffer contains video data, one call to AvbSend must correspond to exactly one video frame. In both cases, the data may be further packetized and send timings are handled by the AVB IL according to the media type of the Talker. The sending behavior for CAN, LIN, and FlexRay messages can be controlled with the parameters StartTimeOffset and FramesPerPacket . See AvbSetProperty for details. The AVTP standard in IEEE 1722-2016 offers a second format to transmit CAN frames in control packets denoted as CAN_BRIEF. CAN_BRIEF is identical to CAN except that no message timestamps are contained in the header. A message object in the buffer of form 5 can be sent in CAN_BRIEF mode by setting its flag bit with the bit mask 0x10000000. Technically, it is possible to supply CAN XL frames to the overload of form 5. However, AVTP does not support encapsulation of CAN XL frames. Thus, the AVB IL ignores such frames.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, int buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 1
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, long buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 2
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, qword buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 3
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, byte buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 4
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, message * buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 5
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, linFrame * buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 6
```

```c
dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, frFrame * buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 7
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `talkerHandle` | The Talker handle. |
| `buffer` | The buffer containing the data to be sent. |
| `length` | The length of the data buffer. Note that a reference must be supplied as this is an output parameter. |
| `onSendCallback` | The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbSend ). The type of the buffer parameter of the supplied callback function must match with the type of the buffer parameter of the called overload of AvbSend . Otherwise, the callback will not be invoked.. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function completed successfully. 460609 The operation is pending and will be completed later. Any other value Other Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
includes { } variables { dword gMediaTypeHandle = 0, gTalkerHandle = 0; } void OnAvbConnect(dword gTalkerHandle, dword result) { dword len = 1; message * buffer[1]; dword res = 0; // Send a CAN message res = AvbSend(gTalkerHandle, buffer, len, "OnAvbSend"); if (res != 460609) { OnAvbSend(gTalkerHandle, res, buffer, len); } } void OnAvbSend(dword gTalkerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length) { write(“%i CAN messages were sent.”, length); } on start { byte broadcast[6] = {0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff}; dword res = 0; // Create media type gMediaTypeHandle = MediaCreateMediaType(); MediaSetProperty(gMediaTypeHandle, "MajorType", "MediaType_Control"); // ACF format MediaSetProperty(gMediaTypeHandle, "Subtype", "ControlFormat_CAPL"); // Currently only available subtype // Open a Talker with Stream ID = 1 gTalkerHandle = AvbOpenTalker(1); if (gTalkerHandle == 0) // Talke could not be created { return; } // Configure Talker AvbSetProperty("StreamReservation", 0); AvbSetProperty(gTalkerHandle, "Protocol", "TSCF"); // TSCF or NTSCF supported AvbSetProperty(gTalkerHandle, "FramesPerPacket", 1); // Collect at most these number of frames before sending AvbSetProperty(gTalkerHandle, "StartTimeOffset", 1000000000); // Wait at most this time in ns before sending AvbSetMediaType(gTalkerHandle, gMediaTypeHandle); // Establish connection (internal state handling of AVB IL) res = AvbConnect(gTalkerHandle, broadcast, "OnAvbConnect"); if (res != 460609) { OnAvbConnect(gTalkerHandle, res); } } on preStop { if (gTalkerHandle != 0) { AvbCloseTalker(gTalkerHandle); gTalkerHandle = 0; } if (gMediaTypeHandle != 0) { MediaReleaseMediaType(gMediaTypeHandle); gMediaTypeHandle = 0; } }
```


---

### AvbSetProperty

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbSetProperty.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionAvbSetProperty.htm)

**Description:**

The behavior of the AVB IL can be configured using properties. Properties can be set for the current bus context (form 1) as well as on an object-specific basis for talkers (form 2, 3). The default settings for the properties can be obtained from the table below.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword AvbSetProperty (char propertyName[], int64 value); //form 1
```

```c
dword AvbSetProperty (dword objHandle, char propertyName[], int64 value); // form 2
```

```c
dword AvbSetProperty (dword objHandle, char propertyName[], dword length, byte value[]); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `propertyName` | Name of the property to be set. Name Default Value Description Current bus context (only valid for form 1) StreamReservation 1 Stream Reservation is not supported. Set this property to 0. Listener/Talker (only valid for form 2 and 3) VlanId 2 The VLAN ID to be used for sending or receiving AVB packets. RtpPort 1024 (Listener) Dynamically chosen (Talker) Local client port = destination port of the stream (Listener) Local server port = source port of the stream (Talker) used for RTP via UDP. For talkers set the destination port of the stream by calling AvbConnect . RTP is always used in conjunction with RTCP. The RTCP UDP destination port number is one higher than the RTP port number. Listener RtspAddress Not set/0 Remote server address used for RT(S)P via TCP. If not set, no connection attempt to an RTSP server is made when AvbListen is called. Talker FramesPerPacket Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible). The semantic depends on the configured media type. Uncompressed audio: Constant number of sample frames per AVTPDU. Timestamp: Number of reference timestamps per AVTPDU. For control data (CAN, LIN, and FlexRay frames): Number of frames that are collected across one or more calls to AvbSend before sending, possibly via multiple AVTPDUs, is triggered. For all other formats this property is not used. StartTimeOffset 1s For control data (CAN, LIN, and FlexRay frames): Maximum waiting duration in ns after a call to AvbSend before all stored CAN, LIN, and FlexRay frames are sent in case less than FramesPerPacket frames were supplied. A packet transmission triggered by a call to AvbSend (because enough frames are provided) resets this period. VlanPriority 2 The VLAN Priority to be used for sending AVB packets. PresentationTimeOffset Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible) Presentation Time Offset in [ns]. The Presentation Time Offset is the time difference between the Presentation Time and the time the sample is handed over to the MAC's egress buffer as part of an AVB packet that is automatically filled with the corresponding Presentation Timestamp. This time must account for the Max Transit Time inherent to the SR class of the network as illustrated in IEEE 1722-2011 , Figure 6 - AVTP Timing Reference Planes. RtpPayloadType Automatically deduced from Media Type The RTP Payload Type describes the media encoding of the RTP stream's payload samples. This parameter is automatically set according to the first entry defined in the mapping table in the Options dialog for a specific media type. If a distinct type should be used set this property on the talker. MaxFuSize 1464 (AVTP) / 64K (RTP) Sets the maximum size the lower fragmentation layer uses for transmitting sample data. Lowering this value does not limit the possible overall sample size (which is controlled by the protocol's own packetization mode, e. g. FU-A for RTP). If desired, for RTP the use of IP fragmentation can be omitted entirely by setting this property to a value that is smaller than the maximum payload size possible as of the TCP/IP stack's current MTU setting. |
| `Name` | Default Value |
| `Current bus context (only valid for form 1)` |  |
| `StreamReservation` | 1 |
| `Listener/Talker (only valid for form 2 and 3)` |  |
| `VlanId` | 2 |
| `RtpPort` | 1024 (Listener) Dynamically chosen (Talker) |
| `Listener` |  |
| `RtspAddress` | Not set/0 |
| `Talker` |  |
| `FramesPerPacket` | Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible). |
| `StartTimeOffset` | 1s |
| `VlanPriority` | 2 |
| `PresentationTimeOffset` | Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible) |
| `RtpPayloadType` | Automatically deduced from Media Type |
| `MaxFuSize` | 1464 (AVTP) / 64K (RTP) |
| `objHandle` | Handle of a talker or listener. |
| `length` | Length of the value buffer passed. |
| `value` | New value of the property. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
dword talkerHandle; talkerHandle = AvbOpenTalker(); AvbSetProperty (talkerHandle, "FramesPerPacket", 64);
```

### OnAvbSend

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnAvbSend.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnAvbSend.htm)

**Description:**

This callback is dispatched when an asynchronous send operation on a Talker completes. The type of the buffer parameter must match with the type of the buffer parameter of the corresponding call to AvbSend .


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, int buffer[], dword length); // form 1
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, long buffer[], dword length); // form 2
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, qword buffer[], dword length); // form 3
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, byte buffer[], dword length); // form 4
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length); // form 5
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, linFrame * buffer[], dword length); // form 6
```

```c
void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, frFrame * buffer[], dword length); // form 7
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `talkerHandle` | The Talker handle. |
| `result` | The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. |
| `buffer` | The buffer provided with the send operation. |
| `length` | The number of elements sent. |


**Example(s):**

```c
See example of AvbSend .
```


---

### OnAvbReceive

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnAvbReceive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AVBIL/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnAvbReceive.htm)

**Description:**

This callback is dispatched when an asynchronous receive operation on a Listener completes. The type of the buffer parameter of the supplied callback must match the type of the buffer parameter of the corresponding AvbReceive call. An OnAvbReceive callback is invoked only once per call to AvbReceive . This means that AvbReceive must be called again after receiving some data through a callback to receive more data in the future. Multiple OnAvbReceive callbacks can wait for incoming data at the same time. In this case, the raw data overloads (for int, long, and byte buffers) are called in the same order as their corresponding calls to AvbReceive were processed. In contrast, this is not necessarily true for the CAN, LIN, and FlexRay callbacks. This is due to the fact that one AVTP packet can deliver CAN, LIN, and FlexRay frames at the same time and pending callbacks for one frame type should not result in starvation of callbacks for other frame types. However, the order is still preserved for multiple callbacks for the same frame type. The sequence of bus messages in received ACF packets determines the order in which callbacks of the respective bus message types are called. Consecutive bus messages of the same type are returned together. In contrast, bus messages of the same type are not grouped together if at least one message of another type was transmitted in between. This means that multiple callbacks for the same bus message type may be called for a single packet. For instance, this would be the case for an ACF packet which contains a CAN message, a LIN message, and then another CAN message. In this case, an OnAvbReceive callback is called for the first CAN message, then an OnAvbReceive callback is called for the LIN message, and finally, another OnAvbReceive callback for the second CAN message is called.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, int buffer[], dword length); // form 1
```

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, long buffer[], dword length); // form 2
```

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, byte buffer[], dword length); // form 3
```

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length); // form 4
```

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, linFrame * buffer[], dword length); // form 5
```

```c
void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, frFrame * buffer[], dword length); // form 6
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `listenerHandle` | The Listener handle. |
| `result` | The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. |
| `buffer` | The buffer into which the data was stored. |
| `length` | The length of the received data. |


**Example(s):**

```c
See example of AvbReceive .
```

### on ethernetErrorPacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetErrorPacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetErrorPacket.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the receipt of an invalid Ethernet packet with checksum or length error. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetErrorPacket procedure, to access the data of the packet that has just been received. CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the measurement configuration will block the data flow to its right side. You must explicitly program the passing of messages in CAPL nodes in the evaluation branch. To make the CAPL node transparent to messages you would write: on ethernetErrorPacket * { output(this); };


**Syntax:**

```c
on ethernetErrorPacket *; // form 1
```

```c
on ethernetErrorPacket msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead.
```

```c
on ethernetErrorPacket ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msgChannel<X>` | Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. |
| `ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>` | Ethernet port qualification. |


**Example(s):**

```c
on ethernetErrorPacket * { write("Received Ethernet error packet on Eth%d", this.msgChannel ); output( this ); // only required in CAPL node in measurement setup! }
on ethernetErrorPacket ethernetport::SpecialV30::ECU_1.* { write ("ON ethernetErrorPacket ethernetport::SpecialV30::ECU_1.*: Ch:%d, PortId:%d", this.msgChannel, this.hwPort.portId); }
```


---

### on ethernetStatus

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetStatus.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetStatus.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the change of the status of the Ethernet link. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetStatus procedure, to access the information of the link status.


**Syntax:**

```c
on ethernetStatus *; // form 1
```

```c
on ethernetStatus msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead.
```

```c
on ethernetStatus ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>; // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msgChannel<X>` | Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. |
| `ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>` | Ethernet port qualification. |


**Example(s):**

```c
on ethernetStatus * { switch(this.status) { case 0: write("Ethernet link on Eth%d is down", this.msgChannel ); break; case 1: write("Ethernet link on Eth%d is up with %dbit/sec", this.msgChannel , this.bitrate ); } }
```


---

### on ethernetPacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetPacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnEthernetPacket.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure is called on the receipt of a valid Ethernet packet. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetPacket procedure, to access the data of the packet that has just been received. CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the measurement configuration will block the data flow to its right side. You must explicitly program the passing of messages in CAPL nodes in the evaluation branch. To make the CAPL node transparent to messages you would write: on ethernetPacket * { output(this); };


**Syntax:**

```c
on ethernetPacket *; // form 1
```

```c
on ethernetPacket msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead.
```

```c
on ethernetPacket ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msgChannel<X>` | Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. |
| `ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>` | Ethernet port qualification. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on ethernetPacket * { write("Received Ethernet packet on Eth%d", this.msgChannel ); output( this ); // only required in CAPL node in measurement setup! }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on ethernetPacket msgChannel1.* { write("Received Ethernet packet with length %d", this.Length ); output( this ); // only required in CAPL node in measurement setup! }
```

*Example 3*

```c
on ethernetPacket ethernetPort::Ethernet1::HU.* { write("Received Ethernet packet with length %d", this.Length ); output( this ); // only required in CAPL node in measurement setup! }
```

### AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AUTOSARethIL/Functions/CAPLFunctionAREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/AUTOSARethIL/Functions/CAPLFunctionAREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as client. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.


**Syntax:**

```c
AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `connectionHandle` | Handle of the TLS socket to be used. |
| `configuration` | Name of the TLS configuration. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
dword res; dword connection; if (socket == gServerSocket1) { connection = tcpAccept (socket); connection = TlsOpen (connection); write("%FILE_NAME_NO_EXT%: Server %d connection %d", socket, connection); res = tlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (connection, "PSK K1");
```


---

**Description:**

Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as server. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.


**Syntax:**

```c
AREthTlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `connectionHandle` | Handle of the TLS socket to be used. |
| `configuration` | Name of the TLS configuration. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
dword res; dword connection; if (socket == gServerSocket1) { connection = tcpAccept (socket); connection = TlsOpen (connection); write("%FILE_NAME_NO_EXT%: Server %d connection %d", socket, connection); res = tlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (connection, "PSK K1");
```

### ethernetPacket::protocol::optional-structure::Init

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionProtocolOptionalStructureInit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionProtocolOptionalStructureInit.htm)

**Description:**

Add a protocol option for a specific protocol to the Ethernet packet.
The protocol must support options. Following options are available: Protocol Field IPv4 End of Option list optionEOL No Operation optionNOP Security optionSecurity Loose Source Routing optionLooseSourceRoute Internet Timestamp optionTimeStamp Record Route optionRecordRoute Stream ID optionStreamID Strict Source Routing optionoptionStrictSourceRoute ICMPv4 Address Mask Reply addrMaskReply Addr Mask Request addrMaskRequest Alternate Host Address alternateHostAddress Datagram Conv Error datagramConvError Destination Unreachable destinationUnreachable Domain Name Reply domainNameReply Domain Name Request domainNameRequest Echo echo Echo Reply echoReply Information Reply informationReply Information Request informationRequest IPv 6 I Am Here ipv6IAmHere IPv 6 Where Are You ipv6WhereAreYou Mobile Host Redirect mobileHostRedirect Mobile Reg Reply mobileRegReply Mobile Reg Request mobileRegRequest Msg Utilized by Exp msgUtilizedByExp Parameter Problem parameterProblem Redirect redirect Router Advertisement routerAdvertisement routerSolicitation routerSolicitation securityFailures securityFailures skip skip sourceQuench sourceQuench timeExceeded timeExceeded timestamp timestamp timestampReply timestampReply traceroute traceroute IPv6 Hop-By-Hop Options hopByHopOptions Routing Header routing Fragment Header fragment Authentication Header authentication Encapsulating Security Payload esp Destination Options Header destinationOptions ICMPv6 Destination Unreachable destinationUnreachable Echo Reply echoReply Echo Request echoRequest Home Agent Address Discovery Reply homeAgentAddrDiscReply Home Agent Address Discovery Request homeAgentAddrDiscRequest Mobile Prefix Advertisement mobilePrefixAdvertisement Mobile Prefix Solicitation mobilePrefixSolicitation Multicast Listener Done multicastListenerDone Multicast Listener Query multicastListenerQuery Multicast Listener Report multicastListenerReport Multicast Router Advertisement multicastRouterAdvertisement Multicast Router Solicitation multicastRouterSolicitation Multicast Router Termination multicastRouterTermination Parameter Problem parameterProblem timeExceeded timeExceeded v2MulticastListernerReport v2MulticastListernerReport NDP Inverse Advertisement inverseAdvertisement Inverse Solicitation inverseSolicitation Neighbor Advertisement neighborAdvertisement Neighbor Solicitation neighborSolicitation Redirect redirect routerAdvertisement routerAdvertisement Router Solicitation routerSolicitation IGMP Membership Query membershipQuery Membership Report membershipReport NTP Control control TFTP Acknowledge ack Error error File Data fileData Read Request readRequest Write Request writeRequest


**Syntax:**

```c
long ethernetPacket .< protocol >.<optional-structure>.Init();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Error


**Example(s):**

```c
on key '1' { ethernetPacket pkt; pkt.ipv6.Init();// initialize a packet IPv6 pkt.udp.Init(); // and UDP pkt.ipv6.fragment.Init(); pkt.ipv6.fragment.offset = 100; pkt.ipv6.framgent.flag  = 1; pkt.ipv6.fragment.identification = 0x1234; pkt.CompletePacket(); output( pkt ); }
```

### ethernetPacket::CompletePacket

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionCompletePacket.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Methods/CAPLfunctionCompletePacket.htm)

**Description:**

Calculates the checksum and length field for all protocols contained in the packet. The protocols will be valid, after calling this method and the packet can be sent.


**Syntax:**

```c
word ethernetPacket .CompletePacket();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Error


**Example(s):**

```c
ethernetPacket pkt; // initialize with IPv4 and UDP protocol pkt.udp.Init(); // recalculate checksum and length fields of IPv4 and UDP pkt.CompletePacket(); // send packet output( pkt );
```

### ethStartPacketGenerator

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Functions/CAPLfunctionEthStartPacketGenerator.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Functions/CAPLfunctionEthStartPacketGenerator.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the Ethernet packet generator that sends continuously the configured packets. It is possible to run the packet generator for one packet per Ethernet channel or port.
If you do not specify a number of Frames the generator sends until you call EthStopPacketGenerator . Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate );
```

```c
long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate, dword numberOfFrames );
```

```c
long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate, dword numberOfFrames, dword counterByteOffset );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `txPacket` | The Ethernet packet to send. |
| `transmissionRate` | The rate in frames per second the packet should be transmitted. Range: [1 ... 1.000.000] in channel-based mode. Range: [1 ... 30.000.000] in network-based mode. |
| `numberOfFrames` | The number of Frames that should be transmitted overall.
Range: [0x1 ... 0xFFFFFFFE], 0xFFFFFFFF to send until EthStopPacketGenerator. |
| `counterByteOffset` | The position a 4 byte counter is placed inside the Ethernet-payload on a zero based index. Index 0 is the first byte after the source MAC Address.
Take care that the counter has to fit completely into the payload. E.g. for a packet with 100 byte Ethernet payload the highest position for a 4 byte counter is 96. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success -1 Output on specified Ethernet channel not allowed. -2 Packet generator not supported in simulation mode. -3 Packet generator is only available with Vector Ethernet network hardware. -4 Configuration of packet generator failed. -5 Starting of packet generator failed.


**Example(s):**

*Example for network-based access*

```c
variables { ethernetPacket txPacket; } on key '1' { txPacket.hwPort = ethernetPort::Ethernet1::ChatClient1; txPacket.source = EthGetMacAddressAsNumber( "20:00:00:00:00:01" ); txPacket.destination = EthGetMacAddressAsNumber( "FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF" ); txPacket.Length      = 100; txPacket.type        = 0xF123; ethStartPacketGenerator ( txPacket, 1000 ); } on key '2' { ethStopPacketGenerator(txPacket); }
```

*Example for channel-based access*

```c
variables { ethernetPacket txPacket; } on key '1' { txPacket.msgChannel  = 1; txPacket.source      = EthGetMacAddressAsNumber( "20:00:00:00:00:01" ); txPacket.destination = EthGetMacAddressAsNumber( "FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF" ); txPacket.Length      = 100; txPacket.type        = 0xF123; ethStartPacketGenerator ( txPacket, 1000 ); } on key '2' { ethStopPacketGenerator(txPacket); }
```


---

### ethGetLinkStatus

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Functions/CAPLfunctionEthGetLinkStatus.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/IP/Functions/CAPLfunctionEthGetLinkStatus.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the link status of the channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long ethGetLinkStatus ( long channel ); // form 1
```

```c
long ethGetLinkStatus ( long channel, long hwChannel ); // form 2
```

```c
long ethGetLinkStatus ( ethernetPort port ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32 |
| `hwChannel` | Hardware channel number. Vector network interface must support more than 1 hardware channel to use. Value range: 1..16 |
| `port` | Ethernet port, described by network name and port name. |


**Return Value:** 0 Link down 1 Link up -1 Read link status failed


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on key 'i' { write("Link status has the value: %d", ethGetLinkStatus (1)); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on key 'i' { write("Link status has the value: %d", ethGetLinkStatus (ethernetPort::MyNetworkName::MyPortName)); }
```
