### frSetKeySlot

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFrSetKeySlot.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFrSetKeySlot.htm)

**Description:**

Configures one of two possible key slots that are to be sent for a FlexRay bus. The change will be applied with the next reset of the interface hardware (e.g. by resetFlexRayCC or resetFlexRayCCEx ).


**Syntax:**

```c
void frSetKeySlot (long channel, long channelMask, long keySlotIndex, long keySlotId, long keySlotUsage);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | Channel number (or cluster number) |
| `channelMask` | Channel of the transmission frame. Values: 1 Channel A 2 Channel B 3 Channel A+B |
| `1` | Channel A |
| `2` | Channel B |
| `3` | Channel A+B |
| `keySlotIndex` | Addresses the desired key slot (1 or 2). |
| `keySlotId` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot in the static segment. Its value must be between 1 and 1023. |
| `keySlotUsage` | Defines the mode of the key slot: Value Meaning 0 Off 1 Startup/Sync (Allowing Leading Coldstart) 2 Sync 3 Startup/Sync |
| `Value` | Meaning |
| `0` | Off |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### frSetMode

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetMode.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetMode.htm)

**Description:**

This function initializes the FlexRay bus drivers. Essentially, it defines whether the drivers are set to normal mode or sleep mode. Note If the transceivers had been disabled (sleep mode), and a wakeup pattern is received (see on frPocState ), then the transceivers must explicitly be activated again!


**Syntax:**

```c
frSetMode( int channel, int channelMask, dword mode );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | FlexRay channel (cluster number). |
| `channelMask` | Determines which bus driver is being programmed. Value: 1 Channel A 2 Channel B 3 Channel A+B |
| `1` | Channel A |
| `2` | Channel B |
| `3` | Channel A+B |
| `mode` | Defines the bus driver state: Mode Meaning 0x0000 Normal mode, when starting the Communication Controller a Wakeup will be sent if this is defined in the hardware configuration dialog. 0x0001 Sleep mode 0x0002 Normal mode, when starting the Communication Controller no Wakeup will be sent. |
| `Mode` | Meaning |
| `0x0000` | Normal mode, when starting the Communication Controller a Wakeup will be sent if this is defined in the hardware configuration dialog. |
| `0x0001` | Sleep mode |
| `0x0002` | Normal mode, when starting the Communication Controller no Wakeup will be sent. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on key 'd' { // suspend physical layers: frSetMode(%CHANNEL%, 3, 1); Write("FlexRay physical layers of channel %d are offline (sleep mode).", %CHANNEL%); } on key 'a' { // activate physical layers (with optional wakeup): frSetMode(%CHANNEL%, 3, 0); Write("FlexRay physical layers of channel %d are going online (with optional wakeup to send).", %CHANNEL%); } on key 'q' { //  activate physical layers without any wakeup: frSetMode(%CHANNEL%, 3, 2); Write("FlexRay physical layers of channel %d are going online.", %CHANNEL%); }
```

> The following CAPL program deactivates or activates the physical drivers according to key presses.

*Example 2*

```c
variables { const int cFrChanMask     = 3; // for channel A+B const int cFrModeGoSleep  = 1; const int cFrModeGoNormal = 2; int gClusterSync = -1; int gCntWakeups = 0; } on FRPocState { if (this.MsgChannel != %CHANNEL%) return; if (((gClusterSync == -1) || (gClusterSync == 1)) && ((this.FR_POCState == 4) || (this.FR_PocState == -2))) // FlexRay interface is async { // resetFlexRayCC(%CHANNEL%); gClusterSync = 0; write("FR: Lost Sync Time: %.6f", timenowns()/1000000000.0); gotoSleep(); } else if (((gClusterSync == -1) || (gClusterSync == 0)) && (this.FR_POCState == 2)) // Synchronous again { gClusterSync = 1; write("FR: Get Sync Time: %.6f", timenowns()/1000000000.0); write("FR: received %d Wakeup Symbols", gCntWakeups); gCntWakeups = 0; } if (this.FR_Info2 == 7) { // generated by VN interface receiveWakeup(); } } on start { gotoNormal(); } on frSymbol { if (this.MsgChannel != %CHANNEL%) return; if (this.FR_Symbol == 3) // Wakeup symbol { // generated by FlexCard Cyclone II interface receiveWakeup(); } } void gotoSleep () { frSetMode( %CHANNEL%, cFrChanMask, cFrModeGoSleep); write("FR: Sleep Time: %.6f", timenowns()/1000000000.0); } void gotoNormal () { frSetMode( %CHANNEL%, cFrChanMask, cFrModeGoNormal); write("FR: Wakeup Time: %.6f", timenowns()/1000000000.0); } void receiveWakeup () { gotoNormal(); gCntWakeups++; }
```

> The following CAPL program deactivates or activates the physical drivers according to the clusters synchronisation state and reception of a wakeup symbol.


---

### frSetPayloadLengthInByte

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPayloadLengthInByte.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPayloadLengthInByte.htm)

**Description:**

This function sets the payload (data length) of the object in bytes. In the event of an uneven value, the length of the buffer will be set to the next even value. The payload length can also be set using the FR_PayloadLength frame variables selector. However, in this case, the length is set in 16-bit words. The data length can only be manipulated for frames sent in the dynamic segment!


**Syntax:**

```c
frSetPayloadLengthInByte( <frame var>, dword <dlc> );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<frame var>` | Name of the variable referenced by the frame object. The variable name was defined when the object was created using frFrame . |
| `<dlc>` | Defines the payload (data length) in bytes. |


---

### frSetPduFilter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPDuFilter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPDuFilter.htm)

**Description:**

The function configures a filter for PDUs. With this filter it is possible to receive single PDUs with update bit = 0. The function overrides the network hardware setting Show PDUs with disabled Update Bit (menu path: Configuration|Network Hardware...|FlexRay| Options ) . The advantage of this filter is that only dedicated PDUs with disabled update bit are received instead of all PDUs with disabled update bit in the network.


**Syntax:**

```c
void frSetPduFilter(frPDU message, long mode);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `message` | Name of the PDU to be filtered. |
| `mode` | Configures the filter mode. Value Meaning 0 PDU reception disabled 1 PDU with update bit = 1 will be received 2 PDU will be received, the update bit is not evaluated |
| `Value` | Meaning |
| `0` | PDU reception disabled |
| `1` | PDU with update bit = 1 will be received |
| `2` | PDU will be received, the update bit is not evaluated |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### frSetPOCState

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPocState.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetPocState.htm)

**Description:**

This function puts the FlexRay C ommunication C ontroller (CC) into the desired P rotocol O peration M ode (POC state). The function is non-blocking. That means, the function will return before the CC has reached the desired 
 POC-state. All E-Ray POC-state changes can be monitored with the status- or POC-state-events. A status event is generated as soon as the second CC has reached the desired 
 POC-state. The diagram shows the several states and the corresponding transitions in the protocol operation control process:


**Syntax:**

```c
long frSetPOCState( long channel, long ccNumber, 
 long pocState );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `Channel` | FlexRay channel (cluster number). |
| `ccNumber` | 1: E-Ray 2: Second Startup Controller/Coldstart helper (Fujitsu) |
| `pocState` | Desired POC state: POC State Meaning 0 wakeup 1 normal active 2 halt 3 ready |
| `POC State` | Meaning |
| `0` | wakeup |
| `1` | normal active |
| `2` | halt |
| `3` | ready |


**Return Value:** 0 Error (wrong parameter, or the POC state can not be reached)


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### frSetSendFrame

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFrSetSendFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFrSetSendFrame.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the hardware for sending the specified message. The call must take place in the On preStart routine in the Simulation Setup / transmission branch.


**Syntax:**

```c
void frSetSendFrame( <frame name>, dword flags ); // form 1
```

```c
void frSetSendFrame( int slotId, int channelMask, int len, int cycleStart, int cycleRepetition, dword flags, int channel ); // form 2
```

```c
void frSetSendFrame( <frame object> ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<frame name>` | String that corresponds to a frame name of the database. The necessary parameters slotId , channelMask , len , cycleStart and cycleRepetition are taken from the corresponding frame definition of the database. |
| `slotId` | Identifier of the time slot in which the message should be sent. |
| `channelMask` | Channel of the transmission message. Values: 1 Channel A 2 Channel B 3 Channel A+B |
| `1` | Channel A |
| `2` | Channel B |
| `3` | Channel A+B |
| `len` | Number of databytes (maximum 254 bytes). |
| `cycleStart` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lie in the range between 0 and 63. This value together with the repetition factor determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of the frame. The slot ID and the cycle multiplexing (and the cluster allocation of the FlexRay bus) uniquely identify the message in a FIBEX database. |
| `cycleRepetition` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of the frame. The slot ID and the cycle multiplexing (and the cluster allocation of the FlexRay bus) uniquely identify the message in a FIBEX database. |
| `flags` | Corresponding bits in the flags set or activate special statuses for the frame to be sent: Bitmask Meaning 0x01 Sets the sync bit in the header. Only a frame in the static segment may have set this bit. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. 0x02 Sets the start-up bit in the header. If this bit is set, then the sync bit must also be set. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. 0x10 Sets the send mode to event-triggered. If not set, the frame will be transmitted in time-triggered mode. 0x20 Sets the payload preamble bit. With static frames, the initial data bytes then contain the local NM vector; with dynamic frames, the first two bytes then designate the expanded message ID. 0x100 Inhibit leading cold start. If set, the CC is not allowed to initialize the cluster communication. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. |
| `Bitmask` | Meaning |
| `0x01` | Sets the sync bit in the header. Only a frame in the static segment may have set this bit. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. |
| `0x02` | Sets the start-up bit in the header. If this bit is set, then the sync bit must also be set. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. |
| `0x10` | Sets the send mode to event-triggered. If not set, the frame will be transmitted in time-triggered mode. |
| `0x20` | Sets the payload preamble bit. With static frames, the initial data bytes then contain the local NM vector; with dynamic frames, the first two bytes then designate the expanded message ID. |
| `0x100` | Inhibit leading cold start. If set, the CC is not allowed to initialize the cluster communication. Changes This flag is deprecated! Use function FRSetKeySlot instead. |
| `channel` | Channel number (or cluster number) |


---

### frSetSendGroup

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetSendGroup.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetSendGroup.htm)

**Description:**

Grouped frames, which are transmitted in a CAPL handler (e.g., on frFrame , on timer , etc.), are not written to the CC send buffer until it has been ensured that frames with a payload that has not yet been modified and frames with a modified payload have not been mixed together during a FlexRay cycle. In other words, all frames in 
 the group are sent in the current cycle, or all frames are not sent until the next possible cycle. Restrictions The number of frames per group is restricted to four per FlexRay channel (A, B).


**Syntax:**

```c
void frSetSendGroup (int slotId, int channelMask, 
 int groupNo, int cycleStart, int cycleRepetition, long flags, int channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `slotId` | Slot number of the frame used |
| `channelMask` | FlexRay channel mask Possible values: 1 Channel A 2 Channel B 3 Channel A+B |
| `1` | Channel A |
| `2` | Channel B |
| `3` | Channel A+B |
| `cycleStart, cycleRepetition` | Cycle multiplexing parameter |
| `groupNo` | Group number can be selected as required. |
| `flags` | Reserved, must be 0 |
| `channel` | Channel number (or cluster number) |


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { frFrame MsgChannel1.( 8, 0, 1) gSta1Msg; frFrame MsgChannel1.(40, 0, 1) gSta2Msg; int flags            = 16; // event-driven int channelMask      = 
 1; int payloadlength    = 
 4; int groupNo          = 66; int Cnt              = 0; } on preStart { // register frame 1: gSta1Msg.FR_Channelmask = channelMask; gSta1Msg.FR_Flags = flags; frSetPayloadLengthInByte(gSta1Msg, payloadlength); frSetSendFrame( gSta1Msg ); // add to send group: frSetSendGroup( gSta1Msg.FR_SlotID, gSta1Msg.FR_ChannelMask, groupNo, gSta1Msg.FR_CycleOffset, gSta1Msg.FR_CycleRepetition, 0 ); // register frame 2: gSta2Msg.FR_Channelmask = channelMask; gSta2Msg.FR_Flags = flags; frSetPayloadLengthInByte(gSta2Msg, payloadlength); frSetSendFrame( gSta2Msg ); // add to send group: frSetSendGroup( gSta2Msg.FR_SlotID, gSta2Msg.FR_ChannelMask, groupNo, gSta2Msg.FR_CycleOffset, gSta2Msg.FR_CycleRepetition, 0 ); } on frFrame (10, 0, 1) { // we assume that we receive at least a message // in slot 10 in cycle 0 on channel A: if ((this.FR_Cycle == 0) && (this.FR_ChannelMask == 1)) { // Update is done in cycle 0 only: gSta1Msg.byte(0) = Cnt; gSta2Msg.byte(0) = Cnt; frUpdateStatFrame(gSta1Msg); frUpdateStatFrame(gSta2Msg); Write("Update of Frames in cycle %d with data %d.", this.FR_Cycle, Cnt); if (++Cnt > 255) Cnt = 0; } }
```


---

### frSetSendPDU

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetSendPDU.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRSetSendPDU.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the hardware to transmit the specified PDU. All relevant slots are submitted for transmission. This submission must take place in the On preStart routine in the transmit branch. If a frPDU object was created using frPDU , it can be submitted for transmission 
 with this function.


**Syntax:**

```c
void frSetSendPDU( <PDU object> );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<PDU object>` | Specifies the PDU object that is to be submitted for transmission. |


---

### frSetTrigger

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRTriggerOut.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRTriggerOut.htm)

**Description:**

This function activates the Trigger output of the selected VN interface. The VN interfaces provide three different trigger ports. The ports can be set separately.


**Syntax:**

```c
long frSetTrigger( long channel, long portNo );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | FlexRay channel (cluster number). The channel number identifies the VN interfaces in case several interfaces are active. |
| `portNo` | 1-3: Number of corresponding trigger output. The PIN assignment is defined in the manual of the network interface. |


**Return Value:** 0 Error


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### frUpdatePDU

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRUpdatePDU.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRUpdatePDU.htm)

**Description:**

This function updates the PDU payload in the assigned FlexRay frames. The update bit can also be set.


**Syntax:**

```c
void frUpdatePDU(<PDU var>, dword flags, int updateCounter);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<PDU var>` | Name of the variable referenced by the PDU object. The variable name was defined via frPDU when the object was created. |
| `flags` | Bitmask Meaning 0x01 Update bit is set on transmission. All other bits are reserved and must be set to a value of 0. |
| `Bitmask` | Meaning |
| `0x01` | Update bit is set on transmission. |
| `All other bits are reserved and must be set to a value of 0.` |  |
| `updateCounter` | Value Meaning -1 The PDU is transmitted in every configured slot. 0 The PDU's default value is transmitted. N (>0) The PDU is transmitted at least n times. |
| `Value` | Meaning |
| `-1` | The PDU is transmitted in every configured slot. |
| `0` | The PDU's default value is transmitted. |
| `N (>0)` | The PDU is transmitted at least n times. |


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { frPDU MsgChannel%CHANNEL%.PDU_CNT_02 gPDU1; BYTE gCycle; // remember current FlexRay cycle } on preStart { // Optionally prepare buffer for PDU gPDU1: frSetSendPDU(gPDU1); } on frStartCycle 0 { if (this.MsgChannel != %CHANNEL%) return; gCycle = this.FR_Cycle; gPDU1.counter = gCycle; frUpdatePDU(gPDU1, 1, 1); // set update bit and send only once }
```


---

### frUpdateStatFrame

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRUpdateStatFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFRUpdateStatFrame.htm)

**Description:**

This function updates the FlexRay C ommunication C ontroller's (CC) send buffer with the current data from the send object. This corresponds to a request to send. Only frames in the static segment can be sent using this function!


**Syntax:**

```c
int ret = frUpdateStatFrame( <frame var> );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<frame var>` | Name of the variable referenced by the frame object. The variable name was defined when the object was created using frFrame . |


**Return Value:** 0 Ok, the request to send the frame is forwarded to the interface. This does not guarantee that the frame is really been sent. Refer to the FlexRay protocol rules. -1 Error, either the channel is not available or the slot ID is not in the static segment.


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { // The gMsg1 message is a start-up/sync message for the static segment // that is sent on both channels. frFrame ( 20, 0, 1) gMsg1; const LONG gMsg1Channel  = %CHANNEL%; // send on network the CAPL node is assigned to const LONG gMsg1ChanMask = 3;  // send on FlexRay channel A+B const long gMsg1KeySlotIndex = 1; // use key slot 1 const LONG gMsg1KeySlotID = 20; // send in key slot 20 (static segment) const LONG gMsg1KeySlotUsg = 3; // sync and startup BYTE gMsg1Len; // Payload length BYTE gCycle; // remember current FlexRay cycle frConfiguration gFRParams; } on preStart { // Calculation of Payload length frGetConfiguration(%CHANNEL%, gFRParams); gMsg1Len = gFRParams.gPayloadLengthStatic * 2; // Optionally prepare buffer for message gMsg1: gMsg1.MsgChannel = gMsg1Channel; gMsg1.FR_ChannelMask = gMsg1ChanMask; frSetPayloadLengthInByte(gMsg1, gMsg1Len); frSetKeySlot(gMsg1Channel, gMsg1ChanMask, gMsg1KeySlotIndex, gMsg1KeySlotID, gMsg1KeySlotUsg); } DoFRCalc () { int i; gMsg1.byte(0) = gCycle; // or gMsg1.signame = value; if frame is symbolically declared for (i = 1; i < gMsg1.FR_PayloadLength * 2; ++i) { gMsg1.byte(i) = i; } } DoFRSend () { // Update the message buffer: frUpdateStatFrame(gMsg1); // or FROutputDynFrame(gMsg1); } on frStartCycle * { gCycle = this.FR_Cycle; DoFRCalc(); // calculate and set message contents DoFRSend(); // commit buffer for sending }
```


---

### MessageTimeNS (FR)

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionMessageTimeNS.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionMessageTimeNS.htm)

**Description:**

This function returns the receive time of the frame or slot in nanoseconds.


**Syntax:**

```c
float MessageTimeNS(frSlot slot);
```

```c
float MessageTimeNS(frStartCycle startCycle);
```

```c
float MessageTimeNS(frFrame frame);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `slot/startCycle/frame` | Variable of type frSlot/frStartCycle/frFrame of which to get the receive time. |


**Return Value:** Receive time in nanoseconds since start of measurement.


---

### output (FlexRay)

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFROutput.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionFROutput.htm)

**Description:**

Outputs the object from the program block of the analysis branch. This 
 function must be used inside the appropriate event procedure in order 
 to forward the event to the next block in the analysis branch. If the 
 function is not called, then the event is not forwarded. Thus, events 
 will be filtered by the CAPL program when omitting this function. The function must not be used in the Simulation Setup or transmit branch.


**Syntax:**

```c
void output(flexraymessage msg); // form 1
```

```c
void output(flexraystartcycle msg); // form 2
```

```c
void output(flexraysymbol msg); // form 3
```

```c
void output(flexraypocstate msg); // form 4
```

```c
void output(flexrayerror msg); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `Msg` | Variable of type flexraymessage List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frFrame selectors, on frSlot selectors, on frNullFrame selectors, and on frFrameError selectors. flexraystartcycle List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frStartCycle selectors. flexraysymbol List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frSymbol selectors. flexraypocstate List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frPocState selectors. flexrayerror List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frRError selectors. The parameter variable can be accessed in each event handler by the keyword this . |
| `flexraymessage` | List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frFrame selectors, on frSlot selectors, on frNullFrame selectors, and on frFrameError selectors. |
| `flexraystartcycle` | List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frStartCycle selectors. |
| `flexraysymbol` | List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frSymbol selectors. |
| `flexraypocstate` | List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frPocState selectors. |
| `flexrayerror` | List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under on frRError selectors. |


**Example(s):**

```c
on frFrame * { // do something ... // forward event to next node in Measurement Setup: output(this); }
```


---

### resetFlexRayCC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionResetFlexrayCC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionResetFlexrayCC.htm)

**Description:**

This function initializes the FlexRay C ommunication C ontroller (CC) and begins a new start-up phase for the cluster or a new integrations phase 
 in the cluster - depending on whether a start-up frame is to be sent or not.


**Syntax:**

```c
void resetFlexRayCC (int channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | FlexRay channel (cluster number). |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### resetFlexRayCCEx

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionResetFlexrayCCEX.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Functions/CAPLfunctionResetFlexrayCCEX.htm)

**Description:**

This function initializes the FlexRay C ommunication C ontroller (CC) and generates the specified wakeup pattern before reintegration in the cluster or the start-up.


**Syntax:**

```c
long resetFlexRayCCEx (int channel, int wuChMask, int 
 wuCount, int wuTxIdle, int wuTxLow, char[] cfg);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | FlexRay channel (cluster number) |
| `wuChMask` | Channel for the wakeup pattern. Values: 1 Channel A 2 Channel B |
| `1` | Channel A |
| `2` | Channel B |
| `wuCount` | Number of repetitions (2 – 63) of the wakeup symbol in a wakeup pattern. |
| `wuTxIdle` | This number designates the number of idle bits in a wakeup symbol. According to protocol specification, this should result in 18 µs. |
| `wuTxLow` | This number designates the number of low bits in a wakeup symbol. According to protocol specification, this should result in 6 µs. |
| `cfg` | Character data array. This is currently not used and is of no importance. |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

*Example 1*

```c
on key 'w' { int wuChMask = 3;    // send wakeup on both channels int wuCount  = 4;    // send symbol 2 times (range 2-63) int wuTxIdle = 180;  // idle time of symbol in bit (range 0-255) int wuTxLow = 60;    // low time of symbol in bit (range 0-63) CHAR cfg[1];         // <cfg> -> not used yet resetFlexRayCCEx(%CHANNEL%,wuChMask,wuCount,wuTxIdle,wuTxLow,cfg); Write("FlexRay CC %d is reset and sending a wakeup.", %CHANNEL%); }
```

> The following program resets the FlexRay interface of the attached channel 
 and sends a wakeup (if network is idle), when the <W> key is pressed.

*Example 2*

> In order to prevent the suspension during the call of ResetFlexRayCCEx the reset can be executed asynchronously by manipulating the Protocol Operation Control (POC) state machine of the FlexRay Communication Controller directly. See Reset of the CC by its POC .


---

### frFrame

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFRFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFRFrame.htm)

**Description:**

Can be used to create a FlexRay send object. The object data can be manipulated by selectors associated with this object. Other object characteristics can be read out from selectors. Objects are then registered using the frSetSendFrame and are sent using the frUpdateStatFrame or frOutputDynFrame functions.


**Syntax:**

```c
frFrame <frame name> <frame var>; // form 1
```

```c
frFrame MsgChannel<num>.<frame name> <frame var>; // form 2
```

```c
frFrame (<slot ID>, <base cycle>, <cycle repetition>) <frame var>; // form 3
```

```c
frFrame MsgChannel<num>.(<slot ID>, <base cycle>, <cycle repetition>) <frame var>; // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `MsgChannel<num>` | Defines the send channel/cluster. <num> must be an integer (e.g. 1, 2, 3, etc.) defining the channel number of the corresponding FlexRay interface. |
| `<frame name>` | Character string corresponding to a frame name from the database. The required parameters (<slot ID>, <base cycle>, <cycle repetition> and <channel mask>) are taken from the corresponding frame definition in the database. |
| `<frame var>` | Character string defining the object's variable name. |
| `<slot ID>` | This number designates a specific slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `<base cycle>` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing". |
| `<cycle repetition>` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing". |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

> For an example see function frUpdateStatFrame .

*Example 2*

```c
variables { const dword cFrTTFlag = 0x00; const dword cFrETFlag = 0x10; const dword cFrPPFlag = 0x20; const dword cFrStopFlag = 0x80; // only for VN const dword cFrNullFlag = 0x400; // only for VN } void foo (byte data[], int bytecount) { // evaluate or set the contents of the data array } void example () { frFrame (1,0,1) myFrame  = { FR_Flags = cFrTTFlag | cFrPPFlag, FR_ChannelMask = 1, FR_PayloadLength = 2 }; foo (myFrame.FR_Payload, myFrame.FR_PayloadLength * 2); }
```

> This is an example for forwarding the payload to other functions.

*Example 3*

```c
variables { const dword cFrTTFlag = 0x00; const dword cFrETFlag = 0x10; const dword cFrPPFlag = 0x20; const dword cFrStopFlag = 0x80; // only for VN const dword cFrNullFlag = 0x400; // only for VN frFrame (1,0,1) myFrame  = { FR_Flags = cFrTTFlag | cFrPPFlag, FR_ChannelMask = 1, FR_PayloadLength = 2 }; } void foo (frFrame * frame) { // evaluate or set the contents of the frame object } void example () { foo (myFrame); }
```

> This is an example for using the frame object as a function parameter.

*Example 4*

> This is an example for using the Frame object in arrays.

*Note*

```c
variables { frFrame (1,0,1) frm1; // All objects are equal: frFrame (2,0,1) frArray1[10]; // All objects may be different: frFrame * frArray2[3] = { (3,0,1), <FrameNameFromDB>, frm1 }; }
```

> All objects of an array must be initialized with an appropriate frame definition!


---

### frConfiguration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFRConfiguration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFRConfiguration.htm)

**Description:**

Can be used to create a FlexRay parameter object. The data content of this object is all protocol parameters relevant to FlexRay in the context of initializing a FlexRay Communication Controller. The object data can be manipulated or read out by selectors associated with this object. Initially, all protocol parameters are set to a value of 0. The frGetConfiguration or frSetConfiguration functions are used to read or set parameters from or in the FlexRay interface's Communication Controller.


**Syntax:**

```c
frConfiguration <configuration var>;
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<configuration var>` | Character string defining the object's variable name. |


---

### frPDU

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFrPDU.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/FlexRay/Objects/CAPLfunctionFrPDU.htm)

**Description:**

This can be used to create a FlexRay PDU object. The object data can be manipulated via the object's selectors. Additional object properties can be read from the selectors. The object is then registered using the frSetSendPDU and transmitted using the frUpdatePDU functions.


**Syntax:**

```c
frPDU <FIBEX PDU name> <PDU var>; // form 1
```

```c
frPDU MsgChannel<num>.< FIBEX PDU name> <PDU var>; // form 2
```

```c
frPDU <PDU var>; // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<FIBEX PDU name>` | String that corresponds to a PDU name in the database. The parameters needed to uniquely identify the PDU are taken from the corresponding PDU definition in the database. |
| `<PDU var>` | String that specifies the variable name of the object. |
| `MsgChannel<num>` | Specifies the transmission channel/cluster <num> must be a whole number (e.g. 1, 2, 3, ...) that specifies the channel number of the corresponding FlexRay interface. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

> For an example see function frUpdatePDU .

*Example 2*

```c
void foo (byte[] data, int count) { // evaluate the contents of the data array } void example () { frPDU EngineData myPDU; foo (myPDU.FR_Payload, myPDU.FR_PDULength); }
```

> This is an example for forwarding the payload to other functions.

*Example 3*

```c
variables { frPDU EngineData myPDU; } void foo (FrPDU * pdu) { // evaluate or set the contents of the PDU object } void example () { foo (myPDU); }
```

> This is an example for using the PDU object as a function parameter.

*Example 4*

> This is an example for using the PDU object in arrays.

*Note*

```c
variables { frPDU EngineData pdu1; // All objects are equal: frPDU EngineData frPDUArray1[10]; // All objects may be different: frPDU * frPDUArray2[2] = { EngineStatus, pdu1 }; }
```

> All objects of an array must be initialized with an appropriate PDU definition!

### Debugger

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CANoeCANalyzer/Debugger/Debugger.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CANoeCANalyzer/Debugger/Debugger.htm)

### GetRawSignal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetRawSignal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetRawSignal.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves the current raw value of a signal. These functions can be used for signals with more than 32 bits length. If the signal name is statically known, you can also use $<signal>.raw64 since 7.5 Service Pack 2.


**Syntax:**

```c
Int64 GetRawSignal(dbSignal aSignal); // form 1
```

```c
long GetRawSignal(dbSignal aSignal, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 2
```

```c
Int64 GetRawSignal(char name[]); // form 3
```

```c
long GetRawSignal(char name[], byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dbSignal` | The signal. |
| `data` | Receives the data bytes of the signal. |
| `dataLength` | Length of the data buffer. |


**Return Value:** Form 1 the current raw value of the signal Form 2 Number of bytes written to the data buffer (minimum of dataLength and the size of the signal's data type) -1 on error


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### getSignal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetSignal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetSignal.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the value of a signal. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
float getSignal(Signal aSignal); // form 1
```

```c
float getSignal(char signalName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled. |


**Return Value:** If the signal is available (already appeared on the bus), this function returns the current signal value. If the signal is not yet available (did not appear on the bus until function call), this function returns the Initial Value of the database (GenSigStartValue). If a start value is defined in the Start Values window, or if the old signal value from the last measurement was taken over (options: Values of signal = do not change ), this value is reported.


**Example(s):**

*Example 1
 — Signal is unique*

```c
float value; value = getSignal(OnOff);
```

> You have to use at minimum one more object to identify the signal uniquely. It could be any object from the list of possible objects , see hints for resolving signal ambiguities .

*Example 2
 — Signal is ambiguous*

```c
float value;
//Node and signal value = getSignal(LightSwitch::OnOff);
//Channel and signal value = getSignal(CAN1::Status);
```


---

### getSignalTime

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetSignalTime.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetSignalTime.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the time point (unit: 10 microseconds) relative to the measurement start at which the signal was last sent on the bus. Note It is recommended to use form 1. The runtime environment is faster with form 1, because the signal object is determined during CAPL code compilation before measurement start. With form 2 the signal object is determined during runtime which influences the performance


**Syntax:**

```c
dword getSignalTime(Signal aSignal); // form 1
```

```c
dword  getSignalTime(char signalName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled. |


**Return Value:** Time point or 0 if the signal was not sent yet.


**Example(s):**

```c
dword timePoint; timePoint = getSignalTime(Node_SUT::CrashDetected);
```

### RegisterSignalDriver

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionRegisterSignalDriver.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionRegisterSignalDriver.htm)

**Description:**

Registers the given callback as a 'signal driver' for the signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long RegisterSignalDriver (Signal aSignal , char aCallbackFunction[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | DB signal to be queried. |
| `aCallbackFunction` | Name of a callback function that should be defined as follows: void function(double value). |


**Return Value:** 1 Correct functionality 0 CAPL 'signal driver' could not be registered


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### SetRawSignal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetRawSignal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetRawSignal.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the raw value of a signal. These functions can be used for signals with more than 32 bits length. If the signal name is statically known, you can also use $<signal>.raw64 since 7.5 Service Pack 2.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SetRawSignal(dbSignal aSignal, int64 value); // form 1
```

```c
long SetRawSignal(dbSignal aSignal, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 2
```

```c
long SetRawSignal(char name[], int64 value); // form 3
```

```c
long SetRawSignal(char name[], byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dbSignal` | The signal. |
| `value` | Signal value to be set. |
| `data` | The data bytes of the signal. |
| `dataLength` | Length of the data. |


**Return Value:** Form 2 0 no error -1 general error -3 dataLength is too long (dataLength must correspond to the byte length of the signal)


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### SetSignal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetSignal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetSignal.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the transmitted signal aSignal to the accompanying value. If no suitable signal driver exists and thus no signal can be stimulated, Note


**Syntax:**

```c
void setSignal(Signal aSignal, double aValue); // form 1
```

```c
long setSignal(char signalName[], double aValue); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | Signal to be set. |
| `aValue` | Physical value to be accepted. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1 — Signal is unique*

```c
setSignal(OnOff, 1.0);
```

*Example 2 — Signal is ambiguous*

```c
//Node and signal setSignal(LightSwitch::OnOff, 1.0)
//Channel and signal setSignal(CAN1::Status, 1)
```

> You have to use at minimum one more object to identify the signal uniquely. It could be any object from the list of possible objects , see hints for resolving signal ambiguities .

### checkSignalInRange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionCheckSignalInRange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionCheckSignalInRange.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the signal value or the environment variable value or the system variable value against the condition: aLowLimit <= Value <= aHighLimit Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long checkSignalInRange(Signal aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 1
```

```c
long checkSignalInRange(EnvVarName, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 2
```

```c
long checkSignalInRange(sysVar aSysVar, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 3
```

```c
long checkSignalInRange(sysVar aSysVar, int64 aLowLimit, int64 aHighLimit); // form 4
```

```c
long checkSignalInRange(ServiceSignalNumber aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled. |
| `EnvVarName` | Environment variable to be queried. |
| `aSysVar` | System Variable to be queried. May also be a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array. Note Not available for a single element of a double or integer array. |
| `aLowLimit` | Lower limit of the signal value. |
| `aHighLimit` | Upper limit of the signal value. |


**Return Value:** 1 If the condition is TRUE 0 If the condition is violated or the signal is unavailable, i.e. was not on the bus yet -1 General error -2 Type of the environment or system variable is not valid – only float or integer are valid - or invalid limits of the given range.


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks if the value of the signal is in the given range long result; result = checkSignalInRange (Node_SUT::Velocity, 60, 100); if (result != 1) TestStepFail("Value of signal is not in the allowed range!");
```


---

### testValidateSignalInRange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalInRange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalInRange.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the value of the signal, the environment variable value or the system variable value against the condition: aLowLimit <= Value <= aHighLimit The test step is evaluated as either passed or failed depending on the results Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long testValidateSignalInRange (char aTestStep[], Signal aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 1
```

```c
long testValidateSignalInRange (char aTestStep[], EnvVarName, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 2
```

```c
long testValidateSignalInRange (char aTestStep[], sysvar aSysVar, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 3
```

```c
long testValidateSignalInRange (char aTestStep[], sysvar aSysVar, int64 aLowLimit, int64 aHighLimit); // form 4
```

```c
long testValidateSignalInRange (char aTestStep[], ServiceSignalNumber aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aTestStep` | Name of the test step for the test report |
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled |
| `EnvVarName` | Environment variable to be queried |
| `aSysVar` | System variable to be queried. May also be a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array. Note Not available for a single element of a double or integer array. |
| `aLowLimit` | Lower limit of the value |
| `aHighLimit` | Upper limit of the value |


**Return Value:** -1 General error -2 Type of the environment or system variable is not valid – only float or integer are valid- or invalid limits of the given range 0 Correct functionality


**Example(s):**

```c
// validates the value of the signal against the given range long result; result = testValidateSignalInRange ("Check Velocity", Node_SUT::Velocity, 60, 100); if (result != 0) TestStepFail("Error occurred!");
```


---

### testValidateSignalOutsideRange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalOutsideRange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalOutsideRange.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the signal value, the environment variable value or the system variable value against the condition: The test step is evaluated as passed or failed depending on the results. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long testValidateSignalOutsideRange (char aTestStep[], Signal aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 1
```

```c
long testValidateSignalOutsideRange (char aTestStep[], EnvVarName, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit);  (effective with CANoe 6.0); // form 2
```

```c
long testValidateSignalOutsideRange (char aTestStep[], sysVar aSysVar, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); (effective with CANoe 7.0); // form 3
```

```c
long testValidateSignalOutsideRange (char aTestStep[], sysVar aSysVar, int64 aLowLimit, int64 aHighLimit); // form 4
```

```c
long testValidateSignalOutsideRange (char aTestStep[], ServiceSignalNumber aSignal, float aLowLimit, float aHighLimit); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aTestStep` | Name of the test step for the test report |
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled |
| `EnvVarName` | Environment variable to be queried |
| `aSysVar` | System Variable to be queried. May also be a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array. Note Not available for a single element of a double or integer array. |
| `aLowLimit` | Lower limit of the value |
| `aHighLimit` | Upper limit of the value |


**Return Value:** -1 General error -2 Type of the environment or system variable is not valid – only float or integer are valid- or invalid limits of the given range 0 Correct functionality


**Example(s):**

```c
// validates the value of the signal against the given range long result; result = testValidateSignalOutsideRange ("Check Velocity", Node_SUT::Velocity, 60, 100); if (result != 0) TestStepFail("Error occurred!");
```

### CheckSignalMatch

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionCheckSignalMatch.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionCheckSignalMatch.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the given value against the value of signal, the system or the environment variable. The resolution of the signal is considered.


**Syntax:**

```c
long CheckSignalMatch(Signal aSignal, float aCompareValue); // form 1
```

```c
long CheckSignalMatch(dbEnvVar aEnvVar, float aCompareValue); // form 2
```

```c
long CheckSignalMatch(sysvar aSysVar, float aCompareValue); // form 3
```

```c
long CheckSignalMatch(sysvar aSysVar, int64 aCompareValue); // form 4
```

```c
long CheckSignalMatch(ServiceSignalNumber aSignal, float aCompareValue); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled. |
| `aEnvVar` | The environment variable to be polled. |
| `aSysVar` | The system variable to be polled. May also be a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array. Note Not available for a single element of a double or integer array. |
| `aCompareValue` | Value which is compared to the signal or system variable value. |


**Return Value:** 1 If the condition is TRUE 0 If the condition is violated or the signal or system variable is unavailable, i.e. was not on the bus yet -1 General error


**Example(s):**

*Example1*

```c
// checks if the value of the signal or system variable matches a specified value long result; result = CheckSignalMatch (Node_SUT::Velocity, 80); if (result != 1) TestStepFail("Value of signal matches not the value '80'!");
```

*Example 2*

```c
testfunction SignalCheck(signal * sig, float compareValue) { // checks if the value of the signal matches a specified value long result; result = CheckSignalMatch (sig, compareValue); if (result != 1) TestStepFail("Value of signal matches not the value"); }
```


---

### TestValidateSignalMatch

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalMatch.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestValidateSignalMatch.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the given value against the value of the signal, the system variable or the environment variable. The resolution of the signal is considered. The test step is evaluated as either passed or failed depending on the results.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestValidateSignalMatch (char aTestStep[], Signal aSignal, float aCompareValue); // form 1
```

```c
long TestValidateSignalMatch (char aTestStep[], dbEnvVar aEnvVar, float aCompareValue); // form 2
```

```c
long TestValidateSignalMatch (char aTestStep[], sysvar aSysVar, float aCompareValue); // form 3
```

```c
long TestValidateSignalMatch (char aTestStep[], sysvar aSysVar, int64 aCompareValue); // form 4
```

```c
long TestValidateSignalMatch (char aTestStep[], ServiceSignalNumber aSignal, float aCompareValue); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aTestStep` | Name of the test step for the test report. |
| `aSignal` | The signal to be polled. |
| `aEnvVar` | The environment variable to be polled. |
| `aSysVar` | System Variable to be polled. May also be a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array. Note Not available for a single element of a double or integer array. |
| `aCompareValue` | Value which is compared to the signal value. |


**Return Value:** 0 Correct functionality -1 General error


**Example(s):**

```c
// validates the value of the signal against the given value long result; result = TestValidateSignalMatch ("Check Velocity", Node_SUT::Velocity, 80);
```

### TestGetWaitEventMsgData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventMsgData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventMsgData.htm)

**Description:**

If a message event is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the message content can be called up with the first function. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (message aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (dword index, message aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (linFrame aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (dword index, linFrame aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (gmLanMessage aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (dword index, gmLanMessage aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (a429word aMessage);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventMsgData (dword index, a429word aMessage);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aMessage` | Message variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not a message event


**Example(s):**

```c
// add msg event to the current set of "joined events" and fill the msg data to message 'eventMessage' dword index = 0; TestJoinMessageEvent(VehicleMotion); // ... other join events index = TestWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(2000); TestGetWaitEventMsgData (index, eventMessage);
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinCSErrorData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinCsErrData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinCsErrData.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves the data of a checksum error triggered by the last wait instruction. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinCSErrorData (linCSError apEvent); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinCSErrorData (dword index, linCSError apEvent); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Data structure with the checksum error data. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access was successful -1 Data access failed – the last wait function was not triggered by a checksum error


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linCSError () { linCSError linCSErrorData; if (testWaitForLinCSError(5000) == 1) { if (testGetWaitLinCSErrorData(linCSErrorData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN CS Error event occurred for FrameId=0x%X", linCSErrorData.ID); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinETFSingleResponseData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinEtfSingleResponseData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinEtfSingleResponseData.htm)

**Description:**

If LIN Event-triggered frame with a single response for the specified associated frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the event content can be called up with this function. When the triggering event is a part of a wait instruction for joined events, the index parameter has to correspond to the return value of " testJoin... " function.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinETFSingleResponseData (linFrame apEvent);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinETFSingleResponseData (dword index, linFrame apEvent);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Event variable that is filled with current data from the bus. |
| `index` | Index of the joined event. It corresponds to the return value of " testJoin... " function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an awaited event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linETFSingleResponse () { linFrame 0 linETFSingleResponseData; if (testWaitForLinETFSingleResponse(0x3A, 0x36, 5000) == 1) { if (testGetWaitLinETFSingleResponseData(linETFSingleResponseData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN Event-triggered frame with a single response occurred for FrameId=0x%X", linETFSingleResponseData.ID); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinHdrData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinHdrData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinHdrData.htm)

**Description:**

If LIN Header event is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the header content can be called up with the first function. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinHdrData (linheader aHeader);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinHdrData (dword index, linheader aHeader);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aHeader` | Header variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not a LIN header event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linHdrEvent () { linheader  linHeaderData; if (testWaitForLinHeader(5000) == 1) { if (TestGetWaitLinHdrData(linHeaderData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN Header event occurred for FrameId=0x%X", linHeaderData.ID); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData.htm)

**Description:**

If LIN Receive Error event is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the error content can be called up with the first function. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData (linReceiveError apEvent); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData (dword index, linReceiveError apEvent); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Event variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an awaited event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linReceiveErrorEvent () { linReceiveError linReceiveErrorData; if (testWaitForLinReceiveError(5000) == 1) { if (TestGetWaitLinReceiveErrData(linReceiveErrorData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN Receive Error event occurred for FrameId=0x%X", linReceiveErrorData.ID); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinSyncErrorData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinSyncErrorData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinSyncErrorData.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves the data of a synchronisation error that triggered the last wait instruction. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinSyncErrorData (LinSyncError apEvent); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinSyncErrorData (dword index, LinSyncError apEvent); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Data structure that will be filled with the synchronisation error data. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access was successful -1 Data access failed – the last wait function was not triggered by a synchronisation error


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linSyncErrorEvent () { linSyncError linSyncErrorData; if (testWaitForLinSyncError(5000) == 1) { if (testGetWaitLinSyncErrorData(linSyncErrorData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN Sync Error event occurred. SyncBreak=%d ns; SyncDel=%d ns", linSyncErrorData.breaklen, linSyncErrorData.delimiterlen); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinTransmErrData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinTransmErrData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinTransmErrData.htm)

**Description:**

If LIN Transmission Error event is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the error content can be called up with the first function. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinTransmErrData (linTransmError apEvent); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinTransmErrData (dword index, linTransmError apEvent); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Event variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an awaited event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linNoResponseEvent () { linTransmError linTransmErrorData; if (testWaitForLinTransmError(5000) == 1) { if (testGetWaitLinTransmErrData(linTransmErrorData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN No-Response event occurred for FrameId=0x%X", linTransmErrorData.ID); } } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitLinWakeupData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinWakeupData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitLinWakeupData.htm)

**Description:**

If LIN Wakeup frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the frame content can be called up with the first function. The second function can only be used for "joined events". The number of the "joined event" (return value of " testJoin... ") is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitLinWakeupData (linWakeupFrame apEvent); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitLinWakeupData (dword index, linWakeupFrame apEvent); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apEvent` | Event variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the "joined event" corresponds with the return value of " testJoin... ". |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an awaited event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_linWakeupEvent () { linWakeupFrame linWakeupData; if (testWaitForLinWakeupFrame(5000) == 1) { if (testGetWaitLinWakeupData(linWakeupData) == 0) { testStep("Evaluation", "LIN Wakeup event occurred. Signal length is %d ns", linWakeupData.length_ns); } } }
```


---

### TestJoinLinCSErrorEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinCsErrorEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinCsErrorEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Adds an event of type checksum error to the set of joined events. This is a non-blocking function. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinCSErrorEvent()
```

```c
long TestJoinLinCSErrorEvent(dbMsg apDBMsg)
```

```c
long TestJoinLinCSErrorEvent(dword aFrameId)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `apDBMsg` | Symbolic message |
| `aFrameId` | Frame identifier |


**Return Value:** -3 Error while adding the specified event to the set of joined events -1 General error, e.g. functionality is not available > 0 Number of the newly joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINResponse(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinMessageEvent(frameId); testJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinCSErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinTransmErrorEvent(frameId); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: // valid frame testStepPass("Validation", "IUT has responded correctly"); break; case 2:  // receive error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong number of data bytes"); break; case 3: // checksum error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong checksum"); break; case 4: // transmission error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has not responded"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for response", eventIndex); } }
```


---

### TestJoinLinETFSingleResponseEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinEtfSingleResponseEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinEtfSingleResponseEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Adds an event of type LIN Event-triggered Frame Single Response to the set of joined events. This is a non-blocking function. Note Consider to set always the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment before the function is called. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinETFSingleResponseEvent (dword aETFFrameId, dword aAssocFrameId)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aETFFrameId` | Frame identifier of LIN event-triggered frame to be awaited. |
| `aAssocFrameId` | Frame identifier of a LIN unconditional frame associated with the awaited event-triggered frame. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLinETFSingleResponse(int etfFrameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinLinETFSingleResponseEvent(etfFrameId, linGetProtectedID(0x36)); testJoinLinETFSingleResponseEvent(etfFrameId, linGetProtectedID(0x34)); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: testStepPass("Validation", "ETF single frame response occurred. FrameId=0x36"); break; case 2: testStepPass("Validation", "ETF single frame response occurred. FrameId=0x34"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "ETF single frame response not occurred"); } }
```


---

### TestJoinLinHeaderEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinHeaderEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinHeaderEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of "joined events" with the transmitted event. This function does not wait. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinHeaderEvent (dbMsg aFrame)
```

```c
long TestJoinLinHeaderEvent (dword aFrameId)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aFrame` | Frame whose header should be awaited. |
| `aFrameId` | Numeric ID of a frame whose header should be awaited. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINHeader(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinLinHeaderEvent(frameId); testJoinLinWakeupEvent(); testJoinLinSyncErrorEvent(); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: testStepPass("Validation", "LIN Header for FrameId=0x%X occurred", frameId); break; case 2: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Wakeup signal occurred"); break; case 3: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Sync error occurred"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for any LIN event", eventIndex); } }
```


---

### TestJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of "joined events" with the transmitted event. This function does not wait. Note Consider to set always the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment before the function is called. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent (dword aFrameId)
```

```c
long TestJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent ()
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aFrameId` | Numeric ID of a frame whose Receive Error should be awaited. Default value: wait for any ID. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINResponse(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinMessageEvent(frameId); testJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinCSErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinTransmErrorEvent(frameId); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: // valid frame testStepPass("Validation", "IUT has responded correctly"); break; case 2:  // receive error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong number of data bytes"); break; case 3: // checksum error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong checksum"); break; case 4: // transmission error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has not responded"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for response", eventIndex); } }
```


---

### TestJoinLinSyncErrorEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinSyncErrorEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinSyncErrorEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Adds an event of type synchronisation error to the set of joined events. This is a non-blocking function. Note Consider to set always the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment before the function is called. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinSyncErrorEvent()
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** -3 error while adding the specified event to the set of joined events -1 general error, e.g. functionality is not available > 0 number of the newly joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINHeader(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinLinHeaderEvent(frameId); testJoinLinWakeupEvent(); testJoinLinSyncErrorEvent(); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: testStepPass("Validation", "LIN Header for FrameId=0x%X occurred", frameId); break; case 2: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Wakeup signal occurred"); break; case 3: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Sync error occurred"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for any LIN event", eventIndex); } }
```

### TestJoinLinTransmErrorEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinTransmErrorEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinTransmErrorEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of "joined events" with the transmitted event. This function does not wait. Note Consider to set always the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment before the function is called. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinTransmErrorEvent (dword aFrameId)
```

```c
long TestJoinLinTransmErrorEvent ()
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aFrameId` | Numeric ID of a frame whose Transmission Error should be awaited. Default value: wait for any ID. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINResponse(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinMessageEvent(frameId); testJoinLinReceiveErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinCSErrorEvent(frameId); testJoinLinTransmErrorEvent(frameId); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: // valid frame testStepPass("Validation", "IUT has responded correctly"); break; case 2:  // receive error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong number of data bytes"); break; case 3: // checksum error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has responded with wrong checksum"); break; case 4: // transmission error testStepFail("Validation", "IUT has not responded"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for response", eventIndex); } }
```

### TestJoinLinWakeupEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinWakeupEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinLinWakeupEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of "joined events" with the transmitted event. This function does not wait. Note Consider to set always the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment before the function is called. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinLinWakeupEvent ()
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcTFS_waitForLINHeader(int frameId) { long eventIndex; testJoinLinHeaderEvent(frameId); testJoinLinWakeupEvent(); testJoinLinSyncErrorEvent(); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: testStepPass("Validation", "LIN Header for FrameId=0x%X occurred", frameId); break; case 2: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Wakeup signal occurred"); break; case 3: testStepFail("Validation", "LIN Sync error occurred"); break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Internal error! Unexpected event (return code %d) on waiting for any LIN event", eventIndex); } }
```
