### Occurrence Of Messages

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDOccurrenceOfMessages.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDOccurrenceOfMessages.htm)

**Description:**

This check is used to monitor the minimum and maximum distance of messages. This check is well-suited even for mixed, cyclic and spontaneous transmission modes. When setting up the check the times to be monitored are calculated from the message's cycle time (minimum of GenMsgCycleTime and GenSigCycleTime) , a minimum send delay (GenMsgDelayTime) and relative time entries. The check can be set up for one message or for all Tx messages of a node. When setting up the check for a node, only those Tx messages for which periodic transmission is defined are considered. This involves looking at the attributes GenMsgSendType (Enum values: 0, 4, 5) and GenSigSendType (Enum value 0). If the message attribute or the signal attribute of a signal of this message is set to one of the named Enum values, this message is monitored by the check; otherwise it is ignored. The cycle period of the message is calculated according to the absolutely scheduled timing of the FlexRay frame. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation, ChkStart_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation ChkCreate_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation, ChkStart_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation OccurenceTimeCheck This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures ✔ Number of received messages with invalid send distance. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.) Number of samples ✔ Number of checked messages. The following parameters and statistic data are available for each observed message Parameter Availability (since version) Description Cycle time ✔ Cycle time of the observed message (from network database). Delay time ✔ Delay time of the observed message (from network database). Failure ratio (in %) ✔ Ratio of receiving the observed message with invalid send distance. Ratio is calculated from the number of failures of the observed message and the number of samples of the observed message. Distribution of measured times ✔ The following table demonstrates the distribution of the measured time distances of the observed message.


---

### ChkCreate_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation, ChkStart_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateMsgRelOccurrenceViolation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateMsgRelOccurrenceViolation.htm)

**Description:**

Checks for the occurrence of a periodic message. The check condition is violated if the time between transmissions of the message is less than aMinRelCycleTime * GenMsgDelayTime or greater than aMaxRelCycleTime * Cycle Time. Cycle time is calculated from GenMsgCycleTime and GenSigCycleTime. Limits that should not be checked must be set to 0. At least one limit must be specified. Can only be started in the "on start" area of CAPL or during the measurement. However, the check may be set up as early as in the "pre start" area. The numeric functions/constructors with the parameter 'aMessageId' cannot be used for FlexRay. Instead use the numeric constructors with the parameter 'slotID' (that can only be applied to a FlexRay bus). Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation(dbMsg observedMsg, double aMinRelCycleTime, double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCallback);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation(dbMsg observedMsg, double aMin-RelCycleTime, double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCallback);
```

```c
dword ChkCreate_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation(dword aMessageId, double  aMinRelCycleTime, double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCallback)
```

```c
dword ChkStart_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation(dword aMessageId, double aMinRelCycleTime , double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCllback)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `observedMessage` | The observed message in symbolic form, e.g.: "EngineData", whose occurrence is to be monitored. Message must exist in database. |
| `aMinRelCycleTime` | 0: Limit is not checked. 0 < x < 1: Limit is checked. |
| `aMaxRelCycleTime` | 0: Limit is not checked. 1 < x < ∞: Limit is checked. |
| `aCallback` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |
| `aMessageId` | message ID to be observed. The corresponding message shall be defined in the database. |
| `slotID` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `cycleOffs` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `cycleRep` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 
 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `channelMask` | Identifies the FlexRay channel of the communication controller. A value of 1 will check the 
 frame on channel A, 2 will check it on channel B and 3 on any channel (A/B). |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and may not be referenced. > 0 Check was created successfully and can be referenced by the returned (Handle) value.


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks the periodic occurrence of the message checkId = ChkStart_MsgRelOccurrenceViolation (MsgToObserve, 0.9, 1.1); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```


---

### ChkCreate_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation, ChkStart_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateNodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateNodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation.htm)

**Description:**

Checks for the occurrence of periodic message of the specified send node. The check condition is violated if the time between transmissions of the message is less than aMinRelCycleTime * GenMsgDelayTime or greater than aMaxRelCycleTime * Cycle Time. Cycle time is calculated from GenMsgCycleTime and GenSigCycleTime. Limits that should not be checked must be set to 0. At least one limit must be specified. Can only be started in the "on start" area of CAPL or during the measurement. However, the check may be set up as early as in the "pre start" area. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation(Node observedNode, double aMinRelCycleTime, double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCallback);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation(Node observedNode, double aMinRelCycleTime, double aMaxRelCycleTime, char[] aCallback);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `observedNode` | Must exist in the database. |
| `aminRelCycleTime` | 0: Limit is not checked. 0 < x < 1: Limit is checked. |
| `aMaxRelCycleTime` | 0: Limit is not checked. 1 < x < ∞: Limit is checked. |
| `aCallback` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and may not be referenced. > 0 Check was created successfully and can be referenced with the help of the returned (Handle) value.


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks the periodic occurrence of all messages of the node checkId = ChkStart_NodeMsgsRelOccurrenceViolation (NodeToObserve, 0.9, 1.1); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```

### Message Distance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessageDistance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessageDistance.htm)

**Description:**

This check is useful for spontaneous messages where one message depends to another message; e.g. for token-ring initializations for network management. The following figure shows different constellations of message receipts. Each case describes one "session", that means that after each session the check starts from the beginning. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version Test Command CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ Message Distance ChkCreate_MsgDistViolation, ChkStart_MsgDistViolation —

### Absence of FlexRay Erroneous Frames

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDAbsenceOfFlexRayErroneosFrames.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDAbsenceOfFlexRayErroneosFrames.htm)

**Description:**

The check is used to monitor that certain erroneous frames are not sent on the FlexRay bus. It can also be specified how often a erroneous frame may occur until the check fails. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ TestCheck::CreateFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount TestCheck::CreateNodeFlexRayFrameErrorsOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartNodeFlexRayFrameErrorsOccurrenceCount — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures ✔ Number of check failures due to transmission of observed message. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.) Number of samples ✔ Number of transmitted messages. (This data record is only available if at least one probe is available.) Occurrence of each message ✔ Below this row all observed messages with the number of occurrence are listed. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.)


---

### TestCheck::CreateFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::CreateNodeFlexRayFrameErrorsOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartNodeFlexRayFrameErrorsOccurrenceCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the absence of erroneous frames for the specified frame/slot on the bus. An event is created if more than aMaxCount of the specified erroneous frames were sent. If a DB node is used as reference, then all its Tx frames are observed. The numerical functions allow to define a wildcard event by setting slotID = -1, cycleOffs = 0, cycleRep = 1 in order to check for the absence of any FlexRay Erroneous Frame. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `slotID` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `cycleOffs` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `cycleRep` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 
 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `channelMask` | Identifies the FlexRay channel of the communication controller. A value of 1 will check the 
 frame on channel A, 2 will check it on channel B and 3 on any channel (A/B). |
| `aNode` | The node from the DB whose Tx messages should be observed. |
| `aMaxCount` | The maximum number of message that may be sent without the check to fail. |
| `aMessage` | The message in symbolic form, e.g.: "EngineData", whose occurrence is to be monitored. Message must exist in database. |
| `aCaplCallbackFunction` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |


**Return Value:** Object of type TestCheck .


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { const dword cTesttime = 640; // [ms] per check FrFrame MsgChannel1.(10,0,2) frame10 = { FR_Flags = 0, FR_ChannelMask = 1, FR_PayloadLength = 2 }; const dword cFrFlagTT      = 0x00; const dword cFrFlagET      = 0x10; const dword cFrFlagStop    = 0x80; } testcase GoodCheckFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount_1 () { TestCheck c; long cMaxCount = 0; // Assure check to succeed: frame10.FR_Flags = cFrFlagTT; // start sending the frame FRUpdateStatFrame(frame10); TestWaitForTimeout(12); c = TestCheck::CreateFlexRayFrameErrorOccurrenceCount( frame10.FR_SlotID, frame10.FR_CycleOffset, frame10.FR_CycleRepetition, frame10.FR_ChannelMask, cMaxCount); TestAddCondition(c); c.start(); TestWaitForTimeout(cTesttime); TestRemoveCondition(c); }
```


---

The check is used to monitor that certain Null frames are not sent on the FlexRay bus. It can also be specified how often a defined Null frame may occur until the check fails. Number of failures ✔ Number of check failures due to transmission of observed message. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.) Number of samples ✔ Number of transmitted messages. (This data record is only available if at least one probe is available.) Occurrence of each message ✔ Below this row all observed messages with the number of occurrence are listed. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.)

### TestCheck::CreateFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::CreateNodeFlexRayNullFramesOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartNodeFlexRayNullFramesOccurrenceCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the absence of Null Frames for the specified frame/slot on the bus. An event is created if more than aMaxCount of the specified Null Frames were sent. If a DB node is used as reference, then all its Tx frames are observed. The numerical functions allow to define a wildcard event by setting slotID = -1, cycleOffs = 0, cycleRep = 1 in order to check for the absence of any FlexRay Null Frame. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `slotID` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `cycleOffs` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `cycleRep` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 
 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `channelMask` | Identifies the FlexRay channel of the communication controller. A value of 1 will check the 
 frame on channel A, 2 will check it on channel B and 3 on any channel (A/B). |
| `aMaxCount` | The maximum number of message that may be sent without the check to fail. |
| `aNode` | The node from the DB whose Tx messages should be observed. |
| `aMessage` | The message in symbolic form, e.g.: "EngineData", whose occurrence is to be monitored. Message must exist in database. |
| `aCaplCallbackFunction` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |


**Return Value:** Object of type TestCheck .


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { const dword cTesttime = 640; // [ms] per check FrFrame MsgChannel1.(10,0,2) frame10 = { FR_Flags = 0, FR_ChannelMask = 1, FR_PayloadLength = 2 }; const dword cFrFlagTT      = 0x00; const dword cFrFlagET      = 0x10; const dword cFrFlagStop    = 0x80; } testcase GoodCheckFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount_1 () { TestCheck c; long cMaxCount = 0; // Assure check to succeed: frame10.FR_Flags = cFrFlagStop; // stop sending the frame FRUpdateStatFrame(frame10); TestWaitForTimeout(12); c = TestCheck::CreateFlexRayNullFrameOccurrenceCount( frame10.FR_SlotID, frame10.FR_CycleOffset, frame10.FR_CycleRepetition, frame10.FR_ChannelMask, cMaxCount); TestAddCondition(c); c.start(); TestWaitForTimeout(cTesttime); TestRemoveCondition(c); }
```

### Message Count Observation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessageCountObservation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessageCountObservation.htm)

**Description:**

Monitors the bus and reports if a minimum and/or a maximum number for each of the defined messages occurred within a specified cyclic time interval. It is also allowed to specify one node as reference. In this case, all Tx messages of the node are observed. Then, an occurrence is counted per defined message. That means, for example, if maxcount was set to two, and four different Tx messages were specified as reference, the check fails if any of the messages was sent three times. In contrast to the Occurrence Count (Check Description) check there is not only one counter per check, but one counter per defined message. Additionally a minimum and maximum number of allowed/required messages is observed. When the time interval expires, the minimum counter values are checked, each counter is reset to zero and the interval starts over again. This check is only defined for FlexRay frames and PDUs. Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ TestCheck::CreateMsgSendCountViolation, TestCheck::StartMsgSendCountViolation TestCheck::CreateNodeMsgSendCountViolation, TestCheck::StartNodeMsgSendCountViolation —


---

### TestCheck::CreateMsgSendCountViolation, TestCheck::StartMsgSendCountViolation, TestCheck::CreateNodeMsgSendCountViolation, TestCheck::StartNodeMsgSendCountViolation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateMsgSendCountViolation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestCheckCreateMsgSendCountViolation.htm)

**Description:**

Monitors the bus and reports if at least aMinCount and at most aMaxCount for each of the defined messages occurred within a specified time interval aInterval . If a DB node is used as reference, then all its Tx frames are observed. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `slotID` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `cycleOffs` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `cycleRep` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `channelMask` | Identifies the FlexRay channel of the communication controller. A value of 1 will check the frame on channel A, 2 will check it on channel B and 3 on any channel (A/B). |
| `aMinCount` | The minimum number of message that must be sent without the check to fail. |
| `aMaxCount` | The maximum number of message that may be sent without the check to fail. ('0' means infinite number.) |
| `aInterval` | Defines the cyclic repeating time period in which the minimum and maximum number of defined messages must occur without the check failing. The unit can be set with ChkConfig_SetPrecision . |
| `aNode` | The node from the DB whose Tx messages should be observed. |
| `aMessage` | The message in symbolic form, e.g.: "EngineData", whose occurrence is to be monitored. Message must exist in database. |
| `aCaplCallbackFunction` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |


**Return Value:** CheckId [dword] 0 Check could not be created and may not be referenced. > 0 Check was created successfully and can be referenced with the help of the returned (Handle) value.


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { const dword cTesttime = 640; // [ms] per check // Frames that are sent by node Controller_Node_1: FrFrame MsgChannel1.Sync_Message_1_Ch_A      frame2; FrFrame MsgChannel1.Sync_Message_1_Ch_B      frame2b; FrFrame MsgChannel1.TimeSync_Message_1_Ch_A  frame15; const dword cFrFlagTT      = 0x00; const dword cFrFlagET      = 0x10; const dword cFrFlagStop    = 0x80; } testcase GoodCheckNodeMsgSendCountViolation_1 () { TestCheck c; dword cMinCount = 1; dword cMaxCount = 0; // ignore dword cInterval = 320; // [ms] // Assure check to succeed: frame2.FR_Flags = cFrFlagTT; // start sending the frame FRUpdateStatFrame(frame2); frame2b.FR_Flags = cFrFlagTT; // start sending the frame FRUpdateStatFrame(frame2b); frame15.FR_Flags = cFrFlagTT; // start sending the frame FRUpdateStatFrame(frame15); TestWaitForTimeout(330); c = TestCheck::CreateNodeMsgSendCountViolation( Controller_Node_1, cMinCount, cMaxCount, cInterval ); TestAddCondition(c); c.start(); TestWaitForTimeout(cTesttime); TestRemoveCondition(c); }
```


---

### Messages Known

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessagesKnown.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDMessagesKnown.htm)

**Description:**

This check observes the current bus and reports messages that are not defined in any of the databases that are associated to the bus. This check additionally checks some consistence of the databases. Multiply defined messages (of the same id) are reported by a warning notification during creation of the check. For FlexRay only valid data frames are recognized as communication, Null frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Effective with CANoe 8.1 SP2 for a FlexRay PDU database (FIBEX ≥ 3.0 or AUTOSAR System Description) the mapped frames of all PDUs will be observed to be known. The check will fail on receiving any other frame. Before that CANoe version the check will always succeed for a FlexRay PDU database, because only the PDUs will be observed to be valid. (Unknown PDUs does not exist.) Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_UndefinedMessageReceived, ChkStart_UndefinedMessageReceived — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures ✔ Number of check failures due to transmission of unknown messages. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.) Number of samples ✔ Number of all transmitted messages. Failure ratio (in %) ✔ Ratio of check failure due to unknown messages. Ratio is calculated from number of failures and number of samples. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure is available.) Unknown messages and their occurrence ✔ Below this row all undefined messages with their number of occurrence are listed. (These data records are only available if at least one check failure is available.)


---

### ChkCreate_UndefinedMessageReceived, ChkStart_UndefinedMessageReceived

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateUndefinedMessageReceived.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateUndefinedMessageReceived.htm)

**Description:**

Observes the current bus and reports messages that are not defined. If the CANoe configuration contains multiple buses, the current bus context has to be specified ( SetBusContext ) before the check configuration. For FlexRay either only valid data frames or PDUs (according to the database type) are recognized as communication. Null Frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Note on FlexRay PDU For a FlexRay PDU database this check also ignores all received frames! For a PDU database only PDUs are considered. For a FlexRay PDU database this test makes no sense, because only those PDUs, which are defined in the database, can be retrieved from frames. There cannot exist PDUs that are not defined in the database! Therefore, for a PDU database this check will always pass. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_UndefinedMessageReceived (char [] CaplCallback); // form 1
```

```c
dword ChkStart_UndefinedMessageReceived (char [] CaplCallback); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `CaplCallback` | In simulation nodes this parameter has to be set. In test modules this parameter is optional. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and must not be referenced > 0 Check was created successfully and may be referenced using the returned (handle-) value


**Example(s):**

```c
// observes the bus 'CAN1' for undefined messages SetBusContext(getBusNameContext("CAN1")); checkId = ChkStart_UndefinedMessageReceived (); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId); checkId = ChkCreate_UndefinedMessageReceived("CallbackUnknownMsg");
```


---

### Node Active

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNodeActive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNodeActive.htm)

**Description:**

This check allows the supervision of the activity of nodes. It reports a problem, if the node has not sent any of the configured Tx messages within a given time-interval. Use case: checks if the node still sends any message, e.g. an "alive" message. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_AllNodesDead, ChkStart_AllNodesDead ChkCreate_NodeDead, ChkStart_NodeDead — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of received messages ✔ Number of all received messages.


---

### Node Inactive

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNodeInactive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNodeInactive.htm)

**Description:**

This check allows the observation of the end of the activity of nodes. After the time interval, in which sending is allowed, no further message of the node or the CAN message list should be sent. From this time point forward each transmission of a message leads to a check failure and is reported. This is useful for shut-down phases or for the start of sleep active phases. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_AllNodesBabbling, ChkStart_AllNodesBabbling ChkCreate_NodeBabbling, ChkStart_NodeBabbling — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Nodes with number of transmitted messages ✔ Below this row all observed nodes with the number of transmitted messages are listed. Transmitted messages (without send node) ✔ Below this row all messages that have no transmitter specified are listed with the number of their occurrences. Last transmission ✔ Time of last transmission after start of check.


---

### ChkCreate_AllNodesBabbling, ChkStart_AllNodesBabbling

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateAllNodesBabbling.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateAllNodesBabbling.htm)

**Description:**

There is a time interval where transmissions are tolerated. After the interval has been passed, all nodes may no longer send messages. From now on, each transmission of the nodes is reported. If the min. quiet time is 0, then each message sent by any node leads to the event. Possible application: Supervises the end of a communication. Supervises the transition of nodes' or buses' state to "sleep active". Gateway nodes require that the bus context corresponds to the network that is being observed. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_AllNodesBabbling (Duration aMinQuietTime, char [] CaplCallback);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_AllNodesBabbling (Duration aMinQuietTime, char [] CaplCallback);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aMinQuietTime` | > 0; default unit [ms], if not changed with ChkConfig_SetPrecision . |
| `CaplCallback` | In simulation nodes this parameter has to be set. In test modules this parameter is optional. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and must not be referenced > 0 Check was created successfully and may be referenced using the returned (handle-) value.


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks that after 300 ms no transmission are available checkId = ChkStart_AllNodesBabbling (300); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```


---

### ChkCreate_NodeBabbling, ChkStart_NodeBabbling

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateNodeBabbling.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateNodeBabbling.htm)

**Description:**

This check supervises the end of a communication: There is a time interval where node-transmissions are tolerated. After the interval has been passed, the node may no longer send messages. From now on, each transmission of the node is reported. If the min. quiet time is 0, then each message sent by the node leads to the event. Use Cases : Supervise the transition of nodes' or buses' state to 'sleep active'. Gateway nodes require that the bus context corresponds to the bus that is being observed. This means that the check only works correctly if the current bus context corresponds to the database in which the node is defined. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_NodeBabbling (Node n, Duration aMinQuietTime, char [] CaplCallback); // form 1
```

```c
dword ChkStart_NodeBabbling (Node n, Duration aMinQuietTime, char [] CaplCallback); // form 2
```

```c
dword ChkStart_NodeBabbling (char [] Node, Duration aMinQuietTime, char [] CaplCallback); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `n/Node` | Defined node in database. |
| `aMinQuietTime` | > 0; default unit [ms], if not changed with ChkConfig_SetPrecision . |
| `CaplCallback` | In simulation nodes this parameter has to be set. In test modules this parameter is optional. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and must not be referenced > 0 Check was created successfully and may be referenced using the returned (handle-) value


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks that after 300 ms no transmission of the node is available checkId = ChkStart_NodeBabbling (NodeToObserve, 300); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```


---

### No Value Change

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNoValueChange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDNoValueChange.htm)

**Description:**

The check is useful to observe the constancy of a signal value or an environment/system variable value, while there is no need to specify the actual value. The check is performed only if a value changes. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_SignalValueChange, ChkStart_SignalValueChange — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures (change of signal value) ✔ Number of check failures due to signal value changes. Number of samples ✔ Number of value changes of the specific signal or variable. Measured MinValue ✔ Minimum measured value of the check. Measured MaxValue ✔ Maximum measured value of the check.


---

### Occurrence Count

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDOccurrenceCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDOccurrenceCount.htm)

**Description:**

The check is used to monitor that certain messages are not sent on the bus. It can also be specified how often a defined message may occur until the check fails. The definition of the monitored messages is carried out exclusively via message ID under CAPL. It is also possible to specify several messages at the same time in XML test modules. In this case, an occurrence of one of the defined messages counts as one occurrence. That means, for example, if maxcount was set to two, and three different messages were specified as reference, the check fails if each of the messages was sent once. For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous Frames are ignored. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ OccurrenceCountCheck This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures ✔ Number of check failures due to transmission of observed message. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.) Number of samples ✔ Number of transmitted messages. (This data record is only available if at least one probe is available.) Occurrence of each message ✔ Below this row all observed messages with the number of occurrence are listed. (This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs.)


---

### ChkCreate_MsgOccurrenceCount, ChkStart_MsgOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::CreateNodeMsgsOccurrenceCount, TestCheck::StartNodeMsgsOccurrenceCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateMsgOccurrenceCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateMsgOccurrenceCount.htm)

**Description:**

Checks the absence of the specified message on the bus. An event is created if more than aMaxCount of the specified message were sent. The numeric functions/constructors with the parameter 'aMessageId/aSourceAdr' cannot be used for FlexRay. Instead use the numeric constructors with the parameter 'slotID' (that can only be applied to a FlexRay bus). For FlexRay only valid data frames and PDUs are recognized as communication, Null Frames and Erroneous frames are ignored. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_MsgOccurrenceCount(dbMsg aMessage, long aMaxCount);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_MsgOccurrenceCount(dbMsg aMessage, long aMaxCount);
```

```c
dword ChkCreate_MsgOccurrenceCount(dword aMessageId, long aMaxCount);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_MsgOccurrenceCount(dword aMessageId, long aMaxCount);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aMessage` | The message in symbolic form, e.g.: "EngineData", whose occurrence is to be monitored. Message must exist in database. |
| `aMessageId` | The ID of the message whose occurrence is to be monitored. |
| `aMaxCount` | The maximum number of message that may be sent without the check to fail. |
| `aSourceAdr` | The source address of the message whose occurrence is to be monitored. |
| `aDestAdr` | The destination address of the message whose occurrence is to be monitored. |
| `aSymbolicMsg` | Symbolic message definition of the message which has to be monitored in the format "FBlock.Function.OpType". Widcards can also be used (e.g. "AudioDiskPlayer.*.Status"). |
| `aInstanceId` | The instance ID of the message whose occurrence is to be monitored. |
| `slotID` | This number designates a specific FlexRay slot. Its value must be between 1 and 2047. |
| `cycleOffs` | This number designates the base cycle. This value must be smaller than the repetition factor and lies in the range between 0 and 63. This value, together with the repetition factor, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `cycleRep` | This number designates the cycle repetition factor. The value must be between 1 and 64 and be a multiple of 2 (e.g. 1, 2, 
 4, 8, 16, 32 or 64). This value, together with the base cycle, determines the "Cycle Multiplexing" of a FlexRay frame. |
| `channelMask` | Identifies the FlexRay channel of the communication controller. A value of 1 will check the 
 frame on channel A, 2 will check it on channel B and 3 on any channel (A/B). |
| `aNode` | The node from the DB whose Tx messages should be observed. |
| `aCaplCallbackFunction` | This parameter must be specified in simulation nodes; it is optional in test modules. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and may not be referenced. > 0 Check was created successfully and can be referenced with the help of the returned (Handle) value.


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks that the message is sent less than 3 times checkId = ChkStart_MsgOccurrenceCount (MsgToObserve, 2); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```


---

### Timeout

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDTimeout.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDTimeout.htm)

**Description:**

This check guards a sequence with a accumulated timeout for a certain sequence of test steps. Useful particularly if the sum of timeouts for the individual test steps are much bigger than the allowed timeout for the whole sequence. The check is only useful in test modules. In simulation nodes this task is executed by CAPL timers. Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_Timeout, ChkStart_Timeout — This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic. ✔ Runtime of the check.


---

### ChkCreate_Timeout, ChkStart_Timeout

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateTimeout.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionChkCreateTimeout.htm)

**Description:**

The check fires an event if the time has expired.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword ChkCreate_Timeout (Duration aTimeout, char [] CaplCallback);
```

```c
dword ChkStart_Timeout (Duration aTimeout, char [] CaplCallback);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aTimeout` | > 0; default unit [ms], if not changed with ChkConfig_SetPrecision . |
| `CaplCallback` | In simulation nodes this parameter has to be set. In test modules this parameter is optional. |


**Return Value:** 0 Check could not be created and must not be referenced > 0 Check was created successfully and may be referenced using the returned (handle-) value


**Example(s):**

```c
// checks the maximum duration of a sequence of actions checkId = ChkStart_Timeout (2000); TestAddCondition(checkId); // sequence of different actions and waiting conditions TestWaitForTimeout(1000); TestRemoveCondition(checkId);
```


---

### Value Valid

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDValueValid.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/TestCommands/CheckDescriptions/CDValueValid.htm)

**Description:**

This check is useful to supervise the value of signals and environment/system variables. The check is performed only if a value changes. Value ranges (intervals) can be specified that should never be left or that should never be reached. These checks can be used for the following application use cases: Since Version CAPL Function C# Function Availability ✔ ChkCreate_MsgSignalValueInvalid, ChkStart_MsgSignalValueInvalid ChkCreate_MsgSignalValueRangeViolation, ChkStart_MsgSignalValueRangeViolation ValueCheck This check can report the following statistic data : Parameter Availability (since version) Description Runtime of the statistic ✔ Runtime of the check. Number of failures ✔ Number of check failures due to value condition violations. This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs. Number of samples ✔ Only number of all changes in value of the specific signal or variable. If a symbol is tested against a dynamic range, the value condition is also checked if one of the range symbols is received. Failure ratio (in %) ✔ Ratio of check failure calculated from number of failures and number of samples. This data record is only available if at least one check failure occurs. Measured MinValue ✔ Minimal measured value of the check. Measured MaxValue ✔ Maximal measured value of the check. Distribution of measured values ✔ The following table demonstrates the distribution of the measured values.


---

### ISO11783/J1939 Interaction Layer Error Codes

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAPLfunctionsISOj1939ErrorCodes.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CAPLfunctionsISOj1939ErrorCodes.htm)

### TestWaitForJ1939PG

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939PG.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939PG.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the occurrence of the specified J1939 parameter group. Should the message not occur before the expiration of the time aTimeout , the wait condition is resolved nevertheless. To get the message content of the parameter group which triggered the wait condition you can use function TestGetWaitJ1939PGData . Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939PG (dbMsg aMessage, dword aTimeout);
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939PG (dword aPGN, dword aTimeout);
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939PG (dword aPGN, dword aSourceAddress, dword aDestinationAddress, dword aTimeout);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aMessage` | Message that should be awaited. Must be a J1939 parameter group. |
| `aPGN` | Parameter Group Number (with data page) of the message that should be awaited. |
| `aSourceAddress` | Source address of the message that should be awaited or 0xFFFFFFFF if source address is ignored. |
| `aDestinationAddress` | Destination address of the message that should be awaited or 0xFFFFFFFF if destination address is ignored. |
| `aTimeout` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms] (Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling). |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 Resume due to event occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //wait for RQST PG (sent form SA=0x01 to DA=0xAA) for 2000 ms result = TestWaitForJ1939PG(0xEA00, 0x01, 0xAA, 2000); if (result == 1) { pg RQST pgRQST; //PG RQST has to be defined in the attached DBC file result = TestGetWaitJ1939PGData(pgRQST); if (result == 0)// obtain the received RQST PG { if (pgRQST.ParameterGroupNumber == 0x1234) //check content of the received PG { TestStepPass("Validation", "Request for PGN 0x1234 occurred"); // do something, e.g. send response... } } else { TestStepFail("Validation", "Failed to get data of received PG RQST: unexpected error %d", result); } } else if(result == 0) { TestStepFail("Validation", "Expected PG RQST is not received within 2000 ms"); } else { TestStepFail("Validation", "TestWaitForJ1939PG(0xEA00, 0x01, 0xAA, 2000): unexpected error %d", result); }
```


---

### TestJoinJ1939PGEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinJ1939PGEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinJ1939PGEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of joined events with the transmitted event. This function does not wait. For waiting, either TestWaitForAllJoinedEvents or TestWaitForAnyJoinedEvent should be used depending on the requirement. To get the message content of a parameter group which triggered a wait condition you can use the function TestGetWaitJ1939PGData . Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinJ1939PGEvent (dbMsg aMessage)
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939PGEvent (dword aPGN)
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939PGEvent (dword aPGN, dword aSourceAddress, dword aDestinationAddress)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aMessage` | Message that should be awaited. Must be a J1939 parameter group. |
| `aPGN` | Parameter Group Number (with data page) of the message that should be awaited. |
| `aSourceAddress` | Source address of the message that should be awaited or 0xFFFFFFFF if source address is ignored. |
| `aDestinationAddress` | Destination address of the message that should be awaited or 0xFFFFFFFF if destination address is ignored. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
// Wait until both parameter groups TSC1 and EEC1 are received and analyze its content. pg TSC1 pgTSC1; pg EEC1 pgEEC1; long indexTSC1, indexEEC1; long result; // 1. define combination of waiting conditions indexTSC1 = TestJoinJ1939PGEvent(TSC1); indexEEC1 = TestJoinJ1939PGEvent(EEC1); // 2. Wait for both parameter groups result = TestWaitForAllJoinedEvents(2000); if (result > 0) { // 3. Put data of TSC1 to pgTSC1 and analyze content if (TestGetWaitJ1939PGData(indexTSC1, pgTSC1) == 0) { //4. Validate data of pgTSC1 … } // 5. Put data of EEC1 to pgEEC1 and analyze content if (TestGetWaitJ1939PGData(indexEEC1, pgEEC1) == 0) { //4. Validate data of pgEEC1 … } } else if (result == 0) { TestStepFail("Validation", " EEC1 and TSC are not received within 2000 ms"); } else { TestStepFail ("Validation", " TestWaitForAllJoinedEvents failed with unexpecetd error %i", result); }
```


---

### TestWaitForJ1939DTC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939DTC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939DTC.htm)

**Description:**

Waits until a defined Parameter Group and a defined Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) is received or a timeout occurred. The affected message (specified by the Parameter Group number pgn or the database object message ) must be able to contain a DTC, so only this parameter groups are allowed: DM1, DM2, DM4, DM6, DM12, DM23, DM24, DM27, DM28, DM31, DM35 and DM41-DM54. To get the message content of the parameter group which triggered the wait condition you can use the function TestGetWaitJ1939PGData . Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (dword sourceAddress, Message message, dword spn, dword timeout); // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn, dword timeout); // form 2
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (Node node, Message message, dword spn, dword timeout); // form 3
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (Node node, dword pgn, dword spn, dword timeout); // form 4
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (dword sourceAddress, Message message, dword spn, word fmi, word oc, dword timeout); // form 5
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn, word fmi, word oc, dword timeout); // form 6
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (Node node, Message message, dword spn, word fmi, word oc, dword timeout); // form 7
```

```c
long TestWaitForJ1939DTC (Node node, dword pgn, dword spn, word fmi, word oc, dword timeout); // form 8
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `message` | Message containing the specified DTC. Must be a J1939 Parameter Group. |
| `pgn` | Parameter Group Number (with data page) of the message containing the specified DTC. |
| `node` | Sender of the message containing the specified DTC. |
| `sourceAddress` | Source address of the message containing the specified DTC 0xFFFFFFFF if source address is to be ignored. |
| `spn` | Suspect Parameter Number of the specified DTC 0xFFFFFFFF if spn is to be ignored. |
| `fmi` | Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if fmi is to be ignored. |
| `oc` | Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if oc is to be ignored. |
| `timeout` | Maximum time to wait [ms]. 0 if timeout is to be ignored. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 Resume due to event occurred


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
long result; // wait for the occurrence of DTC with SPN=96 and FMI=12 within message DM1 (pgn=0xFECA) from node with address=0x2 result = TestWaitForJ1939DTC(0x2, DM01.pgn, 96, 12, 0xFFFF, 2000); // wait for the occurrence of DTC with SPN=98765 and OC=1 within message DM2 (pgn=0xFECB) from node with address=0x3 result = TestWaitForJ1939DTC(0x3, 0xFECB, 98765, 0xFFFF, 1, 2000); // wait for the occurrence of DTC with OC=10 within message DM35 (pgn=0x9F00) from any node result = TestWaitForJ1939DTC(0xFFFFFFFF, 0x9F00, 0xFFFFFFFF, 0xFFFF, 10, 2000);
```

*Example 2*

```c
long result; pg DM01 pgDM01; // wait for the occurrence of DTC with SPN=96 and FMI=12 // within message DM1 (pgn=0xFECA) from node with address=0x2 result = TestWaitForJ1939DTC(0x2, DM01.pgn, 96, 12, 0xFFFF, 2000); if (result == 1) { // validate lamp status of DM01 result = TestGetWaitJ1939PGData(pgDM01); if (result == 0) { if (pgDM01.ProtectLampStatus != 1) { testStepFail("Validation", "ProtectLampStatus of DM01 is not 1"); } else { testStepPass("Validation", "DM01 with expected DTC (SPN 96, FMI12)  and  ProtectLampStatus 1 occurred"); } } else { testStepFail("Validation", "Failed to get data of DM01 (return code of TestGetWaitJ1939PGData: %d)", result); } } else if (result == 0) { testStepFail ("Validation", "DM01 with expected DTC is not received within 2000 ms"); } else { testStepFail ("Validation", "TestWaitForJ1939PG(0xEA00, 0x01, 0xAA, 2000): unexpecetd error %i", result); }
```


---

### TestWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN.htm)

**Description:**

Waits until a defined Parameter Group and a defined Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) disappears within a given timeout. The affected diagnostic message (specified by the Parameter Group number pgn ) must be able to contain a DTC, so only these parameter groups are allowed: DM1, DM2, DM4, DM6, DM12, DM23, DM24, DM27, DM28, DM31, DM35 and DM41-DM54. To get the message content of the parameter group which triggered the wait condition you can use the function TestGetWaitJ1939PGData .


**Syntax:**

```c
long testWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn, dword timeout); // form 1
```

```c
long testWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn, word fmi, word oc, dword timeout); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `sourceAddress` | Source address of the message containing the specified DTC. |
| `pgn` | Parameter Group Number (with data page) of the Diagnostic Message. |
| `spn` | Suspect Paramter Number of the specified DTC. 0xFFFFFFFF if spn is to be ignored. |
| `fmi` | Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if fmi is to be ignored. |
| `oc` | Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if oc is to be ignored. |
| `timeout` | Maximum time to wait [ms]. 0 if timeout is to be ignored. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 Resume due to event occurred


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
char kBusContextJ1939_1[100] = "J1939_1"; export testfunction TestWaitForDtcCycle(long sa, dword pgn, dword spn, dword timeoutToAppear, dword timeoutToDisappear) { long result; testStepPass(); setBusContext( getBusNameContext(kBusContextJ1939_1) ); // Set the channel, the test function is applied to // Wait for the given SPN result = TestWaitForJ1939DTC(sa, pgn, spn, timeoutToAppear ); if (result != 1) { // SPN did not occur - Test failed TestStepFail ("DTC did not occur"); } // Expect the SPN to be dismissed. result = testWaitForJ1939DmWithoutSPN(sa, pgn, spn, timeoutToDisappear); if (result != 1) { // SPN is still sent - Test failed TestStepFail ("SPN is still part of the given DM"); } }
```


---

### TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinJ1939DTCEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinJ1939DTCEvent.htm)

**Description:**

Completes the current set of joined events with the additional Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) event – transmitted with one of the J1939 diagnostic messages. This function does not wait. For waiting, either TestWaitForAllJoinedEvents or TestWaitForAnyJoinedEvent should be used depending on the requirement. The affected message (specified by the Parameter Group number pgn or the database object message ) must be able to contain a DTC, so only this parameter groups are allowed: DM1, DM2, DM4, DM6, DM12, DM23, DM24, DM27, DM28, DM31, DM35 and DM41-DM54. To get the message content of a Parameter Group which triggered a wait condition you can use function TestGetWaitJ1939PGData . Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (dword sourceAddress, Message message, dword spn); // form 1
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn); // form 2
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (Node node, Message message, dword spn); // form 3
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (Node node, dword pgn, dword spn); // form 4
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (dword sourceAddress, Message message, dword spn, word fmi, word oc); // form 5
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (dword sourceAddress, dword pgn, dword spn, word fmi, word oc); // form 6
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (Node node, Message message, dword spn, word fmi, word oc); // form 7
```

```c
long TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (Node node, dword pgn, dword spn, word fmi, word oc); // form 8
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `message` | Message containing the specified DTC. Must be a J1939 Parameter Group. |
| `pgn` | Parameter Group Number (with data page) of the message containing the specified DTC. |
| `node` | Sender of the message containing the specified DTC. |
| `sourceAddress` | Source address of the message containing the specified DTC 0xFFFFFFFF if source address is to be ignored. |
| `spn` | Suspect Parameter Number of the specified DTC 0xFFFFFFFF if spn is to be ignored. |
| `fmi` | Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if fmi is to be ignored. |
| `oc` | Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC 0xFFFF if oc is to be ignored. |


**Return Value:** -3 Join error -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available > 0 Number of the joined event


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcWaitForOneOfDTCs(dword sourceAddress) { long eventIndex; dword sa = 0x1; pg DM12 pgDM12; //All DTCs are transmitted with the message DM12 (pgn = 0xFED4). //DTC Condition 1: spn = 111, fmi = 12, oc to be ignored TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (sourceAddress, DM12.pgn, 111, 12, 0xFFFF); //DTC Condition 2: spn = 222, fmi to be ignored, oc = 5 TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (sourceAddress, DM12.pgn, 222, 0xFFFF, 5); //DTC Condition 3: spn = 333, fmi and oc to be ignored TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent (sourceAddress, DM12.pgn, 333, 0xFFFF, 0xFFFF); eventIndex = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent(5000); switch (eventIndex) { case 1: testStepPass("Validation", "DTC with spn=111 and fmi=12 occurred"); if (testGetWaitJ1939PGData(eventIndex, pgDM12) == 0) { // validate data of received DM12 via pgDM12 } break; case 2: testStepPass("Validation", "DTC with spn=222 and oc=5 occurred"); if (testGetWaitJ1939PGData(eventIndex, pgDM12) == 0) { // validate data of received DM12 via pgDM12 } break; case 3: testStepPass("Validation", "DTC with spn=333 occurred"); if (testGetWaitJ1939PGData(eventIndex, pgDM12) == 0) { // validate data of received DM12 via pgDM12 } break; default: testStepFail("Validation", "Unexpected event or timeout (return code of testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent: %d)", eventIndex); break; } }
```


---

### TestGetWaitJ1939PGData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitJ1939PGData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitJ1939PGData.htm)

**Description:**

You can use this function to get the message content of J1939 Parameter Group that triggers a wait instruction. Form 1 should be used if a wait operation for only one event is used (functions TestWaitForJ1939PG or TestWaitForJ1939DTC ) Form 2 can only be used for joined events. The number of the joined event (return value of TestJoinJ1939PGEvent or TestJoinJ1939DTCEvent ) is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitJ1939PGData (pg * aPG); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitJ1939PGData (dword index, pg * aPG); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aPG` | J1939 parameter group variable that should be filled with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the joined event corresponds with the return value of testJoin... . |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful. -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not a parameter group event. -3 The DLC is larger than the number of copied bytes in the parameter aPG . When initializing aPG , increase its DLC.


---

### _Diag_ChannelStateChanged

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagChannelStateChanged.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagChannelStateChanged.htm)

**Description:**

Callback function in a tester that is called every time the state of the diagnostic channel changes. Note: Form 1 requires a diagnostic target to be set, by calling diagSetTarget . Note: Only one of the two forms may be implemented at a time and the function name is case-sensitive. Note: In combination with the CAPL Callback Interface (CCI) , only the states Closed and Connected are supported.


**Syntax:**

```c
void _Diag_ChannelStateChanged(long newState); // form 1
```

```c
void _Diag_ChannelStateChanged(char target[], long newState); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `target` | Qualifier of the ECU whose communication channel's state has changed. |
| `newState` | New state of the channel. 0 Closed Channel is not yet opened or already closed. 1 Opened Channel is opened but no request has successfully been sent yet or the attempt to send a request failed. 2 Connected Request was successfully sent but no response has been received yet or the expected response was missed. 3 Online Request and response have successfully been sent and received. 4 ClosePending Closing of the communication was requested but is still pending. State Closed will follow. 5 ConnectPending Start of the communication was requested but is still pending, in case of success state Connected will follow, otherwise state Opened . |
| `0` | Closed Channel is not yet opened or already closed. |
| `1` | Opened Channel is opened but no request has successfully been sent yet or the attempt to send a request failed. |
| `2` | Connected Request was successfully sent but no response has been received yet or the expected response was missed. |
| `3` | Online Request and response have successfully been sent and received. |
| `4` | ClosePending Closing of the communication was requested but is still pending. State Closed will follow. |
| `5` | ConnectPending Start of the communication was requested but is still pending, in case of success state Connected will follow, otherwise state Opened . |


**Example(s):**

```c
_Diag_ChannelStateChanged(char target[], long newState) { write("Channel state changed for target %s changed to %d", target, newState); gCurrentChannelState = newState; }
```

### diagCloseChannel

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagCloseChannel.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagCloseChannel.htm)

**Description:**

Closes a diagnostic channel. State of the channel is Closed afterwards. The channel the function refers to is set with the last call of diagSetTarget .


**Syntax:**

```c
long diagCloseChannel(); // form 1
```

```c
long DiagCloseChannel(char ecuQualifier[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Qualifier of the ECU or target as set in the diagnostic configuration dialog for the respective diagnostic description. |


**Return Value:** On success 0, otherwise error code .


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### diagConnectChannel

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagConnectChannel.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDiagConnectChannel.htm)

**Description:**

Initiates the connection of a diagnostic channel. This function is called asynchronously, i.e. it immediately returns even if establishing the connection takes some more time. You can query the state of the diagnostic channel using diagIsChannelConnected . Additionally, you can wait until the connection is established using TestWaitForDiagChannelConnected . For form 1, the channel the function refers to is set with the last call of diagSetTarget .


**Syntax:**

```c
long diagConnectChannel(); // form 1
```

```c
DiagConnectChannel(char ecuQualifier[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Qualifier of the ECU or target as set in the diagnostic configuration dialog for the respective diagnostic description. |


**Return Value:** On success 0, otherwise error code .


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### TestGetWaitEventKLineByte

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventKLineByte.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventKLineByte.htm)

**Description:**

If a byte is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the content can be called up with the first function. The second function allows to call the content and the end of byte time stamp.


**Syntax:**

```c
long testGetWaitEventKLineByte();
```

```c
long testGetWaitEventKLineByte(int64 timeStampOut);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `timeStampOut` | End of byte time stamp. |


**Return Value:** Value of the byte


**Example(s):**

```c
DiagRequest EcuIdentification_Read req; int64 timeStamps; Byte byteValue; DiagSetTarget("DUT"); req.SendRequest(); if (1 == TestWaitForDiagKLinebyteTransmitted(5000)) { TestStepPass("Transmitted KLine frame."); byteValue =  TestGetWaitEventKLineByte( timeStamp) ; write( "TRANSMITTED BYTE VALUE: 0x%x. TIMESTAMP [%.3f]", byteValue, timeStamp/1000000000.0); } else { TestStepFail("No confirmation of transmitted K-Line byte."); }
```


---

### TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventKLineFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitEventKLineFrame.htm)

**Description:**

If a K-Line frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the content can be called up with the first function. The second function allows to call the content and the end of byte time stamps.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame(BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferLen);
```

```c
long TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame(BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferLen, int64 timeStampOut[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bufferOut` | Byte data buffer of the frame. |
| `bufferLen` | Size of the data buffer. |
| `timeStampOut` | Buffer of the end of byte stamps. |


**Return Value:** On success a value greater 0, otherwise a value less than 0.


**Example(s):**

```c
DiagRequest EcuIdentification_Read req; int64 timeStamps[100]; BYTE buffer[100]; dword bufferLength = 6; int i = 0; long rc; DiagSetTarget("DUT"); req.SendRequest(); if (1 == TestWaitForDiagKLineFrameTransmitted(5000)) { TestStepPass("Transmitted KLine frame."); rc = TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame(buffer, bufferLength, timeStamps); if (rc > 0) { TeststepPass("TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame returned with success."); for (i = 0; i < rc; ++i) { write( "TRANSMITTED FRAME: BYTE VALUE: 0x%x. TIMESTAMP [%.3f]", buffer[i], timeStamps[i]/1000000000.0); } } } else { TestStepFail("No confirmation of transmitted K-Line frame."); } if (1 == TestWaitForDiagKLineFrameReceived(5000)) { TestStepPass("Received KLine frame."); rc = TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame(buffer, bufferLength, timeStamps); if (rc > 0) { TeststepPass("TestGetWaitEventKLineFrame returned with success."); for (i = 0; i < rc; ++i) { write( "RECEIVED FRAME: BYTE VALUE: 0x%x. TIMESTAMP [%.3f]", buffer[i], timeStamps[i]/1000000000.0); } } } else { TestStepFail("No indication of received K-Line frame."); }
```


---

### TestWaitForDiagChannelClosed

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForDiagChannelClosed.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForDiagChannelClosed.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the occurrence of the state change of a diagnostic channel to the state Closed . Should the closing not occur before the expiration of the time timeout_ms , the wait condition is resolved nevertheless.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForDiagChannelClosed(dword timeout_ms) // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForDiagChannelClosed(char ecuQualifier[], dword timeout_ms); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `timeout_ms` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |
| `ecuQualifier` | Qualifier of the ECU or target as set in the diagnostic configuration dialog for the respective diagnostic description. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 Resume due to event occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
if (0 == DiagCloseChannel()) { TestStepPass(t"Channel close called successfully"); } else { TestStepFail("Call to channel close returned an error."); } if (1 == TestWaitForDiagChannelClosed(1000)) { TestStepPass( "Channel closed successfully."); } else { TestStepFail( "Channel could not be closed."); }
```


---

### TestWaitForDiagChannelConnected

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForDiagChannelConnected.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForDiagChannelConnected.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the occurrence of the state change of a diagnostic channel to the state Connected . Should the connection not occur before the expiration of the time timeout_ms , the wait condition is resolved nevertheless.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForDiagChannelConnected(dword timeout_ms); // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForDiagChannelConnected(char ecuQualifier[], dword timeout_ms); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `timeout_ms` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |
| `ecuQualifier` | Qualifier of the ECU or target as set in the diagnostic configuration dialog for the respective diagnostic description. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 Resume due to event occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
long status; if (0 == DiagConnectChannel()) { TestStepPass( "Channel connect called successfully."); } else { TestStepFail( "Channel connect returned with an error."); success = kRetFail; } status = TestWaitForDiagChannelConnected(2000); if (1 == status) { TestStepPass("Connected to target ECU"); } else { TestStepFail( "Error connecting to target ECU"); }
```


---

### SetStationAttributeValueDouble

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ADAS/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetStationAttributeValueDouble.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ADAS/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetStationAttributeValueDouble.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the double value for a station's attribute. Internally a keypoint is created at the current point in time.


**Syntax:**

```c
bool SetStationAttributeValueDouble.Call(string stationName, string attribute, double value);
```

```c
int SetStationAttributeValueDouble.Call(char[] stationName, char[] attribute, double value);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `stationName` | The name of the station for which the attribute shall be set. |
| `attribute` | The name of the attribute which shall be set. |
| `value` | The value to be set. |


**Return Value:** C# true Set was successful. false Set failed. Check for incorrect attribute name. CAPL 1 Set was successful. 0 Set failed. Check for incorrect attribute name.

### SetStationAttributeValueInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ADAS/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetStationAttributeValueInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ADAS/Functions/CAPLfunctionSetStationAttributeValueInt64.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the 64-bit integer value for a station's attribute. Internally a keypoint is created at the current point in time.


**Syntax:**

```c
bool SetStationAttributeValueInt64.Call(string stationName, string attribute, int64 value);
```

```c
int SetStationAttributeValueInt64.Call(char[] stationName, char[] attribute, int64 value);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `stationName` | The name of the station for which the attribute shall be set. |
| `attribute` | The name of the attribute which shall be set. There are no default int64 attributes, user-defined values must be created first in the Scenario Editor timeline. |
| `value` | The value to be set. |


**Return Value:** C# true Set was successful. false Set failed. Check for incorrect attribute name. CAPL 1 Set was successful. 0 Set failed. Check for incorrect attribute name.
