### CAN FD Light CAPL Functions

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/CAPLfunctionsCANfdLightOverview.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/CAPLfunctionsCANfdLightOverview.htm)


---

### canFDLightActivateCommander

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightActivateCommander.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightActivateCommander.htm)

**Description:**

Enables or disables the CAN FD Light Commander on the given channel. Transmission of CAN FD Light frames is only possible if the Commander is active. The function will only succeed if the given channel is in CAN FD Light mode (see Network Hardware Configuration dialog).


**Syntax:**

```c
long canFDLightActivateCommander ((long channel, long active);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |
| `active` | 0: disable 1: enable |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on prestart { canFDLightActivateCommander (1, 1); // Enable CAN FD Light Commander on CAN channel 1 }
```


---

### canFDLightSendBroadcast

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendBroadcast.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendBroadcast.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a CAN FD broadcast frame to the given channel. Transmission of CAN FD Light frames is only possible if the Commander is active.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canFDLightSendBroadcast (long channel, long address, byte[] data, long dataLength);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |
| `address` | CAN FD Light address to transmit. Only the lowest 10 bits will be used. |
| `Data` | Data bytes to transmit. Only the bytes up to dataLength will be used. If dataLength is 0, no bytes will be used. |
| `dataLength` | Number of data bytes to transmit (0-64). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key ‘u’ { byte data[8]; canFDLightSendBroadcast (1, 101, data, 8); // Send broadcast frame on channel 1 with address “101” and 8 data bytes }
```


---

### canFDLightSendCalibration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendCalibration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendCalibration.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a CAN FD Light calibration pattern on the given channel. Transmission of CAN FD Light frames is only possible if the Commander is active.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canFDLightSendCalibration (long channel, long length);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |
| `length` | Number of data bytes containing the calibration pattern (0-64). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { canFDLightSendCalibration (1, 1); // Send calibration pattern on CAN channel 1 with one data byte }
```


---

### canFDLightSendGoToSleep

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendGoToSleep.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendGoToSleep.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a CAN FD Light Go-To-Sleep command to the given channel. Transmission of CAN FD Light frames is only possible if the Commander is active.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canFDLightSendGoToSleep (long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key ‘s’ { canFDLightSendGoToSleep (1); // Send CAN FD Light Go-To-Sleep command on CAN channel 1 }
```


---

### canFDLightSendUnicast

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendUnicast.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendUnicast.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a CAN FD unicast frame to the given channel. Transmission of CAN FD Light frames is only possible if the Commander is active.


**Syntax:**

```c
long canFDLightSendUnicast (long channel, long address, byte[] data, long dataLength);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |
| `address` | CAN FD Light address to transmit. Only the lowest 9 bits will be used. |
| `Data` | Data bytes to transmit. Only the bytes up to dataLength will be used. If dataLength is 0, no bytes will be used. |
| `dataLength` | Number of data bytes to transmit (0-64). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key ‘u’ { byte data[8]; canFDLightSendUnicast (1, 101, data, 8); // Send unicast frame on channel 1 with address “101” and 8 data bytes }
```

### canFDLightSendWakeup

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendWakeup.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/CANfdLight/Functions/CAPLfunctionCanFDLightSendWakeup.htm)

```c

```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | CAN channel (1…255). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. 0 Failure.


**Example(s):**

### C2xSetStationPropertyInt64

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Car2x/Functions/CAPLfunctionC2xSetStationPropertyInt64.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Car2x/Functions/CAPLfunctionC2xSetStationPropertyInt64.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a station property of type int64 in a V2x scenario.


**Syntax:**

```c
long C2xSetStationPropertyInt64(char* propertyName, int64 propertyValue); // form 1
```

```c
long C2xSetStationPropertyInt64(char* stationName, char* propertyName, int64 propertyValue); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `stationName` | Name of the station. |
| `propertyName` | Name of the station’s property. Possible property values are: StationType (only if scenario is not running). |
| `propertyValue` | The value to set for the specified property. |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 Error


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 's' { C2xSetStationPropertyInt64("StationType", 15); }
```

### C2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Car2x/Functions/CAPLfunctionC2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Car2x/Functions/CAPLfunctionC2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule.htm)

**Description:**

Add a user defined rule declaration to the Protocol Analyzer. The rule will appear in the rule tree of Protocol Analyzer before any rule violation appears. This allows the user to see all available rules in case no rule violation occurs.


**Syntax:**

```c
C2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule(char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] ruleDescription, char[] helpText, long severity)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `scope` | Text value indicating the position of the rule in the Protocol Analyzer rule tree, each level is separated by the pipe
 character "|" and corresponds to a separate folder in the tree. E.g. My
 rules|EU|CAM|Custom Rules . |
| `ruleId` | Integer number which identify the rule, please use numbers above 50000 to avoid interfering with your CANoe DE product build-in rules. |
| `ruleName` | Short rule name, will be shown in the Protocol Analyzer rule tree. |
| `ruleDescription` | Detailed description of the behavior and/or token values expected by this rule. |
| `helpText` | Additional optional information about the rule, like reference to a specific chapter of some standard or specification expecting
 the behavior described by this rule. |
| `severity` | 0: the protocol violation finding will be shown as
 error. 1: the protocol violation finding will be shown as
 warning. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success not 0 No custom rule was created.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### Diagnostic CAPL Functions

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/CAPLfunctionsDiagnosticsOverview.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/CAPLfunctionsDiagnosticsOverview.htm)

### DoIP_DisableDiagnosticAckMessages

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDoIPDisableDiagnosticAckMessages.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Diagnostics/Functions/CAPLfunctionDoIPDisableDiagnosticAckMessages.htm)

**Description:**

If called for a tester, the tester will not expect diagnostic acknowledgments from the ECU. Not receiving an acknowledgment is therefore expected and does not raise an error. Unexpected (negative) acknowledgments are ignored and do not cause errors. If called in an ECU simulation, the ECU will not send diagnostic acknowledgments to the tester, even in cases where it would normally send a negative acknowledgment.


**Syntax:**

```c
void DoIP_DisableDiagnosticAckMessages(long disabled);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `disabled` | 0: enabled: send diagnostic acknowledgments (default) 1: disabled: do not send acknowledgments |


**Example(s):**

*Example Tester*

```c
void _Diag_ConfigureChannel(char target[]) { // […] long disabled; disabled = diagGetCommParameter(target, 1, DoIP.DisableDoIPDiagnosticAckMessages"); DoIP_DisableDiagnosticAckMessages(disabled); // […] }
```

*Example ECU Simulation*

```c
on preStart { // […] long disabled; disabled = diagGetCommParameter("ecuQualifier", 0, "DoIP.DisableDoIPDiagnosticAckMessages"); DoIP_DisableDiagnosticAckMessages(disabled); // […] }
```


---

**Return Value:** Value1 Timeout for routing activation [ms].

```c

```

### TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData.htm)

**Description:**

If a valid SOME/IP message is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the SOME/IP message content can be called up with form 1. Form 2 can only be used for joined events. The number of the joined event (return value of TestJoinSomeIpMessage ) is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData( someipMessage msg ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData( dword index, someipMessage msg ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | A someipMessage variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the joined event corresponds with the return value of TestJoinSomeIpMessage . |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful. -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an SOME/IP message.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
someipMessage *msg; result = testWaitForSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, 5, 1000, 1, 1, 0x8001, 0x02, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP message received on ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received a SOME/IP message" ); break; default: testStepFail( "", "testWaitForSomeIpMessage failed with result %d", result ); return; } if (TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData( msg ) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData failed" ); return; } if (msg.ReturnCode != 0) { testStepFail( "", "Return code is %d, but 0 expected", msg.ReturnCode ); }
```

> Wait for SOME/IP message with VLAN ID 5, Service ID 1000, Version 1, Instance ID 1 and Message Type Notification. Then check return code of received message.

*Example*

```c
someipMessage *msg; long someipCondition; long textCondition; someipCondition = testJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, 5, 1000, 1, -1, 0x8001, 0x02 ); textCondition = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipCondition) { if (TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData( someipCondition, msg ) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData failed" ); return; } if (msg.ReturnCode != 0) { testStepFail( "", "Return code is %d, but 0 expected", msg.ReturnCode ); } testStepPass( "Received SOME/IP message with successful return code" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for SOME/IP message with VLAN ID 5, Service ID 1000, Version 1, Instance ID 1 and Message Type Notification or a text event. If SOME/IP message occured, then check return code of received message.


---

### TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitSomeIpSDData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetWaitSomeIpSDData.htm)

**Description:**

If a valid SOME/IP Service Discovery entry is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the content of the SOME/IP Service Discovery entry can be called up with form 1. Form 2 can only be used for joined events. The number of the joined event (return value of TestJoinSomeIpSD ) is here being used as an index.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData( someipSD sdEntry ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData( dword index, someipSD sdEntry ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | A someipMessage variable that should be filled in with this function. |
| `index` | Number of the joined event corresponds with the return value of TestJoinSomeIpMessage . |


**Return Value:** 0 Data access successful. -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an SOME/IP Service Discovery entry. -2 Data too big for sdEntry variable.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
enum SDType { kSDFindService = 0x00, kSDOfferService = 0x01, kSDSubscribe = 0x06, kSDSubscribeACK = 0x07 }; const long kAnyVLANID = -1; const long kAnyServiceID = -1; const long kAnyMajorVersion = -1; const long kAnyServiceInstanceID = -1; const long kAnyEventgroupID = -1; someipSD sdEntry; ip_endpoint offeredEndpoint; ip_endpoint UDP:192.168.0.1:30500 expectedEndpoint; result = testWaitForSomeIpSD( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, kAnyVLANID, kSDOfferService, 1000, kAnyMajorVersion, kAnyServiceInstanceID, kAnyEventgroupID, 2000 // ms ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry received on ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1" ); return; case 1: break; default: testStepFail( "", " testWaitForSomeIpSD failed with result %d", result ); return; } if (TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData( sdEntry) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData failed" ); return; } // get IPv4 endpoint option of Offer-Service entry if (sdEntry.GetOptionIPEndpoint( 0x04, offeredEndpoint ) != 0) { testStepFail( "OfferService for service ID 1000 does not contain IPv4 endpoint option" ); } if (expectedEndpoint.MatchesEndpoint( offeredEndpoint ) == 0) { char epStr[100]; offeredEndpoint.PrintEndpointToString( epStr ); testStepFail( "", "Service ID 1000 is offered on %s, this is not the expected IP endpoint", epStr ); } testStepPass( "Received expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry" );
```

> Wait for SOME/IP Service Discovery entry with Offer-Service ID 1000. Then check IP endpoint on which the service is provided.


---

### TestJoinSomeIpMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinSomeIpMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinSomeIpMessage.htm)

**Description:**

Adds a SOME/IP message to the set of joined events. Use TestWaitForAnyJoinedEvent or TestWaitForAllJoinedEvent to wait. The different forms of TestJoinSomeIpMessage offer the possibility to define the condition of the expected SOME/IP message in detail. For the numeric values, e.g. serviceID, -1 (wildcard) can be used to exclude a condition from the comparison. Forms 1 and 3 can be used to specify the Ethernet port on which the SOME/IP message must occur. Forms 2 and 4 also specify the IP endpoints with which the SOME/IP message is transmitted. If the expected SOME/IP message occurred, then the content of the message can be fetched with TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData and the index with the return value of this function. Note The order of the argument for servicedID, interfaceVersion, serviceInstanceID and methodID in form 1 to 4 corresponds to the qualifier of on someipMessage .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpMessage( long vlanID, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType ); // form 2
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType ); // form 3
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpMessage( long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethPort` | Ethernet port of the specified SOME/IP message. |
| `vlanID` | VLAN identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any VLAN identifier. Valid range 0..FFFh |
| `sourceEP` | Source IP endpoint of
 expected SOME/IP message [MT1] [MT2] |
| `destinationEP` | Destination IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP message |
| `serviceID` | Service identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `interfaceVersion` | Interface version of expected SOME/IP message or -1 for any version. Valid range 0..FFh |
| `serviceInstanceID` | Service instance identifier of expected SOME/IP
 message or -1 for any instance identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `methodID` | Method identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1 for any method identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `messageType` | Message type of expected SOME/IP message or -1 for any type. Valid range 0..FFh |


**Return Value:** >0 Number of the joined event. -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -2 Invalid argument value, see Write Windows for details.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long someipCondition; long textCondition; someipCondition = testJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1 ); textCondition = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipCondition) { testStepPass( "Received any SOME/IP message" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP message on specific Ethernet port or a text event.

*Example*

```c
someipMessage *msg; long someipCondition; long textCondition; someipCondition = testJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, 5, 1000, 1, -1, 0x8001, 0x02 ); textCondition = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipCondition) { if (TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData( someipCondition, msg ) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData failed" ); return; } if (msg.ReturnCode != 0) { testStepFail( "", "Return code is %d, but 0 expected", msg.ReturnCode ); } testStepPass( "Received SOME/IP message with successful return code" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for SOME/IP message with VLAN ID 5, Service ID 1000, Version 1, Instance ID 1 and Message Type Notification or a text event. If SOME/IP message occured, then check return code of received message.

*Example*

```c
long someipCondition; long textCondition; someipCondition = testJoinSomeIpMessage( ethernetPort::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, ip_endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.1:30500), // source IP endpoint ip_Endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.2:30500), // destination IP endpoint -1, -1, -1, -1, -1 ); textCondition   = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipCondition) { testStepPass( "Received any SOME/IP message" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP message from UDP:192.168.0.1:30500 to UDP:192.168.0.2:30500 or a text event.


---

### TestJoinSomeIpSD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinSomeIpSD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestJoinSomeIpSD.htm)

**Description:**

Adds a SOME/IP Service Discovery entry to the set of joined events. Use TestWaitForAnyJoinedEvent or TestWaitForAllJoinedEvent to wait. The different forms of TestJoinSomeIpSD offer the possibility to define the condition of the expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry in detail. For the numeric values, e.g. serviceID, -1 (wildcard) can be used to exclude a condition from the comparison. Forms 1 and 3 can be used to specify the Ethernet port on which the SOME/IP message must occur. Forms 2 and 4 also specify the IP endpoints with which the SOME/IP message is transmitted. If the expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry occurred, then the content of the message can be fetched with TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData and the index with the return value of this function.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpSD( long vlanID, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpSD( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID ); // form 2
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpSD( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID); // form 3
```

```c
long TestJoinSomeIpSD( long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethPort` | Ethernet port of the specified SOME/IP message. |
| `vlanID` | VLAN identifier of expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry or -1. Valid range 0..FFFh |
| `sourceEP` | Source IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP message. |
| `destinationEP` | Destination IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP message. |
| `sdType` | SD record type of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for any type. Valid range 0..FFh 0x00 - Find Service 0x01 - Offer Service 0x06 - Subscribe Eventgroup 0x07 - Subscribe Acknowledge |
| `serviceID` | Service identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `majorVersion` | Major version of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for
 any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFh |
| `instanceID` | Service instance identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for any service instance identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `eventGroupID` | Event group identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for any event group identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh Only supported for Subscribe, SubscribeACK and StopSubscribe record. Must be -1 for Offer and Find |


**Return Value:** >0 Number of the joined event. -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -2 Invalid argument value, see Write Windows for details.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long someipSDCondition; long textCondition; someipSDCondition = testJoinSomeipSD( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1 ); textCondition = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipSDCondition) { testStepPass( "Received any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry on specific Ethernet port or a text event.

*Example*

```c
enum SDType { kSDFindService = 0x00, kSDOfferService = 0x01, kSDSubscribe = 0x06, kSDSubscribeACK = 0x07 }; const long kAnyVLANID = -1; const long kAnyServiceID = -1; const long kAnyMajorVersion = -1; const long kAnyServiceInstanceID = -1; const long kAnyEventgroupID = -1; someipSD sdEntry; long someipCondition; long textCondition; someipCondition = testJoinSomeIpSD( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, kAnyVLANID, ip_endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.1:30490), // source IP endpoint ip_endpoint(UDP:239.0.0.1:30490), // destination IP endpoint kSDOfferService, 1000, 2, kAnyServiceInstanceID, kAnyEventgroupID ); textCondition   = testJoinTextEvent( "Hello" ); result = testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent( 200 ); if (result == 0) { testStepFail( "Timeout, no joined event occured" ); } else if (result == someipCondition) { if (TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData( someipCondition, sdEntry ) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData failed" ); return; } if (sdEntry.TTL == 300) { testStepFail( "", "TTL is %d, but 300 expected", sdEntry.TTL ); } testStepPass( "Received SOME/IP Service Discovery entry" ); } else if (result == textCondition) { testStepPass( "Someone said hello" ); } else { testStepFail( "", "testWaitForAnyJoinedEvent failed with result %d", result ); }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry from UDP:192.168.0.1:30500 to UDP:192.168.0.2:30500 with Service ID 1000 version 2 or a text event.


---

### TestWaitForSomeIpMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSomeIpMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSomeIpMessage.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the next SOME/IP message to occur that meets the conditions passed as arguments. If the message does not occur before the timeout expires, the wait condition is still resolved. The different forms of TestWaitForSomeIpMessage offer the possibility to define the condition of the expected SOME/IP message in detail. For the numeric values, e.g. serviceID, -1 (wildcard) can be used to exclude a condition from the comparison. Forms 1 and 3 can be used to specify the Ethernet port on which the SOME/IP message must occur. Forms 2 and 4 also specify the IP endpoints with which the SOME/IP message is transmitted. If the expected SOME/IP message occurred (return value is 1), then the content of the message can be fetched with TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData . Note The order of the argument for servicedID, interfaceVersion, serviceInstanceID and methodID in form 1 to 4 corresponds to the qualifier of on someipMessage .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpMessage( long vlanID, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType, dword timeout ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeI  pMessage( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType, dword timeout ); // form 2
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpMessage( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType, dword timeout ); // form 3
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpMessage( long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long serviceID, long interfaceVersion, long serviceInstanceID, long methodID, long messageType, dword timeout ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethPort` | Ethernet port of the specified SOME/IP message. |
| `vlanID` | VLAN identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any VLAN identifier. Valid range 0..FFFh |
| `sourceEP` | Source IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP message. |
| `destinationEP` | Destination IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP message |
| `serviceID` | Service identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `interfaceVersion` | Interface version of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any version. Valid range 0..FFh |
| `serviceInstanceID` | Service instance identifier of expected SOME/IP
 message or -1 for any instance identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `methodID` | Method identifier of expected SOME/IP message or -1
 for any method identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `messageType` | Message type of expected SOME/IP message or -1 for
 any type. Valid range 0..FFh |
| `timeout` | Maximum time to wait [ms] or 0 for no timeout. |


**Return Value:** 0 Resume due to timeout. 1 Resume due to event occurred. -2 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -1 Invalid argument value, see Write Windows for details.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
result = testWaitForSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1,-1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP message received on ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received a SOME/IP message" ); break; default: testStepFail( "", "testWaitForSomeipMessage failed with result %d", result ); return; }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP message on specific Ethernet port.

*Example*

```c
someipMessage *msg; result = testWaitForSomeIpMessage( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, 5, 1000, 1, 1, 0x8001, 0x02, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP message received on ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received a SOME/IP message" ); break; default: testStepFail( "", "testWaitForSomeIpMessage failed with result %d", result ); return; } if (TestGetWaitSomeipMessageData( msg ) != 0) { testStepFail( "TestGetWaitSomeIpMessageData failed" ); return; } if (msg.ReturnCode != 0) { testStepFail( "", "Return code is %d, but 0 expected", msg.ReturnCode ); }
```

> Wait for SOME/IP message with VLAN ID 5, Service ID 1000, Version 1, Instance ID 1 and Message Type Notification. Then check return code of received message.

*Example*

```c
long result; result = testWaitForSomeIpMessage( ethernetPort::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, ip_endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.1:30500), // source IP endpoint ip_Endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.2:30500), // destination IP endpoint -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP message received" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received a SOME/IP message" ); break; default: testStepFail( "", "testWaitForSomeIpMessage failed with result %d", result ); return; }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP message from UDP:192.168.0.1:30500 to UDP:192.168.0.2:30500.


---

### TestWaitForSomeIpSD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSomeIpSD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSomeIpSD.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the next SOME/IP Service Discovery entry to occur that meets the conditions passed as arguments. If the message does not occur before the timeout expires, the wait condition is still resolved. The different forms of TestWaitForSomeIpSD offer the possibility to define the condition of the expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry in detail. For the numeric values, e.g. serviceID, -1 (wildcard) can be used to exclude a condition from the comparison. Forms 1 and 3 can be used to specify the Ethernet port on which the SOME/IP message must occur. Forms 2 and 4 also specify the IP endpoints with which the SOME/IP message is transmitted. If the expected SOME/IP Service Discovery entry occurred (return value is 1), then the content of the message can be fetched with TestGetWaitSomeIpSDData .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpSD( long vlanID, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID, dword timeout ); // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpSD  ( ethernetPort  ethPort, long vlanID, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, long servceInstanceID , long eventGroupID, dword timeout ); // form 2
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpSD( ethernetPort ethPort, long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID, dword timeout ); // form 3
```

```c
long TestWaitForSomeIpSD( long vlanID, ip_endpoint sourceEP, ip_endpoint destinationEP, long sdType, long serviceID, long majorVersion, servceInstanceID, long eventGroupID, dword timeout ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ethPort` | Ethernet port of the specified SOME/IP SD entry. |
| `vlanID` | VLAN identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1
 for any VLAN identifier. Valid range 0..FFFh |
| `sourceEP` | Source IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP SD entry |
| `destinationEP` | Destination IP endpoint of expected SOME/IP SD entry |
| `sdType` | SD record type of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1
 for any type. Valid range 0..FFh 0x00 - Find Service 0x01 - Offer Service 0x06 - Subscribe Eventgroup 0x07 - Subscribe Acknowledge |
| `serviceID` | Service identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or
 -1 for any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `majorVersion` | Major version of expected SOME/IP SD entry or -1 for
 any service identifier. Valid range 0..FFh |
| `serviceInstanceID` | Service instance identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry
 or -1 for any service instance identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh |
| `eventGroupID` | Event group identifier of expected SOME/IP SD entry or
 -1 for any event group identifier. Valid range 0..FFFFh Only supported for Subscribe, SubscribeACK and StopSubscribe record. Must be -1 for Offer and Find |
| `timeout` | Maximum time to wait [ms] or 0 for no timeout. |


**Return Value:** 0 Resume due to timeout. 1 Resume due to event occurred. -2 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -1 Invalid argument value, see Write Windows for details.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long result; result = testWaitForSomeIpSD( ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, not any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry received on ethernetport::VehicleNetwork::Port1" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received a SOME/IP Service Discovery entry" ); break; default: testStepFail( "", " testWaitForSomeIpSD failed with result %d", result ); return; }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry on specific Ethernet port.

*Example*

```c
result = testWaitForSomeIpSD( ethernetPort::VehicleNetwork::Port1, -1, // any VLAN ID ip_endpoint(UDP:192.168.0.1:30490), // source IP endpoint ip_endpoint(UDP:239.0.0.1:30490), // destination IP endpoint 0x01, 1000, -1, -1, -1, 200 ); switch(result) { case 0: testStepFail( "Timeout, Offer-Service 1000 not received" ); return; case 1: testStepPass( "Received Offer-Service 1000 " ); break; default: testStepFail( "", " testWaitForSomeIpSD failed with result %d", result ); return; }
```

> Wait for any SOME/IP Service Discovery entry with Offer of service identifier 1000 from UDP:192.168.0.1:30500 to UDP:192.168.0.2:30500.long result;

### SecurityLocalExtractKeyFromSubjectPublicKeyInfo

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalExtractKeyFromSubjectPublicKeyInfo.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalExtractKeyFromSubjectPublicKeyInfo.htm)

**Description:**

Extracts the raw subjectPublicKey from an X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo structure in PEM or DER format. Note that the bytes inside the subjectPublicKey are extracted and not the complete BIT STRING ASN.1 structure.


**Syntax:**

```c
SecurityLocalExtractKeyFromSubjectPublicKeyInfo (byte[] subjectPublicKeyInfo, dword subjectPublicKeyInfoLength, byte[] rawKey, dword* rawKeyLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte subjectPublicKeyInfo[]` | The X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo structure in PEM or DER format. |
| `dword subjectPublicKeyInfoLength` | The length of the SubjectPublicKeyInfo. |
| `byte rawKey[] [IN/OUT]` | The raw subjectPublicKey to be extracted. |
| `dword rawKeyLength [IN/OUT]` | The length of the raw subjectPublicKey to be extracted. |


**Return Value:** 1 Success. A value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error. A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
byte subjectPublicKeyInfo[91]= {0x30,0x59,0x30,0x13,0x06,0x07,0x2a,0x86,0x48,0xce,0x3d,0x02,0x01,0x06,0x08,0x2a,0x86,0x48,0xce,0x3d,0x03,0x01,0x07,0x03,0x42,0x00,0x04,0x32,0x56,0x19,0x89,0xa7,0xff,0x74,0x27,0xc3,0x6e,0x86,0xfe,0xb5,0xed,0xcf,0x42,0x7c,0x31,0xbe,0x33,0x44,0xf5,0xbe,0x60,0x58,0x95,0xb3,0xd8,0xf8,0xa4,0x57,0xf9,0xbb,0x22,0xa8,0xaa,0x4b,0x1a,0x4d,0x12,0xfd,0xb2,0x7e,0x0a,0x67,0xcd,0xc6,0xa8,0x1f,0x6a,0x98,0xa2,0x16,0x00,0xdb,0xf0,0xfb,0x11,0x95,0x20,0x53,0x7e,0xf2,0xa8}; dword subjectPublicKeyInfoLength = elcount (subjectPublicKeyInfo); byte rawKey[100]; dword rawKeyLength = elcount (rawKey); long result; result = SecurityLocalExtractKeyFromSubjectPublicKeyInfo(subjectPublicKeyInfo, subjectPublicKeyInfoLength, rawKey, rawKeyLength);
```


---

### SecurityLocalEncryptAES

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES.htm)

**Description:**

Encrypts the data using AES with a specified key, block cipher mode and padding mode. For ECB mode, the initialization vector (IV) is ignored. For CTR mode, the padding mode is ignored.


**Syntax:**

```c
SecurityLocalEncryptAES(dword blockCipherMode, dword paddingMode, byte key[], dword keyLength, byte data[], dword dataLength, byte initVector[], dword initVectorLength, byte cipheredData[], dword cipheredDataLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword
 blockCipherMode` | The block cipher mode. 0 = CBC, 1 = ECB, 2 = CTR (ECB and CTR
 supported for CANoe 17 and higher). |
| `dword paddingMode` | The padding mode. 0 = PKCS #5, 1 = No padding, 2 =
 One with zeroes padding. Ignored for CTR mode. |
| `byte
 key[]` | The key to be used for AES encryption. |
| `dword
 keyLength` | The key length. Either 16 (128 bit), 24 (192 bit) or
 32 (256 bit). |
| `byte
 data[]` | The data to be encrypted. |
| `dword
 dataLength` | The length of the data to be encrypted. |
| `byte
 initVector[]` | The initialization vector. Ignored for ECB. |
| `dword
 initVectorLength` | The length of the initialization vector. |
| `byte
 cipheredData[]` | The buffer in which the ciphered data is stored. |
| `dword cipheredDataLength [In/Out]` | Input: The length of the ciphered data buffer. If there is no padding, the ciphered data length
 must be at least as large as the data length. If there is padding, the ciphered data length must
 be larger than the data length and rounded up to the next multiple of 16. Output: The amount of data written to the buffer. |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
byte keyData[16] = {0xc2, 0x86, 0x69, 0x6d, 0x88, 0x7c, 0x9a, 0xa0, 0x61, 0x1b, 0xbb, 0x3e, 0x20, 0x25, 0xa4, 0x5a}; dword keyLength = elcount(keyData); byte plainData[30] = {0x75, 0x86, 0x60, 0x2d, 0x25, 0x3c, 0xff, 0xf9, 0x1b, 0x82, 0x66, 0xbe, 0xa6, 0xd6, 0x1a, 0xb1, 0xbc, 0xfd, 0x81, 0x02, 0x22, 0x02, 0x36, 0x6b, 0xde, 0x6d, 0xd2, 0x60, 0xa1, 0x58}; dword plainDataLength = elcount(plainData); byte initVector[16] = {0x56, 0x2e, 0x17, 0x99, 0x6d, 0x09, 0x3d, 0x28, 0xdd, 0xb3, 0xba, 0x69, 0x5a, 0x2e, 0x6f, 0x58}; dword initVectorLength = elcount(initVector); byte cipheredOutput[32]; dword cipheredOutputLength = elcount(cipheredOutput); long result; result = SecurityLocalEncryptAES(0, 0, keyData, keyLength, plainData, plainDataLength, initVector, initVectorLength, cipheredOutput, cipheredOutputLength);
```


---

### SecurityLocalDecryptAES

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalDecryptAES.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalDecryptAES.htm)

**Description:**

Decrypts the data using AES with a given key, block cipher mode and padding mode. For ECB mode, the initialization vector (IV) is ignored. For CTR mode, the padding mode is ignored.


**Syntax:**

```c
SecurityLocalDecryptAES(dword blockCipherMode, dword paddingMode, byte key[], dword keyLength, byte data[], dword dataLength, byte initVector[], dword initVectorLength, byte plainData[], dword plainDataLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword
 blockCipherMode` | The block cipher mode. 0 = CBC, 1 = ECB, 2 = CTR (ECB and CTR
 supported for CANoe 17 and higher). |
| `dword paddingMode` | The padding mode. 0 = PKCS #5, 1 = No padding, 2 =
 One with zeroes padding. Ignored for CTR mode. |
| `byte
 key[]` | The key to be used for AES decryption. |
| `dword
 keyLength` | The key length. Either 16 (128 bit), 24 (192 bit) or
 32 (256 bit). |
| `byte
 data[]` | The data to be decrypted. |
| `dword
 dataLength` | The length of the data to decrypted. |
| `byte
 initVector[]` | The initialization vector. Ignored for ECB. |
| `dword
 initVectorLength` | The length of the initialization vector. |
| `byte
 plainData[]` | The buffer in which the plain data is stored. |
| `dword plainData Length [In/Out]` | Input: The length of the plain data buffer. Typically, this buffer should have
 the same length as the ciphered data buffer. Output: The amount of data written to the buffer. |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
byte keyData[16] = {0xc2, 0x86, 0x69, 0x6d, 0x88, 0x7c, 0x9a, 0xa0, 0x61, 0x1b, 0xbb, 0x3e, 0x20, 0x25, 0xa4, 0x5a}; dword keyLength = elcount(keyData); byte cipheredData[48] = {0xd2, 0x96, 0xcd, 0x94, 0xc2, 0xcc, 0xcf, 0x8a, 0x3a, 0x86, 0x30, 0x28, 0xb5, 0xe1, 0xdc, 0x0a, 0x75, 0x86, 0x60, 0x2d, 0x25, 0x3c, 0xff, 0xf9, 0x1b, 0x82, 0x66, 0xbe, 0xa6, 0xd6, 0x1a, 0xb1, 0xbc, 0xfd, 0x81, 0x02, 0x22, 0x02, 0x36, 0x6b, 0xde, 0x6d, 0xd2, 0x60, 0xa1, 0x58, 0x41, 0xa1}; dword cipheredDataLength = elcount(cipheredData); byte initVector[16] = {0x56, 0x2e, 0x17, 0x99, 0x6d, 0x09, 0x3d, 0x28, 0xdd, 0xb3, 0xba, 0x69, 0x5a, 0x2e, 0x6f, 0x58}; dword initVectorLength = elcount(initVector) byte plainOutput[48]; dword plainOutputLength = elcount(plainOutput); long result; result = SecurityLocalDecryptAES(0, 0, keyData, keyLength, cipheredData, cipheredDataLength, initVector, initVectorLength, plainOutput, plainOutputLength);
```

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCGetConfiguredEMAID.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCGetConfiguredEMAID.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the eMAID configured in the XML file of the vehicle. See also eMAID of Configuration of Vehicle SCC Nodes .


**Syntax:**

```c
bool SCC_GetConfiguredEMAID ( char eMAID[], long& StringLength )
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `eMAID` | Queries the eMAID configured in the XML file (to the given char buffer). |
| `StringLength` | In&Out-Parameter: Used to tell the length of out character array and gets the string length after reading the parameter. (via reference). |


**Return Value:** 0 Not successful 1 Successful


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### SCC_GetConfiguredContractCert

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCGetConfiguredContractCert.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCGetConfiguredContractCert.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the contract certificate name configured in the XML file of the vehicle. See also ContractCert of Configuration of Vehicle SCC Nodes .


**Syntax:**

```c
bool SCC_GetConfiguredContractCert ( char ContractCertName[], long& StringLength )
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ContractCertName` | Queries the the contract certificate name configured in the XML file (to the given char buffer). |
| `StringLength` | In&Out-Parameter: Used to tell the length of out character array and gets the string length after reading the parameter. (via reference). |


**Return Value:** 0 Not successful 1 Successful


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

**Description:**

Specifies the delay time between reaching the state protocol finished (e.g. because of SessionStop or a timeout / error) and the closure of the TCP connection with ACK FIN.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType SetTCPShutdownDelay(int32 DelayMs)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `DelayMs` | 0 = Close immediately. > 0 = Close after the specified time has passed. |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### GetCertificateByName

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionGetCertificateByName.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionGetCertificateByName.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves a certificate by its name and returns its DER-encoded data and length through the output parameters.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType GetCertificateByName(string certificateName, out bytes DEREncodedCertificateData_out, out uint16 certificateLength_out)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `certificateName` | Name of the certificate to be retrieved. |
| `DEREncodedCertificateData_out` | Queries DER-encoded (Distinguished Encoding Rules) data of the certificate (to the given byte buffer). |
| `certificateLength_out` | Gets length of the certificate data (via reference) |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
char certName[20] = "DE-8AA-CA2B3C4D5-7"; byte encodedCert[1500]; word certLen; EV.GetCertificateByName.Call(certName, encodedCert, certLen); //outputs the name and the certificate length to the write window writeLineEx(0,1,"certificate name = %s", certName); writeLineEx(0,1,"certificate length = %d", certLen);
```

### GetCertificatePrivateKey

**Description:**

Retrieves the private key associated with a specified certificate and returns its BER-decoded data and length through the output parameters.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType GetCertificatePrivateKey(string certificateName, out bytes BERDecodedPrivateKeyData_out, out uint16 privateKeyLength_out)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `certificateName` | Name of the certificate to be retrieved. |
| `BERDecodedPrivateKeyData_out` | Retrieves BER-decoded data of the private key (to the given byte buffer). |
| `certificateLength_out` | Gets length of the certificate data (via reference). |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
char certName[20] = "DE-8AA-CA2B3C4D5-7"; byte pKey[900]; word keyLen; EV.GetCertificatePrivateKey.Call(certName, pKey, keyLen); //outputs the name and the key length to the write window writeLineEx(0,1,"certificate name = %s", certName); writeLineEx(0,1,"key length = %d", keyLen);
```

### AvoidTLSAuthentication

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionAvoidTLSAuthentication.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionAvoidTLSAuthentication.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to avoid the TLS authentication from the client or the server. The TLS handshake will not be started from client or avoided from server. The port is kept open by the simulation and continues to be listened to. Note : Internal flag is reset after first avoided handshake, during the reset ( ResetSimulation ) and during the TCP close, so that the function must be called again for next connection establishment.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType AvoidTLSAuthentication (bool avoidAuthentication)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `avoidAuthentication` | 1: enable 0: disable |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'x' { //avoid TLS handshake from client (EV) EV.AvoidTLSAuthentication.Call(1); } on key 'y' { //avoid TLS handshake from server (EVSE) EVSE.AvoidTLSAuthentication.Call(1); }
```


---

### OnSocketReceivedRawInd

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Callbacks/CAPLfunctionSCCOnSocketReceivedRawInd.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Callbacks/CAPLfunctionSCCOnSocketReceivedRawInd.htm)

**Description:**

This callback is called each time a TCP/UDP packet is received on the socket by the DO.  It allows querying the payload and its length, as well as the source and destination ports. Compared to OnSocketReceivedInd it does not check the content before and is called regardless of whether a V2G / SDP message is contained or not. Note : Is called when closing a TCP connection with 0 bytes.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnSocketReceivedRawInd(SocketProtocolType protocolType, uint32 sourcePort, uint32 destinationPort, inout bytes payload, uint32 payloadLength);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `protocolType` | Protocol type of the socket on which data is received: SocketProtocolType::UDP SocketProtocolType::TCP |
| `sourcePort` | The source port of the received packet. |
| `destinationPort` | The destination port of the received packet. |
| `payload` | The payload of the received packet. |
| `payloadLength` | The length of the payload of the received packet. |


**Example(s):**

```c
on fct_returning EV.OnSocketReceivedRawInd { byte payloadBuffer[1600]; int i; // Copy data to buffer to allow manipulation memcpy(payloadBuffer, this.payload); // print all parameters to write window (just showing access) writeLineEx(0,1,"protocolType = %d", this.protocolType); writeLineEx(0,1,"sourcePort = %d", this.sourcePort); writeLineEx(0,1,"destinationPort = %d", this.destinationPort); writeLineEx(0,1,"payloadLength = %d", this.payloadLength); }
```


---

### GetLastSocketError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionGetLastSocketError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionGetLastSocketError.htm)

**Description:**

Function to retrieve socket errors in case of any error sending data. Error codes match CAPL function IpGetLastSocketError .


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType GetLastSocketError(out int32 SocketError_out)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `SocketError_out` | Out parameter to retrieve the last socket error code. |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'y' { long error = 0; EV.GetLastSocketError.Call(error);	//retrieve error writeLineEx(0,3,"error = %d", error);	//print error to write window }
```

### SocketPayloadTransmit

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSocketPayloadTransmit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSocketPayloadTransmit.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be called at any time to send either a TCP or UDP payload. If the corresponding socket is open, it transmits the given data as the payload. In case of any error, the function GetLastSocketError can be used to retrieve the error details.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType SocketPayloadTransmit(bytes payload, SocketProtocolType protocolType)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payload` | UDP or TCP payload that shall be transmitted given in a byte buffer. |
| `protocolType` | Type of protocol to be used by the socket: SocketProtocolType::UDP SocketProtocolType::TCP |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'x' { //this is the data of a SDPResponse, normally send by EVSE. Any other data can also be used byte data[28] = {0x01, 0xFE, 0x90, 0x01, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x14, 0xFE, 0x80, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x02, 0xC7, 0xA7, 0x10, 0x00}; //using TCP socket to inject the SDPResponse from EV EV.SocketPayloadTransmit.Call(data, _CCS::DataTypes::SocketProtocolType::TCP); }
```

### SetCertificateFaultInjection

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSetCertificateFaultInjection.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSetCertificateFaultInjection.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to manipulate the certificates used within the V2G communication. Will be reset after calling ResetSimulation or using RemoveAllCertificateFaultInjections .


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType SetCertificateFaultInjection(string certificateName, CertificateFaultInjectionType certificateFaultInjectionType, bool enable)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `certificateName` | Name of the certificate that shall be manipulated. |
| `certificateFaultInjectionType` | Enum with following values: DisturbIssuer = 0 (reverse issuer) DisturbSignature = 1 (flips second byte of the certificate signature) DisturbValidity = 2 (sets validity to back to 2001) OmitDomainComponent = 3 (removes domain component) UnencryptedPrivateKey = 4 (forces EVSE to send the private key within certificate installation unencrypted) DisturbPrivateKey = 5 (flip second byte of the private key within certificate installation before AES encrypt) DisturbEncryptedPrivateKey = 6 (flip second byte of the private key within certificate installation after AES encrypt) DisturbPublicKey = 7 (flip second byte of public key within certificate) OmitOrganization = 8 (removes organization from cert - if extisting -) OmitCommonName = 9 (removes common name from cert) DisturbSerialNumber = 10 (flip second byte of serial number) DisturbCertPolicies = 11 (flip second byte of certificate policies) ReferenceWrongCA = 12 (loads issuer name of issuer certificate and copies it to current cert - e.g. leaf certificate issuer will be changed from SubCA2 to SubCA1) WrongSignatureHashFunction = 13 (uses SHA-256 to sign certificate – instead of SHA512 which is mandatory for ISO-20) |
| `enable` | 1: enable 0: disable |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
on key ‘x’ { EV.SetCertificateFaultInjection.Call("DE-8AA-CA2B3C4D5-7" /*certificate name*/, _CCS::DataTypes::CertificateFaultInjectionType::DisturbCertPolicies /*fault injection type*/, 1 /*enable*/); }
```


---

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionRemoveAllCertificateFaultInjections.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionRemoveAllCertificateFaultInjections.htm)

This function can be used to reset the flags for V2G certificate manipulation set by SetCertificateFaultInjection .

```c
MethodReturnValueType RemoveAllCertificateFaultInjections()
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |

### SetSignatureFaultInjection

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSetSignatureFaultInjection.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingFunctions/CAPLfunctionSetSignatureFaultInjection.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to manipulate the signature of signed V2G messages. Must be called before the corresponding message is transmitted. Will be reset after calling ResetSimulation or after first usage.


**Syntax:**

```c
MethodReturnValueType SetSignatureFaultInjection(SignatureFaultInjectionType signatureFaultInjectionType, bool enable)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `signatureFaultInjectionType` | Enum with following values: DisturbSignature = 0 (flips second byte of the signature) DisturbDigest = 1 (flips second byte of the first digest) DisturbSignatureReference = 2 (flips second byte of the URI) |
| `enable` | 1: enable 0: disable |


**Return Value:** All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values:


**Example(s):**

```c
on key ‘x’ { EV.SetSignatureFaultInjection.Call(_CCS::DataTypes::SignatureFaultInjectionType::DisturbSignatureReference /*fault injetion type*/, 1/*enable*/); }
```

### SCC_SLAC_SetPSD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCSLACSetPSD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/Functions/CAPLfunctionSCCSLACSetPSD.htm)

**Description:**

Fills the power spectral density map as a prerequisite for Amplitude Map Exchange. The data format is equal to the message CM_SLAC_AmpMap.Req, so the data will be sent exactly as set with this function. To deactivate Amplitude Map Exchange (default), set a map of {0..0}
. To activate Amplitude Map Exchange, set a map that contains values != 0
. Note : Because the PSD is defined in values of 4 bit, only the lower half of each byte is considered.


**Syntax:**

```c
SCC_SLAC_SetPSD ( byte PSD[], dword PSDLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `PSD` | The Power Spectral Density in differences to the specified mean value and in [-dBm/Hz]. This must be a 58 or 917 byte array, depending on the setting in the XML configuration (see help pages Configuration of Vehicle SCC Nodes (Smart Charging) and Configuration of Charge Point SCC Nodes (Smart Charging) ). |
| `PSDLength` | Length of the byte array. |


**Return Value:** 0 Not successful 1 Successful


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

Gets the content of the RootCertificateID element with the specified index. Function shall only be used for ISO 15118.

```c
char IssuerName [], byte SerialNumber[], 
long& SerialNumberLength, long& IssuerNameLength )
```

| `IssuerName` | Queries the X509IssuerName of the target RootCertificate (to the given char buffer). |
| `SerialNumber` | Queries the X509SerialNumber of the target RootCertificate (to the given byte buffer). |
| `SerialNumberLength` | Gets the Byte length of X509SerialNumber (via reference). |
| `IssuerNameLength` | Gets the String length of IssuerName (via reference). |

```c

```

### OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingCallbacks/CAPLfunctionOnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SmartCharging/CCSBindingCallbacks/CAPLfunctionOnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged.htm)

**Description:**

This callback is called as soon as a message is received. It is available for the whole Distributed Object and for the following embedded members: SLAC SDP SAP DIN ISO2 ISO20 ScheduleRenegotiation MeteringConfirmation


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged ( LastRxMessageIdType LastRxMessageId )
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `LastRxMessageId` | ID of the last message received. See MessageIDs for more information. |


**Example(s):**

```c
// Handle message transistions whenever a message change happens. See MessageID for references to specific messages on fct_called EVSE.OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged { // for example: store message ID in a systemvariable @Common::LastReceivedMessageId = $EVSE.OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged.LatestCall.lastRxMessageId; // test for certain messages if($EV.OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged.LatestCall.lastRxMessageId == _CS::DataTypes::MessageIdType::DCChargeLoopRes) { // Do something only when the DC charging loop response was received } }
```

### SecurityLocalGenerateEphemeralKeyPair

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateEphemeralKeyPair.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateEphemeralKeyPair.htm)

**Description:**

Generates a key pair to use in shared secret generation using the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) protocol. Currently the curves NIST P-256, NIST P-384, NIST P-521, Curve448 and Curve25519 are supported.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalGenerateEphemeralKeyPair(dword curve, byte publicKey[], dword* publicKeyLength, byte privateKey[], dword* privateKeyLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword curve` | The elliptic curve to use for key pair generation. Possible Values: 10 → NIST P-256 (also known as secp256r1) 11 → NIST P-384 (also known as secp384r1) 12 → NIST P-521 (also known as secp521r1) 22 → Curve25519 23 → Curve448 |
| `byte publicKey[] [OUT]` | The generated public key. For the curves NIST P-256, P-384, P-521 the key is in the uncompressed format 0x04 | HEX(x) | HEX(y). For Curve25519 and Curve448 the key is in the raw format. |
| `dword publicKeyLength [IN/OUT]` | The length of the generated public key NIST P-256: 65 bytes (uncompressed) NIST P-384: 97 bytes (uncompressed) NIST P-521 133 bytes (uncompressed) Curve25519: 32 bytes Curve448: 56 bytes |
| `byte privateKey[] [OUT]` | The generated private key. |
| `dword privateKeyLength [IN/OUT]` | The length of the generated private key. NIST P-256: 32 bytes NIST P-384: 48 bytes NIST P-521: 66 bytes Curve25519: 32 bytes Curve448: 56 bytes |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

```c
dword curve = 10; byte publicKey[65]; dword publicKeyLength = 65; byte privateKey[32]; dword privateKeyLength = 32; long result; result = SecurityLocalGenerateEphemeralKeyPair(curve, publicKey, publicKeyLength, privateKey, privateKeyLength);
```


---

### SecurityLocalGenerateSharedSecret

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSharedSecret.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSharedSecret.htm)

**Description:**

Generates a shared secret between A and B using the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) protocol. Currently the curves NIST P-256, NIST P-384, NIST P-521, Curve448 and Curve25519 are supported.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalGenerateSharedSecret(dword curve, byte publicKey[], dword publicKeyLength, byte privateKey[], dword privateKeyLength, byte sharedSecret[], dword* sharedSecretLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword curve` | The elliptic curve used for shared secret generation. Possible Values: 10 → NIST P-256 (also known as secp256r1) 11 → NIST P-384 (also known as secp384r1) 12 → NIST P-521 (also known as secp521r1) 22 → Curve25519 23 → Curve448 |
| `byte publicKey[]` | The public key of A. For the curves NIST P-256, P-384, P-521 the uncompressed format 0x04 | HEX(x) | HEX(y) as well as the compressed formats 0x02 | HEX(x) (for even y) OR 0x03 | HEX(x) (for odd y) are supported. For Curve25519 and Curve448 the raw format is supported. |
| `dword publicKeyLength` | The length of the generated public key. NIST P-256: 65 bytes (uncompressed) or 32 bytes (compressed) NIST P-384: 97 bytes (uncompressed) or 49 bytes (compressed) NIST P-521 133 bytes (uncompressed) or 67 bytes (compressed) Curve25519: 32 bytes Curve448: 56 bytes |
| `byte privateKey[]` | The private key of B. |
| `dword privateKeyLength` | The length of the private key of B. NIST P-256: 32 bytes NIST P-384: 48 bytes NIST P-521: 66 bytes Curve25519: 32 bytes Curve448: 56 bytes |
| `byte sharedSecret[] [OUT]` | The generated shared secret. |
| `dword sharedSecretLength [IN/OUT]` | The length of the generated shared secret. NIST P-256: 32 bytes NIST P-384: 48 bytes NIST P-521: 66 bytes Curve25519: 32 bytes Curve448: 56 bytes |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

```c
dword curve = 10; byte publicKey[65] = {0x04, 0xDA, 0xD0, 0xB6, 0x53, 0x94, 0x22, 0x1C, 0xF9, 0xB0, 0x51, 0xE1, 0xFE, 0xCA, 0x57, 0x87, 0xD0, 0x98, 0xDF, 0xE6, 0x37, 0xFC, 0x90, 0xB9, 0xEF, 0x94, 0x5D, 0x0C, 0x37, 0x72, 0x58, 0x11, 0x80, 0x52, 0x71, 0xA0, 0x46, 0x1C, 0xDB, 0x82, 0x52, 0xD6, 0x1F, 0x1C, 0x45, 0x6F, 0xA3, 0xE5, 0x9A, 0xB1, 0xF4, 0x5B, 0x33, 0xAC, 0xCF, 0x5F, 0x58, 0x38, 0x9E, 0x05, 0x77, 0xB8, 0x99, 0x0B, 0xB3}; dword publicKeyLength = 65; byte privateKey[32] = {0xC6, 0xEF, 0x9C, 0x5D, 0x78, 0xAE, 0x01, 0x2A, 0x01, 0x11, 0x64, 0xAC, 0xB3, 0x97, 0xCE, 0x20, 0x88, 0x68, 0x5D, 0x8F, 0x06, 0xBF, 0x9B, 0xE0, 0xB2, 0x83, 0xAB, 0x46, 0x47, 0x6B, 0xEE, 0x53}; dword privateKeyLength = 32; byte sharedSecret[32]; dword sharedSecretLength = 32; long result; result = SecurityLocalGenerateSharedSecret(curve, publicKey, publicKeyLength, privateKey, privateKeyLength, sharedSecret, sharedSecretLength);
```

### TLS CAPL Functions

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/CAPLfunctionsTLSOverview.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/CAPLfunctionsTLSOverview.htm)

### TestGetTestExecutionVerdict

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetTestExecutionVerdict.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestGetTestExecutionVerdict.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the verdict of a finished test execution.


**Syntax:**

```c
long testGetTestExecutionVerdict(long handle);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `handle` | Handle of the previously started test execution. |


**Return Value:** 0 The verdict of the finished test execution is: Passed 1 The verdict of the finished test execution is: Failed 2 The verdict of the finished test execution is: None 3 The verdict of the finished test execution is:Inconclusive 4 The verdict of the finished test execution is: Error in Test System -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available -2 The handle is not available -3 The test execution was not finished yet


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long handle; long verdict; handle = testStartTestExecution("TestUnit", "TestCaseID"); testWaitForTestExecutionFinished(handle, 1000); verdict = testGetTestExecutionVerdict(handle);
```


---

### TestStartTestExecution

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestStartTestExecution.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestStartTestExecution.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the test execution. The function returns at the start of the test case. In case a previously started test execution is not finished yet, for example report generation is still in progress, the start function will wait for the end of the previous test execution. This makes it possible to start the same test execution continuously in consecutive test cases.


**Syntax:**

```c
long testStartTestExecution(char testUnit[], char testCaseID[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `testUnit` | Name of the test unit. |
| `testCAseID` | Identifier of the test case. |


**Return Value:** > 0 Handle of the started test execution. 0 The test unit or the test case identifier could not be found or the test case identifier is not unique within the test unit. -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -2 The test execution could not be started because the test unit is already in execution.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long handle; long verdict; handle = testStartTestExecution("TestUnit", "TestCaseID"); testWaitForTestExecutionFinished(handle, 1000); verdict = testGetTestExecutionVerdict(handle);
```


---

### TestStopTestExecution

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestStopTestExecution.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestStopTestExecution.htm)

**Description:**

Stops the test execution.


**Syntax:**

```c
long testStopTestExecution(long handle);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `handle` | Handle of the previously started test execution. |


**Return Value:** 1 The test execution was stoped. -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -2 The handle is not available. -3 The test unit is in progress but was not started by this test execution handle.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long handle; handle = testStartTestExecution("TestUnit", "TestCaseID"); testStopTestExecution(handle);
```


---

### TestWaitForTestExecutionFinished

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForTestExecutionFinished.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForTestExecutionFinished.htm)

**Description:**

Waits until the test execution is finished. This function returns at the end of the test case.


**Syntax:**

```c
long testWaitForTestExecutionFinished(long handle, dword timeout);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `handle` | Handle of the previously started test execution. |
| `timeout` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling |


**Return Value:** 1 Resume due to event occurred (test execution is finished). 0 Resume due to timeout. -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. -2 The handle is not available.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
long handle; long verdict; handle = testStartTestExecution("TestUnit", "TestCaseID"); testWaitForTestExecutionFinished(handle, 1000); verdict = testGetTestExecutionVerdict(handle);
```


---

```c
—
```

### StartStimulation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/VTSystem/Functions/CAPLfunctionVTSStartStimulation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/VTSystem/Functions/CAPLfunctionVTSStartStimulation.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the output of a stimulation signal. A corresponding output mode must be set in advance.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SysVarNamespace.StartStimulation ();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -4 The function wasn't called in the context of the main method of a test module. So it is not possible to wait until the setting will be taken over from the VT System. Otherwise the call was successful but it is not sure if the settings have been taken over already when the call returns.


---

### vtsStartStimulation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/VTSystem/Functions/CAPLfunctionVTSvtsStartStimulation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/VTSystem/Functions/CAPLfunctionVTSvtsStartStimulation.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the output of a stimulation signal. A corresponding output mode must be set in advance.


**Syntax:**

```c
long vtsStartStimulation (char Target[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `Target` | Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. |


**Return Value:** 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -4 The function wasn't called in the context of the main method of a test module. So it is not possible to wait until the setting will be taken over from the VT System. Otherwise the call was successful but it is not sure if the settings have been taken over already when the call returns.

### Configuration: CAPL

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CANoeCANalyzer/Ribbon/File/Options/Programming/ProgrammingCAPL.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CANoeCANalyzer/Ribbon/File/Options/Programming/ProgrammingCAPL.htm)
