### SecurityLocalEncryptAES128CBC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES128CBC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES128CBC.htm)

**Description:**

Encrypts data with a given key and initialization vector using AES128 (CBC), Padding Mode PKCS5.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalEncryptAES128CBC(byte key[], dword keyLength, byte data[], dword dataLength, byte initVector[], dword initVectorLength, byte cipheredData[], dword cipheredDataLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte key[]` | The key to be used for AES (128 bit). |
| `dword keyLength` | 16 (bytes). |
| `byte data[]` | The data to encrypt. |
| `dword dataLength` | The length of the data to encrypt. |
| `byte initVector[]` | The init vector to be used. |
| `dword initVectorLength` | 16 (bytes). |
| `byte cipheredData [] [Out]` | The buffer in which the ciphered data is stored. |
| `dword cipheredData Length [In/Out]` | The length of the buffer.Typically this buffer has to be 16 bytes longer than the length of the data to encrypt. |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. <= 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
byte keyData[16] = {0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}; dword keyLength = elcount(keyData); byte data[16] = {0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}; dword dataLength = elcount(data); byte initVector[16] = {0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}; dword initVectorLength = elcount(initVector); byte cipheredOutput[32]; dword cipheredOutputLength = elcount(cipheredOutput); long result; result = SecurityLocalEncryptAES128CBC(keyData, keyLength, data, dataLength, initVector, initVectorLength, cipheredOutput, cipheredOutputLength); —
```


---

### SecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB.htm)

**Description:**

Encrypts data with a given key using AES128 (ECB), Padding Mode PKCS5.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB(byte key[], dword keyLength, byte data[], dword dataLength, byte cipheredData[], dword cipheredDataLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte key[]` | The key to be used for AES (128 bit). |
| `dword keyLength` | 16 (bytes). |
| `byte data[]` | The data to encrypt. |
| `dword dataLength` | The length of the data to encrypt. |
| `byte cipheredData [] [Out]` | The buffer in which the ciphered data is stored. |
| `dword cipheredData Length [In/Out]` | The length of the buffer. Typically this buffer has to be 16 bytes longer than the length of the data to encrypt. |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. <= 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

```c
void Encrypt1(byte E1[]) { byte BaseKey[16] = {0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00}; byte Data[16] = {0x01,0x23,0x45,0x67,0x89,0xAB,0xCD,0xEF,0x01,0x23,0x45,0x67,0x89,0xAB,0xCD,0xEF}; byte Result[32]; dword length = elCount(Result); int i; dword res; res = SecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB(BaseKey, elCount(BaseKey),Data, elCount(Data),Result, length); write("SecurityLocalEncryptAES128ECB Result: %d",res); for(i =0;i<length ;i++) write("Result Byte %d : %x",i, Result[i]); }
```

### SecurityLocalGenerateCMAC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateCMAC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateCMAC.htm)

**Description:**

Generates a hash for the given data and key. The generated hash is a CMAC.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalGenerateCMAC(byte key[], dword keyLength, byte data[], dword dataLength, byte cmac[], dword cmacLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte key[]` | The key to be used. |
| `dword keyLength` | 16 or 32 (bytes). |
| `byte data[]` | The data for which the CMAC should be computed. |
| `dword dataLength` | The length of the datafor which the CMAC should be computed. |
| `byte cmac[] [Out]` | The generated CMAC. |
| `dword cmacLength [In/Out]` | The length of the generated CMAC (16 bytes). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. ≤ 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

*Example*

```c
byte keyData[16] = {0x2b, 0x7e, 0x15, 0x16, 0x28, 0xae, 0xd2, 0xa6, 0xab, 0xf7, 0x15, 0x88, 0x09, 0xcf, 0x4f, 0x3c}; dword keyLength = elcount(keyData); byte data[16] = {0x6b, 0xc1, 0xbe, 0xe2, 0x2e, 0x40, 0x9f, 0x96, 0xe9, 0x3d, 0x7e, 0x11, 0x73, 0x93, 0x17, 0x2a}; dword dataLength = elcount(data); byte cmac[16]; dword cmacLength = elcount(cmac); long result; result = SecurityLocalGenerateCMAC(keyData, keyLength, data, dataLength, cmac, cmacLength); —
```


---

### SecurityLocalGenerateSHA256

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSHA256.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSHA256.htm)

**Description:**

Generates a SHA256 hash for the given data and using the given key.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalGenerateSHA256(byte data[], dword dataLength, byte sha256[], dword sha256Length)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte data[]` | The data to encrypt. |
| `dword dataLength` | The length of the data to encrypt. |
| `byte sha256[] [Out]` | The generated SHA256 hash. |
| `dword sha256Length [In/Out]` | The length of the generated SHA256 hash. (32 bytes) |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. <= 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

```c
byte data[3] = { 0x61, 0x62, 0x63 }; byte refOutput[32] ={ 0xba, 0x78, 0x16, 0xbf, 0x8f, 0x01, 0xcf, 0xea, 0x41, 0x41, 0x40, 0xde, 0x5d, 0xae, 0x22, 0x23, 0xb0, 0x03, 0x61, 0xa3, 0x96, 0x17, 0x7a, 0x9c, 0xb4, 0x10, 0xff, 0x61, 0xf2, 0x00, 0x15, 0xad }; dword refOutputLength = 32; byte output[32]; dword outputLength = 32; dword result = 255; dword counter = 0; result = SecurityLocalGenerateSHA256(data, elCount(data), output, outputLength);
```

### SecurityLocalGenerateSHA512

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSHA512.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalGenerateSHA512.htm)

**Description:**

Generates a SHA512 hash for the given data and using the given key.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalGenerateSHA512(byte data[], dword dataLength, byte sha512[], dword sha512Length)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byte data[]` | The data to encrypt. |
| `dword dataLength` | The length of the data to encrypt. |
| `byte sha512[] [Out]` | The generated SHA512 hash. |
| `dword sha512Length [In/Out]` | The length of the generated SHA512 hash (64 bytes). |


**Return Value:** 1 Success A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. <= 0 Error A value less than or equal to 0 means error.


**Example(s):**

```c
byte data[3] = { 0x61, 0x62, 0x63 }; byte refOutput[64] ={ 0xdd, 0xaf, 0x35, 0xa1, 0x93, 0x61, 0x7a, 0xba , 0xcc, 0x41, 0x73, 0x49, 0xae, 0x20, 0x41, 0x31 , 0x12, 0xe6, 0xfa, 0x4e, 0x89, 0xa9, 0x7e, 0xa2 , 0x0a, 0x9e, 0xee, 0xe6, 0x4b, 0x55, 0xd3, 0x9a , 0x21, 0x92, 0x99, 0x2a, 0x27, 0x4f, 0xc1, 0xa8 , 0x36, 0xba, 0x3c, 0x23, 0xa3, 0xfe, 0xeb, 0xbd , 0x45, 0x4d, 0x44, 0x23, 0x64, 0x3c, 0xe8, 0x0e , 0x2a, 0x9a, 0xc9, 0x4f, 0xa5, 0x4c, 0xa4, 0x9f }; dword refOutputLength = 64; byte output[64]; dword outputLength = 64; dword result = 255; dword counter = 0; result = SecurityLocalGenerateSHA512(data, elCount(data), output, outputLength);
```

### SecurityLocalSetVerbosity

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalSetVerbosity.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalSetVerbosity.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the notification level for a node from low (0) to high (5).


**Syntax:**

```c
void SecurityLocalSetVerbosity(dword level)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword level` | Desired notification level. Range: 0-5 0: Only critical errors are printed. 1: All errors are printed 2: Additionally warnings are printed 3: Information messages are printed too. 4-5: Debug messages are active. |


### SecurityLocalVerifyAuthenticationInformation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalVerifyAuthenticationInformation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/Functions/CAPLfunctionSecurityLocalVerifyAuthenticationInformation.htm)

**Description:**

Verifies the specified authentication information (MAC and freshness) of the PDU. The (complete) current freshness value must be provided in the function call. Other freshness sources will be ignored. The payload is also an input parameter to the verification logic. The result of this method depends on the security profile which is mapped on the network.


**Syntax:**

```c
long SecurityLocalVerifyAuthenticationInformation(dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword truncatedAuthenticatorHigh, qword truncatedAuthenticator, dword truncatedAuthenticatorBitLength, qword rxFreshness, dword rxFreshnessBitLength, qword currentFreshness, dword freshnessValueLength, dword freshnessValueId, dword verificationResult) \\ form 1
```

```c
long SecurityLocalVerifyAuthenticationInformation(dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword truncatedAuthenticatorHigh, qword truncatedAuthenticator, dword truncatedAuthenticatorBitLength, qword rxFreshness, dword rxFreshnessBitLength, qword currentFreshness, dword freshnessValueLength, dword freshnessValueId, dword keyId, dword verificationResult) \\ form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword dataId` | Id to lookup the desired key in the security source. |
| `byte payload[]` | The payload of the PDU. |
| `dword payloadLength` | The payload length of the PDU in bytes. |
| `qword truncatedAuthenticatorHigh` | Upper 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits. Otherwise 0. |
| `qword truncatedAuthenticator` | The full authenticator value or the lower 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits. |
| `dword truncatedAuthenticatorBitLength` | Length of complete authenticator to transmit( length of authInfo if the authenticator is <= 64 bit, otherwise length of authInfo + authInfoHigh). |
| `qword rxFreshness` | The received freshness value. |
| `dword rxFreshnessBitLength` | The length of the received (typically truncated) freshness in bits. |
| `qword currentFreshness` | The freshness value to be used for calculation. |
| `dword freshnessValueBitLength` | The length of currentFreshness in bits. |
| `dword freshnessValueId` | The freshness value ID of the PDU. |
| `dword keyId` | The ID used to identify the key for verification of the authenticator, if the key is not identified by the data ID. |
| `dword verificationResult [Out]` | 1: Valid MAC 0: Invalid MAC |


**Return Value:** A Value of 1 means that the action was successful. A value less than or equal to 0 means error. dword verificationResult [Out] dword verificationResult [Out] 1 Success 0 Error, no details. -1 Invalid handle. -2 Data incomplete. -3 Signal length does not fit. -4 Security source error, no details. -6 Not supported. -10 Security is not usable. Reasons can be: Security Manager version is too old. Tool Version is too old. Security Profile is invalid.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### OnSecurityLocalPDUPreTx

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalPDUPreTx.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalPDUPreTx.htm)

**Description:**

This callback handler is called, after all data updates and the automatic Authenticator (MAC) calculation has be done. Payload, AuthInfo and freshness can be modified in this handler before the transmission starts. Fault injection and evaluation of new algorithm are typical examples.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnSecurityLocalPDUPreTx(char pduName[], dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword& authInfoHigh, qword& authInfo, dword authInfoBitLength, qword& freshness, dword freshnessBitLength, dword freshnessValueId)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `char pduName[]` | The name of the PDU. |
| `dword dataId` | The data ID of the PDU. |
| `byte payload[]` | The payload of the PDU. |
| `dword payloadLength` | The payload length of the PDU in bytes. |
| `qword& authInfoHigh [Out]` | Upper 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits.Otherwise 0; |
| `qword& authInfo [Out]` | The full authenticator value or the lower 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits. |
| `dword authInfoBitLength` | Length of complete authenticator to transmit( length of authInfo if the authenticator is <= 64 bit, otherwise length of authInfo + authInfoHigh) |
| `qword& freshness [Out]` | The transmitted freshness value. |
| `dword freshnessBitLength` | The transmitted freshness value length in bits. |
| `dword freshnessValueId` | The freshness value ID of the PDU. |


**Return Value:** byte payload[] The payload of the PDU. qword& authInfoHigh [Out] Upper 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits.Otherwise 0; qword& authInfo [Out] The full authenticator value or the lower 64 bits of the authenticator (MAC), if  authenticator is larger than 64 bits. qword& freshness [Out] The freshness value.


**Example(s):**

```c
// this example modifies the MAC of PDU with Data ID 1 void OnSecurityLocalPDUPreTx(char pduName[], dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword& authInfoHigh, qword& authInfo, dword authInfoBitLength, qword& freshness, dword freshnessBitLength, dword freshnessValueId) { if (dataId == 1) { authInfo = 0x123456; // change mac // freshness = 0x02; // change freshness // payload[0] = 0xFF; // change payload; } }
```


---

### OnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness.htm)

**Description:**

This callback is called for every secured PDU for which a MAC has to be calculated of verified. The callback is triggered before the MAC calculation starts. The callback provides the possibility the calculate a freshness value in CAPL and force the Security Manager to use this freshness value for the next MAC calculation / verification.


**Syntax:**

```c
long OnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness(dword context, char pduName[], dword dataId, dword freshnessValueId, dword attemptNr, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword& freshness, dword& freshnessLength, qword truncatedRxFreshness, dword truncatedRxFreshnessBitLength)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dword context` | 1: PDU is received (MAC verification) 0: PDU is transmitted (MAC calculation |
| `char pduName[]` | The name of the PDU. |
| `dword dataId` | The data ID of the PDU. |
| `dword freshnessValueId` | The freshness value ID of the PDU. |
| `dword attemptNr` | The number of the attempted verification. |
| `byte payload[]` | The payload of the PDU. |
| `dword payloadLength` | The payload length of the PDU in bytes. |
| `qword& freshness [Out]` | The freshness the CAPL code provides. |
| `dword& freshnessLength [Out]` | The freshness length of the freshness the CAPL code provides in bits. |
| `qword truncatedRxFreshness` | The received freshness (only in case context is 1). |
| `dword truncatedRxFreshnessBitLength` | The received freshness length in bits (only in case context is 1). |


**Return Value:** qword& freshness [Out] The freshness the CAPL code provides. dword& freshnessLength [Out] The freshness length of the freshness the CAPL code provides in bits. long:
0 No freshness is provided by this callback. long: 1 The frehness and freshnessLength shall be used for MAC calculation / verification.


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
// Example 1: // implement this callback at the transmitting node of the pdu // This example shows how to specify an older or newer freshness value, the MAC will be "correct" but the freshness is "wrong" (compared to the freshness of the system) long OnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness( dword context, char pduName[], dword dataId, dword freshnessValueId, dword attemptNr, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword& freshness, dword& freshnessLength, qword truncatedRxFreshness, dword truncatedRxFreshnessBitLength) { long result = 0; char  idString[2] = ""; dword idValue; BYTE freshnessValue[6]; dword freshnessValueLength; // filter for tx pdus (tx = 0; rx = 1) if (context != 0) { return 0; // use internal freshness model } // filter for the correct data id to influence if (dataId != 0) { return 0; // use internal freshness model } // now get the freshness currently stored in the internal freshness model // the following parameter size and values are depending on the selected Security Profile. // Please refer to the Security Source Manual to get more detailled information // about parameter values, identifier and freshness value layouts. idValue = 0; freshnessValueLength= 6; result = SecurityLocalGetFreshness("", idValue, freshnessValue, freshnessValueLength); // change the freshness value freshnessValue[5] = 0xFF; // write the new Freshness value back. // the last parameter is currently reserved and shall be set to 0 result = SecurityLocalSetFreshness("", idValue, freshnessValue, freshnessValueLength, 0); // as Set and Get Freshness is changing the internal freshness model, return 0 return 0; }
```

*Example          2*

```c
// Example 2: // This example shows how to provide a USER based freshness value long OnSecurityLocalQueryFreshness( dword context, char pduName[], dword dataId, dword freshnessValueId, dword attemptNr, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword& freshness, dword& freshnessLength, qword truncatedRxFreshness, dword truncatedRxFreshnessBitLength) { if ((dataId == 1) && (context == 0)) // check for PDU with data ID and context [(0 = tx) (1 = rx)] to specify a freshness for { freshness = 7; // new Freshness Value freshnessLength = 64; // new freshness length return 1; // use my values } return 0; // for all others use internal calculated freshness values }
```


---

### OnSecurityLocalPDUValidatedWithOemInfo

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalPDUValidatedWithOemInfo.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityLocalPDUValidatedWithOemInfo.htm)

**Description:**

This callback handler is called, when a secured PDU is received in the node. Besides to the verification result all values which have been used for verification can be analyzed. It provides information about the specific error if the validation failed. Additionally, you get information about the freshness value and length used for the validation. The parameter VerificationResult indicates if the validation was successful or not.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnSecurityLocalPDUValidatedWithOemInfo(char pduName[], dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword pduExtractedAuthInfoHigh, qword pduExtractedAuthInfo, dword pduExtractedAuthInfoBitLength, qword pduExtractedFreshness, dword pduExtractedFreshnessBitLength, dword freshnessValueId, dword verificationResult, long oemSpecificFailureReason, qword freshnessValueUsedToValidatePDU, dword freshnessBitLengthUsedToValidatePDU)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `pduName[]` | The name of the PDU. |
| `dataId` | The used Data-ID. |
| `payload` | The byte array with the received data. |
| `payloadLength` | The length of the payload array in bytes. |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfoHigh` | Received authentication (MAC). Bytes 8-15. (Not relevant for RNA) |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfo` | Received authentication (MAC). Bytes 0-7. |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfoBitLength` | Length of the Authentication in bit. |
| `pduExtractedFreshness` | Received Freshness value from PDU. |
| `pduExtractedFreshnessBitLength` | Length of the Freshness in bit. |
| `freshnessValueId` | Freshness value id. |
| `verificationResult` | Verification result. Not verified = 0 Verified = 1. |
| `oemSpecificFailureReason` | OEM security specific error codes, which give additional information about why the validation failed. |
| `freshnessValueUsedToValidatePDU` | The (full) freshness value used to validate the PDU. |
| `freshnessBitLengthUsedToValidatePDU` | The freshness length in bits used to validate the PDU. |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### OnSecurityOfNodePDUValidatedWithOemInfo

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityOfNodePDUValidatedWithOemInfo.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Security/CallbackHandler/CAPLfunctionOnSecurityOfNodePDUValidatedWithOemInfo.htm)

**Description:**

This callback handler is called, when a secured PDU is received in the node. Besides to the verification result all values which have been used for verification can be analyzed. It provides information about the specific error if the validation failed. Additionally, you get information about the freshness value and length used for the validation. The parameter VerificationResult indicates if the validation was successful or not.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnSecurityOfNodePDUValidatedWithOemInfo(char nodeName[], char networkName[], char pduName[], dword dataId, byte payload[], dword payloadLength, qword pduExtractedAuthInfoHigh, qword pduExtractedAuthInfo, dword pduExtractedAuthInfoBitLength, qword pduExtractedFreshness, dword pduExtractedFreshnessBitLength, dword freshnessValueId, dword verificationResult, long oemSpecificFailureReason, qword freshnessValueUsedToValidatePDU, dword freshnessBitLengthUsedToValidatePDU)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `char nodeName[]` | The name of the node. |
| `char networkName[]` | The name of the network the node is on. |
| `pduName[]` | The name of the PDU. |
| `dataId` | The used Data-ID. |
| `payload` | The byte array with the received data. |
| `payloadLength` | Length of the payload array in bytes. |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfoHigh` | Received authentication (MAC). Bytes 8-15. (not relevant  
  for RNA) |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfo` | Received authentication (MAC). Bytes 0-7. |
| `pduExtractedAuthInfoBitLength` | Length of the Authentication in bit |
| `pduExtractedFreshness` | Received Freshness value from PDU |
| `pduExtractedFreshnessBitLength` | Length of the Freshness in bit |
| `freshnessValueId` | Freshness value id |
| `verificationResult` | Verification result. Not verified = 0 Verified = 1 |
| `oemSpecificFailureReason` | OEM security specific error codes, which give additional information about why the validation failed. |
| `freshnessValueUsedToValidatePDU` | The (full) freshness value used to validate the PDU |
| `freshnessBitLengthUsedToValidatePDU` | The freshness length in bits used to validate the PDU |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

Cyclical sending is stopped; setting signals is possible. Cyclical sending is continued only after an ILControlResume .


**Syntax:**

```c
long ILControlWait()
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** 0 No error. 1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. 50 Node layer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

```c

```

### TestWaitForSignalAvailable

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSignalAvailable.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSignalAvailable.htm)

**Description:**

Tests the availability of a specific signal and waits if necessary until its availability. A signal that is received at least once from the bus after the measurement starts is classified as "available". This function is useful when you want to assure that single signals are available before starting a signal-based test, i.e. to synchronize the tester with the bus. Note Dependent on the used parameter type the appropriate bus context in a multibus environment has only to be set before the function is called if the corresponding database object will be ambiguous. Further information on site MultiBus Environment .


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForSignalAvailable (Signal aSignal, dword aTimeout); // form 1
```

```c
long TestWaitForSignalAvailable (ServiceSignal aSignal, dword aTimeout); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aSignal` | Signal whose availability is being tested or for which is waited |
| `aTimeout` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |


**Return Value:** -2 Wait state is exited due to a constraint/condition violation -1 General error, e.g. functionality is unavailable 0 Wait state is exited due to a timeout 1 Wait state is exited; signal is available for further tests


**Example(s):**

```c
// waits for the occurrence of signal 'EngineRunning' long result; result = TestWaitForSignalAvailable (EngineRunning, 2000);
```


---

### TestWaitForSignalsAvailable

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSignalsAvailable.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForSignalsAvailable.htm)

**Description:**

Tests the availability of all the send signals of a node and waits if necessary until all the send signals of the node are available. Signals that are received at least once from the bus after the measurement starts are classified as "available". This function is useful when you want to assure that all signals are available before starting a signal-based test, i.e. to synchronize the tester with the bus.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForSignalsAvailable (dbNode aNode, dword aTimeout);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aNode` | Node whose send signals should all be available For CAN and FlexRay all signals are considered! In contrast to the corresponding XML pattern <awaitsignalsavailable> this function considers FlexRay signals from all frames and PDUs; not only FlexRay signals from frames and PDUs of service type 'Application'. For LIN nodes only signals from unconditional and event-triggered frames of all schedule tables are considered. Also the signals of diagnostic frames are ignored. |
| `aTimeout` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |


**Return Value:** -2 Wait state is exited due to a constraint/condition violation -1 General error, e.g. functionality is unavailable 0 Wait state is exited due to a timeout; not all signals are available 1 The wait state is exited; all of the node's send signals are available for further tests


**Example(s):**

```c
// waits for the availability of all tx signals of node 'SUT' long result; result = TestWaitForSignalsAvailable (SUT, 2000);
```


---

### TestWaitForImplValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValue.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the impl encoding of a valueHandle to reach a certain value. This function can only be used for valueHandles with a complex data type (array or struct). It cannot be used for system variables or bus system signals.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForImplValue(valueHandle <ValueType> value, ValueType awaitedValue, dword timeoutMs);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `value` | Value handle of a communication object or distributed object. |
| `awaitedValue` | Value which is awaited. |
| `timeoutMS` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 resume due to event occurred (value has been reached)


**Example(s):**

```c
struct Mirrors::Position pos; long ret; pos.x = 100; pos.y = 50; ret = testWaitForImplValue(MirrorAdjustment.consumerSide[0,0].CurrentPosition, pos, 200);
```


---

### TestWaitForImplValueFloat

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueFloat.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueFloat.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the impl encoding of a valueHandle to reach a certain value. This function can only be used for valueHandles with a floating point data type. It cannot be used for system variables or bus system signals.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForImplValueFloat(valueHandle * value, float awaitedValue, dword timeoutMs);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `value` | Value handle of a communication object or distributed object. |
| `awaitedValue` | Value which is awaited. |
| `timeoutMS` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 resume due to event occurred (value has been reached)


**Example(s):**

```c
long ret; ret = testWaitForImplValueFloat(MirrorStatus[CANoe].Temperature, 20.0, 200);
```


---

### TestWaitForImplValueSInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueSInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueSInt.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the impl encoding of a valueHandle to reach a certain value. This function can only be used for valueHandles with a signed integer data type. It cannot be used for system variables or bus system signals.


**Syntax:**

```c
long TestWaitForImplValueSInt(valueHandle * value, int64 awaitedValue, dword timeoutMs);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `value` | Value handle of a communication object or distributed object. |
| `awaitedValue` | Value which is awaited. |
| `timeoutMS` | Timeout in milliseconds. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 resume due to event occurred (value has been reached)


**Example(s):**

```c
long ret; ret = testWaitForImplValueSInt(ErrorSignal[LeftMirror], -1, 200);
```

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueString.htm)

**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 resume due to event occurred (value has been reached)


**Example(s):**

```c
long ret; ret = testWaitForImplValueString(ErrorSignal[LeftMirror], "GeneralError", 200);
```

### TestWaitForImplValueUInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueUInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Test/Functions/CAPLfunctionTestWaitForImplValueUInt.htm)

**Description:**

Waits for the impl encoding of a valueHandle to reach a certain value. This function can only be used for valueHandles with an unsigned integer data type. It cannot be used for system variables or bus system signals.

```c
long TestWaitForImplValueUInt(valueHandle * value, qword awaitedValue, dword timeoutMs);
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `value` | Value handle of a communication object or distributed object. |
| `awaitedValue` | Value which is awaited. |
| `timeoutMS` | Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling. |


**Return Value:** -2 Resume due to constraint violation -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available 0 Resume due to timeout 1 resume due to event occurred (value has been reached)

### tlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/Functions/CAPLFunctiontlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/Functions/CAPLFunctiontlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as client. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.


**Syntax:**

```c
tlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `connectionHandle` | Handle of the TLS socket to be used. |
| `configuration` | Name of the TLS configuration. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
void OnTcpConnect( dword socket, long result) { if (result == 0) { socket = tlsOpen(socket); tlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration(socket, "PSK K1"); if ((tlsGetLastError(socket) != 0) && (tlsGetLastError(socket) != 997)) { // an error occurred return; } } }
```


---

### tlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/Functions/CAPLFunctiontlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/TLSAPI/Functions/CAPLFunctiontlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as server. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.


**Syntax:**

```c
tlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `connectionHandle` | Handle of the TLS socket to be used. |
| `configuration` | Name of the TLS configuration. |


**Return Value:** 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code


**Example(s):**

```c
dword res; dword connection; if (socket == gServerSocket1) { connection = tcpAccept (socket); connection = TlsOpen (connection); write("%FILE_NAME_NO_EXT%: Server %d connection %d", socket, connection); res = tlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (connection, "PSK K1");
```

### on replaySourceStatusChanged

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnReplaySourceStatusChanged.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnReplaySourceStatusChanged.htm)

**Description:**

The event is triggered whenever the state of a replay block changes. This is when replay starts, at the first event of the replayed file, and after the last event of the replayed file. Within the event, the following information can be used via the this operator: this.timestamp The timestamp of the event, in ns. this.block The name of the replay block that triggered the event. this.status The new state of the block. The following values are possible: this.filename The full path of the replayed file.


**Syntax:**

```c
on replaySourceStatusChanged
```


**Example(s):**

```c
on replaySourceStatusChanged { write("%I64d: %s: new state %d (file %s)", this.timestamp, this.block, this.status, this.source); }
```


---

### on offlineSourceStatusChanged

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnOfflineSourceStatusChanged.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnOfflineSourceStatusChanged.htm)

**Description:**

The event is triggered whenever the state of an offline source changes. This happens  when offline measurement starts, at the first event of the offline source, and after the last event of the offline source. Within the event, the following information can be used via the this operator: this.timestamp The timestamp of the event, in ns. this.status The new state of the offline source. The following values are possible: this.filename The full path of the offline source.


**Syntax:**

```c
on offlineSourceStatusChanged
```


**Example(s):**

```c
on offlineSourceStatusChanged { write("%I64d: new state %d (file %s)", this.timestamp, this.status, this.source); }
```


---

### Events of the Measurement System

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionsEventproceduresMeasurementSystem.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionsEventproceduresMeasurementSystem.htm)

### on PDU

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnPDU.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnPDU.htm)

**Description:**

This event procedure is called if the corresponding PDU is received of which the updated bit is set or undefined.

For the event procedure of type * and [*]  keyword this is an untyped PDU object . For all other event procedures keyword this is a typed PDU object . Note The form on pdu * is also called for SOME/IP PDUs. Keyword this is an untyped PDU object . The data contains the SOME/IP header and payload. Security selectors are not available.


**Syntax:**

```c
on PDU <PDU name>;
```

```c
on PDU short <short header ID>;
```

```c
on PDU long <long header ID>;
```

```c
on PDU <header ID>;
```

```c
on PDU *;
```

```c
on PDU [*];
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
on PDU short 5 { write("on pdu: %s", this.name); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
on PDU EngineData { write("EngineData received at: %d s", this.time); }
```

*Example 3*

```c
on PDU * { write( "on pdu: LongHeaderID %X length %d", this.LongHeaderID, this.PduLength ); }
```

### PDU

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Objects/CAPLfunctionPDU.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Objects/CAPLfunctionPDU.htm)

**Description:**

Can be used to create a PDU object. The object data can be manipulated via the object's selectors. Additional object properties can be read from the selectors. A PDU object can be sent using the TriggerPDU function.


**Syntax:**

```c
PDU short <AUTOSAR short header ID> <PDU var>
```

```c
PDU long <AUTOSAR long header ID> <PDU var>
```

```c
PDU <AUTOSAR header ID> <PDU var>
```

```c
PDU <AUTOSAR PDU name> <PDU var>
```

```c
PDU * <PDU var>
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `<PDU var>` | String that specifies the variable name of the object. |


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
On key 'a' { PDU short 4 myPDU; myPDU.signal1 = 25; TriggerPDU(myPDU); }
```

*Example 2*

```c
void foo (PDU* parPDU) { // evaluate PDU } void example () { PDU EngineData myPDU; foo (myPDU); }
```

*Example 3*

```c
on PDU CAN_1::SOMEPDU1 { PDU CAN_2::SOMEPDU1 mypdu2; mypdu2.Payload = this.Payload; triggerPDU(mypdu2); }
```

### getProductVersion

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProductVersion.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProductVersion.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves version information for the running product.


**Syntax:**

```c
void GetProductVersion(dword& major, dword& minor, dword& build, dword& patch, dword& servicePack);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `major` | The major version of your product, e.g., 18 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. |
| `minor` | The minor version of your product, e.g., 3 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. |
| `build` | The build number of your product, e.g., 118 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. |
| `patch` | The patch number of your product, e.g., 16 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3 or 0 for CANoe 18.0.185. |
| `servicePack` | The service pack number of your product, e.g., 3 for CANoe 18.3.118 SP3 or 0 for CANoe 18.0.185. |


**Example(s):**

```c
dword major, minor, build, patch, servicePack; GetProductVersion(major, minor, build, patch, servicePack); write("%I32u.%I32u.%I32u.%I32u SP%I32u", major, minor, build, patch, servicePack);
```


---

### msgBeep

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionMsgBeep.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionMsgBeep.htm)

**Description:**

The msgBeep function plays back a sound predefined by the Windows system. It replaces the previous beep function.


**Syntax:**

```c
void msgBeep (long soundType);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `soundType` | Integer for the predefined sound. Specifically these are: 0 MB_ICONASTERISK SystemAsterisk 1 MB_ICONEXCLAMATION SystemExclamation 2 MB_ICONHAND SystemHand 3 MB_ICONQUESTION SystemQuestion 4 MB_OK SystemDefault 5 Standard beep using the computer speaker (default) |
| `0` | MB_ICONASTERISK SystemAsterisk |
| `1` | MB_ICONEXCLAMATION SystemExclamation |
| `2` | MB_ICONHAND SystemHand |
| `3` | MB_ICONQUESTION SystemQuestion |
| `4` | MB_OK SystemDefault |
| `5` | Standard beep using the computer speaker (default) |


**Example(s):**

```c
void sound() { // Standard signal question msgBeep (3); }
```


---

### getProfileArray

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileArray.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileArray.htm)

**Description:**

Searches the file under section section for the variable entry . Entry is interpreted as a list of numerical values, separated by comma, tab, space, semicolon or slash. A 0x prefix indicates hex values.


**Syntax:**

```c
long getProfileArray(char section[], char entry[],char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
long getProfileArray(char section[], char entry[],char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `buff` | Buffer for the read-in numerical values. Due to the char data type, the values must be in the range -128 .. 127 (inclusive). |
| `buffsize` | Size of buff : Maximum number of read in numerical values (max. 1279 characters). |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** Number of numerical values read in.


**Example(s):**

```c
See getProfileArray
```

*Note*

> Using 256 hex values (format 0x??) and int values (format ??? plus signed) respectively as well as separators the string length is 4 * 256 + 255 = 1279 characters. The first 1279 characters are read from the ini entry and are converted to numerical values. The above mentioned format is sufficiently for 256 numerical values. Does the string contain figures with only one figure as well as separators (e.g. 3,1,4,1,5,9,2,6,5,3,5...), 640 numerical values can be read.


---

### getProfileFloat

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileFloat.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileFloat.htm)

**Description:**

Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . If its value is a number, this number is returned as the functional result. If the file or entry is not found, the default value def is returned as the functional result. If the entry does not contain a valid number, positive zero is returned. If the value is out of range for float, positive infinity is returned instead.


**Syntax:**

```c
float getProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
float getProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `def` | Default value in case of error as a float. |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** Float that was read in.


**Example(s):**

```c
See getProfileFloat
```


---

### getProfileInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileInt.htm)

**Description:**

Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . If its value is a number, this number is returned as the functional result. If the file or entry is not found, or if entry does not contain a valid number, the default value def is returned as the functional result.


**Syntax:**

```c
long getProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
long getProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `def` | Default value in case of error as an integer. |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** Integer that was read in.


**Example(s):**

```c
See getProfileInt
```


---

### getProfileString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionGetProFileString.htm)

**Description:**

Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . Its contents (value) are written to the buffer buff . Its length must be passed correctly in buffsize . If the file or entry is not found, the default value def is copied to buffer. If the read string length is longer than the buffer, the string will be cut to the buffer length.


**Syntax:**

```c
long getProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char def[], char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
long getProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char def[], char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `def` | Default value in case of error as a string. |
| `buff` | Buffer for the read-in character as a string. |
| `buffersize` | Size of buff in bytes. |
| `filename` | Filename as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present. The string written to buff will be converted to the CAPL encoding. |


**Return Value:** Length of the read string. Length of the default string: Fault Key not found -1 : Fault File not found


**Example(s):**

```c
See getProfileString
```


---

### writeProfileFloat

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileFloat.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileFloat.htm)

**Description:**

Opens the file filename , searches the section section and writes the variable entry with the value value. If entry already exists the old value is overwritten.


**Syntax:**

```c
long writeProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], float value, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
long writeProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], float value, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `value` | Value as a float. |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be UTF-16LE encoded if it is newly written, or interpreted as such, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** The functional result is 0 in case of an error.


**Example(s):**

```c
See WriteProfileFloat
```


---

### writeProfileInt

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileInt.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileInt.htm)

**Description:**

Opens the file filename , searches the section section and writes the variable entry with the value value. If entry already exists the old value is overwritten. WriteProfileInt always writes passed values in heaxedezimal with prefix 0x to a file.


**Syntax:**

```c
long writeProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long value, char filename[]); // form 1
```

```c
long writeProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long value, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `value` | Value as a integer. |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be UTF-16LE encoded if it is newly written, or interpreted as such, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** The functional result is 0 in case of an error.


**Example(s):**

```c
See WriteProfileInt
```


---

### writeProfileString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionWriteProFileString.htm)

**Description:**

Opens the file filename , searches the section section and writes the variable entry with the value value. If entry already exists the old value is overwritten. The functional result is the number of characters written or 0 in case of an error.


**Syntax:**

```c
long writeProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char value[], char filename[]); form 1
```

```c
long writeProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char value[], char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `section` | Section of the file as a string. |
| `entry` | Variable name as a string. |
| `value` | Value as a string. |
| `filename` | File path as a string. |
| `utf16` | If this flag is set the file will be UTF-16LE encoded if it is newly written, or interpreted as such, if the corresponding BOM is also present. |


**Return Value:** 0 Write error > 1 Write access. Number of characters will not be returned.


**Example(s):**

```c
See WriteProfileString
```

### SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionSymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/Other/Functions/CAPLfunctionSymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet.htm)

**Description:**

Changes the dynamic mapping set that will be used for mapping in the CANoe DE product Symbol Mapping dialog. Only the mapping relations contained in the given dynamic mapping set and in the static mapping set are considered.


**Syntax:**

```c
void SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet(char mappingSetName[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `mappingSetName` | Name of a dynamic set which shall be activated. |


**Return Value:** -1 if no mapping set with the given name is found 0 if the mapping set with the given name is found and its contained mapping relations are mapped


**Example(s):**

```c
on key '1' { // Activate DataSet1 and map its contained mapping relations SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet ("DataSet1"); }
```

### Iso11783IL_TIMSetDurationForTotalValueRange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILtimSetDurationForTotalValueRange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILtimSetDurationForTotalValueRange.htm)

**Description:**

You can use this function to determine how quickly the server reaches a value requested by the client by increasing or decreasing the function value linearly.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_TIMSetDurationForTotalValueRange (dword functionID, dword duration, dword cycleTimeOfFunctionStatusMessage); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_TIMSetDurationForTotalValueRange (dbNode server, dword functionID, dword duration, dword cycleTimeOfFunctionStatusMessage); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `functionID` | ID of the function. Function ID Description Available since TIM version 1-32 (1h-20h) Auxiliary Value 1 – 32 1 64 (40h) Front PTO 1 65 (41h) Rear PTO 1 66 (42h) Front hitch 1 67 (43h) Rear hitch 1 68 (44h) Vehicle speed 1 70 (46h) External guidance 1 71 (47h) Front top linkage 2 72 (48h) Rear top linkage 2 |
| `Function ID` | Description |
| `1-32 (1h-20h)` | Auxiliary Value 1 – 32 |
| `64 (40h)` | Front PTO |
| `65 (41h)` | Rear PTO |
| `66 (42h)` | Front hitch |
| `67 (43h)` | Rear hitch |
| `68 (44h)` | Vehicle speed |
| `70 (46h)` | External guidance |
| `71 (47h)` | Front top linkage |
| `72 (48h)` | Rear top linkage |
| `duration` | The time required by the TIM server to reach the requested value of a function if the function currently has the lowest possible value and the requested value is the highest possible value of the function (or vice versa). Unit: [ms], Default value: 0 If the duration is set to 0, the server immediately sets the function value to the value requested by the TIM client. |
| `cycleTimeOfFunctionStatusMessage` | Cycle time to send the function status message of the function while the function value is rising or falling to the value requested by the client. Unit: [ms], Range: 100 to 10000, Default value: 100 |
| `server` | TIM server simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Property has been set successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### Iso11783IL_TIMSetStateOfFunctionStatusMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILtimSetStateOfFunctionStatusMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILtimSetStateOfFunctionStatusMessage.htm)

**Description:**

You can use this function to set the status value of the TIM_v2_Front(Rear)Hitch_PercentagePosition_Status and TIM_v2_Front(Rear)TopLinkage_PercentagePosition_Status messages sent by the TIM server.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_TIMSetStateOfFunctionStatusMessage (dword functionID, byte state); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_TIMSetStateOfFunctionStatusMessage (dbNode server, dword functionID, byte state); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `functionID` | ID of the function. Function ID Description 66 (42h) Front hitch 67 (43h) Rear hitch 71 (47h) Front top linkage 72 (48h) Rear top linkage |
| `Function ID` | Description |
| `66 (42h)` | Front hitch |
| `67 (43h)` | Rear hitch |
| `71 (47h)` | Front top linkage |
| `72 (48h)` | Rear top linkage |
| `state` | Fucntion state (0..15). State Description 0 Float 1 Stop 2 Lowering/Retracting 3 Raising/Extending 14 Error 15 Not available |
| `State` | Description |
| `0` | Float |
| `1` | Stop |
| `2` | Lowering/Retracting |
| `3` | Raising/Extending |
| `14` | Error |
| `15` | Not available |
| `server` | TIM server simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Property has been set successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### Iso11783IL_SendData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILSendData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILSendData.htm)

**Description:**

Sends data up to 117.440.505 bytes to another device. If data length is bigger than 8 bytes the RTS/CTS or BAM transport protocol is used. If data length is bigger than 1785 byte the Extendend Transport Protocol (ETP) is used. If you use form 1 or 3, the specified control byte is added before the data to be sent. This allows you to easily send an object pool transfer message to the Virtual Terminal or the Task Controller, for example.


**Syntax:**

```c
long ISO11783IL_SendData(dword destinationAddress, dword pgn, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 1
```

```c
long ISO11783IL_SendData(dword destinationAddress, dword pgn, byte controlByte, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 2
```

```c
long ISO11783IL_SendData(dbNode node, dword destinationAddress, dword pgn, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 3
```

```c
long ISO11783IL_SendData(dbNode node, dword destinationAddress, dword pgn, byte controlByte, byte data[], dword dataLength); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `destinationAddress` | Address of the receiver of the data. |
| `pgn` | The data is transferred with a Parameter Group that uses this Parameter Group Number (PGN). |
| `controlByte` | Additional byte that is inserted before the data to be transferred. |
| `data` | Data to transfer. |
| `dataLength` | Length of parameter data. |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes .


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { dword gHandle = 0; dword gReadBytes = 0; byte gBuffer[0xFFFFFF]; } on Start { gHandle = openFileRead ("Data.Bin", 1); if (gHandle != 0) { gReadBytes = fileGetBinaryBlock(gBuffer,elcount(gBuffer), gHandle); fileClose(gHandle); } else { write("Failed to open file Data.Bin"); } } void SendFileData(word destinationAddress, word pgn) { if (gReadBytes !=0 ) { if (ISO11783IL_SendData(destinationAddress, pgn, gBuffer, gReadBytes) != 0) { write("Call of ISO11783IL_SendData failed"); } } else { write("There is no data to send"); } }
```

### Iso11783IL_AddDTC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILAddDTC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILAddDTC.htm)

**Description:**

This function adds a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) to a diagnostics message. If the diagnostics message with the specified PGN is requested, the response contains the added DTC. The added DTC uses conversion method 4 (SPN represented as Intel format for all 19 bits with the SPN Conversion Method set to 0). You can remove an added DTC with function Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC . Note : You can use this function only if support of ISO11783 Diagnostics is enabled by function . Iso11783IL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport .


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_AddDTC (dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_AddDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `spn` | Suspect Parameter Number of the deactivated DTC, 0..524287. |
| `fmi` | Failure Mode Indicator of the deactivated DTC, 0.. 31. |
| `State` | Returns the current state of the DTC. 0: DTC is not active and hasn't been previously active. 1: DTC is active. 2: DTC has been previously active. |
| `occurrenceCount` | Occurrence Count of the activated DTC, 0..127. |
| `pgn` | Parameter Group Number (PGN) of the message which shall contain the DTC. Possible values are: PGN Acronym Message FECAh DM1 Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes FECBh DM2 Previously Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes FECFh DM6 Emission-Related Pending Diagnostic Trouble Codes FED4h DM12 Emission-Related MIL-On Diagnostic Trouble Codes FDB5h DM23 Emission-Related Previously MIL-On Diagnostic Trouble Codes FD82h DM27 All Pending DTCs FD80h DM28 Emission-Related Permanent Diagnostic Trouble Codes 9F00h DM35 Immediate Fault Status FD5Fh DM41 DTCs - A, Pending FD5Eh DM42 DTCs - A, Confirmed and Active FD5Dh DM43 DTCs - A, Previously Active FD5Ch DM44 DTCs - B1, Pending FD5Bh DM45 DTCs - B1, Confirmed and Active FD5Ah DM46 DTCs - B1, Previously Active FD59h DM47 DTCs - B2, Pending FD58h DM48 DTCs - B2, Confirmed and Active FD57h DM49 DTCs - B2, Previously Active FD56h DM50 DTCs - C, Pending FD55h DM51 DTCs - C, Confirmed and Active FD54h DM52 DTCs - C, Previously Active |
| `PGN` | Acronym |
| `FECAh` | DM1 |
| `FECBh` | DM2 |
| `FECFh` | DM6 |
| `FED4h` | DM12 |
| `FDB5h` | DM23 |
| `FD82h` | DM27 |
| `FD80h` | DM28 |
| `9F00h` | DM35 |
| `FD5Fh` | DM41 |
| `FD5Eh` | DM42 |
| `FD5Dh` | DM43 |
| `FD5Ch` | DM44 |
| `FD5Bh` | DM45 |
| `FD5Ah` | DM46 |
| `FD59h` | DM47 |
| `FD58h` | DM48 |
| `FD57h` | DM49 |
| `FD56h` | DM50 |
| `FD55h` | DM51 |
| `FD54h` | DM52 |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILRemoveDTC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILRemoveDTC.htm)

**Description:**

This function removes a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) from a diagnostics message which has been added by Iso11783IL_AddDTC . Form 2 and 4 removes all DTCs with the specified SPN (regardless of FMI). A removed DTC is no longer reported by the message if the message is requested. Note: You can use this function only if support of ISO11783 Diagnostics is enabled by function Iso11783IL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport .


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC (dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC (dword pgn, dword spn); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi); // form 3
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_RemoveDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `pgn` | Parameter Group Number (PGN) of the message which shall contain the DTC. |
| `spn` | Suspect Parameter Number of the deactivated DTC, 0..524287. |
| `fmi` | Failure Mode Indicator of the deactivated DTC, 0.. 31. |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated -804 Failed to activate DTC because DTC not found


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### Iso11783IL_GetLastError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILGetLastError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILGetLastError.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the return value of the last called ISO11783 IL function.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastError (); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastError (dbNode node); // frorm 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 error code


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 't' { BYTE date = { 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06 }; ISO11783ILSetSignalRaw( EEC1::EngSpeed, 6, data ); write( "Errorcode = %d", Iso11783IL_GetLastError () ); }
```

### Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILGetLastErrorText.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILGetLastErrorText.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the result of the last called ISO11783 IL function as string.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText ( dword bufferSize, char buffer[] ); // form 1: deprecated.
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText ( char buffer[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText ( dbNode node, dword bufferSize, char buffer[] ); // form 3: deprecated.
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText ( dbNode node, char buffer[] ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bufferSize` | size of the return buffer |
| `buffer` | return buffer |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 error code


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 't' { char error[64]; BYTE date = { 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06 }; ISO11783ILSetSignalRaw( EEC1::EngSpeed, 6, data ); Iso11783IL_GetLastErrorText ( error ); write( "Errorcode:  %s", error ); }
```

### Iso11783IL_PDDGetValueRaw, Iso11783IL_PDDGetValuePhysical

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDGetValueRaw.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDGetValueRaw.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the value of a data entity (process variable) without sending any command.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDGetValueRaw(dword ddi, dword elementNumber, long& value); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDGetValueRaw(dbNode implement, dword ddi, dword elementNumber, long& value); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDGetValuePhysical(dword ddi, dword elementNumber, double& value); //form 3
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDGetValuePhysical(dbNode implement, dword ddi, dword elementNumber, double& value); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ddi` | Data Dictionary Identifier, 0x0000.0xFFFF. |
| `elementNumber` | Element number, 0x000.0xFFF. |
| `value` | Returns the value of the data entity (process variable). |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

*Example form 2*

```c
double currentValue; long result; result = Iso11783IL_PDDGetValuePhysical(120, 2, currentValue);
```

### Iso11783IL_OPActivate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPActivate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPActivate.htm)

**Description:**

The function activates the Object Pool API. The initialization procedure with the Virtual Terminal is performed and the object pool is transmitted to the Virtual Terminal. During the initialization procedure some information from the Virtual Terminal is requested. This can be suppressed with the options parameter. The requested information can be get with the function Iso11783IL_OPGetVTInfo . Note This function is not necessary if a node was configured completely in the database (DBC): ISO11783IOPFilename and ISO11783IOPVersion are defined and VT21 message was assigned to the node.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPActivate ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPActivate ( dword options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPActivate ( dbNode implement, dword options ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `options` | Options bit 0 = 1: suppress Get Memory command bit 1 = 1:-suppress Get Number of Softkeys command bit 2 = 1: suppress Get Text Font Data command bit 3 = 1: suppress Get Hardware command |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty("Version", 5); Iso11783IL_OPLoad(  "ObjectPool.iop", "pool001" ); Iso11783IL_OPActivate ( );
```

*Example 2 
 Move to another Virtual Terminal (VT)*

```c
void MoveToVtWithFunctionInstance1() { Iso11783IL_OPDeactivate(); Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty( "VTFunctionInstance", 1 ); SetTimer( mMoveTimer, 1000 ); } on timer mMoveTimer { Iso11783IL_OPLoad( "Sprayer.iop" ); Iso11783IL_OPActivate (); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildLocation

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeChildLocation.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeChildLocation.htm)

**Description:**

The function moves an object. The object is moved relative inside the 
 parent object. A Change Child Location command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildLocation ( dword parentID, dword objectID, long dx, long dy ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildLocation ( dbNode implement, dword parentID, dword objectID, long dx, long dy ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `parentID` | Parent object |
| `objectID` | Object ID of the object which should be moved |
| `dx` | Move X position relative by this value [pixel] |
| `dy` | Move Y position relative by this value [pixel] |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
// move object 5 pixels to the right Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildLocation ( 1000, 
 1200, 5, 0 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildPosition

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeChildPosition.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeChildPosition.htm)

**Description:**

The function changes the absolute position of an object inside its parent 
 object. A Change Child Position command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildPosition ( dword parentID, dword objectID, long x, long y ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildPosition ( dbNode implement, dword parentID, dword objectID, long x, long y ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `parentID` | Parent object |
| `objectID` | Object which should change its position |
| `x` | Absolute X Position inside the parent object |
| `y` | Absolute Y Position inside the parent object |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPChangeChildPosition ( 1000, 1200, 10, 10 );
```

### Iso11783IL_OPChangeEndPoint

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeEndPoint.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeEndPoint.htm)

**Description:**

The function changes the length and alignment of a line object. A Change End Point command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeEndPoint ( dword objectID, dword width, dword height, dword direction ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeEndPoint ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword width, dword height, dword direction ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | Object ID of the line object |
| `width` | Width of the bounding rectangle of the object |
| `height` | Height of the bounding rectangle of the object |
| `direction` | Alignment of the line 0: from top/left to bottom/right 1: from bottom/left to top/right |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPChangeEndPoint ( 1200, 50, 20, 0 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPChangeFontAttribute

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeFontAttr.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeFontAttr.htm)

**Description:**

The function changes the properties of a font attribute object. A Change Font Attribute command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeFontAttribute ( dword 
 objectID, dword color, dword size, dword type, dword style ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeFontAttribute ( dbNode implement, dword 
 objectID, dword color, dword size, dword type, dword style ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | Object ID of the font attribute object |
| `color` | Color index |
| `size` | Size of the font: 0: 6x8 1: 8x8 2: 8x12 3: 12x16 4: 16x16 5: 16x24 6: 24x32 7: 32x32 8: 32x48 9: 48x64 10: 64x64 11: 64x96 12: 96x128 13: 128x128 14: 128x192 |
| `type` | Font type: 0: ISO8859-1 (ISO Latin 1) 1: ISO8859-15 (ISO Latin 9) 2: ISO8859-2 (ISO Latin 2) |
| `style` | Font style: Bit 0: 1 Bold Bit 1: 1 Crossed out Bit 2: 1 Underlined Bit 3: 1 Italic Bit 4: 1 Inverted Bit 5: 1 Flashing |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPChangeFontAttribute ( 1100, 10, 3, 0, 0x01 );
```

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeLineAttr.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeLineAttr.htm)
