```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeLineAttribute ( dword 
 objectID, dword color, dword width, dword art ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeLineAttribute ( dbNode implement, dword 
 objectID, dword color, dword width, dword art ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | Object ID of a line attribute object |
| `color` | Color index |
| `width` | Width of line in pixel |
| `art` | Bit pattern which is used to draw the line |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPChangeLineAttribute ( 1100, 10, 3, 0xaa );
```

```c
 maskID, dword priority ); // form 2
```

 codes

```c
 1200, 80, 40 );
```

### Iso11783IL_OPChangeSoftKeyMask

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeSoftKeyMask.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPChangeSoftKeyMask.htm)

**Description:**

The function changes the soft key mask of a data mask. A Change 
 Soft Key Mask command is sent to the Virtual Terminal. If parameter maskType is not used the mask type is determined by the mask object.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeSoftKeyMask ( dword maskID, dword softKeyMaskID ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeSoftKeyMask ( dword maskType, dword maskID, dword softKeyMaskID ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPChangeSoftKeyMask ( dbNode implement, dword maskType, dword maskID, dword softKeyMaskID ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `maskID` | object ID of the data mask |
| `softkeyMaskID` | object ID of the data mask |
| `maskType` | type of mask 1: data mask 2: alarm mask |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPChangeSoftKeyMask ( 1000, 1050 );
```

### Iso11783IL_OPControlAudio

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPControlAudio.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPControlAudio.htm)

**Description:**

The function plays an acoustic signal on the Virtual Terminal. A Control Audio Device command is sent 
 to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPControlAudio ( dword activations, dword frequency, dword onTime, dword offTime ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPControlAudio ( dbNode implement, dword activations, dword frequency, dword onTime, dword offTime ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `activations` | number of tones |
| `frequency` | frequency in [Hz] |
| `onTime` | duration of the tone in [ms] |
| `offTime` | duration of the gaps between the tones |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPControlAudio ( 
 2, 2000, 100, 50 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPDeactivate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPDeactivate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPDeactivate.htm)

**Description:**

The function terminates the connection to the Virtual Terminal. The Delete Object Pool command 
 is executed to log off from the Virtual Terminal. The Maintenance message is not longer sent. The Object Pool API changes to state Not Active .


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPDeactivate ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPDeactivate ( dword options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPDeactivate ( dbNode implement, dword options ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `options` | Options bit 0 = 1: suppress Delete Object 
 Pool command bit 1 = 1: delete local object pool |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPEnableObject

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPEnableObject.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPEnableObject.htm)

**Description:**

The function activates or deactivates an input object. A Enable 
 Object command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPEnableObject ( dword objectID, dword enable ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPEnableObject ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword enable ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of an input object |
| `enable` | 1: activate 0: deactivate |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPEnableObject ( 
 1200, 1 );
```


---

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPESC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPESC.htm)

**Description:**

The function aborts user input on the Virtual Terminal. A ESC command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPESC ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPESC ( dbNode implement ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPESC ( );
```

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPExecuteMacro.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPExecuteMacro.htm)

**Description:**

The function executes a macro object. An Execute 
 Macro command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPExecuteMacro (  dword 
 macroID ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPExecuteMacro ( dbNode implement, dword 
 macroID ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `macroID` | object ID of a macro object |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPExecuteMacro ( 1200 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetAttribute

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetAttribute.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetAttribute.htm)

**Description:**

The function returns a value of an object attribute from the local object 
 pool. A Get Attribute command 
 is not sent to the Virtual Terminal. 
 The object must exist in the object pool and support the attribute ID.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetAttribute ( dword objectID, dword attributeID ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetAttribute ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword attributeID ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object identifier of an object in the object pool |
| `attributeID` | attribute identifier, see ISO11783-6 |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** Integer value of the object. If a value of 0 is returned, you can check with the function Iso11783IL_GetLastError if the function succeeded.


**Example(s):**

```c
LONG objType; objType = Iso11783IL_OPGetAttribute ( 1000, 0 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetNumericValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetNumericValue.htm)

**Description:**

The function returns a numeric value of an object. The object must exist 
 in the object pool and must support a numeric value.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue ( dword objectID, long& value ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, long& value ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue ( dword objectID ); // form 3 deprecated
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID ); // form 4 deprecated
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | Object Identifier of the object. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |
| `value` | Returns the integer value of the object. |


**Return Value:** Form 1, 2 Form 1, 2 0 on success or error code Form 3, 4 Form 3, 4 Integer value of the object. If a value of 0 is returned, you can check with the function Iso11783IL_GetLastError if the function succeeded.


**Example(s):**

```c
LONG val, result result = Iso11783IL_OPGetNumericValue ( 1000, value);
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetProperty

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetProperty.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetProperty.htm)

**Description:**

Returns a property of the Object Pool API, e.g. the supported VT version.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetProperty ( char propertyName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetProperty ( dbNode implement, char propertyName[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `propertyName` | Key of the information: "Version": VT version support 2..4, Default 3 "DebugLevel": controls the output to the Write Window 0: No output 1: Only errors 2: Warnings and errors 3: Information, warnings and errors |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** ≥ 0 Value < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes -102 Invalid parameter -1113 Unknown property


**Example(s):**

```c
LONG version; version = Iso11783IL_OPGetProperty ( "Version" );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetState

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetState.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetState.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the state of the Object Pool API.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetState ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetState ( dbNode implement ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Not active 1 Wait until ECU is online 2 Wait for status message from VT 3 Initialization phase with the VT 4 Object Pool Upload active or Load Version active 5 Active < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
state = Iso11783IL_OPGetState ( );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetStringValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetStringValue.htm)

**Description:**

The function copies a string value of an object in the object pool into 
 a buffer. The object must exist in the object pool and support a string 
 value.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue ( dword 
 objectID, dword bufferSize, char buffer[] ); // form 1: deprecated.
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue ( dword objectID, char buffer[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue ( dbNode implement, dword 
 objectID, dword bufferSize, char buffer[] ); // form 3: deprecated.
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, char buffer[] ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object Identifier of the object |
| `bufferSize` | size of the string buffer |
| `buffer` | string buffer in which the string is copied |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** ≥ 0 number of copied characters < 0 an error has occurred, see error codes -1110 object ID does not exist -1112 string Value not supported


**Example(s):**

```c
char buffer[100]; Iso11783IL_OPGetStringValue ( 1000, buffer );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPGetVersion

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetVersion.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetVersion.htm)

**Description:**

The function requests the stored object pools from the Virtual Terminal. A Get Versions command or a Extended Get Versions command is sent 
 to the Virtual Terminal. If a response from the Virtual Terminal is received, 
the callback function Iso11783IL_OPOnResponse is called.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetVersion ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetVersion ( byte extendedVersions ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetVersion ( dbNode implement, byte extendedVersions ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `extendedVersions` | kind of versions to query. To get extended versions a VT version 5 or higher is necessary. 0: get standard version strings (7 characters) 1: get extended version strings (32 characters) |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPGetVersion ( 
); [...] void Iso11783IL_OPOnResponse( dword handle, pg VTtoECU 
 response ) { switch(response.BYTE(0)) { case 224: // handle GetVersions response here break; case 211: // handle Extended Get Versions response here break; } }
```

### Iso11783IL_OPGetVTInfo

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetVTInfo.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPGetVTInfo.htm)

**Description:**

During initialization phase the Object Pool API request some information 
 about the capabilities from Virtual Terminal. These information can be 
 get with this function.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetVTInfo ( char key[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPGetVTInfo ( dbNode implement, char key[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `key` | Key of information: Key Message Description "VersionNumber" Get memory Supported VT version. "NavigationSoftKeyCount" Get number of Soft Keys The number of Physical Soft Keys that are used by the VT for navigation among the Virtual Soft Keys. "SoftKeyWidth" Get number of Soft Keys Number of pixels on the x-axis for a Soft Key descriptor. "SoftKeyHeight" Get number of Soft Keys Number of pixels on the y-axis for a Soft Key descriptor. "VirtualSoftKeyCount" Get number of Soft Keys Number of possible virtual Soft Keys in a Soft Key Mask. "PhysicalSoftKeyCount" Get number of Soft Keys Number of Physical Soft Keys. "SmallFontSizes" Get Text Font Data Supported small font sizes. "LargeFontSizes" Get Text Font Data Supported large font sizes. "FontTypeAttribute" Get Text Font Data Supported font styles. "BootTime" Get Hardware Maximum number of seconds from a VT power startup. "GraphicType" Get Hardware Supported graphic modes. "Hardware" Get Hardware Supported hardware features. "Width" Get Hardware Number of divisions on the horizontal axis in the Data Mask Area. "Height" Get Hardware Number of divisions on the vertical axis in the Data Mask Area. |
| `Key` | Message |
| `"VersionNumber"` | Get memory |
| `"NavigationSoftKeyCount"` | Get number of Soft Keys |
| `"SoftKeyWidth"` | Get number of Soft Keys |
| `"SoftKeyHeight"` | Get number of Soft Keys |
| `"VirtualSoftKeyCount"` | Get number of Soft Keys |
| `"PhysicalSoftKeyCount"` | Get number of Soft Keys |
| `"SmallFontSizes"` | Get Text Font Data |
| `"LargeFontSizes"` | Get Text Font Data |
| `"FontTypeAttribute"` | Get Text Font Data |
| `"BootTime"` | Get Hardware |
| `"GraphicType"` | Get Hardware |
| `"Hardware"` | Get Hardware |
| `"Width"` | Get Hardware |
| `"Height"` | Get Hardware |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** ≥ 0 Value < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
LONG vtWidth; vtWidth = Iso11783IL_OPGetVTInfo ( "Width" );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPLoad

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoad.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoad.htm)

**Description:**

The function loads an object pool file (*.iop or *.iopx). All other object pool access functions can only be used, if an object pool is loaded. If the activation of the object pool was already done, the object pool is transmitted to the Virtual Terminal immediately. If not, this happens when the activation is done with Iso11873IL_OPActivate . Optional a version designator for the object pool can be specified. The node layer tries to load the version with the Load Version command. If this is successful the object pool must not be transmitted to the Virtual Terminal. It is possible to send an object pool in separate files. Therefore, the function syntax form 4 and form 5 are used. By setting a flag in the options parameter the End of Object Pool message is omitted. By default, the interaction layer tries to parse the given object pool. If an error occurs the interaction layer stops the upload. In some test cases it may be helpful to send an object pool as it is without any checks to upload an erroneous object pool. For that case form 4 and form 5 provide another flag in the options parameter to avoid the checks. Note This function is not necessary if a node was configured completely in the database (DBC): ISO11783IOPFilename and ISO11783IOPVersion are defined and VT21 message is assigned to the node.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( char filename[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( char filename[], char version[] ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( dbNode implement, char filename[], char version[] ); // form 3
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( char filename[], char version[], long options ); // form 4
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( dbNode implement, char filename[], char version[], long options ); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filename` | Filename of an object pool file (*.iop or *.iopx) |
| `version` | Designator of the object pool version. Pass an empty string if no version should be loaded and the defined object pool should be sent directly to the virtual terminal. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |
| `options` | bitmask: Bit 0: Suppress End of Object Pool message Bit 1: Suppress parsing before sending, send raw data. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
Iso11783IL_OPLoad ( "ObjectPool.iop", "pool001"  ); Iso11783IL_OPActivate( );
```

*Example 2*

```c
//Upload Object Pool separated in multiple files. Iso11783IL_OPLoad( "ObjectPool_Part1.iop", "pool001", 0x01 ); Iso11783IL_OPLoad( "ObjectPool_Part2.iop", "pool001", 0 ); Iso11783IL_OPActivate( );
```

*Example 3*

```c
//Upload Object Pool and suppress parsing to be able to upload an erroneous Object Pool Iso11783IL_OPLoad( "ObjectPool.iop", "pool001" 0x02 ); Iso11783IL_OPActivate( );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPLoadAuxAssignment

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoadAuxAssignment.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoadAuxAssignment.htm)

**Description:**

This function loads the Preferred Auxiliary Input Assignment from an INI file. If the ECU is active the Preferred Assignment Message is sent immediately. Otherwise it is sent if the ECU gets active. The Preferred Assignment must be saved with Iso11783IL_OPSaveAuxAssignment before.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoadAuxAssignment ( char filename[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoadAuxAssignment ( dbNode implement, char filename[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filename` | Filename of an INI file |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPLoad( "ObjectPool.iop", "pool001"  ); Iso11783IL_OPLoadAuxAssignment ( "Sprayer.INI"); Iso11783IL_OPActivate( );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPLoadVersion

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoadVersion.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLoadVersion.htm)

**Description:**

The function loads an object pool, which is stored on the Virtual Terminal. 
 A Load Version command is sent 
 to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoadVersion ( char versionName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLoadVersion ( dbNode implement, char versionName[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `versionName` | designator of the version For VT version smaller than 5 the name must be 7 characters long. For VT version 5 and higher a name with more than 7 characters is sent with the Load Extended Version command. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPLoadVersion ( 
"POOL01A" );
```

### Iso11783IL_OPLock

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLock.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPLock.htm)

**Description:**

The function locks the screen updates on the Virtual Terminal. A Lock command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLock ( dword lock, dword maskID, dword timeout ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPLock ( dbNode implement, dword lock, dword maskID, dword timeout ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `lock` | 0: unlock 1: lock |
| `maskID` | object ID of the data mask object |
| `timeout` | timeout in [ms] |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPLock ( 1200, 1, 500 );
```



| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filename` | Filename of an object pool file (*.IOP) |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |

### Iso11783IL_OPSaveAuxAssignment

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSaveAuxAssignment.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSaveAuxAssignment.htm)

**Description:**

The function saves the current auxiliary input assignment as Preferred Auxiliary Input Assignment in an INI file. With the function Iso11783IL_OPLoadAuxAssignment the Preferred Assignment can be load and used again.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSaveAuxAssignment ( char filename[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSaveAuxAssignment ( dbNode implement, char filename[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filename` | Filename of an INI file (*.ini) |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Function has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSaveAuxAssignment ( "Sprayer.INI");
```

```c
 colorMapID ); // form 2
```

| Parameter | Description |

### Iso11783IL_OPSelectInput

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSelectInput.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSelectInput.htm)

**Description:**

The function selects an input object. A Select Input command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.

```c

```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSelectInput ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword select ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of an input object |
| `select` | 0: no input object shall be selected (i.e. focus is removed) 1: select input object 2: open input object for user input (only version >= 3) |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes

### Iso11783IL_OPSetAttribute

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetAttribute.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetAttribute.htm)

**Description:**

The function sets an attribute value of an object. The object must exist 
 in the object pool and support the attribute ID. If the Object Pool API 
 is active, the Change Attribute command (175) is sent to the Virtual Terminal. This can be suppress with 
 Bit 0 in options .


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetAttribute ( dword objectID, dword attributeID, long value ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetAttribute ( dword objectID, dword attributeID, long value, long options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetAttribute ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword attributeID, long value, long options ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of an object in the object pool |
| `attributeID` | attribute ID, see ISO11783-6 |
| `value` | new value |
| `options` | options Bit 0 and 1 = 0: send Change Attribute Value command if parameters are valid Bit 0 and 1 = 1: suppress Change Attribute Value command Bit 0 and 1 = 2: force sending Change Attribute Value command even parameters are invalid Bit 0 and 1 = 3: reserved |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes -1108 value is out of range -1109 attribute is read only -1110 object ID not found -1111 attribute ID not supported


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetAttribute ( 
 1000, 3, 20 );
```


---

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `volume` | volume, 0..100% |

**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetAudioVolume ( 100 );
```


```c
 1200, 1250, 1, 1260 );
```

### Iso11783IL_OPSetNumericValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetNumericValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetNumericValue.htm)

**Description:**

The function sets the numerical value of an object. The object must 
 exist in the object pool and must support a numerical value. If the Object 
 Pool API is active, a Change Numeric 
 Value command is sent to the Virtual Terminal. This can be suppress 
 with Bit 0 in options.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetNumericValue ( dword objectID, long numericValue ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetNumericValue ( dword objectID, long numericValue, long options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetNumericValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, long numericValue, long options ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of the object that has an updated value |
| `numericValue` | new value within the value range of the object |
| `options` | options Bit 0 and 1 = 0: send Change Numeric Value command if parameters are valid Bit 0 and 1 = 1: suppress Change Numeric Value command Bit 0 and 1 = 2: force sending Change Numeric Value command even parameters are invalid Bit 0 and 1 = 3: reserved |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetNumericValue ( 1000, 100 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetProperty.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetProperty.htm)

**Description:**

The function sets a property of the Object Pool API, i.e. the supported Virtual Terminal version.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty ( char propertyName[], long newValue ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty ( dbNode implement, char propertyName[], long newValue ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `propertyName` | Key of the information to set: Version Supported version of the ISO11783 Virtual Terminal specification. Supported values: 2, 3 (default), 4 and 5 If used, the version value obtained from the database (node attribute ISO11783OPVersion) is overwritten. SendPreferredAssignmentEvenIfEmpty Enforces sending of the Preferred Assigning message to the Virtual Terminal, even if no of auxiliary function has a preferred assigned auxiliary input, i.e. the corresponding INI file (i.g. Sprayer.ini ) contains following lines: [AuxAssignment] AuxAssignmentCount=0 Supported values: 0: Preferred Assigning message is sent to the Virtual Terminal if at least one preferred assignment exists 1: Preferred Assigning message is sent to the Virtual Terminal even if no preferred assignment exists VTFunctionInstance On startup the node connects to the Virtual Terminal (VT) which has this function instance. By default the node connects to the primary VT with function instance zero (0). If value -1 is used then the node connects to the first visible VT. ModelIdentificationCode Model identification code of the implement which is sent in the Auxiliary Input Type 2 Maintenance message. |
| `Version` | Supported version of the ISO11783 Virtual Terminal specification. Supported values: 2, 3 (default), 4 and 5 If used, the version value obtained from the database (node attribute ISO11783OPVersion) is overwritten. |
| `SendPreferredAssignmentEvenIfEmpty` | Enforces sending of the Preferred Assigning message to the Virtual Terminal, even if no of auxiliary function has a preferred assigned auxiliary input, i.e. the corresponding INI file (i.g. Sprayer.ini ) contains following lines: [AuxAssignment] AuxAssignmentCount=0 Supported values: 0: Preferred Assigning message is sent to the Virtual Terminal if at least one preferred assignment exists 1: Preferred Assigning message is sent to the Virtual Terminal even if no preferred assignment exists |
| `VTFunctionInstance` | On startup the node connects to the Virtual Terminal (VT) which has this function instance. By default the node connects to the primary VT with function instance zero (0). If value -1 is used then the node connects to the first visible VT. |
| `ModelIdentificationCode` | Model identification code of the implement which is sent in the Auxiliary Input Type 2 Maintenance message. |
| `newValue` | New value for the parameter. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 Unction has been executed successfully < 0 An error has occurred, see error codes -102 Invalid parameter -1113 Unknown property


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetProperty ( "Version", 3 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetStringValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPSetStringValue.htm)

**Description:**

The functions set the string value of an object in the object pool. 
 The object must exist in the object pool and must support a string value. 
 If the Object Pool API is active, a Change 
 String Value command is sent to the Virtual Terminal. This can 
 be suppressed with Bit 0 in options . Forms 1, 2, and 4 store the string as an 8-bit string. Forms 3 and 5 allow you to specify whether to encode the string as an 8-bit or 16-bit string. For 16-bit strings, the byte order mark (BOM) character 0xFEFF is inserted at the beginning.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( dword objectID, char stringValue[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( dword objectID, char stringValue[], dword options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, char stringValue[], dword options, dword encodingBehavior ); // form 3
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, char stringValue[], dword options ); // form 4
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, char stringValue[], dword options, dword encodingBehavior ); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of the object that has an updated value |
| `stringValue` | buffer containing new string value |
| `options` | options Bit 0 and 1 = 0: send Change String Value command if parameters are valid Bit 0 and 1 = 1: suppress Change String Value command Bit 0 and 1 = 2: force sending Change String Value command even parameters are invalid Bit 0 and 1 = 3: reserved |
| `encodingBehavior` | Specifies the encoding of the string value to be set. -1: Do not use any encoding (do not modify string value) 0: Encode string to 16-bit character string (WideString) 1: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-1 (ISO Latin 1) 2: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-2 (ISO Latin 2) 4: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-4 (ISO Latin 4) 5: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-5 (Cyrillic) 7: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-7 (Greek) 15: Encode string to 8-bit character string according to ISO 8859-15 (ISO Latin 9) Note: If you want to use non-ASCII characters, the code page in the first line of the CAPL file should be set to 65001 , which corresponds to UTF-8: /*@!Encoding:65001*/ |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error codes -1109 string is read only -1110 object ID not found -1112 string Value not supported


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPSetStringValue ( 1000, "Hello World" );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPShowObject

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPShowObject.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPShowObject.htm)

**Description:**

The function shows or hides an object. The Show Object command is sent to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPShowObject ( dword objectID, dword show ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPShowObject ( dbNode implement, dword objectID, dword show ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | object ID of the object to show or hide |
| `show` | 1: show object 0: hide object |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPShowObject ( 
 1200, 1 );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_OPStoreVersion

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPStoreVersion.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILOPStoreVersion.htm)

**Description:**

The function stores the current object pool on the Virtual Terminal. 
 A Store Version command is sent 
 to the Virtual Terminal.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPStoreVersion ( char 
 versionName[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_OPStoreVersion ( dbNode implement, char 
 versionName[] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `versionName` | name which is used to store the object pool For VT version smaller than 5 the name must be 7 characters long. For VT version 5 and higher a name with more than 7 characters is sent with the Store Extended Version command. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred, see error 
 codes


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_OPStoreVersion ( 
 "POOL01A" );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDChangeDesignator.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDChangeDesignator.htm)

**Description:**

The function sends a Change Designator message with a new object designator to the Task Controller. Form 1 uses the current string encoding and converts the designator string to an UTF-8 string if the supported version is 2 or higher, or to an ASCII string if the supported version is 1. Form 2 and Form 3 allow you to specify whether the designator string is already UTF-8 encoded, or whether the designator shall be converted to UTF-8 using the current CAPL program encoding (e.g. /*@!Encoding:1252*/).


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( word objectID, char designator[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( word objectID, char designator[], byte convert ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( dbNode implement, word objectID, char designator[], byte convert ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `objectID` | ID of the object |
| `designator` | new object designator |
| `convert` | specifies whether or not to convert the identifier to UTF-8 before sending 0: designator string shall be not converted to UTF-8 and sent as it is 1: designator string shall be converted to UTF-8 (if necessary) |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** error code


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator (1, "myNewDesignator" ) == 0) { write( "Change Designator command is sent successfully" ); }
```

*Example 2
 (avoid converting because string is already UTF-8 string encoded)*

```c
char euroSign[255]; euroSign.byte(0) = 0xE2; euroSign.byte(1) = 0x82; euroSign.byte(2) = 0xAC; euroSign.byte(3) = 0; if ( Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( 1, euroSign, 0 ) == 0) { write( "Command to change designator to '€' is sent successfully" ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDDelete

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpdddelete.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpdddelete.htm)

**Description:**

Deletes the process data directory and disconnects from the Task Controller.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDDelete ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDDelete ( dbNode implement ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 process data directory has been deleted successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_PDDDelete ( );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddloaddevicedescription.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddloaddevicedescription.htm)

**Description:**

The function (form 1 or 3) creates a process data dictionary from a valid device descriptor object pool  file (XML) and transfers the device descriptor object pool to the Task Controller. The process variables and properties can be accessed via CAPL functions. To load and transfer a file with a device descriptor object pool without checking it for validity , you must use form 2 or 4 and set the options to 1. The ddopPath must no longer point to an XML file, but to a binary file . In this case, the content of the DDOP file is not validated, no process data dictionary is created and you cannot access process variables or properties via CAPL functions. The content of the binary file is placed in the Object Pool Transfer message from the second byte onwards.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription ( char ddopPath[] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription ( char ddopPath[], dword options ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription ( dbNode implement, char ddopPath[] ); // form 3
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription ( dbNode implement, char ddopPath[], dword options ); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ddopPath` | File path of the device descriptor object pool (DDOP) file. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |
| `options` | 1: Load binary representation of a device descriptor object pool. The content of the DDOP file is not validated, no process data dictionary is created and you cannot access process variables or properties via CAPL functions. |


**Return Value:** 0 process data dictionary has been created successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDLoadDeviceDescription ( "Sprayer.XML" ) == 0) { Iso11783IL_PDDConnectSysVar ( 0x0060, 1, "EvSprayer_TankVolume" ); Iso11783IL_PPDDConnectSysVar ( 0x0048, 2, "EvSprayer_AppRatePerArea" ); } else { write( "Error while load task file" ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDeactivate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDObjectPoolDeactivate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDObjectPoolDeactivate.htm)

**Description:**

The function sends an Object-pool Deactivate message to the Task Controller and disconnects the connection to the Task Controller.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDeactivate ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDeactivate ( dbNode implement ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** error code


**Example(s):**

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDeactivate ( ) == 0) { write( "Node has disconnected from the Task Controller" ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDelete

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDObjectPoolDelete.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDObjectPoolDelete.htm)

**Description:**

The function deletes the current device description object pool and sends an Object-pool Delete message to the Task Controller Contrary to Iso11783IL_PDDDelete the connection to the Task Controller is not removed.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDelete ( ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDelete ( dbNode implement ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** error code


**Example(s):**

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDObjectPoolDelete ( ) == 0) { write("Object pool is deleted and Object-Pool delete message is sent successfully"); }
```

### Iso11783IL_PDDSetDistance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddsetdistance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddsetdistance.htm)

**Description:**

The distance covered is set with this function. The value is needed 
 for the distance trigger and should be updated cyclically.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetDistance ( dword 
 distance ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetDistance ( dbNode implement, dword 
 distance ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `distance` | distance covered in [m] |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
void ReceiveGBSDFromTractor( 
 pg GroundBasesSpeedPG thisPG ){ Iso11783IL_PDDSetDistance ( thisPG.GroundBasedDistance.phys ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDSetLogTrigger

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDSetLogTrigger.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDSetLogTrigger.htm)

**Description:**

A measurement command can be executed with this function. It can be used in the callback function Iso11783IL_PDDOnDefaultLogRequest to activate the default logging.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetLogTrigger ( dword command, ulong ddi, ulong elNum, ulong value ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetLogTrigger ( dbNode implement, dword command, ulong ddi, ulong elNum, ulong value ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `command` | measurement command: 4: Time Interval 5: Distance Interval 6: Minimum within Threshold 7: Maximum within Threshold 8: Change Threshold |
| `ddi` | Data Dictionary Identifier, 0x0000..0xFFFF |
| `elNum` | element number, 0x000..0xFFF |
| `value` | depends on command Command Value Time Interval time in ms Distance Interval distance in mm Minimum within Threshold Maximum within Threshold Change Threshold value of the process variable use value 0 to disable a trigger |
| `Command` | Value |
| `Time Interval` | time in ms |
| `Distance Interval` | distance in mm |
| `Minimum within Threshold Maximum within Threshold Change Threshold` | value of the process variable |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
void Iso11783IL_PDDOnDefaultLogRequest ( ) { Iso11783IL_PDDSetLogTrigger ( 4, 0x0100, 0, 1000 ); Iso11783IL_PDDSetLogTrigger ( 4, 0x0101, 0, 1000 ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDSetParameter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDSetParameter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDSetParameter.htm)

**Description:**

Changes an internal parameter of the PDD API.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetParameter ( char paramName[], float paramValue ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetParameter ( dbNode simulNode, char paramName[], float paramValue ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `paramName` | Value Description debug activates debug outputs in the Write Window 1: on 0: off autoUpdateVariable process variable is updated if value command is received 1: auto update 0: process variable have to be updated manually by Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue e.g. in callback function Iso11783IL_PDDOnDataChanged version Note Using version the version value obtained from the database (node attribute ISO11783PDDVersion ) is overwritten. supported version of the ISO11783 specification: 1,2 (default),3, and 4 If version is set to 3 or higher, further parameter values can be used for the Version message that is sent by the implement (see specification ISO11783-10, B.5.3). Value Description BootTimeTC" maximum number of seconds from a power cycle to transmission of the first Task Controller Status message, default: 255 ImplementSectionControl 1: supports implement section control default: doesn't support. PeerControlAssignment 1: supports peer control assignment default: doesn't support TC_GEO_DefinitionPositionBasedControl 1: TC-GEO definition, position based control default: doesn't support. TimeAndPositionForDataLogging 1: supports time and position for data logging default: doesn't support Documentation 1: supports documentation default: doesn't support NumberOfBooms minimum required  number of booms that the  Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 NumberOfSection minimum required number of sections that the Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 NumberOfControlChannels minimum required number of individual control channels that the Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 If version is set to 3 or higher, further parameter values can be used for the Request Structure Label message that is sent by the implement (see specification ISO11783-10, B.6.2). Value Description RequestForLatestStructureLabel 1: bytes 2..8 of the Request Structure Label message are transmitted as FF. It is used to determine the version of the latest device description structure present at the Task Controller or Data Logger. 0 :default |
| `Value` | Description |
| `debug` | activates debug outputs in the Write Window 1: on 0: off |
| `autoUpdateVariable` | process variable is updated if value command is received 1: auto update 0: process variable have to be updated manually by Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue e.g. in callback function Iso11783IL_PDDOnDataChanged |
| `version` | Note Using version the version value obtained from the database (node attribute ISO11783PDDVersion ) is overwritten. supported version of the ISO11783 specification: 1,2 (default),3, and 4 |
| `BootTimeTC"` | maximum number of seconds from a power cycle to transmission of the first Task Controller Status message, default: 255 |
| `ImplementSectionControl` | 1: supports implement section control default: doesn't support. |
| `PeerControlAssignment` | 1: supports peer control assignment default: doesn't support |
| `TC_GEO_DefinitionPositionBasedControl` | 1: TC-GEO definition, position based control default: doesn't support. |
| `TimeAndPositionForDataLogging` | 1: supports time and position for data logging default: doesn't support |
| `Documentation` | 1: supports documentation default: doesn't support |
| `NumberOfBooms` | minimum required  number of booms that the  Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 |
| `NumberOfSection` | minimum required number of sections that the Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 |
| `NumberOfControlChannels` | minimum required number of individual control channels that the Working Set requires to operate correctly, default: 0 |
| `RequestForLatestStructureLabel` | 1: bytes 2..8 of the Request Structure Label message are transmitted as FF. It is used to determine the version of the latest device description structure present at the Task Controller or Data Logger. 0 :default |
| `paramValue` | new value for the parameter |
| `simulNode` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 process data dictionary has been created successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDSetParameter ( "debug", 1 ) == 0) { write( "Debug mode activated" ); }
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddsetvalue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILpddsetvalue.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a process variable.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue ( dbSignal signal, dword elementNumber, float value ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue ( dword ddi, dword elementNumber, float value ); // form 2
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue ( dbNode implement, dword ddi, dword elementNumber, float value ); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `signal` | signal from the database The attribute ddi must have been defined for the signal. The signal must be defined in the same database as the node! |
| `ddi` | Data Dictionary Identifier, 0x0000.0xFFFF |
| `elementNumber` | element number, 0x000.0xFFF |
| `value` | new value of the process variable |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 function has been executed successfully < 0 an error has occurred


**Example(s):**

```c
Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue ( 0x200. 3, value ); Iso11783IL_PDDSetValue ( 0x200. 4, value );
```


---

### Iso11783IL_PDDUpdateDeviceDescription

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDUpdateDeviceDescription.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/ISO11783/ISOInteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionIso11783ILPDDUpdateDeviceDescription.htm)

**Description:**

The function updates the current device description object pool at runtime. Only the device object and objects of type DeviceValuePresentation of the specified file are transmitted.


**Syntax:**

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDUpdateDeviceDescription ( char deviceCfgPath [] ); // form 1
```

```c
long Iso11783IL_PDDUpdateDeviceDescription ( dbNode implement, char deviceCfgPath [] ); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `deviceCfgPath` | filename of the device configuration file The file must be located in the directory of the CANoe configuration. |
| `implement` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** error code


**Example(s):**

```c
if ( Iso11783IL_PDDUpdateDeviceDescription ( "Sprayer01.XML" ) == 0) { // after  the device value presentation has changed to English // the designators are changed too Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( 1, "Tank volume" ); Iso11783IL_PDDChangeDesignator ( 2, "App rate per area" ); } else { write( "Error while update file" ); }
```


## J1587 {#j1587}


---

### on J1587ErrorMessage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587ErrorMessage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587ErrorMessage.htm)

**Description:**

Defines the event handler for an erroneous J1708 message. The error code is contained in J1587ErrorMessage::J1587_Error . For passing this message to other node, output(this) must be called inside the event handler.


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `* (receives all MIDs) // Msg with J1587_Error != 0` |  |


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587ErrorMessage 50 //50 is Sender MID, can be dbNode name or MID { write ("Msg with ErrorCode %d received, MID %d", this.J1587_MID, this.J1587_Error); }
```


---

### on J1587Message

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587Message.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587Message.htm)

**Description:**

Defines the event handler for a valid J1708 message, the this pointer is of type J1587Message . For passing this message to other node, output(this) must be called inside the event handler.


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dbNode | MID | * // Msg with J1587_Error = 0` |  |


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587Message 50 // 50 is Sender MID, can be dbNode name or MID { J1587Param 0 param; if (GetParameterByPID(this, 30, param) == 0) { output(this); } }
```


---

### on J1587Param

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587Param.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/EventHandler/CAPLfunctionOnJ1587Param.htm)

**Description:**

Defines the event handler for a valid J1587 parameter, the this pointer is of type J1587Param .


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dbMsg | PID | *` |  |


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587Param TextMessageAcknowledged { if (this.MID == gECU_MID) { if (this.MessageDisplayed == 1) { gDisplayState = kDisplayed; } else { gDisplayState = kTimeout; } } }
```


---

### J1587AppendParameter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587AppendParameter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587AppendParameter.htm)

**Description:**

Appends a J1587 parameter to a J1708 message.


**Syntax:**

```c
byte J1587AppendParameter (J1587Message msg /*in/out*/, J1587Param param /*in*/)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | J1708 message |
| `param` | returned parameter at the given index |


**Return Value:** Value Description 0 no error 0x1 0x2 The parameter to append is of a different page than the first parameter (if yet any = DLC > 0) of the J1708 message. 0x3 The parameter to append is of a different type (parameter, proprietary parameter, PCMD data) than the first parameter (if yet any = DLC > 0) of the J1708 message. 0x4 The DLC of the resulting J1708 message would exceed 3825 bytes (limit for the TP!). 0x5 The receiver of the parameter to append to the message is different from the receiver of the message. A (proprietary) J1708 message can only have one receiver. 0x6 The data content of the J1708 message is invalid. This can be due to a mismatch between the DLC and the actual content of the message or due to a message content, from which valid parameter data cannot be deduced. Therefore it is impossible to evaluate if the append operation is possible at all and if yes to find the position for appending an additional parameter to the message. 0xFF general error


**Example(s):**

```c
J1587Message 50 txMsg; J1587Param EngineSpeed txEngSpeed; J1587Param EngineCoolantTemp txEngCool; J1587AppendParameter (txMsg, txEngSpeed); J1587AppendParameter (txMsg, txEngCool); output(txMsg);
```


---

### J1587GetParameter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameter.htm)

**Description:**

Gets a J1587 parameter inside a J1708 message at a given index.


**Syntax:**

```c
byte J1587GetParameter (J1587Message msg /*in*/, J1587Param param /*out*/, word index /*in*/)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | J1708 message |
| `param` | returned parameter at the given index |
| `index` | index of parameter to retrieve |


**Return Value:** 0 or error code


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587Message 50 // 50 is Sender MID, can be dbNode name or MID { dword count, i; J1587Param param; count = GetParameterCount(this); for (i = 0; i < count; i++) { if ( J1587GetParameter (this, param, i) == 0) { write ("Parameter with PID %d received: %d", param.J1587_PID); } } }
```


---

### J1587GetParameterByPID

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameterByPID.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameterByPID.htm)

**Description:**

Gets a J1587 parameter inside a J1708 message given a specific PID. The function returns an error if a parameter with the specified dbPID is not found inside the J1708 message.


**Syntax:**

```c
byte J1587GetParameterByPID (J1587Message msg /*in*/, J1587Param param /*out*/, dword dbPID /*in*/)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | J1708 message |
| `param` | returned parameter at the given index |
| `dbPID` | database PID |


**Return Value:** 0 or error code


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587Message 50 // 50 is Sender MID, can be dbNode name or MID { dword count, i; J1587Param param; if ( J1587GetParameterByPID (this, param, 80) == 0) { write ("Parameter with PID 80 received."); } }
```


---

### J1587GetParameterCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameterCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587GetParameterCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the count of J1587 parameters inside a given J1708 message that can be used to further calls to J1587GetParameter() .


**Syntax:**

```c
word J1587GetParameterCount (J1587Message msg /*in*/)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | J1708 message |


**Return Value:** number of parameters


**Example(s):**

```c
on J1587Message 50 // 50 is Sender MID, can be dbNode name or MID { write ("Number of parameters received: %d", J1587GetParameterCount (this)); }
```

### setJ1587TP

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587SetJ1587TP.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1587/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1587SetJ1587TP.htm)

**Description:**

Selects the setting for the transport protocol to use when sending a J1587 parameter via the output function.


**Syntax:**

```c
void setJ1587TP ( j1587Param param, dword mode );
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `param` | J1587 parameter for which the setting is used |
| `mode` | 0 (default) select the transport protocol by means of the receiver (selector J1587_ReceiverMID) MID 127: Multisection other MID: CMP/CDP 1 disable use of any transport protocol 2 use Multisection 3 use CMP/CDP |
| `0 (default)` | select the transport protocol by means of the receiver (selector J1587_ReceiverMID) MID 127: Multisection other MID: CMP/CDP |
| `1` | disable use of any transport protocol |
| `2` | use Multisection |
| `3` | use CMP/CDP |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

The data length value for the specified message will be adjusted.


| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `msg` | Message whose data length is to be adjusted. |
| `dataLength` | New data length of the message. |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code .


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### J1939ILNodeDisturbChecksum

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/J1939InteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939ILNodeDisturbChecksum.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/J1939InteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939ILNodeDisturbChecksum.htm)

**Description:**

This function modifies the message checksum (see AUTOSAR E2E Protection ).


**Syntax:**

```c
J1939ILNodeDisturbChecksum (char messageName[], char signalGroupName[], long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue); // form 1
```

```c
J1939ILNodeDisturbChecksum (dbNode node, char messageName[], char signalGroupName[], long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `messageName` | Name of the message that should be modified. Supported qualification patterns: [DBName::][NodeName::]aMessageName |
| `signalGroupName` | Apply the disturbance to a dedicated signal group within a message. Use an empty character array if the checksum of the whole message should be affected. Supported qualification patterns for form 3: [DBName::][NodeName::][MessageName::]aSignalgroupName |
| `disturbanceMode` | Identifies the disturbance mode: 0 Value The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as checksum. 1 Freeze The current checksum value is transmitted. 2 Random A random value is transmitted as checksum. 3 Offset The checksum is incremented with the value in disturbanceValue . |
| `0` | Value |
| `1` | Freeze |
| `2` | Random |
| `3` | Offset |
| `disturbanceCount` | -1 Infinite. 0 Stops the disturbance, e.g.a infinite disturbance. n Number of disturbances. |
| `-1` | Infinite. |
| `n` | Number of disturbances. |
| `disturbanceValue` | This value is used in combination with the disturbanceMode . |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code .


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### J1939ILNodeDisturbCounter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/J1939InteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939ILNodeDisturbCounter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/J1939InteractionLayer/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939ILNodeDisturbCounter.htm)

**Description:**

This function modifies the message counter (see AUTOSAR E2E Protection ).


**Syntax:**

```c
J1939ILNodeDisturbCounter (char messageName[], char signalGroupname[], long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue, long continueMode); // form 1
```

```c
J1939ILNodeDisturbCounter (dbNode node, char messageName[], char signalGroupname[], long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue, long continueMode); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `messageName` | Name of the message that should be modified. Supported qualification patterns: [DBName::][NodeName::]aMessageName |
| `signalGroupName` | Apply the disturbance to a dedicated signal group within a message. Use an empty character array if the checksum of the whole message should be affected. Supported qualification patterns for form 3: [DBName::][NodeName::][MessageName::]aSignalgroupName |
| `disturbanceMode` | Identifies the disturbance mode: 0 Value The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as counter. 1 Freeze The current counter value is transmitted. 2 Random A random value is transmitted as counter. 3 Offset The counter is incremented with the value in disturbanceValue . |
| `0` | Value |
| `1` | Freeze |
| `2` | Random |
| `3` | Offset |
| `disturbanceCount` | -1 Infinite. 0 Stops the disturbance, e.g.a infinite disturbance. n Number of disturbances. |
| `-1` | Infinite. |
| `n` | Number of disturbances. |
| `disturbanceValue` | This value is used in combination with the disturbanceMode . |
| `continueMode` | Defines the behavior of the counter after the disturbances are finished: 0 CorrectCounter The counter will be incremented with counter value + number of disturbances. 1 LastValidCounter The counters next value bases on the last value before the disturbance has started. 2 LastValue The counter increments the last counter value (last disturbance value). |
| `node` | Simulation node to apply the function. |


**Return Value:** 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code .


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### J1939GetAddress

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939GetAddress.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/J1939/Functions/CAPLfunctionJ1939GetAddress.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the current address of a J1939 node even the address has been changed by the J1939 network management.


**Syntax:**

```c
long J1939GetAddress (dbNode aNode);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `aNode` | The function returns the address of this node. |


**Return Value:** Current address of the node ≥ 0 Current address of the node. -1 General error, for example, node does not exist in database.


**Example(s):**

```c
dword address; address = J1939GetAddress (N1); Write("Address of node N1 is = %i", address );
```
