### linActivateResps

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINActivateResps.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINActivateResps.htm)

**Description:**

Reactivates all frame responses published by the calling Slave node according to the LIN database file (LDF), after having been previously 
 deactivated by linDeactiveResps() or linResetSlave() . Per default all frame responses are activated for Slave nodes on measurement start i.e. it is assumed that Slave nodes have nonvolatile memory. LIN2.0 Slave nodes will automatically activate responses for re-configurable frames on receiving valid reconfiguration commands i.e. AssignFrameID. Individual frame responses can be activated manually using the function linSetRespCounter . With form 2 you can activate the Slave responses of the selected Slave node (nodeName ). If the parameter nodeName is not set or an empty string is given, the Slave responses of the node invoking this function will be deactivated. Note Form 2 has only an effect if the target is a program node that was assigned to a Slave node from the selected LIN database. Reactivates all frame responses published by the calling Slave node according to the LIN database file (LDF), after having been previously 
 deactivated by linDeactiveResps() or linResetSlave() . Per default all frame responses are activated for Slave nodes on measurement start i.e. it is assumed that Slave nodes have nonvolatile memory. LIN2.0 Slave nodes will automatically activate responses for re-configurable frames on receiving valid reconfiguration commands i.e. AssignFrameID. Individual frame responses can be activated manually using the function linSetRespCounter . With form 2 you can activate the Slave responses of the selected Slave node (nodeName ). If the parameter nodeName is not set or an empty string is given, the Slave responses of the node invoking this function will be deactivated. Note Form 2 has only an effect if the target is a program node that was assigned to a Slave node from the selected LIN database.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linActivateResps(); // form 1
```

```c
long linActivateResps(char nodeName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `nodeName` | Slave node |


**Return Value:** Number of activated frame responses or -1 if an error occurs.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linDeactivateResps

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDeactivateResps.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDeactivateResps.htm)

**Description:**

This function deactivates frame responses for all frames published by 
 the calling Slave node. The frame responses can be deactivated individually using the function linSetRespCounter() . With form 2 you can deactivate the Slave responses of the selected Slave node (nodeName ). If the parameter nodeName is not set or an empty string is given, the Slave responses of the node invoking this function will be deactivated. Note If the configurable_frames list for the targeted Slave node is not complete, the function will not work correctly.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linDeactivateResps(); // form 1
```

```c
long linDeactivateResps(char nodeName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `nodeName` | Slave node |


**Return Value:** Number of deactivated frame responses or -1 on failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

With this function it is possible to activate/deactivate the automatic responses on a certain event-triggered frame request in the case of a 
 signal update on its associated frame(s). This concerns all Slave tasks publishing the responses to the specified event-triggered frame.

```c
long linETFSendOnSignalUpdate(long etfId, long active);
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `etfId` | Identifier of event-triggered frame. Value range: 0-59 |
| `active` | 1: Activate 0: Deactivate |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linETFSetDirtyFlag

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINETFSetDirtyFlag.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINETFSetDirtyFlag.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to set/clear the dirty flag of an associated frame. If the dirty flag of an associated frame is set when the corresponding event-triggered frame is being requested, then the LIN hardware will try to transmit the associated frame's data. The dirty flag gets reset automatically 
 when the associated frame has been sent successfully – either via the event-triggered frame or unconditionally.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linETFSetDirtyFlag(long assocId, long dirty);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `assocId` | Identifier of unconditional frame LIN frame identifier of associated (unconditional) frame whose dirty flag should be set or cleared. Value range: 0 .. 63 |
| `dirty` | 1: Set the dirty flag. 0: Clear the dirty flag. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

**Return Value:** -1 Function call not allowed, execution failed or invalid slave name given. 0 Queried data indication bit is not set. >0 Queried data indication bit is set.

### linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback.htm)

**Description:**

Registers a callback function which is invoked whenever the response data of a LIN frame has been changed. The invocation of the callback happens right before the data is passed to the LIN hardware or the LIN bus simulator. The data of the linFrame parameter can be modified by this function. This can be used to ensure consistency of the frame's data before it is sent. Note This function may only be called in the event procedure on preStart .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback (char[] callbackName);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `callbackName` | The name of the callback function which should be registered. The callback must have the following signature: void func (linFrame* frame) |


**Return Value:** Returns 1 if the registration succeeded, otherwise 0 .


**Example(s):**

```c
on preStart { if (linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback("OnFrameResponseDataChanged")) { write("linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback succeeded."); } else { write("linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback failed."); } } void OnFrameResponseDataChanged(linFrame* frm) { byte checksum; dword i; if (frm.id != 50 || frm.msgChannel != 1) { // filter for one specific frame return; } // pseudo checksum calculation over bytes 1 - dlc checksum = 0x55; for (i = 1; i < frm.dlc; i++) { checksum ^= frm.byte(i); } // set first byte of frame to calculated checksum frm.byte(0) = checksum; }
```


---

### linResetNAD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetNad.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetNad.htm)

**Description:**

This function resets NAD (Node Address for Diagnostic) of the Slave 
 node determined by the CAPL program context to its initial NAD. See Option .LIN: Notes to the way initial NAD is determined to understand how initial NAD is determined in CANoe. Note If the CAPL program calling this function, does not model a LIN 2.x Slave node, then this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linResetNAD();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** On success this function returns a value unequal to -1, otherwise -1.


**Example(s):**

```c
// Force NAD reset on a keyboard event on key 'r' { linFrame 0x3C frameMasterReq; linFrame 0x3D frameSlaveResp; // reset NAD of the modeled Slave node // we assume that by default initial NAD differs from configured NAD linResetNAD(); } CAPL program modeling Master node ... // Test Slave node reaction after its NAD has been 
 reset. // Prepare AssignFrameId command for one of the Slave's configurable frames // In this example it is assumed that the configured NAD differs from the initial // NAD // set command's data frameMasterReq.byte(0) = 0x02; // configured NAD frameMasterReq.byte(1) = 0x06; // PCI frameMasterReq.byte(2) = 0xB1; // SID frameMasterReq.byte(3) = 0x1E; // Supplier Id LSB frameMasterReq.byte(4) = 0x0;  // Supplier Id MSB frameMasterReq.byte(5) = 0x22; // Message Id LSB frameMasterReq.byte(6) = 0x22; // Message Id MSB frameMasterReq.byte(7) = linGetProtectedID(0x33); // Protected ID // update response data frameMasterReq.RTR = 0; output(frameMasterReq); // send the command (issue request header) frameMasterReq.RTR = 1; output(frameMasterReq); // send header of SlaveResponse frame frameSlaveResp.RTR = 1; output(frameSlaveResp); // In Trace Window transmission error shall occur for SlaveResponse frame because // Slave doesn't know the configured NAD yet
```


---

### linResetSlave

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetSlave.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetSlave.htm)

**Description:**

This function resets the NAD (Node Address for Diagnostic) of the modeled 
 Slave node and marks all its protected identifiers as invalid or it will 
 load the last saved configuration if resetToLastConfiguration is set. See Notes to the way initial NAD is determined to understand how initial NAD is determined in CANoe. Note If the CAPL-program calling this function, does not model a LIN 2.x Slave node, then this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linResetSlave (); // form 1
```

```c
long linResetSlave(dword resetToLastConfiguration); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `resetToLastConfiguration` | 0: perform a complete reset non-zero: load last saved configuration |


**Return Value:** On success this function returns a value unequal to -1, otherwise -1.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'r' { if ( linResetSlave() != -1) { // from now on the Slave node stops publishing responses ... } }
```

This function sets a new bus idle timeout. Depending on the protocol 
 version, the timeout will be specified in bit times (LIN 1.x and Cooling) or in milliseconds (all others).

```c
dword linSetBusIdleTimeout(dword timeout);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `timeout` | The timeout in bit times (LIN 1.x and Cooling) or milliseconds (all other LIN protocols). Value range: 1 - 32767 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


### linSetExpectedRespLength

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetExpectedRespLength.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetExpectedRespLength.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to configure how many data bytes of the frame response will be expected on reception. Reception of a LIN frame with a number of data bytes deviant from the expected number of bytes will lead to either a CRC error (if more bytes were received than expected) or a RcvError (if less bytes were received than expected). The actual length of frames transmitted on the bus will remain unchanged. Note A channel receiving a LIN response can be reconfigured, using linSetExpectedRespLength , to accept a LIN frame with a differing DLC from what was initially configured (e.g. by the LDF database). To change the maximum number of bytes transmitted, use linSetRespLength . linSetRespLength and linSetExpectedRespLength cannot be used together to correctly reconfigure a LIN frame to be sent with a different length. Use output(linFrame) for this purpose. Note Use this function (as well as linSetRespLength ) with care, because it is possible to get the LIN interface stuck in configurations which aren't easy to resolve. It's not recommended to use linSetRespLength and linSetExpectedRespLength in combination on the same channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetExpectedRespLength (long frameID, long numberOfBytes);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | Identifier of the LIN frame to be configured. Value range: 0…63 |
| `numberOfBytes` | Number of expected data bytes (excluding the CRC byte). Value range: 1..8 |


**Return Value:** On success, returns 1, otherwise 0.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linSetRespCounter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespCounter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespCounter.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to limit the number of responses sent 
 for the specified frame identifier. By setting count = 0 it is possible to deactivate the frame response completely. With count = -1 a frame response is always sent when a correct frame header is received. This is the default configuration for newly configured 
 frames.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetRespCounter(long frameID, long count);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | Identifier of the LIN frame to be configured Value range: 0…63 |
| `count` | Number of responses to be sent. n-times: 0..254 unlimited: -1 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linSetRespError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespError.htm)

**Description:**

Sets/resets the response error flag for a calling slave node. Note This function has no effect with LIN 1.x.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetRespError(long activate);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `activate` | 0: reset 1: set |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linSetRespLength

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespLength.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespLength.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to configure how many data bytes of the frame 
 response should be sent. The frame's length is not changed by this function. To send a correct response the numberOfBytes should be set equal to DLC+1 i.e for the frame data bytes and their checksum. If the numberOfBytes is to set to a value larger than DLC + 1, then a complete response is also sent. If the numberOfBytes is set to a value smaller than DLC+1, then a receiving error will occur or if numberOfBytes = 0 a transmission error. Note This function can be used to simulate collisions during sending of a frame response e.g. after receiving an event triggered frame.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetRespLength(long frameID, long numberOfBytes);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | Identifier of the LIN frame to be configured. Value range: 0…63 |
| `numberOfBytes` | Number of bytes to be sent in the frame response (including checksum). Value range: 0..DLC+1 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
linFrame MotorControl frameMotorControl; ... if (linSetRespLength (frameMotorControl.ID, frameMotorControl.DLC) != 0) { // from now on a receive error notified for the MotorControl frame ... } if (linSetRespLength (frameMotorControl.ID, 0) != 0) { // from now on a transmission error notified for the MotorControl frame ... } }
```


---

### linSetValidBreakLimits

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetValidBreakLimits.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetValidBreakLimits.htm)

**Description:**

Sets limits for the accepted sync break and delimiter lengths. Any sync 
 break and delimiter outside of this range is considered invalid, resulting 
 in a receive error. Note that this function does not change the current 
 sync break and delimiter length sent by a simulated master. This function can also be called in slave mode to test an external master. This function does not change the header length restrictions of 47.6 
 bit times for LIN 2.x and 49 bit times for LIN 1.x. This means that a 
 header can still be invalid even if the modified sync break and delimiter limits are met.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linSetValidBreakLimits(dword breakMin16thBits, 
 dword breakMax16thBits, dword delMin16thBits, dword delMax16thBits);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `breakMin16thBits` | The minimum legal sync break length, specified in units of 1/16th bit 
 times. Range: 152 (9.5 bit times) – 784 (49 bit times) |
| `breakMax16thBits` | The maximum legal sync break length, specified in units of 1/16th bit 
 times. Range: 152 (9.5 bit times) – 784 (49 bit times), has to be greater than 
 breakMin16thBits |
| `delMin16thBits` | The minimum legal sync delimiter length, specified in units of 1/16th 
 bit times. Range: 1 – 784 (49 bit times) |
| `delMax16thBits` | The maximum legal sync delimiter length, specified in units of 1/16th bit times. Range: 1 – 784 (49 bit times), has to be greater than delMin16thBits |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

|-----------|-------------|
| `active` | 0: deactivate 1: activate |

**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linGotoSleepInternal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGotoSleepInternal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGotoSleepInternal.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the LIN hardware to Sleep mode without sending a go-to-sleep-command on the network. Calling this function in the event procedure on prestart leads to measurement start in sleep mode.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGotoSleepInternal();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// start measurement in Sleep mode on preStart { linGotoSleepInternal(); }
```

### linSetWakeupBehavior

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetWakeupBahvior.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetWakeupBahvior.htm)

**Description:**

Defines the behavior after wakeup signal or an internal wakeup. Note When LIN hardware is not in sleep mode calling this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
linSetWakeupBehavior(dword restartScheduler, dword wakeupIdentifier); // form 1
```

```c
linSetWakeupBehavior(dword restartScheduler, dword wakeupIdentifier, dword sendShortHeader); // form 2
```

```c
linSetWakeupBehavior(dword restartScheduler, dword wakeupIdentifier, dword sendShortHeader, dword wakeupDelimiterLength); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `restartScheduler` | Determines if index of the slot to be started with after wakeup has to be reset, i.e. it becomes 0. If it's not reset the next slot before entering sleep mode is used. In the case no schedule tables are defined this parameter is ignored. Value range: 0..1 |
| `wakeupIdentifier` | LIN frame identifier to be sent additionally directly after sending a wakeup signal. 
If an invalid identifier is specified i.e. not in the range 0..63: When schedule tables are defined no special wakeup identifier is sent. When no schedule tables are defined a SynchBreak/SynchField pair without an identifier is sent. Value range: 0..0xFF |
| `sendShortHeader` | Determines whether a SynchBreak/SynchField pair without an identifier should be sent or not, when the conditions described above hold true. That is, when No valid wakeupIdentifier is specified No schedule tables are defined sendShortHeader is true
then a SynchBreak/SynchField will be sent. No such "short header" will be sent otherwise. Value range: 0..1 |
| `wakeupDelimiterLength` | This parameter specifies the wakeup delimiter length, i.e. the time between the end of wakeup frame and the first header sent to detect a wakeup reason. Units of this parameter as well as default value depend on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . Value range:: 3..255 (ms or bit times) |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { // do not take any action and don't send anything automatically after the channel is // set to awake mode. Use these settings if you'd like to simulate a scheduler using // CAPL timers. linSetWakeupBehavior (0, 0xff, 0); }
on linSleepModeEvent { linSetWakeupBehavior (0, 0x1, 0, 100); // don't restart scheduler, use 0x1 as wakeup identifier, start scheduling or send the wakeup identifier after 100 ms. }
```

> Change wakeup behavior on receiving a sleep mode event


---

### linSetwakeupCondition

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetwakeupCondition.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetwakeupCondition.htm)

**Description:**

Determines the conditions under which the LIN hardware can be reactivated (leaves the sleep mode). Note When LIN hardware is not in master mode calling this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
linSetWakeupCondition(dword numWakeupSignals); // form 1
```

```c
linSetWakeupCondition(dword numWakeupSignals, dword forgetWupsAfterMS); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `numWakeupSignals` | This parameter specifies the number of wakeup signals, after that the LIN hardware is reactivated. If the value 0 is set, the LIN hardware will never leave the sleep mode. Value range: 0..31 Default value: 1 |
| `forgetWupsAfterMS` | This parameter defines the time after that earlier wakeup signals are not any longer taken into account for the wakeup behavior. Default value: 0 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// configure LIN hardware to wakeup after 3 wakeup signals linSetWakeupCondition (3);
```


---

### linSetWakeupTimings

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetWakeupTimings.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetWakeupTimings.htm)

**Description:**

Configures wakeup timings. Wakeup timings can be configured per node for nodes defined in a LIN database.


**Syntax:**

```c
linSetWakeupTimings(long ttobrk, long wakeupSignalcount, dword widthInMicSec); // form 1
```

```c
linSetWakeupTimings(long ttobrk, long wakeupSignalcount, dword widthInMicSec, char nodeName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ttobrk` | This parameter specifies the time difference between the transmissions of two consecutive Wakeup frames, i.e. the time between end of one wakeup frame and start of the next one. 
Units of this parameter as well as default value depend on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . Value range (for units expected in bit times): 20..50000 Value range (for units expected in ms): 1..65536 |
| `wakeupSignalCount` | Sets the number of Wakeup frame retransmissions. Value range: 1..255 Default value depends on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . |
| `widthInMicSec` | This parameter sets the width of the wakeup frame to be sent in microseconds. The resolution is 50 µs. 
This parameter is only used by LIN2.0 slave nodes. Value range: 250..5000 µs Default value depends on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . |
| `nodeName` | Name of a LIN node defined in the database. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// Configures the wake up timings for the database node "LINMaster". linSetWakeupTimings(150, 3, 250, "LINMaster") // send a wakeup signal via a keyboard event within the CAPL programe of the node // "LINMaster". The configured timings will be used. on key 'w' { linWakeup(); }
```


---

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINWakeInternal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINWakeInternal.htm)

**Description:**

Wakes up the LIN bus without sending any wakeup signals. Note

```c
long linWakeupInternal();
```

|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |

```c

```

### linWakeup

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINWakeUp.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINWakeUp.htm)

**Description:**

Sends wakeup signals. Wakeup signals can only be sent while the LIN hardware is in Sleep mode. If no parameters are given, the default parameters or the parameters configured with linSetWakeupTimings are used. Wake up timings can be configured per node for nodes defined in a LIN database. If no database is used the signature using wake parameters shall be used. Note When LIN hardware is not in Sleep mode calling this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linWakeup(); // form 1
```

```c
linWakeup(dword ttobrk, dword wakeupSignalCount, dword widthInMicSec); // from 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ttobrk` | This parameter specifies the time difference between the transmissions of two consecutive Wakeup frames, i.e. the time between end of one wakeup frame and start of the next one. 
Units of this parameter as well as default value depend on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN). Value range (for units expected in bit times): 20..50000 Value range (for units expected in ms): 1..65536 |
| `wakeupSignalCount` | Sets the number of Wakeup frame retransmissions. Value range: 1…255
Default value depends on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . |
| `widthInMicSec` | This parameter sets the width of the wakeup frame to be sent in microseconds. The resolution is 50 µs. 
This parameter is only used by slave nodes applying LIN protocol starting with version 2.0. Value range: 250..5000 µs Default value depends on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN ) . |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// send a wakeup signal via a keyboard event on key 'w' { linWakeup(); } // send wakeup signal 3 times with 150 ms delay between 2 consecutive signals and a length of 300 us linWakeup(150, 3, 300); ...
```


---

### linEnableStatistics

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINEnableStatistics.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINEnableStatistics.htm)

**Description:**

Enables or disables LIN statistics on a specific (form 1) or all (form 2) LIN channels. Note LIN bus statistics for CAPL is disabled by default to avoid performance issues while measurement is running. However, you can enable it by calling these functions, e.g. in an on start {} handler (see example below).


**Syntax:**

```c
void linEnableStatistics(long channel, long flag); // form 1
```

```c
void linEnableStatistics(long flag); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1...64) |
| `flag` | 0 = disable; 1 = enable |


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable statistics on channel 1 or... //linEnableStatistics(1);    // on all channels } on key 'd' { linEnableStatistics(1, 0); // Disable statistics on channel 1 }
```


---

### linGetBaudRateDevMaster

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBaudRateDevMaster.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBaudRateDevMaster.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the measured baudrate deviation of the master node on a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
double linGetBaudRateDevMaster(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Baudrate deviation of the master node in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long value; value = linGetBaudRateDevMaster (1, eMaxValue); // Maximum baudrate deviation of master on channel 1 // … do something with the value … }
```


---

### linGetBaudRateMaster

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBaudRateMaster.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBaudRateMaster.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the measured baudrate of the master node on a LIN channel in bits per second [bit/s]. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetBaudRateMaster(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Baudrate of the master node in bits per second, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long value; value = linGetBaudRateMaster (1, eCurrValue); // Current frame cycle time on channel 1 // … do something with the value … }
```


---

### linGetBusErrorCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBusErrorCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBusErrorCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the total count of bus errors of a LIN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetBusErrorCount(long channel)
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |


**Return Value:** Total count of bus errors, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long count; count = linGetBusErrorCount (1); // Bus errors on channel 1 // … do something with the count … }
```

### linGetBusLoad

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBusLoad.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetBusLoad.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the busload of a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
double linGetBusLoad(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Busload in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { double value; value = linGetBusLoad(1, eAvgValue); // Average busload of channel 1 // … do something with the value, e.g.: if (value > 30.0) { // alert } }
```


---

### linGetDetectedRespErrorCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDetectedRespErrorCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDetectedRespErrorCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the total count of response errors detected by a response error bit on a LIN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetDetectedRespErrorCount(long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |


**Return Value:** Total count of response errors detected by response error bit, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long count; count = linGetDetectedRespErrorCount (1); // Detected response errors on channel 1 // … do something with the count … }
```


---

### linGetDiagNoRespCount

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDiagNoRespCount.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDiagNoRespCount.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the total count of unanswered Slave responses following a Master request of a LIN channel.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetDiagNoRespCount(long channel);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |


**Return Value:** Total count of unanswered Slave responses, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long count; count = linGetDiagNoRespCount (1); // Unanswered Slave responses on channel 1 // … do something with the count, e.g.: if (count > 10) { // alert } }
```

### linGetDurationHeader

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDurationHeader.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDurationHeader.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the duration of the frame header of a LIN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetDurationHeader(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Duration of the frame header in microseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { double value; value = linGetDurationHeader (1, eCurrValue); // Current header duration on channel 1 // … do something with the value, e.g.: … write("Header duration: %i [us]", value); }
```


---

### linGetDurationResp

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDurationResp.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDurationResp.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the duration of the frame response of a LIN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetDurationResp(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | LIN channel (1…64). |
| `selector` | Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average |


**Return Value:** Duration of the frame response in microseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { linEnableStatistics(1, 1); // Enable LIN statistics on channel 1 } void myStatisticFunc() { long value; value = linGetDurationResp (1, eCurrValue); // Current response duration on channel 1 // … do something with the value, e.g.: write("Response duration: %i [us]", value); }
```


### on linFrame

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINFrame.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linFrame is called on the receipt of a valid LIN frame. The keyword this and the selectors (see LIN: linFrame selectors ) can be used to access the data of the frame that has just been received. CAPL nodes are by default not transparent to LIN frames. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the Measurement Setup will block the data flow to its right side. To propagate the received frame to a next node in a chain a call to output(this) can be used. However, please note that such a call from a CANoe's Simulation Setup node may lead to a recursion. Note If for example a CAPL program contains the event procedures on linFrame 12 and on linFrame * , the procedure on linFrame 12 will be called up, 
 when a frame with the identifier 12 is received. For all other frames the procedure on linFrame * will be called up.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame MotorControl { writeLineEx(1, 1, "Entire frame transmission time: %d bit times", this.LIN_Fulltime); }
on linFrame 0x33 { writeLineEx(1, 1, "Frame header transmission time: %d bit times", this.LIN_HeaderTime); }
// Warning: This code causes recursion when called 
 from Simulation Setup node on linFrame * { this.RTR=1; output(this); }
```

> Display in Write Window the header transmission time for a frame ID=0x33 Propagate all frames to a next chain node


---

### on linBaudrateEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINBaudrateEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINBaudrateEvent.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linBaudrateEvent is called when the LIN hardware has successfully measured the baudrate of an external master or if the baudrate deviates by more than 0.5% from the last reported value. This only occurs if an external master is being used (in which case the LIN hardware is running in Slave mode). The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### on linDlcInfo

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINDlcInfo.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINDlcInfo.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linDlcInfo is called when the LIN hardware has successfully measured the length of 
 an unknown frame. This only occurs when the LIN hardware is in Slave mode. In Master mode, when no deviating values are specified by the database or CAPL functions, the LIN hardware assumes the frame length derived from 
 the frame identifier. The keyword this and the selectors 
 can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### on linLongDominantSignal

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINLongDominantSignal.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINLongDominantSignal.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linLongDominantSignal is called when the LIN hardware detects a long dominant signal on the bus (i.e. a dominant signal longer than a valid wakeup frame). The keyword this and the selectors (see Option .LIN: linLongDominantSignal selectors) can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### on linSchedulerModeChange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSchedulerModeChange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSchedulerModeChange.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linSchedulerModeChange is called when modeled LIN Master switches from the current schedule table to another one. This only occurs when the LIN hardware is in Master mode. The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### on linSleepModeEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSleepModeEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSleepModeEvent.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linSleepModeEvent is called when the wake status of the LIN hardware changes. The keyword this and the selectors 
 can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### on linSpikeEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSpikeEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSpikeEvent.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linSpikeEvent is called when the LIN hardware detects a spike on the bus (i.e. a short dominant signal that is too short to be a valid wakeup frame or to be accepted as a start bit and be reported by the UART). The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINWakeupFrame.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINWakeupFrame.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linWakeupFrame is called when the LIN hardware detects a wakeup signal on the bus. The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### on linCsError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINCsError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINCsError.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linCsError is called when a frame was transmitted without a failure, but with an incorrect checksum. The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'c' { int index; byte checksum; linFrame MotorControl frmMotorControl; // set data byte values and calculate resulting checksum for (index=0; index < frmMotorControl.DLC; ++index) { frmMotorControl.byte(index) = 0xFF; } // Set permanent CS error for the current frame linSetChecksumError(frmMotorControl.id, 
 1); // update response data frmMotorControl.RTR=0; output(frmMotorControl); // apply frame's header on the bus frmMotorControl.RTR=1; output(frmMotorControl); } ... on linCsError { int index; linFrame MotorControl frmMotorControl; // compare correct checksum and the received one if (this.ID == frmMotorControl.ID) { // set data byte values and calculate correct checksum for (index=0; index < frmMotorControl.DLC; ++index) { frmMotorControl.byte(index) = this.byte(index); } WriteLineEx(0,0,"received 
 checksum=%d", getChecksum(this)); WriteLineEx(0,0,"correct checksum=%d", getChecksum(frmMotorControl)); } }
```


---

### on linReceiveError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINReceiveError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINReceiveError.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linReceiveError is called when an error occurred during a frame transmission. The exact reason of the error is specified by lin_StateReason selector. The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
linFrame MotorControl frmMotorControl; ... // Simulate Receive Error by setting wrong response length if (linSetRespLength (frmMotorControl.ID, frmMotorControl.DLC) 
 != 0) { // from now on a receive error notified for the MotorControl frame ... } ... // Handle the error on linReceiveError { int state, reason; if (this.lin_ShortError != 0) { // The received data was too short. No additional info is available. ... } else { // extract identifier of LIN hardware state at the time the error has occurred state = this.lin_StateReason & 0x0F; // extract identifier of reason caused the error reason = this.lin_StateReason & 0xF0; ... } }
```


---

### on linSyncError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSyncError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINSyncError.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linSyncError is called when the LIN hardware was unable to become synchronized to an external master. The keyword this and the selectors can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### on linTransmError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINTransmError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/EventProcedures/CAPLfunctionOnLINTransmError.htm)

**Description:**

The event procedure on linTransmError is called when no Slave responded to a transmission request. The keyword this and the selectors (see Option .LIN: linTransmError selectors) can be used to access the data of the event that has just been received.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linTransmError { if (this.MsgChannel == 1 && this.ID == 0x33) { … } }
```


---

### linBits2Time_ns

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINBits2TimeNS.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINBits2TimeNS.htm)

**Description:**

Converts specified bit time to an absolute time. The absolute time is calculated using the current baudrate on the channel determined by the CAPL program context. Converts specified bit time to an absolute time. The absolute time is calculated using the current baudrate on the channel determined by the CAPL program context.


**Syntax:**

```c
int64 linBits2Time_ns(dword bitTimes);
```

```c
int64 linBits2Time_ns(dword channel, dword bitTimes);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bitTimes` | Time in bits. |
| `channel` | Channel number, whose baudrate will be used. |


**Return Value:** Absolute time in ns.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame * { int64 headerTimeInNanoSeconds; // time in ns headerTimeInNanoSeconds = linBits2Time_ns(this.LIN_HeaderTime); }
```

### linGetChecksum

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetChecksum.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetChecksum.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the checksum of a LIN frame or LIN checksum error. Checksum model (classic/enhanced) is determined automatically. Returns the checksum of a LIN frame or LIN checksum error. Checksum model (classic/enhanced) is determined automatically.


**Syntax:**

```c
byte linGetChecksum(linFrame linFrame); form 1
```

```c
byte linGetChecksum(linCSError linCsError); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `linFrame` | LIN frame object |
| `linCsError` | LIN checksum error object |


**Return Value:** Calculated checksum


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame myframe { byte checksum; checksum = linGetChecksum(this); } or // Calculate the checksum of a LIN frame object ... byte checksum; // create a LIN frame object linFrame 0x1 myLinFrame = { dlc = 1, byte(0) = 1 }; // calculate its checksum checksum = linGetChecksum(myLinFrame);
```

### linGetDlc

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDLC.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetDLC.htm)

**Description:**

Queries the Data Length Code (i.e. length in bytes) of a LIN frame. Per default the DLC of LIN frames is initialized according to the LIN Description File (LDF). If no LDF is used, this function returns the DLC initialized in on preStart for LIN frames sent by simulated LIN nodes. For LIN frames whose DLC has not been initialized, this function will return the default DLC for the selected ID as defined in the LIN1.1 protocol specification. For LIN frames sent by external LIN nodes, the measured DLC is returned. Queries the Data Length Code (i.e. length in bytes) of a LIN frame. Per default the DLC of LIN frames is initialized according to the LIN Description File (LDF). If no LDF is used, this function returns the DLC initialized in on preStart e.g. using linSetDlc , for LIN frames sent by simulated LIN nodes. For LIN frames whose DLC has not been initialized, this function will return the default DLC for the selected ID as defined in the LIN1.1 protocol specification. For LIN frames sent by external LIN nodes, the measured DLC is returned.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linGetDlc(long frameID);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63. |


**Return Value:** If successful the frame length [in bytes] or -1 on failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
... if ( linGetDLC(0x22) != 5) { linSetExpectedRespLength(0x22, 5); // set DLC of frame with identifier 0x22 to be 5 }
```


---

### linGetHWReceiveAccuracy

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetHWReceiveAccuracy.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetHWReceiveAccuracy.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to query the receive resolution of the LIN hardware in units of 1 Hz.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetHWReceiveAccuracy();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** Returns the receive resolution of the LIN hardware.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 't' { write("Transmit accuracy = %d", linGetHWTransmitAccuracy()); write("Receive  accuracy = %d", linGetHWReceiveAccuracy()); }
```


---

### linGetHWTransmitAccuracy

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetHWTransmitAccuracy.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetHWTransmitAccuracy.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to query the transmit resolution of the LIN hardware in units of 1 Hz.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetHWTransmitAccuracy();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** Returns the transmit resolution of the LIN hardware.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 't' { write("Transmit accuracy = %d", linGetHWTransmitAccuracy()); write("Receive  accuracy = %d", linGetHWReceiveAccuracy()); }
```

### linGetMeasBaudrate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasBaudrate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasBaudrate.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to retrieve results of a last baudrate measurement, which is done by calling linMeasHeaderBaudrate() or linMeasRespBaudrate() functions.


**Syntax:**

```c
float linGetMeasBaudrate();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** Returns the last measured baudrate [in bit/sec] or -1 on failure.


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
testcase tcMeasureHeaderBaudrate () { long waitResult, measBaudrate; // set request to measure baudrate according to Synch field of a LIN header linMeasHeaderBaudrate(0); // wait maximum 1000 [ms] for LIN header with identifier 0x33 waitResult = TestWaitForLinHeader(0x33, 1000); // declare failure if Wait has resumed not due to expected event if (1 != waitResult) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1","No LIN header 
 with ID=0x33 occurred during 1000 ms!"); } // retrieve measured baudrate measBaudrate = linGetMeasBaudrate(); if (-1 == measBaudrate) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1", "Failed to measure header baudrate!"); } TestStepPass("Test 1.1", "Header baudrate measurement done...");
```

> Test case for measuring baudrate using LIN header event. These test cases can only be used in the context of test module nodes.

*Example 2*

```c
testcase tcMeasureDatabyteBaudrate (int byteIndex) { long waitResult, measBaudrate; // set request to measure baudrate using the specified 
 byte of a frame with ID=0x33 linMeasRespBaudrate(0x33, byteIndex); // wait maximum 1000 [ms] for a frame with ID=0x33 waitResult = TestWaitForMessage(0x33, 1000); // declare failure if Wait has resumed not due to expected event if (1 != waitResult) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1","Expected frame has not occurred during 1000 ms!"); } // retrieve measured baudrate measBaudrate = linGetMeasBaudrate(); if (-1 == measBaudrate) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1", "Failed to measure response baudrate!"); } TestStepPass("Test 1.1", "Response baudrate measurement done..."); }
```

> Test case for measuring baudrate using certain data byte of a LIN frame. These test cases can only be used in the context of test module nodes.


---

### linGetMeasByteBitRates

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasByteBitRates.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasByteBitRates.htm)

**Description:**

This function calculates the bitrates for every byte which has been selected for edge time measurement. It uses the received data as well as the measured edge differences. Therefore, the function linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs must be called in advance. Note that for each measured byte a bitrate will be returned, although it might be 0 (this happens if there had been only one falling edge in the measured byte).


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetMeasByteBitRates(dword arrSize, float 
 byteBitRates[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `arrSize` | The size of the byteBitRates array. |
| `byteBitRates` | The array which will receive the measured byte bitrates. The unit of the measured bitrates is bits/second. |


**Return Value:** Returns the number of bitrates copied into the byteBitRates-array.


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase ByteBitRatesTest() { long indices[2] = { -2, -1 }; float byteBitRates[2]; dword numByteBitRates, i; char variableDesc[32]; // start a new edge difference measurement for the next frame if (linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(2, indices) == 0) { TestStepFail("ByteBitRatesTest", "Starting of edge measurement failed."); return; } // wait for any lin header if (testWaitForLinHeader(1000) != 1) { TestStepFail("ByteBitRatesTest", "No LIN header received within 1000 ms."); return; } // receive the measured bitrates numByteBitRates = linGetMeasByteBitRates(elCount(byteBitRates), byteBitRates); if (numByteBitRates != elCount(indices)) { TestStepFail("ByteBitRatesTest", "Received implausible number of bitrates."); return; } for (i = 0; i < numByteBitRates; i++) { snprintf(variableDesc, elCount(variableDesc), "Bitrate of byte %d", indices[i]); testCaseReportMeasuredValue(variableDesc, byteBitRates[i], "bit/s"); } TestStepPass("ByteBitRatesTest", "All byte bitrates have been received."); }
```


---

### linGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs.htm)

**Description:**

This function retrieves the result of a falling edge difference measurement which has been started 
 with linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs . Note that for each byte measured four time 
 differences will be returned, although all of them might be 0 (if there had been only one falling edge in the measured byte). This means that time differences 0 to 3 contain the values for the first measured byte, time differences 4 to 7 contain the values for the second measured byte, 
 etc. The results are sorted in ascending order by the indices of the measured bytes.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword arrSize, float 
 timeDiffs[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `arrSize` | The size of the timeDiffs array. |
| `timeDiffs` | The array which will receive the measured time differences. The unit of the measured times is nanoseconds. The first time difference of a byte always relates to the falling edge of the start bit. |


**Return Value:** Returns the number of time differences copied into the timeDiffs-array.


---

### linGetProtectedID

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetProtectedID.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetProtectedID.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to calculate protected ID for the corresponding LIN frame identifier (i.e. the frame identifier with parity bits).


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetProtectedID (long frameID);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | LIN frame identifier whose protected ID will be calculated. Value range: 0 .. 63 |


**Return Value:** Returns the calculated protected identifier or a value greater than 255 on failure.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame * { dword pid; pid = linGetProtectedID(this.ID); writeLineEx(0,0, "Protected ID for LIN frame identifier 0x%x is 0x%x", this.ID, pid);
```


---

### linGetResponseData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetResponseData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetResponseData.htm)

**Description:**

Queries LIN frame response data for specified FrameId on certain LIN channel. The FrameId and desired LIN channel number are expected to be set in the LIN frameprior to calling this function. The frame length, dataybte and checksum byte values will be copied into corresponding fields of LIN frame object. Note, that in the case there is no response data for the specified FrameId no data is copied. Queries LIN frame response data for specified FrameId on certain LIN channel. The FrameId and desired LIN channel number are expected to be set in the LIN frameprior to calling this function. The frame length, dataybte and checksum byte values will be copied into corresponding fields of LIN frame object. Note, that in the case there is no response data for the specified FrameId no data is copied.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword LinGetResponseData(linFrame frameObject);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameObject` | The data structure to be filled in. Note, that the following selectors have to be set prior to calling this function: ID MsgChannel |


**Return Value:** Non-zero on success. Zero on failure or when there is no response data defined. The non-zero value in real mode is the initial and in simulated mode the actual response counter value.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'a' { linFrame 0x1 testMsg; testMsg.MsgChannel = 1; testMsg.ID = 0; if (LINGetResponseData(testMsg) > 0) { writelineex(1,1,"FrameId=%d Length=%d, 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X 0x%X;", testMsg.ID,           testMsg.DLC, testMsg.byte(0), testMsg.byte(1), testMsg.byte(2), testMsg.byte(3), testMsg.byte(4), testMsg.byte(5), testMsg.byte(6), testMsg.byte(7)); } }
```


---

### linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasEdgeTimeDiffs.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasEdgeTimeDiffs.htm)

**Description:**

This function activates the measurement of the falling edges of the specified bytes in the next frame with the correct id if specified. If the first form is used (without specifying an id), or by specifying the id 0xff/-1 , the measurement takes place in the next frame. The measured values can be queried with linGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs . The bitrates of the measured bytes can be queried via linGetMeasByteBitRates .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword numIndices, long indices[]); // form 1
```

```c
dword linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword numIndices, long indices[], dword id); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `numIndices` | The number of specified byte indices. A pending measurement may be canceled by specifying numIndices=0 , before the frame to be measured occurs on the bus. A pending, uncompleted measurement must be canceled, before a new measurement can be activated. |
| `indices` | An array containing the indices of the bytes to be measured. Note that the minimum size for this array is numIndices . The array is sorted ascending internally. Therefore, the results of the measurement are sorted as well from the lowest to the highest byte index. Furthermore, duplicate entries are ignored. Index range: -2...N, where N = length [in bytes] of specified LIN frame -2: Sync field
-1: PID byte 0...N-1: Data bytes 1...N N: Checksum byte |
| `id` | The LIN identifier of the frame to be measured. Value range: 0..63 Note :
If the function is configured to measure header bytes (index < 0) on interfaces of the XL family, the measurement always takes place in the next frame regardless of the specified id. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// Test case for measuring edges of a number of data bytes of a LIN frame. // Note: This test case can only be used in the context of test module nodes testcase tcMeasureEdges(int byteIndex) { long indices[1]; float timeDiffs[4]; dword numDiffs; indices[0] = byteIndex; // 
 set request to measure the edges of the specified byte of the frame with ID=0x33 linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(1, indices, 0x33); // 
 wait maximum 1000 [ms] for frame with ID=0x33 waitResult = TestWaitForMessage(0x33, 1000); // declare failure if Wait has resumed not due to expected event if (1 != waitResult) { TestStepFail("Test1.1","Expected frame has not occurred during 1000 ms!"); } // retrieve measured edges numDiffs = LINGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(4, timeDiffs); if (numDiffs == 0) { TestStepFail("Test1.1", "Failed to measure edges!"); } else { TestStepPass("Test 1.1", "Edge measurement done..."); } }
```


---

### linMeasHeaderBaudrate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasHeaderBaudrate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasHeaderBaudrate.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to set request to measure the baudrate value for next LIN header event. Channel, where the baudrate is 
 measured, is determined by the CAPL program context. This function only initiates the baudrate measurement. The result becomes 
 available when the next LIN header occurs and it remains valid until next 
 measurement request. To retrieve the result the function linGetMeasBaudrate() shall be used. Note When LIN hardware is in Master mode calling this function will have no effect. The baudrate can only be measured if the specified byte (PID or Sync Byte) contains at least one "0"-bit followed by a "1"-bit. This means that bytes with the following values cannot be measured: 0x0   (binary: 00000000) 0x80 (binary: 10000000) 0xC0 (binary: 11000000) 0xE0 (binary: 11100000) 0xF0 (binary: 11110000) 0xF8 (binary: 11111000) 0xFC (binary: 11111100) 0xFE (binary: 11111110) 0xFF (binary: 11111111)


**Syntax:**

```c
long linMeasHeaderBaudrate(int index);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `index` | This parameter specifies a field of LIN header which will be used for baudrate measurement. 0: Synch field 1: Identifier field |


**Return Value:** On successful request returns zero, otherwise -1.


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcMeasureHeaderBaudrate () { long waitResult, measBaudrate; // set request to measure baudrate according to Synch 
 field of a LIN header linMeasHeaderBaudrate(0); // wait maximum 1000 [ms] for LIN header with identifier 0x33 waitResult = TestWaitForLinHeader(0x33, 1000); // declare failure if Wait has resumed not due to 
 expected event if (1 != waitResult) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1","No LIN header 
 with ID=0x33 occurred during 1000 ms!"); } // retrieve measured baudrate measBaudrate = linGetMeasBaudrate(); if (-1 == measBaudrate) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1", "Failed to measure header baudrate!"); } TestStepPass("Test 1.1", "Header baudrate measurement done..."); }
```


---

### linMeasRespBaudrate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasRespBaudrate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINMeasRespBaudrate.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to send a request to measure the baudrate value of a certain data byte of a certain LIN frame. The channel  where the baudrate is measured is determined by the CAPL program context. This function only initiates the baudrate measurement. The result becomes available when the next bus event for the specified ID occurs and it remains valid until the next measurement request. To retrieve the result the function linGetMeasBaudrate() shall be used. Note The baudrate can only be measured if the specified data byte contains at least one "0"-bit followed by a "1"-bit. This means that bytes with the following values cannot be measured: 0x0   (binary: 00000000) 0x80 (binary: 10000000) 0xC0 (binary: 11000000) 0xE0 (binary: 11100000) 0xF0 (binary: 11110000) 0xF8 (binary: 11111000) 0xFC (binary: 11111100) 0xFE (binary: 11111110) 0xFF (binary: 11111111)


**Syntax:**

```c
long linMeasRespBaudrate(long frameID, int index);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | LIN frame identifier whose baudrate will be measured. Value range: 0…63 |
| `index` | Data byte index. Value range: 0..N, where N = length [in bytes] of specified LIN frame [0..N-1]: data bytes 1..N N: checksum byte |


**Return Value:** On successful request returns zero, otherwise -1.


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase tcMeasureDatabyteBaudrate (int byteIndex) { long waitResult, measBaudrate; // set request to measure baudrate using the specified byte of a frame with ID=0x33 linMeasRespBaudrate(0x33, byteIndex); // wait maximum 1000 [ms] for a frame with ID=0x33 waitResult = TestWaitForMessage(0x33, 1000); // declare failure if Wait has resumed not due to expected event if (1 != waitResult) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1","Expected frame has not occurred during 1000 ms!"); } // retrieve measured baudrate measBaudrate = linGetMeasBaudrate(); if (-1 == measBaudrate) { TestStepFail("Test 1.1", "Failed to measure response baudrate!"); } TestStepPass("Test 1.1", "Response baudrate measurement done..."); }
```


---

### linResetStatistics

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetStatistics.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetStatistics.htm)

Resets LIN channel statistics. Note There are two ways to call this function:


|-----------|-------------|

### linSetBaudrateDetectionRange

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetBaudrateDetectionRange.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetBaudrateDetectionRange.htm)

 decreased. The default range is +/- 15%. Note that the detection range 
 will always be symmetrical.

```c
long linSetBaudrateDetectionRange(long rangeInPercent);
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `rangeInPercent` | The detection range in percent. Value range: 0-30 Default: 15 |


**Return Value:** If successful a value unequal to zero.


### linSetMaxHeaderLength

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetMaxHeaderLength.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetMaxHeaderLength.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the maximum header length in bit times.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linSetMaxHeaderLength(long bitTimes);
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bitTimes` | The new maximum header length in bit times. Headers longer than the maximum header length will be considered illegal. CANoe will not generate any response to these headers. Value range: 31-255 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

### linSetRespTolerance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespTolerance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespTolerance.htm)

Sets the response tolerance for a specified frame in percent.

```c
dword linSetRespTolerance(long frameId, long toleranceInPercent);
```

| Parameter | Description |

| `frameId` | The identifier of the frame for which the response tolerance shall be 
 set. |
| `toleranceInPercent` | The response tolerance to be used for the specified frame. Value range: 0-255 |
