### linTime2Bits_ns

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINTime2BitsNS.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINTime2BitsNS.htm)

**Description:**

This function converts specified system times to bit times. The conversion 
 is done using the  current 
 baudrate on the channel determined by the CAPL program context. Note Calling this function with the explicit channel is only possible from 
 a CAPL program defined in the Measurement Setup .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linTime2Bits_ns(int64 time): // form 1
```

```c
dword linTime2Bits_ns(dword channel, int64 time); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `time` | System time to be converted [in ns]. |
| `channel` | Channel number, whose baudrate will be used. |


**Return Value:** Resulting bit time.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame * { ..dword bitTimeSyncBreak; bitTimeSyncBreak = linTime2Bits_ns(this.breakLen); }
```


---

### linDeactivateBitInversion

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDeactivateBitInversion.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDeactivateBitInversion.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to cancel a previously activated bits inversion for LIN header or response. This function is useful when after calling linInvertHeaderBit() no header occurred yet on the bus or when after calling linInvertRespBit() no frame occurred yet.


**Syntax:**

```c
long LINDeactivateBitInversion();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** On success a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'd' { if (0==linDeactivateBitInversion()) { write("CAPL: Bit inversion deactivation failure!"); } }
```


---

### linDetectMultipleErrors

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDetectMultipleErrors.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDetectMultipleErrors.htm)

**Description:**

This function can be used to instruct LIN Hardware to enable/disable 
 detection of more than one error per a LIN frame. This function can be useful during tests of the stress functionality. 
 For example, an inversion of bits in a frame response may lead to a receive error and checksum error simultaneously. By default only one error per frame is detected, but with this function the detection of both errors can be activated.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linDetectMultipleErrors(long activate);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `activate` | 0: Disable 1: Enable |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linDisturbHeaderWithBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a bitstream.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linDisturbHeaderWithBitStream(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte bitStream[], dword numberOfBits, dword timeoutPrevention);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndex` | Start disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitStream` | The bitstream to be used for the interruption. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linDisturbHeaderWithHeader

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithHeader.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithHeader.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a new header (id=<disturbanceHeaderID>).


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linDisturbHeaderWithHeader(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, long disturbingFrameId);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndex` | Starts disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndex` | Starts disturbance at bit position <bitIndex>. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `disturbanceHeaderID` | Frame ID to be used for the header disturbance. Note This is not the ID of the header to be disturbed, but the ID of the header disturbance itself. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a variable bitstream.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndex` | Start disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitStream` | The bitstream to be used for the interruption. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `lengthInNS` | The length of each bit in nanoseconds. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. Note that while the dataBuffer-array will usually have a size of ceil (numberOfBits / 8), the size of lengthInNS will need to be at least numberOfBits . |
| `roundup` | If true, the lengths specified in lengthInNS will be rounded up to the next possible length that can be transmitted by the LIN hardware, otherwise the lengths will be rounded down. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linDisturbRespWithBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbRespWithBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINDisturbRespWithBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the specified response with a bitstream.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linDisturbRespWithBitStream(long disturbedFrameId, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte bitStream[], dword numberOfBits, dword timeoutPrevention);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `disturbedFrameID` | ID of the bus event to be disturbed. Value range: 0..63 |
| `byteIndex` | The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitStream` | The bitstream to be used for the interruption. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

**Description:**

Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the specified response with a new header (id=<disturbanceHeaderID>).

```c
dword linDisturbRespWithHeader(long disturbedFrameId, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, long disturbingFrameId);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `disturbedFrameID` | ID of the bus event to be disturbed. Value range: 0..63 |
| `byteIndex` | The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit where the interrupting header will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `disturbingFrameId` | The identifier of the header that will interrupt the specified header byte. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

| Parameter | Description |

| `byteIndex` | The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitStream` | The bitstream to be used for the interruption. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `lengthInNS` | The length of each bit in nanoseconds. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. Note that while the dataBuffer-array will usually have a size of ceil (numberOfBits / 8), the size of lengthInNS will need to be at least numberOfBits . |

| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. |

### linInvertHeaderBit

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertHeaderBit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertHeaderBit.htm)

**Description:**

Inverts the specified bit in the next LIN header.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, 
 dword level); // form 2
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, 
 dword level, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 3
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 4
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges, long disturbAfterHeaderID, dword waitForHeaders); // form 5
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndex` | Index of the byte. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not disturb the very first bit (i.e. the start bit) but the first data bit. Note that it is technically impossible to disturb the first bit of a dominant signal since by the time the falling edge has been seen, it is already to late to disturb that bit (i.e. you cannot undo the falling edge). This type of indexing means that in order to disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter you have to use the index <length of sync break> - 1 . I.e. in case of an 18 bit break, index 17 will disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter. Value range: 0..255 |
| `level` | Level of the disturbance 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) Note The default level used for the first type of the function is 0! |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive headers in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |
| `reportFallingEdges` | Flag indicating whether time stamps of the falling edges in the resulting pseudo-byte have to be reported. The time stamps can be retrieved by calling linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte . 0: Deactivate report 1: Activate report |
| `disturbAfterHeaderID` | With this parameter and the next one it is possible to define exactly which header will be disturbed. The LIN hardware will first wait for a header with ID = disturbAfterHeaderID before additionally awaiting the number of headers defined by waitForHeaders . The next header following these headers will then be disturbed. For example: To disturb the next header directly after a header with the ID=5, set the disturbAfterHeaderID parameter to 5 and the waitForHeaders to 0. |
| `waitForHeaders` | See explanation for disturbAfterHeaderID . |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'i' { if (0!=linInvertHeaderBit(0, 0, 0)) { // for the next LIN header invert first bit (it's the recessive one) of the Sync byte } }
```


---

### linInvertHeaderBitEx

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertHeaderBitEx.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertHeaderBitEx.htm)

**Description:**

Inverts the specified number of 1/16 th bits at the specified position in the next LIN header. Note Partial bit disturbances require the activation of the flash mode in order to get the edges of the disturbance fast enough. Call linActivateFlashMode for that purpose.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 2
```

```c
dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges, long disturbAfterHeaderID, dword waitForHeaders); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndex` | Index of the byte. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not disturb the very first bit (i.e. the start bit) but the first data bit. Note that it is technically impossible to disturb the first bit of a dominant signal since by the time the falling edge has been seen, it is already to late to disturb that bit (i.e. you cannot undo the falling edge). This type of indexing means that in order to disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter you have to use the index <length of sync break> - 1 . I.e. in case of an 18 bit break, index 17 will disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitOffsetInSixteenthBits` | The offset in 1/16 th bits into the bit specified in bitIndex . Value range: 0..15 |
| `distLengthInSixteenthBits` | The length of the disturbance in units of 1/16 th bit. Value range: 0..65535 |
| `Level` | Level of the disturbance. 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive headers in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |
| `reportFallingEdges` | Flag indicating whether time stamps of the falling edges in the resulting pseudo-byte have to be reported. The time stamps can be retrieved by calling linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte . 0: Deactivate report |
| `disturbAfterHeaderID` | With this parameter and the next one it is possible to define exactly which header will be disturbed. The LIN hardware will first wait for a header with ID = disturbAfterHeaderID before additionally awaiting the number of headers defined by waitForHeaders . The next header following these headers will then be disturbed. For example: To disturb the next header directly after a header with the ID=5, set the disturbAfterHeaderID parameter to 5 and the waitForHeaders to 0. |
| `waitForHeaders` | See explanation for disturbAfterHeaderID . |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'i' { if (0!=linInvertHeaderBitEx(0, 0, 8, 8, 0)) { // for the next LIN header invert the second half of the first bit (it's the recessive one) of the Sync byte } }
```


---

### linInvertMultipleHeaderBits

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertMultipleHeaderBits.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertMultipleHeaderBits.htm)

**Description:**

Inverts the multiple bits in specified locations in the next LIN header. Note Some driver versions only support inverting multiple (consecutive) bits in one position (arrSize == 1).


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 2
```

```c
dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize, dword numberOfExecutions, long disturbAfterHeaderID, dword waitForHeaders); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `byteIndices` | An array of the index of the bytes. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte |
| `bitIndices` | The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not disturb the very first bit (i.e. the start bit) but the first data bit. Note that it is technically impossible to disturb the first bit of a dominant signal since by the time the falling edge has been seen, it is already to late to disturb that bit (i.e. you cannot undo the falling edge). This type of indexing means that in order to disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter you have to use the index <length of sync break> - 1 . I.e. in case of an 18 bit break, index 17 will disturb the first bit in the sync delimiter. Value range: 0..255 |
| `numberOfDisturbedBits` | An array specifying how many consecutive bits shall be inverted at the specified locations. |
| `level` | An array of the levels of the disturbance 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `arrSize` | The number of elements in the above arrays. This value should not be greater than the size of the smallest array. |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive headers in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |
| `disturbAfterHeaderID` | With this parameter and the next one it is possible to define exactly which header will be disturbed. The LIN hardware will first wait for a header with ID = disturbAfterHeaderID before additionally awaiting the number of headers defined by waitForHeaders . The next header following these headers will then be disturbed. For example: To disturb the next header directly after a header with the ID=5, set the < disturbAfterHeaderID > parameter to 5 and the < waitForHeaders > to 0. |
| `waitForHeaders` | See explanation for disturbAfterHeaderID . |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'i' { dword byteIndices[2] = { 0, 1}; // invert in sync field and in id-byte dword bitIndices[2]   = { 0, 0 }; // invert the first data bits in each byte dword numberOfDisturbedBits[2] = { 1, 2 }; // invert 1 bit in sync field, 2 bits in id-byte ,dword levels[2] = { 0, 1 }; // push sync field bit to dominant, id-byte bits to recessive if (0!=linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(byteIndices, bitIndices, numberOfDisturbedBits, levels, 2)) { } }
```


---

### linInvertMultipleRespBits

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertMultipleRespBits.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertMultipleRespBits.htm)

**Description:**

Inverts the multiple bits in specified locations in the next LIN header. Note Please note hardware-related restrictions .


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertMultipleRespBits (long frameID, dword byteIndices[],dword 
 bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[], dword levels[], dword arrSize); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertMultipleRespBits (long frameID, dword byteIndices[],dword 
 bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[], dword levels[], dword arrSize, 
 dword numberOfExecutions); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | ID of the bus event to be manipulated. Value range: 0..63 |
| `byteIndices` | An array of the indices of the bytes to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). If an index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length |
| `bitIndices` | An array of the indices of the bits to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `numberOfDisturbedBits` | An array specifying how many consecutive bits shall be inverted at the 
 specified locations. |
| `levels` | An array of the levels of the disturbance 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `arrSize` | The number of elements in the above arrays. This value should not be greater than the size of the smallest array. |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive frames with the specified identifier in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// Invert response bits on keyboard event on key 'i' { dword byteIndices[2] = { 1, 5 }; dword bitIndices[2]  =  { 0, 1 
 }; dword numberOfDisturbedBits[2] = { 1, 2 }; dword levels[2] = { 0, 1 }; ... // Invert first bit 0 of byte 1 to dominant and bits 1 and 2 // (numberOfDisturbedBits[1] is 2) of byte 5 to recessive linInvertMultipleRespBits(0x33, byteIndices, bitIndices, numberOfDisturbedBits, levels, 2); ... }
```


---

### linInvertRespBit

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertRespBit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertRespBit.htm)

**Description:**

This function inverts the specified bit when the next bus event for 
 the specified ID occurs, provided that the bus event contains the specified data byte.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, 
 dword bitIndex); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, 
 dword bitIndex, dword level); // form 2
```

```c
word linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, 
 dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 3
```

```c
dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | ID of the bus event to be manipulated. Value range: 0..63 |
| `byteIndex` | The index of the byte to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). 
 If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `level` | Level of the disturbance 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive frames with the specified identifier in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |
| `eportFallingEdges` | Flag indicating whether time stamps of the falling edges in the resulting pseudo-byte have to be reported. The time stamps can be retrieved by calling linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte . 0: Deactivate report 1: Activate report |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'i' { ... // Invert first bit of byte field 8 for LIN frame 
 with ID=0x33 linInvertRespBit(0x33, 7, 0); ... // Invert bit 7 of checksum byte field for LIN frame 
 with ID=0x33 linInvertRespBit(0x33, 8, 6); ... // Invert stop bit of byte field 8 for LIN frame 
 with ID=0x33 linInvertRespBit(0x33, 7, 8); ... }
```


---

### linInvertRespBitEx

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertRespBitEx.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINInvertRespBitEx.htm)

**Description:**

Inverts the specified number of 1/16 th bits at the specified position in the next LIN frame's response with the specified frame ID. Note Partial bit disturbances require the activation of the flash mode in order to get the edges of the disturbance fast enough. Call linActivateFlashMode for that purpose.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linInvertRespBitEx(long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level); // form 1
```

```c
dword linInvertRespBitEx(long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameID` | ID of the bus event to be manipulated.
Value range: 0..63 |
| `byteIndex` | The index of the byte to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value Range: 0..N: where N is the frame length |
| `bitIndex` | The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 |
| `bitOffsetInSixteenthBits` | The offset in 1/16 th bits into the bit specified in bitIndex . Value range: 0..15 |
| `distLengthInSixteenthBits` | The length of the disturbance in units of 1/16 th bit. Value range: 0..65535 |
| `Level` | Level of the disturbance. 0: Dominant (inverts recessive bit) 1: Recessive (inverts dominant bit – requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `numberOfExecutions` | The number of consecutive frames with the specified identifier in which the bit inversion will be executed. Default: 1 (single shot). The largest possible value is 255. If a larger value is given, 255 will be used. |
| `reportFallingEdges` | Flag indicating whether time stamps of the falling edges in the resulting pseudo-byte have to be reported. The time stamps can be retrieved by calling linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte . 0: Deactivate report |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'i' { if (0!=linInvertRespBitEx(42, 1, 0, 8, 8, 0)) { // in the next response of lin frame 42 invert the second half of the first bit of the first data byte (the bit has to be recessive) } }
```

### linSetGlobalTimeoutPrevention

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetGlobalTimeoutPrevention.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetGlobalTimeoutPrevention.htm)

**Description:**

Activates/deactivates the global timeout prevention for transceivers with a dominant timeout. If active, the cab/piggy will use an inbuilt transistor to keep the bus signal at a dominant level after the dominant timeout stopped the transceiver from transmitting a dominant signal.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linSetGlobalTimeoutPrevention(dword on);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `on` | 0: Disable 1: Enable |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linSetRespDisturbance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespDisturbance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespDisturbance.htm)

**Description:**

Activates a disturbance in the response space of the specified LIN frame. 
 A normal response by a simulated slave will be deactivated as long as 
 the disturbance is active. Note The disturbance starts with the next occurrence of the specified frame. 
 To stop the disturbance the functions linResetRespDisturbance() shall be used.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetRespDisturbance (long frameId, long lengthInBits, 
 long level, long offsetInSixteenthBits);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameId` | LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63. |
| `lengthInBits` | The duration of the disturbance [in bit times]. |
| `level` | The level of the disturbance. 0: dominant disturbance  (inverts 
 recessive bits) 1: recessive disturbance (inverts dominant bits - requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |
| `offsetInSixteenthBits` | The offset [in 1/16th bits] of the disturbance relative to the end of the header, i.e. to the PID byte. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'c' { linFrame Motor1State_Cycl mMotor1State_Cycl; if (0!=linSetRespDisturbance (mMotor1State_Cycl.id, 
 1, 0, 20)) { // for the next the first bit of the response space 
 or of the first databyte // (if the response space is zero) is inverted from recessive to dominant } }
```

### linStartDisturbance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINStartDisturbance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINStartDisturbance.htm)

Starts a disturbance on the bus. Note that disturbances shorter than 64 bit times

```c
long linStartDisturbance(dword length, dword level);
```

| `length` | Length of the disturbance in bit times. -1: permanent disturbance |
| `level` | The level of the disturbance. 0: dominant disturbance (inverts 
 recessive bits) 1: recessive disturbance (inverts dominant bits - requires mag-Cab/Piggyback and external power supply) |


**Return Value:** On success a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linStopDisturbance

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINStopDisturbance.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINStopDisturbance.htm)

Stops the disturbance on the bus which is started with linStartDisturbance() . Note that disturbances shorter than 64 bit times cannot be stopped.

```c
long linStopDisturbance();
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |

### linActivateFlashMode

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINActivateFlashMode.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINActivateFlashMode.htm)

**Description:**

This function activates flash mode on high-speed capable transceivers. Note that in flash mode such a transceiver will use faster rising and falling edges and will disregard the EMC limitations of the LIN network. Note also, that the activation of the flash mode cannot be done while the channel is transmitting or the scheduler is running.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linActivateFlashMode(byte activate);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `activate` | 1 will activate the flash mode, 0 will deactivated it. |


**Return Value:** On successful request returns 1, otherwise 0.


**Example(s):**

```c
// test case for disturbing parts of a bit // note, that test cases can only be used in the context of test module nodes testcase tcDisturbPartialBit() { dword flashModeActive; flashModeActive = 0; do { if (!linActivateFlashMode(1)) // request activation of flash mode { break; // if the request has been denied, either the cab/piggy is incapable of // flash mode or the scheduler is still running } testWaitForTimeout(10); // give the hardware time to activate the flash mode flashModeActive = linIsFlashModeActive(); // check if flash mode has been // activated successfully } while (!flashModeActive); if (!flashModeActive) { testStepFail("tcDisturbPartialBit", "Flash mode could not be activated because of active scheduler or because the cab/piggy does not support flash mode."); return; } linInvertRespBitEx(0, 0, 8, 4, 8, 0); // invert the middle part of the stop bit of the // first byte of the response to id 0 ... }
```


---

### linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to retrieve time stamps of all falling edges in the disturbed byte or in the pseudo-byte caused by the last bit inversion. Note, that prior to calling this function the measurement of the falling edges has to be activated and the bit inversion has to be executed (see linInvertRespBit , linInvertHeaderBit )


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte(int64 fallingEdges[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `fallingEdges` | An array which will receive the measured time stamps [in nanoseconds]. The array size is 6. Unused time stamps will be set to 0 (i.e. in case of less than 6 falling edges). |


**Return Value:** On successful request returns 1, otherwise 0.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linIsBitStreamBusy

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINisBitStreamBusy.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINisBitStreamBusy.htm)

**Description:**

Queries the current LIN bitstream sending status.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linIsBitStreamBusy (); // form 1
```

```c
dword linIsBitStreamBusy (dword channel); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `channel` | The LIN channel number which will be queried. |


**Return Value:** Returns 1 if a bitstream is currently being sent, otherwise 0 .


**Example(s):**

```c
// Make sure that there is no bitstream sending active before a new transmission is started if (linIsBitStreamBusy() == 0) { linSendBitStream(data, numberOfBits); }
```


---

### linIsFlashModeActive

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINIsFlashModeActive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINIsFlashModeActive.htm)

**Description:**

This function reports the flash mode state on high-speed capable transceivers.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linIsFlashModeActive();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Return Value:** Returns 1 if flash mode is active, otherwise 0.


**Example(s):**

```c
// test case for disturbing parts of a bit // note, that test cases can only be used in the context of test module nodes testcase tcDisturbPartialBit() { dword flashModeActive; flashModeActive = 0; do { if (!linActivateFlashMode(1)) // request activation of flash mode { break; // if the request has been denied, either the cab/piggy is incapable of // flash mode or the scheduler is still running } testWaitForTimeout(10); // give the hardware time to activate the flash mode flashModeActive = linIsFlashModeActive(); // check if flash mode has been // activated successfully } while (!flashModeActive); if (!flashModeActive) { testStepFail("tcDisturbPartialBit", "Flash mode could not be activated because of active scheduler or because the cab/piggy does not support flash mode."); return; } linInvertRespBitEx(0, 0, 8, 4, 8, 0); // invert the middle part of the stop bit of the // first byte of the response to id 0 ... }
```


---

### linResetManualChecksum

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetManualChecksum.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetManualChecksum.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the correct checksum of a LIN frame, whose checksum has been changed by using linSetManualChecksum() function. The checksum is calculated using the frame data.


**Syntax:**

```c
void linResetManualChecksum(linFrame linFrame);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `linFrame` | LIN frame for which the correct checksum will be used again. |


**Example(s):**

```c
... linFrame MotorControl frmMotorControl; linResetManualChecksum(frmMotorControl); output(frmMotorControl); // it is important to call output() to make the changes active
```


---

### linResetRespBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetRespBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINResetRespBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Deactivates the bitstream response of the specified frame.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linResetRespBitStream (long frameId);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameId` | LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### linSendBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Sends an arbitrary bitstream on the LIN bus.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSendBitStream(byte bitStream[], long numberOfBits); // form 1
```

```c
long linSendBitStream(byte bitStream[], long numberOfBits, long timeoutPrevention); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `bitStream` | The bitstream to be sent. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. |


**Return Value:** On success a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
int SendSpecialHeader(int breakLength, int delimiterLength, byte syncByte, byte idByte) { byte data[25]; int headerLength; int currentPosition; int i; // Header consists of Break, Sync and PID bytes headerLength = breakLength + delimiterLength + 20; if (headerLength > 200) { return 0; // maximum is exceeded } currentPosition = 0; for (i = 0; i < 25; ++i) { data[i] = 0xff; // initialize } for (i = breakLength; i > 0;) // set sync break { if (i >= 8) { data[currentPosition >> 3] = 0; i -= 8; currentPosition += 8; } else { data[currentPosition >> 3] &= ~(1 << 
 (currentPosition & 0x7)); --i; ++currentPosition; } } currentPosition += delimiterLength; // set synch delimiter // set sync byte data[currentPosition >> 3] &= ~(1 << (currentPosition & 0x7)); // start bit ++currentPosition; for (i = 0; i < 8; ++i) { if ((syncByte & (1 << i)) == 0) // dominant bit in syncByte { data[currentPosition >> 3] &= ~(1 << (currentPosition & 0x7)); } ++currentPosition; } ++currentPosition; // set recessive stop bit // set protected id data[currentPosition >> 3] &= ~(1 << (currentPosition & 0x7)); // start bit ++currentPosition; for (i = 0; i < 8; ++i) { if ((idByte & (1 << i)) == 0) // dominant bit in idByte { data[currentPosition >> 3] &= ~(1 << (currentPosition & 0x7)); } ++currentPosition; } ++currentPosition; // set recessive stop bit linSendBitStream(data, headerLength); // send created header return headerLength; }
```


---

### linSendHeaderError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendHeaderError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendHeaderError.htm)

**Description:**

This function sends a header with the current sync break, sync delimiter and interbyte space settings and the specified sync byte and id byte. Note If the LIN hardware is not in Master mode calling this function will have no effect.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword linSendHeaderError(byte syncByte, byte idWithParity, byte StopAfterError);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `syncByte` | Data value to be sent in the Synch Byte field. Correct value is 0x55 Value range: 0..0xFF |
| `idWithParity` | Data value to be sent in the Protected Identifier field. The lower 6 bits specify the identifier to be used. The upper 2 bits specify the identifier parity to be used. The correct value can be calculated using the linGetProtectedID function. Value range: 0..0xFF |
| `StopAfterError` | Specifies whether the transmission should be stopped after an error. If an incorrect synch byte is set by this function and StopAfterError is 1, the identifier will not be sent. A simulated message response (has to be activated independently) will generally not be sent if there is an error in the header. Value range: 0..1 |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// Force an error in header of LIN frame with ID=0x33 by setting wrong protected ID on key 'h' { byte linID, protectedID, corParity, errParity, errPID; // calculate protected ID with wrong parity bits linID = 0x33; // use frame ID=0x33 protectedID = linGetProtectedID(linID); // get protected ID corParity = (protectedID & 0xC0) >> 6; // extract parity (0xC=0=11000000) errParity = (corParity ^ 0x2) & 0x3; // calculate wrong parity using XOR errPID = linID | (errParity << 6); // calculate PID with wrong parity linSendHeaderError(0x55, errPID, 0); } ... or ... // Force an error in header of LIN frame with ID=0x33 by setting wrong Synch byte linSendHeaderError(0x50, linGetProtectedID(0x33), 0);
```


---

### linSendSamplingTestHeader

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendSamplingTestHeader.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendSamplingTestHeader.htm)

**Description:**

Sends a slave response header with the start bit of the id byte set 
 to the specified length.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSendSamplingTestHeader(dword startBitLengthInMicSec);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `startBitLengthInMicSec` | The length of the start bit in microseconds. The first data bit will be adjusted to ensure that the start bit and the first data bit have a total length of two bit times. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### linSendVariableBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendVariableBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSendVariableBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Sends an arbitrary bitstream with bits of variable length on the LIN bus.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSendVariableBitStream(byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp); // form 1
```

```c
long linSendVariableBitStream(byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention) // form 2
```

```c
long linSendVariableBitStream(byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention, dword interruptingTx) // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `dataBuffer` | The bitstream to be sent. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `lengthInNS` | The length of each bit in nanoseconds. |
| `numberOfBits` | The number of bits in the bitStream-array. Note that while the dataBuffer-array will usually have a size of ceil(numberOfBits / 8), the size of lengthInNS will need to be at least numberOfBits |
| `roundUp` | If true, the lengths specified in lengthInNS will be rounded up to the next possible length that can be transmitted by the LIN hardware, otherwise the lengths will be rounded down. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. |
| `interruptingTx` | When activated (= 1) the transmission of the bitstream starts immediately without waiting for the bus to be free. Note that this mode may disturb a running transmission. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// This function sends a disturbance of 1 second, followed by a pause of 1 second, // followed by a disturbance of 7 seconds using the variable bitstream functionality void SendDisturbances() { byte data[1] = { 2 }; int64 lengthInNS[3] = { 1000000000LL, 1000000000LL, 7000000000LL }; linSendVariableBitStream(data, lengthInNS, 3, 1, 1);  byte data[25]; }
```


---

### linSetBaudrate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetBaudrate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetBaudrate.htm)

**Description:**

With this function it is possible to change the baudrate during the measurement. It is also possible to activate automatic baudrate detection in a specified range. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
void linSetBaudrate(long baudrate); // form 1
```

```c
Void linSetBaudrate(long minBaudrate, long maxBaudrate); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `baudrate` | Baudrate to be set [in bit/sec]. Value range: 200 Baud – 30500 Baud |
| `minBaudrate` | The lower border of the automatic baudrate detection range. Value range: 200 Baud – maxBaudrate |
| `maxBaudrate` | The upper border of the automatic baudrate detection range. Value range: minBaudrate – 30500 Baud |


**Example(s):**

```c
on key 'w' { linSetBaudrate(9600);  // change baudrate to 9600 bit/sec }
```


---

### linSetChecksumError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetChecksumError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetChecksumError.htm)

**Description:**

Sets/resets a checksum error of a LIN frame. The activated checksum error is then constant and will not be affected by changes in the frame data.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetChecksumError(long frameId, long activate);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameId` | LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63. |
| `activate` | 0: deactivate checksum error 1: activate checksum error |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
on linFrame * { linSetChecksumError(this.id, 1); }
```


---

### linSetManualChecksum

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetManualChecksum.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetManualChecksum.htm)

**Description:**

Sets a checksum (that is usually not a correct one) for a LIN frame.


**Syntax:**

```c
void linSetManualChecksum(linFrame linFrame, byte csValue);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `linFrame` | LIN frame for which a checksum will be set. |
| `csValue` | The checksum value to be set. |


**Example(s):**

```c
... linFrame MotorControl frmMotorControl; linSetManualChecksum(frmMotorControl, 0x1A); output(frmMotorControl); // it is important to call output() to make the changes active
```


---

### linSetRespBitStream

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespBitStream.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINSetRespBitStream.htm)

**Description:**

Sets up a bitstream as the response to the specified frame.


**Syntax:**

```c
long linSetRespBitStream (long frameId, byte bitStream[], 
 long numberOfBits); // form 1
```

```c
long linSetRespBitStream (long frameId, byte bitStream[], 
 long numberOfBits, long timeoutPrevention); // form 2
```

```c
long linSetRespBitStream (long frameId, byte bitStream[], long numberOfBits, long timeoutPrevention, long count); // form 3
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `frameId` | LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63 |
| `bitStream` | The bitstream array to be sent as a response. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1. |
| `numberOfBits` | Number of bits in the bitStream array to be sent. |
| `timeoutPrevention` | 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259-transceiver. |
| `count` | Number of times, the bitstream shall be sent as a response before deactivating itself. |


**Return Value:** On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.


**Example(s):**

```c
// The response consists of 8 data bytes and checksum 
 byte. There are no interbyte spaces. // Response: [D0=0x1, D1=0x2, D2=0x4, D3=0x8, D4=0x10, D5=0x20, D6=0x40, D7=0x80, CS=0x52] on key 'r' { linFrame MotorControl mMotorControl; byte bitStream[12]; long numberOfBits; // binary stream = 0100000001 0010000001 0001000001 0000100001 0000010001 // binary stream (continue) = 0000001001 0000000101 0000000011 0010010101 bitStream[0] = 0x2; bitStream[1] = 0x12; bitStream[2] = 0x88; bitStream[3] = 0x20; bitStream[4] = 0x84; bitStream[5] = 0x20; bitStream[6] = 0x2; bitStream[7] = 0x9; bitStream[8] = 0x28; bitStream[9] = 0xC0; bitStream[10] = 0xA4; bitStream[11] = 0x2; numberOfBits = 90; // Each byte requires 10 bits (start bit + data bits + stop bit) if (0!=linSetRespBitStream(mMotorControl.id, bitStream, numberOfBits)) { // for the next response of frame "MotorControl" is as specified } }
```


---

### LINtp_ActivateFCTransmission

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpActivateFCTransmission.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpActivateFCTransmission.htm)

**Description:**

Activate the usage of FlowControl frames. These frames will be indicated in the optional callback LINtp_FCPreTransmit before sending.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_ActivateFCTransmission (long blockSize);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `blockSize` | <0: Stops sending FlowControl frames [0, 255]		Sends FlowControl frames with this block size Other		reserved |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### LINtp_DataReq

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataReq.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataReq.htm)

**Description:**

Send the given data. Once all data has been sent, the callback LINtp_DataCon is called. If there has been an error, LINtp_ErrorInd is called.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_DataReq (byte data[], long numData, long NAD);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `data` | Data to send. |
| `numData` | Number of bytes to send. |
| `NAD` | Slave node address |


**Example(s):**

```c
BYTE data[20]; LINtp_DataReq( data, elcount(data), 0x12);
```


---

### LINtp_GetRxData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpGetRxData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpGetRxData.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieve data bytes received.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_GetRxData (byte data[], long size);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `data` | Copy bytes into this buffer. |
| `size` | Maximum number of bytes to copy. |


**Example(s):**

```c
LINtp_DataInd(long count, DWORD nad) { byte rxBuffer[4096]; LINtp_GetRxData(rxBuffer, count); write( "received <%02x ...> from/for node %02x" , rxBuffer[0], nad); }
```


---

### LINtp_InitAsMaster

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpInitAsMaster.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpInitAsMaster.htm)

**Description:**

Can only be called in on prestart . Normally the LINtp implementation will detect whether it runs in the LIN master node automatically. But if the LINtp.DLL shall be able to send diagnostics request from a node that is not the master, e.g. a test module, this is not the case. The automatic detection would assume the slave role. Calling this function in the on prestart phase will initialize the transport protocol in master role, i.e. sending of diagnostics requests on TP level is possible.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_InitAsMaster ();
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `—` |  |


**Example(s):**

```c
on preStart { if( cIsTester) LINtp_InitAsMaster(); }
```


---

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetFillByteValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetFillByteValue.htm)

**Description:**

Sets fill byte used to pad frames.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_SetFillByteValue (long fillByte);
```

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `fillByte` | This value will be used to fill up the frames [0, 255]. |

```c
—
```

### LINtp_SetMaximumReceiveLength

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetMaximumReceiveLength.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetMaximumReceiveLength.htm)

**Description:**

Sets maximum number of bytes node can receive. If more data is indicated in a FirstFrame and FlowControl frames have been activated, an overflow FlowControl frame is sent. Note Note that only a maximum of 4095 bytes can be sent using LINtp.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_SetMaximumReceiveLength (dword maxRecLen);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `maxRcLen` | The maximum number of bytes of segmented data accepted. |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### LINtp_SetNAD

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetNAD.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetNAD.htm)

**Description:**

In a slave node, sets its node address for communication with the master.


**Syntax:**

```c
long LINtp_SetNAD (long NAD);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `NAD` | Node address [1, 126] |


**Return Value:** 0 Setting address failed (this is a master or node address is invalid) 1 Success other reserved


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### LINtp_SetSTmin

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetSTmin.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetSTmin.htm)

**Description:**

Sets minimum separation time to use.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_SetSTmin (dword STmin_ms);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `STmin_ms` | [0, 127]		Separation time in milliseconds other:		127 ms will be used |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### LINtp_SetWaiting

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetWaiting.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpSetWaiting.htm)

**Description:**

Sets ISO TP waiting state for the node. When waiting has been activated the node will send FlowControl.Wait frames instead of ConsecutiveFrames, if sending of FlowControl frames has been activated.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_SetWaiting (long isWaiting);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `isWaiting` | 0:	Deactivate waiting 1:	Activate waiting other:	reserved |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### LINtp_DataCon

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataCon.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataCon.htm)

**Description:**

This CAPL callback confirms the number of data bytes successfully sent.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_DataCon (long count);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `count` | Number of bytes sent. |


**Example(s):**

```c
void LINtp_DataCon(long count) { write( "sent %d byte", count); }
```


---

### LINtp_DataInd

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataInd.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpDataInd.htm)

**Description:**

Callback indicates the reception of data. In a Slave node the NAD indicates the address of the destination node or 0x7E if data was sent to a functional group, in a Master the address of the sending slave. Use LINtp_GetRxData to retrieve the data.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_DataInd (long count); // form 1
```

```c
void LINtp_DataInd (long count, dword NAD); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `count` | Number of bytes received. |
| `NAD` | Node address of the slave node that is the sender (if called in the master node) or receiver of the data (if called in a slave node). |


**Example(s):**

```c
// Before CANoe 8.5, use this signature: // LINtp_DataInd(long count) { ... } // Only in CANoe 8.5 or later LINtp_DataInd(long count, DWORD nad) { byte rxBuffer[4096]; LINtp_GetRxData(rxBuffer, count); if( nad == 0x7E) write( "received functional request of %d byte", count); else write( "received %d bytes from/for node %02x", count, nad); }
```


---

### LINtp_ErrorInd

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpErrorInd.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpErrorInd.htm)

**Description:**

Callback in case of a LIN-TP error.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_ErrorInd (int errorcode);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `errorcode` | LIN-TP transport protocol error code. |


**Example(s):**

```c
/*@@caplFunc:LINtp_ErrorInd(int):*///callback LINtp_ErrorInd(int error) { } /*@@end*/
```


---

### LINtp_FCPreTransmit

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpFCPreTransmit.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpFCPreTransmit.htm)

**Description:**

When the usage of FlowControl frames has been activated with LINtp_ActivateFCTransmission , each FlowControl frame is forwarded to this callback function before it is sent.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_FCPreTransmit (byte fcData[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `fcData` | Data of the FC frame. |


**Example(s):**

```c
void LINtp_FCPreTransmit (byte fcData[]) { write( "FlowControl to be sent: %02x %02x %02x %02x (%d byte)" , fcData[0] , fcData[1] , fcData[2] , fcData[3], elcount(fcData)); }
```


---

### LINtp_FirstFrameIndication

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpFirstFrameIndication.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpFirstFrameIndication.htm)

**Description:**

Indicates the start of a segmented reception.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_FirstFrameIndication (long sender, long receiver, long announcedLen);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `sender` | Address of sender node, or 0 for master. |
| `receiver` | Address of receiver node, or 0 for master. |
| `announcedLen` | Number of bytes to be expected. |


**Example(s):**

```c
LINtp_FirstFrameIndication(long sender, long receiver, long announcedLen) { write( "Start reception of %d bytes", announcedLen); }
```


---

### LINtp_PreSend

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpPreSend.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/LIN/Functions/CAPLfunctionLINtpPreSend.htm)

**Description:**

The callback function is called before a LIN message is sent with the provided data.


**Syntax:**

```c
void LINtp_PreSend (byte data[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `data` | Message data |


**Example(s):**

```c
LINtp_PreSend(byte data[]) { // ... // change sequence number of the consecutive frame data[1] = data[1] + 1; // ... }
```
