### sysGetVariableSVType

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionsysGetVariableSVType.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionsysGetVariableSVType.htm)

**Description:**

Gets the type of a system variable encoded as a long integer.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysGetVariableSVType(sysvar);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `sysvar` | A value of type system variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Invalid 1 Double (64 Bit) 2 Integer (32 Bit) 3 String 4 Double Array 5 Integer Array 6 Integer (64 Bit) 7 Data


**Example(s):**

```c
long t1, t2; t1 = sysGetVariableSVType(lookupSysvar("mysysvar")); t2 = sysGetVariableSVType(sysvar::mysysvar);
```


---

### sysGetVariableTimeNS

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableTimeNS.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableTimeNS.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the time stamp of the last update to the variable value. Note Direct access to values from system variables .


**Syntax:**

```c
int64 sysGetVariableTimeNS(SysVarName);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by " :: ". The name must be preceded by " sysVar:: ". |


**Return Value:** Time stamp of the last update to the variable value, in nanoseconds since measurement start.


**Example(s):**

```c
int64 timeStamp; timeStamp = sysGetVariableTimeNS(sysvar::MyNamespace::FloatVar);
```


---

### sysGetVariableValueForDescription

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableValueForDescription.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableValueForDescription.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves the value for a value description of a system variable of type long or long array. In this way, you can access the value table of the system variable.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysGetVariableValueForDescription(SysVarName, char description[], long& value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysGetVariableValueForDescription(char namespace[], char variable[], char description[], long& value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `description` | Description for which the value shall be retrieved. |
| `value` | Receives the value for the description. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 5 there is no description of this value for the variable


**Example(s):**

```c
long val; sysGetVariableValueForDescription("Dynamic", "IntVar", "Zero", val);
```


---

### sysIsVariableTypeSigned

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysIsVariableTypeSigned.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysIsVariableTypeSigned.htm)

**Description:**

Returns whether the data type of the system variable is a signed numeric type. The function returns 1 for system variables of type Int64, Int32, Double, Integer Array and Double Array. It returns 0 for other variables, in particular for variables of type UInt32 and UInt64.


**Syntax:**

```c
dword sysIsVariableTypeSigned(sysvar * variable);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `variable` | the variable |


**Return Value:** 1 if the variable has a signed numeric type 0 otherwise


**Example(s):**

```c
sysvar * var; var = lookupSysvar("SomeNamespace::SomeVariable"); write("Signed: %d", sysIsVariableTypeSigned(var));
```


---

### sysResetValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysResetValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysResetValue.htm)

**Description:**

Resets a system variable to its initial value.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysResetValue(sysvar * variable);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success 1 The system variable has no initial value assigned or is only used for analysis purposes -1 Parameter is invalid (e.g. failure return from lookupSysVar )


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### sysResetValues

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysResetValues.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysResetValues.htm)

**Description:**

Resets all system variables in a namespace to their initial values. This recursively includes all variables in sub-namespaces.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysResetValues(char namespace[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |


**Return Value:** 0 Success 1 At least one variable in the namespace has no initial value assigned -1 The namespace doesn't exist


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### sysSetAnalysisOnlyVariable

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetAnalysIsOnlyVariable.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetAnalysIsOnlyVariable.htm)

**Description:**

Determines whether the variable is meant to be used for analysis purposes. If this is true (anlyzLocal is set to 1), and the variable is changed in a CAPL program in the Measurement setup, the value change is not transmitted to the realtime part of CANoe, but used immediately in the analysis area. This is the default. If it is false (anlyzLocal is set to 0), value changes are always transmitted to the realtime part. Note Direct access to values from system variables .


**Syntax:**

```c
void sysSetAnalysisOnlyVariable(SysVarName, long anlyzLocal);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `anlyzLocal` | Defines whether the variable shall be used only in the analysis area of CANoe. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". |


**Example(s):**

```c
sysSetAnalysisOnlyVariable(sysvar::MyNamespace::StringVar, 1);
```


---

### sysSetVariableAsync

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableAsync.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableAsync.htm)

**Description:**

Assigns the given value (data, value or values[]) to the system variable with identifier SysVarName . The bytes of data buffers are copied to struct, generic data and byte array system variables (form 1). The content of a character string is copied to String system variables (form 7). Floating point or integer numbers and arrays are assigned to the corresponding types of system variables (form 2 – 6). The assignment is executed asynchronously. When measurement setup parallelization is activated, other branches in the measurement setup will see the new value earlier or later than the branch where the function is called. In contrast to sysSetVariable* functions, sysSetVariableAsync do not prevent a measurement setup branch from being parallelized. Note sysSetVariableAsync is useful to set system variables during the simulation and read their value using the System COM object after the simulation has been stopped. For this purpose, system variables have to be configured to be used for analysis only. The sysSetVariableAsync function can also be used for specific elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you directly give the element name to the function instead of using strings, precede the name by sysvarMember:: instead of sysvar:: . Example sysvarMember::XCP::ECU_2::KL2.Curve2[0]


**Syntax:**

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, byte data[], long size); // form 1
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, float value); // form 2
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, float values[], long arraySize); // form 3
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, long value); // form 4
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, long values[], long arraySize); // form 5
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, int64 value); // form 6
```

```c
void sysSetVariableAsync(SysVarName, char value[]); // form 7
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `SysVarName` | System variable name. Must exist in the database. |
| `data, value or values[]` | New value for the system variable (form 1, 2) or buffer with new data (form 3, 4). |
| `size` | The number of bytes to be copied (form 1). |
| `arraySize` | The number of elements to be copied (form 3, 5). |


**Example(s):**

```c
byte dataBuf[64]; ... // Assign the value 0 to system variable Switch sysSetVariableAsync(sysvar::Switch, 0); // Assign the value 22.5 to system variable Temperature sysSetVariableAsync (sysvar::Temperature, 22.5); // Assign the value Master to system variable NodeName sysSetVariableAsync (sysvar::NodeName, "Master"); // Copy 64 bytes of the data buffer into the system variable DiagData sysSetVariableAsync (sysvar::DiagData, dataBuf, 64); ...
```


---

### SysSetVariableArraySize

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableArraySize.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableArraySize.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the current array size of a system variable which has a generic array data type. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableArraySize(sysvar variable, dword newSize); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableArraySize(char namespace[], char variable[], dword newSize); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `variable (form 1)` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |
| `namespace (form 2)` | Name of the namespace |
| `variable (form 2)` | Name of the variable |
| `arraySize (form 2)` | new size of the array |


**Return Value:** 0 no error 2 variable not found 4 variable is not of a generic array type, or the array type doesn't allow changing the size, or the new size is smaller than the minimum or bigger than the maximum size of the array type.


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### sysSetVariableData

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableData.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableData.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of the type data, string, struct or generic array. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableData(char namespace[], char variable[], byte data[], long size); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableData(SysVarName, byte data[], long size); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `data` | New values for the variable. |
| `size` | New size for the variable. Must not exceed the length of the data array. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Note*

```c
on key 'e' { sysvarData * svData1; char valueSysVarData[100]; byte buf[2]; long size = 2; long copiedBytes; byte data[2] = {0xAF, 0xEF}; svData1 = getSysVarData(0); sysGetVariableData(svData1, buf, copiedBytes); write("Variable is %s, Value is %x", svData1.name, buf[0]); sysSetVariableData(svData1, data, size); sysGetVariableData(svData1, buf, copiedBytes); write("Variable is %s, Value is now: %x", svData1.name, buf[0]); svData1 = getSysVarData(1); sysGetVariableData(svData1, buf, copiedBytes); write("Variable is %s, Value is %x", svData1.name, buf[0]); svData1 = getSysVarData(2); sysGetVariableData(svData1, buf, copiedBytes); write("Variable is %s, Value is %x", svData1.name, buf[0]); } sysvarData * getSysVarData(int eKey) { switch (eKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::KeyData; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignalData; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineStateData; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::KeyData , DCM::SpeedSignalData , and Engine::EngineData in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.


---

### sysSetVariableDescriptionForValue

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableDescriptionForValue.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableDescriptionForValue.htm)

**Description:**

Sets a description for a particular value of a system variable of type Integer or Integer Array


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableDescriptionForValue(char namespace[], char variable[], long value, char description[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | Value for which the description shall be set. |
| `description` | New description for the value. |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable


**Example(s):**

```c
sysSetVariableDescriptionForValue("Dynamic", "IntVar", 0, "Zero");
```


---

### sysSetVariableDWord

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableDWord.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableDWord.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of type unsigned 32 bit integer. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableDWord(char namespace[], char variable[], dword value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableDWord(SysVarName, dword value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | Receives the current value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found 2 variable was not found 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

```c
sysSetVariableDWord(sysvar::MyNamespace::UInt32Var, 1);
```


---

### sysSetVariableFloat

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFloat.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFloat.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of the double type. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableFloat(char namespace[], char variable[], float value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableFloat(SysVarName, float value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | New value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
float fVar; ... sysSetVariableFloat(sysvar::MyNamespace::FloatVar, fVar);
```

*Example 2*

> This example shows the use of system variable types as a return value of functions and as a local variable. The user-defined CAPL function getSysVarFloat returns any system variable of type Float . The function is therefore defined with the system variable type sysvarFloat as the return value. The system variable sv1 (defined with system variable type Float ) represents any system variable depending on the counter of the getSysVarFloat function. The value of a system variable is set with the CAPL function sysSetVariableFloat . The value of the system variable is get with the CAPL function sysGetVariableFloat and output in the Write Window.

*Note*

```c
on key 'c' { sysvarFloat * svFloat1;  //defines a local system variable 'sv1Float' with the system variable type 'sysvarFloat' float valueSysVar; svFloat1 = getSysVarFloat(0); //@svFloat1 = 1.5; write("Variable is %s, Value is %7.3f", svFloat1.name, @svFloat1); //@svFloat1 = @svFloat1*2.5; sysSetVariableFloat(svFloat1, @svFloat1*2.5); write("Variable is %s, Value is higher: %7.3f", svFloat1.name, @svFloat1); svFloat1 = getSysVarFloat(1); write("Variable is %s, Value is %7.3f", svFloat1.name, @svFloat1); svFloat1 = getSysVarFloat(2); valueSysVar = sysGetVariableFloat(svFloat1); write("Variable is %s, Value is %d", svFloat1.name, valueSysVar); } sysvarFloat * getSysVarFloat(int cKey) { switch (cKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::KeyFloat; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignalFloat; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineFloat; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::KeyFloat , DCM::SpeedSignalFloat , and Engine::EngineFloat in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.


---

### sysSetVariableFloatArray

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFloatArray.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFloatArray.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of the float[] type. The call will only succeed if the array size given as parameter is equal to the actual array size of the system variable (you cannot change that size dynamically). The current values of the system variable will then be set to the elements in the values array in the range from 0 to arraySize . Never give as parameter an array size which is larger than real size of the values array, this would lead to unpredictable behavior. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableFloatArray(char namespace[], char variable[], float values[], long arraySize); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableFloatArray(SysVarName,float values[], long arraySize); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `values` | New values of the variable. |
| `arraySize` | Number of values in the array. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
float fVarArr[10]; ... sysSetVariableFloatArray(sysvar::MyNamespace::FloatArrayVar, fVarArr, elcount(fVarArr));
```

*Example 2*

> This example shows the use of system variable types as a return value of functions and as a local variable. The user-defined CAPL function getSysVarFloatArray returns any system variable of type FloatArray . The function is therefore defined with the system variable type sysvarFloatArray as the return value. The system variable sv1 (defined with system variable type FloatArray ) represents any system variable depending on the counter of the getSysVarFloatArray function. The value of a system variable is set with the CAPL function sysSetVariableFloatArray . The value of the system variable is get with the CAPL function sysGetVariableFloatArray and output in the Write Window.

*Note*

```c
on key 'g' { sysvarFloatArray * sv1; float fVarArr [5] = {7.3, 7.3, 7.3, 7.3, 7.3}; sv1 = getSysVarFloatArray(0); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is %2.1lf", sv1.name, "[0]", @sv1[0]); sysSetVariableFloatArray(sv1,fVarArr, elcount(fVarArr)); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is: %2.1lf", sv1.name, "[0]",@sv1[0]); sv1 = getSysVarFloatArray(1); write("Variable is %s%s, Valgue is %2.1lf", sv1.name, "[0]", @sv1[0]); sv1 = getSysVarFloatArray(2); sysGetVariableFloatArray (sv1, fVarArr, elcount(fVarArr)); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is %2.1lf", sv1.name, "[0]",fVarArr[0]); } sysvarFloatArray * getSysVarFloatArray (int gKey) { switch (gKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::KeyFloatArray; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignalFloatArray; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineFloatArray; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::KeyFloatArray , DCM::SpeedSignalFloatArray , and Engine::EngineFloatArray in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.


---

### sysSetVariableLong

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLong.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLong.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a system variable of type long (32bit signed integer). Note The function can also be used for variables of type unsigned integer. You can simply cast the value to long.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableLong (char namespace[], char variable[], long value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableLong (SysVarName, long value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | New value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
long lVar; ... sysSetVariableLong (sysvar::MyNamespace::IntVar, lVar);
```

*Example 2*

> This example shows the use of system variable types as a return value of functions and as a local variable. The user-defined CAPL function getSysVar returns any system variable of type Int . The function is therefore defined with the system variable type sysvarInt as the return value. The system variable sv1 (defined with system variable type Int ) represents any system variable depending on the counter of the getSysVar function. The value of a system variable is set with the CAPL function sysSetVariableLong . The value of the system variable is get with the CAPL function sysGetVariableLong and output in the Write Window.

*Note*

```c
on key 'a' { sysvarInt * sv1; //defines a local system variable 'sv1' with the system variable type 'sysvarInt' int  valueSysVar; sv1 = getSysVar(0); write("Variable is %s, Value is %d", sv1.name, @sv1); //@sv1++; sysSetVariableLong(sv1, @sv1+1); write("Variable is %s, Value is higher: %d", sv1.name, @sv1); sv1 = getSysVar(1); write("Variable is %s, Value is %d", sv1.name, @sv1); sv1 = getSysVar(2); valueSysVar = sysGetVariableLong(sv1); write("Variable is %s, Value is %d", sv1.name, valueSysVar); } sysvarInt * getSysVar(int aKey) { switch (aKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::Key; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignal; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineStateSwitch; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::Key , DCM::SpeedSignal , and Engine::EngineStateSwitch in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.


---

### sysSetVariableLongArray

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLongArray.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLongArray.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of the long[] type. The call will only succeed if the array size given as parameter is equal to the actual array size of the system variable (you cannot change that size dynamically). The current values of the system variable will then be set to the elements in the values array in the range from 0 to arraySize . Never give as parameter an array size which is larger than real size of the values array, this would lead to unpredictable behavior. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableLongArray (char namespace[], char variable[], long values[], long arraySize); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableLongArray (SysVarName, long values[], long arraySize); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `values` | Values of the variable. |
| `arraySize` | Number of values in the array. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
long lVarArr[10]; ... sysSetVariableLongArray(sysvar::MyNamespace::LongArrayVar, lVarArr, elcount(lVarArr));
```

*Example 2*

> This example shows the use of system variable types as a return value of functions and as a local variable. The user-defined CAPL function getSysVarIntArray returns any system variable of type IntArray . The function is therefore defined with the system variable type sysvarIntArray as the return value. The system variable sv1 (defined with system variable type IntArray ) represents any system variable depending on the counter of the getSysVarIntArray function. The value of a system variable is set with the CAPL function sysSetVariableLongArray . The value of the system variable is get with the CAPL function sysGetVariableLongArray and output in the Write Window.

*Note*

```c
on key 'f' { sysvarIntArray * sv1; long lVarArr [5] = {7, 7, 7, 7, 7}; sv1 = getSysVarIntArray(0); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is %d", sv1.name, "[0]", @sv1[0]); sysSetVariableLongArray(sv1,lVarArr, elcount(lVarArr)); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is: %d", sv1.name, "[0]",@sv1[0]); sv1 = getSysVarIntArray(1); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is %d", sv1.name, "[0]", @sv1[0]); sv1 = getSysVarIntArray(2); sysGetVariableLongArray (sv1, lVarArr, elcount(lVarArr)); write("Variable is %s%s, Value is %d", sv1.name, "[0]",lVarArr[0]); } sysvarIntArray * getSysVarIntArray (int fKey) { switch (fKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::KeyIntegerArray; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignalIntegerArray; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineIntegerArray; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::KeyIntegerArray , DCM::SpeedSignalIntegerArray , and Engine::EngineIntegerArray in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.

### sysSetVariableLongLong

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLongLong.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableLongLong.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of a 64 bit integer type. Note The function can also be used for variables of type unsigned 64bit integer. You can simply cast the value to int64.

```

long sysSetVariableLongLong(SysVarName, int64 value); // form 2
```

|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | New value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found 2 variable was not found 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

```c
int64 llVar; ... sysSetVariableLongLong(sysvar::MyNamespace::Int64Var, llVar);
```

### sysSetVariableQWord

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableQWord.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableQWord.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of type unsigned 64bit integer. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableQWord(char namespace[], char variable[], qword value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableQWord(SysVarName, qword value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | Receives the current value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |

### sysSetVariableString

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableString.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableString.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the value of a variable of the String (char[]) type. Note


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableString(char namespace[], char variable[], char value[]); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableString(SysVarName, char value[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable. |
| `value` | New value of the variable. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

*Example 1*

```c
sysSetVariableString(sysvar::MyNamespace::StringVar, "Vector");
```

*Example 2*

> This example shows the use of system variable types as a return value of functions and as a local variable. The user-defined CAPL function getSysVarString returns any system variable of type String . The function is therefore defined with the system variable type sysvarString as the return value. The system variable sv1 (defined with system variable type String ) represents any system variable depending on the counter of the getSysVarString function. The value of a system variable is set with the CAPL function sysSetVariableString . The value of the system variable is get with the CAPL function sysGetVariableString and output in the Write Window.

*Note*

```c
on key 'd' { sysvarString * svString1; char valueSysVarString[100]; char buf[100]; svString1 = getSysVarString(0); sysGetVariableString(svString1, buf, elcount(buf)); write("Variable is %s, Value is %s", svString1.name, buf); sysSetVariableString(svString1, "OFF"); sysGetVariableString(svString1, buf, elcount(buf)); write("Variable is %s, Value is now: %s", svString1.name, buf); svString1 = getSysVarString(1); sysGetVariableString(svString1, buf, elcount(buf)); write("Variable is %s, Value is %s", svString1.name, buf); svString1 = getSysVarString(2); sysGetVariableString(svString1, buf, elcount(buf)); write("Variable is %s, Value is %s", svString1.name, buf); } sysvarString * getSysVarString(int cKey) { switch (cKey) { case 0: return sysvar::FMW1::KeyString; case 1: return sysvar::DCM::SpeedSignalString; default: return sysvar::Engine::EngineStateString; } }
```

> You must define the system variables FMW1::KeyString , DCM::SpeedSignalString , and Engine::EngineString in the System Variables Configuration dialog first.

### System Variables

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/Shared/SystemVariables/SysVar.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/Shared/SystemVariables/SysVar.htm)

### sysBeginVariableStructUpdate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysBeginVariableStructUpdate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysBeginVariableStructUpdate.htm)

**Description:**

Starts the update of several elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . Use this function and the corresponding sysEndVariableStructUpdate to change several elements at the same time without the variable having an intermediate value where only some elements are changed. Each call must be followed by a call to sysEndVariableStructUpdate , else the variable value will not change.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysBeginVariableStructUpdate(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1
```

```c
long sysBeginVariableStructUpdate(sysvarName); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable
SysVarName: Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful -1 variable was not found


**Example(s):**

```c
sysBeginVariableStructUpdate(sysvar::XCP::ECU_2::KL2);
```


---

### sysEndVariableStructUpdate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysEndVariableStructUpdate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysEndVariableStructUpdate.htm)

**Description:**

Ends the update of several elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . Use this function and the corresponding sysBeginVariableStructUpdate to change several elements at the same time without the variable having an intermediate value where only some elements are changed.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysEndVariableStructUpdate(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1
```

```c
long sysEndVariableStructUpdate(sysvarName); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variable` | Name of the variable
SysVarName: Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful -1 variable was not found


**Example(s):**

```c
sysEndVariableStructUpdate(sysvar::XCP::ECU_2::KL2);
```


---

### sysGetVariableMemberPhys

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableMemberPhys.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysGetVariableMemberPhys.htm)

**Description:**

Retrieves the physical value of a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array . Note Direct access to values from system variables


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysGetVariableMemberPhys(char namespace[], char variableAndMemberName[], double& value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysGetVariableMemberPhys(SysVarMemberName, double& value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variableAndMemberName` | Name of the variable and the element of the struct/array. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable element, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVarMember::". |
| `value` | Receives the current physical value of the element. |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 2 variable or element were not found 4 the element has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

```c
double val; sysGetVariableMemberPhys(sysvarMember::SomeNamespace::SomeStructVariable.SomeArrayMember[0], val);
```


---

### sysSetVariableMemberPhys

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableMemberPhys.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableMemberPhys.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the physical value of a specific element of a variable of type struct or generic array . Note Direct access to values from system variables


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableMemberPhys(char namespace[], char variableAndMemberName[], double value); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableMemberPhys(SysVarMemberName, double value); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Name of the namespace. |
| `variableAndMemberName` | Name of the variable and the element of the struct/array. |
| `SysVarName` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable element, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVarMember::". |
| `value` | Sets the new physical value of the element. |


**Return Value:** 0 no error, function successful 2 variable or element were not found 3 no writing right for the variable available 4 the element has no suitable type for the function


**Example(s):**

```c
sysSetVariableMemberPhys(sysvarMember::SomeNamespace::SomeStructVariable.SomeArrayMember[0], 1.2);
```


---

### sysCreateVariableFilter

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysCreateVariableFilter.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysCreateVariableFilter.htm)

**Description:**

Creates a new variable filter behind the node calling the function.
If no filterName is given, the default filter with an empty name is created.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysCreateVariableFilter(long filterType); // form 1
```

```c
long sysCreateVariableFilter(long filterType, char filterName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filterType` | Specifies the filter behavior: 0: Stop Filter. Stops all variables that are added to it. 1: Pass Filter. Stops all variables that are not added to it. |
| `filterName` | The name of the new filter. The name is used to identify the filter later, when variables or namespaces are added. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 1 The filter type is not valid. The filter cannot be created. 4 A filter with the given name already exists. The filter cannot be created.
Note: if no name or an empty name was given, this return value means that the default filter already exists. It cannot be created a second time.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //create the default filter as a Stop Filter. result = sysCreateVariableFilter(0);
```


---

### sysFilterAddNamespace

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterAddNamespace.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterAddNamespace.htm)

**Description:**

Adds a namespace to the variable filter. If no filterName is given, the namespace is added to the default filter.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysFilterAddNamespace(char namespace[]); // form 1
```

```c
long sysFilterAddNamespace(char namespace[], char filterName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | The namespace to add. The existence of the namespace is not checked, and adding a namespace that is not defined is not an error. If the namespace gets defined after it is added to the filter, it is affected by the filter normally. |
| `filterName` | The name of the filter the namespace should be added to. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The namespace cannot be added. 3 The variable is already added to the filter. The namespace cannot be added.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //create the default filter as a Stop Filter result = sysCreateVariableFilter(0); //add a namespace to the filter. result = sysFilterAddNamespace("myNamespace");
```


---

### sysFilterAddVariable

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterAddVariable.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterAddVariable.htm)

**Description:**

Adds a variable to a filter. If no filterName is given, the variable will be added to the default filter.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysFilterAddVariable(sysVar variable); // form 1
```

```c
long sysFilterAddVariable(sysVar variable, char filterName[]); // form 2
```

```c
long sysFilterAddVariable(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 3
```

```c
long sysFilterAddVariable(char namespace[], char variable[], char filterName[]); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `variable` | The variable to add. Note If a struct member is specified, the whole struct will be added. |
| `filterName` | The name of the filter the variable should be added to. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |
| `namespace` | The namespace that contains the variable. |
| `variable (char[])` | The name of the variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The variable cannot be added. 3 The variable is already added to the filter. 6 The variable does not exist. The variable cannot be added.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //create default filter as Stop Filter result = sysCreateVariableFilter(0); //add a variable that should be stopped by the filter result = sysFilterAddVariable(sysvar::myNamespace::myVariable);
```


---

### sysFilterRemoveNamespace

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterRemoveNamespace.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterRemoveNamespace.htm)

**Description:**

Removes a namespace from the variable filter. If no filterName is given, the namespace is removed from the default filter.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysFilterRemoveNamespace(char namespace[]); // form 1
```

```c
long sysFilterRemoveNamespace(char namespace[], char filterName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | The namespace to remove. |
| `filterName` | The name of the filter the namespace should be removed from. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The namespace cannot be removed. 3 The namespace is not filtered by the filter. The namespace cannot be removed.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //remove a namespace from the default filter. result = sysFilterRemoveNamespace("myNamespace");
```


---

### sysFilterRemoveVariable

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterRemoveVariable.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysFilterRemoveVariable.htm)

**Description:**

Removes a variable from a variable filter. If no filterName is given, the variable will be removed from the default filter.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysFilterRemoveVariable(sysVar variable); // form 1
```

```c
long sysFilterRemoveVariable(sysVar variable, char filterName[]); // form 2
```

```c
long sysFilterRemoveVariable(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 3
```

```c
long sysFilterRemoveVariable(char namespace[], char variable[], char filterName[]); // form 4
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `variable` | The variable to remove. Note If a struct member is specified, the whole struct will be removed. |
| `filterName` | The name of the filter the variable should be removed from. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |
| `namespace` | The namespace that contains the variable. |
| `variable (char[])` | The name of the variable. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The variable cannot be removed. 5 The variable is not filtered by the filter. The variable cannot be removed. 6 The variable does not exist. The variable cannot be removed.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //remove a variable from the default filter result = sysFilterRemoveVariable(sysvar::myNamespace::myVariable);
```


---

### sysSetVariableFilterActive

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFilterActive.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/Functions/CAPLfunctionSysSetVariableFilterActive.htm)

**Description:**

Activates or deactivates a variable filter. If no filterName is given, the default filter is affected.


**Syntax:**

```c
long sysSetVariableFilterActive(dword active); // form 1
```

```c
long sysSetVariableFilterActive(dword active, char filterName[]); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `active` | Specifies the new filter status: 0: deactivated. All other values: activated. |
| `filterName` | The name of the filter that should be affected. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. |


**Return Value:** 0 No error, function successful. 2 The filter does not exist. The filter status cannot be changed.


**Example(s):**

```c
long result; //create the default filter as a Stop Filter. result = sysCreateVariableFilter(0); //deactivate the default filter. result = sysSetVariableFilterActive(0);
```


---

### System Variables CAPL Functions

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/CAPLfunctionsSystemVariablesOverview.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/SystemVariables/CAPLfunctionsSystemVariablesOverview.htm)


## XCP/CCP {#xcp-ccp}


---

### xcpConnect

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPConnect.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPConnect.htm)

**Description:**

Establishes a connection to the XCP/CCP device and starts the configured DAQ measurement. If the connection is successfully depends on the response of the device. Use xcpIsConnected to be aware of the connection. After establishing the connection and before start of the DAQ measurement the callback function OnXcpConnect is called. Iit is possible to use same NAX in several XCP devices. But only one such device can be connected at the moment. To do this set AutoConnect property = false for all devices with the same NAX, start XCP connection and then use CAPL xcpConnect() to start one of the device with the same NAX. If you need to start another device, call xcpDiconnect() for the first device. If you try to connect other device with the same NAX if one device is already connected following error is shown in the Write Window: XCP: Device XcpFR2 cannot be connected. Same NAX is already in use in connected device.


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpConnect (char ecuQualifier[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already connected -6 Only for FlexRay: Another ECU with the same FlexRay NAX is already connected. Hint : Disconnect connected ECU first.


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase TC_ConnectToECU () { xcpConnect("ECU"); .... } //Callback function: void OnXcpConnect (char ecuQualifier[]) { //Set calibration page to RAM xcpSetCalPage(ecuQualifier, 0x83, 0, 0); }
```


---

### xcpDisconnect

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPDisconnect.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPDisconnect.htm)

**Description:**

Disconnects from a XCP/CCP device. Use xcpIsConnected to be aware of the disconnection. The callback function OnXcpDisconnect is called after the ECU acknowledged the disconnect command.


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpDisconnect(char ecuQualifier[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already disconnected


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### OnXcpError

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnXcpError.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnXcpError.htm)

**Description:**

Whenever the XCP slaves answers with a negative response (i.e. not 0xFF) the OnXCPError callback function is called.


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnXcpError (char ecuQualifier[], byte xcpCmd, byte xcpErrorCode);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `xcpCmd` | XCP command sent by CANoe DE Family. |
| `xcpErrorCode` | Error code returned by the XCP slave. |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```

### OnXcpEvent

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnXcpEvent.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionOnXcpEvent.htm)

**Description:**

This callback provides access to data that is sent from an XCP or CCP slave in an Event packet. The first data byte contains the type of the Event (named Code in the XCP specification).


**Syntax:**

```c
void OnXcpEvent(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `data` | Byte array with the event data. |
| `dataSize` | Size of the event data. |


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```


---

### xcpGetCalPage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPGetCalPage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPGetCalPage.htm)

**Description:**

If the XCP slave device supports calibration data page switching, this command gets the currently active page and access mode. The callback returns the currently active calibration data page of the ECU.


**Syntax:**

```c
void void xcpGetCalPage(char ecuQualifier[], byte mode, byte segmentNr);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `mode` | Access mode of the currently active page. The values may be 0x01 (ECU access) or 0x02 (XCP access). All other values are invalid. |
| `segmentNr` | Logical data segment number. |
| `pageNr` | Logical data page number. Usually 0 identifies the page in RAM and 1 the page in FLASH memory. Option AMD/XCP can only work on parameters stored in RAM. |


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { long mMode; long mSegmentNr; } void OnXcpConnect(char ecuName[]) { mMode = 255; mSegmentNr = 0; write("Connect callback! ECU: %s", ecuName); XcpGetCalPage(ecuName, mMode, mSegmentNr); } void OnXcpGetCalPage(char ecu[], long reserved1, long reserved2, long pageNumber) { Write("OnXcpGetCalPage callback! ECU: %s. Current PageNumber: %d", ecu, pageNumber); if (pageNumber == 0) { pageNumber = 1; } else { pageNumber = 0; } XcpSetCalPage(ecu, mMode, mSegmentNr, pageNumber); } void OnXcpSetCalPage(char ecu[]) { Write("OnXcpSetCalPage callback! ECU: %s", ecu); }
```


---

### xcpSendRaw

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSendRaw.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSendRaw.htm)

**Description:**

Sends any data as control command to an XCP/CCP device. The first data byte defines the control command code. The callback is called with the response to a raw XCP command sent by xcpSendRaw .


**Syntax:**

```c
void xcpSendRaw(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize, byte expectResponse);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `data` | Byte array of the data to be sent. |
| `dataSize` | Length of the data to be sent. |
| `expectResponse` | 0: The XCP slave will not respond to this command 1: The XCP slave is expected to respond to this command and the callback will be called |


**Example(s):**

```c
//This example shows an upload of 4 bytes from address 0x21A1B4 of the XCP slave device "XCPsim" when the user presses the key 't'. //Please note that this device is a PC application, hence the address is given in Intel format. on key 't' { byte data[8] = {0xF4, 4, 0, 0, 0xB4, 0xA1, 0x21, 0}; xcpSendRaw("XcpSim", data, 8, 1); } OnXcpSendRaw(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize) { char buffer[200]; long i; buffer[0] = 0; for (i = 0; i < dataSize; i++) { snprintf(buffer, elcount(buffer), "%s 0x%2X", buffer, data[i]); } write("Raw response: %d bytes:%s", dataSize, buffer); }
```


---

### xcpSetCalPage

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSetCalPage.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSetCalPage.htm)

**Description:**

If the XCP slave device supports calibration data page switching, this command sets the current page and access mode. The callback returns that the ECU switched the calibration data page.


**Syntax:**

```c
void xcpSetCalPage(char ecuQualifier[], byte mode, byte segmentNr, byte pageNr);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `mode` | Bit field defined in the XCP specification. Bit 7 Use for all segments, i.e. parameter segmentNr is ignored. Bit 1 The slave device XCP driver will access the given page. Bit 0 The given page will be used by the slave device application. |
| `Bit 7` | Use for all segments, i.e. parameter segmentNr is ignored. |
| `Bit 1` | The slave device XCP driver will access the given page. |
| `Bit 0` | The given page will be used by the slave device application. |
| `segmentNr` | Logical data segment number. Ignored if bit 7 of the mode parameter is set to 1. |
| `pageNr` | Logical data page number. Usually 0 identifies the page in RAM and 1 the page in FLASH memory. Option AMD/XCP can only work on parameters stored in RAM. |


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { long mMode; long mSegmentNr; } void OnXcpConnect(char ecuName[]) { mMode = 255; mSegmentNr = 0; write("Connect callback! ECU: %s", ecuName); XcpGetCalPage(ecuName, mMode, mSegmentNr); } void OnXcpGetCalPage(char ecu[], long reserved1, long reserved2, long pageNumber) { Write("OnXcpGetCalPage callback! ECU: %s. Current PageNumber: %d", ecu, pageNumber); if (pageNumber == 0) { pageNumber = 1; } else { pageNumber = 0; } XcpSetCalPage(ecu, mMode, mSegmentNr, pageNumber); } void OnXcpSetCalPage(char ecu[]) { Write("OnXcpSetCalPage callback! ECU: %s", ecu); }
```


---

### xcpUpload

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPUpload.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPUpload.htm)

**Description:**

Initiates an upload of the XCP parameter from ECU and updates the dedicated system variable. After finishing of the upload the callback function OnXcpUpload is called to indicate the upload status. Use the return value of xcpUpload to check if an error occurred during initiation of the upload. Use the errorIndication parameter of the OnXcpUpload callback function to check for errors occurred during the upload, if an error during call of xcpUpload occurs OnXcpUpload is not called.


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpUpload(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1
```

```c
long xcpUpload(sysvar sysvar); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Namespace of the corresponding system variable. |
| `variable` | Name of the corresponding system variable. |
| `sysvar` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::" |
| `errorIndication` | 0: OK -4: Device is not connected |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 System variable of the parameter was not found -2 Operation not allowed


**Example(s):**

```c
testcase TC_SignalInactive () { xcpUpload("XCP::ECU","testword0"); .... } //Callback function: void OnXcpUpload (char namespace[], char variable[], long returnValue) { if (returnValue==0) write("Systemvariable updated: %s %s", namespace,variable); }
```


---

### xcpUserCommand

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPUserCommand.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPUserCommand.htm)

**Description:**

Sends user-defined data to the XCP slave. The callback returns the response on the user command.


**Syntax:**

```c
void xcpUserCommand(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |
| `data` | Byte array for user-defined data. |
| `dataSize` | Size of the user-defined data. |


**Example(s):**

```c
variables { long mMode; long mSegmentNr; byte mUsrCmdResponse = 0; } void OnXcpConnect(char ecuName[]) { mMode = 255; mSegmentNr = 0; write("Connect callback! ECU: %s", ecuName); XcpGetCalPage(ecuName, mMode, mSegmentNr); } void OnXcpGetCalPage(char ecu[], long reserved1, long reserved2, long pageNumber) { Write("OnXcpGetCalPage callback! ECU: %s. Current PageNumber: %d", ecu, pageNumber); if (pageNumber == 0) { pageNumber = 1; } else { pageNumber = 0; } XcpSetCalPage(ecu, mMode, mSegmentNr, pageNumber); } void OnXcpSetCalPage(char ecu[]) { Write("OnXcpSetCalPage callback! ECU: %s", ecu); ProcessUserCmd(); } void ProcessUserCmd() { byte adata[12] = { 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06, 0x07, 0x08, 0x09, 0x0A, 0x0B, 0x0C }; XCPUserCommand("XcpSim", adata, 12); } void OnXcpUserCommand(char ecu[],byte data[], long dataSize) { mUsrCmdResponse = data[0]; Write("OnXcpUserCommandResponse callback! ECU: %s. Data: %d. Data size: %d", ecu, data[0], dataSize); }
```


---

### xcpActivate

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPActivate.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPActivate.htm)

**Description:**

Activates an a2l parameter for upload and DAQ measurement. Note Using system variables structs , the function must be used for the variable valid for the entire struct but not for single members.


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpActivate (char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1
```

```c
long xcpActivate (sysvar sysvar);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Namespace of the corresponding system variable. |
| `variable` | Name of the corresponding system variable. |
| `sysVar` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::" |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 System variable was not found -2 Operation not allowed


**Example(s):**

```c
on start { if(0 == XcpActivate(sysvar::XCP::XcpSim::ampl)) { write("Parameter ampl activated for measurement"); } }
```


---

### xcpActivateMeasurementGroup

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPActivateMeasurementGroup.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPActivateMeasurementGroup.htm)

Changes the measurement group that will be used for the next connection to the XCP/CCP device.

| `groupName` | Name of the measurement group. |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already connected -5 groupName does not exist

```

| `variable` | Name of the corresponding system variable. |

### xcpDeactivateAll

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPDeactivateAll.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPDeactivateAll.htm)

### xcpGetPollingCycle

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPGetPollingCycle.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPGetPollingCycle.htm)

Returns the cycle time of a parameter, if the read mode of the parameter is set to polling .

```

| `variable` | Name of the corresponding system variable. |
| `sysvar` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::" |

### xcpIsConnected

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPIsConnected.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPIsConnected.htm)

**Description:**

Returns the current connection status of a XCP/CCP device.


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpIsConnected (char ecuQualifier[]);
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `ecuQualifier` | Name of the device, configured within the CANoe DE Family XCP/CCP Window . |


**Return Value:** 0 Not connected 1 Connected -1 A device with this name does not exist

### xcpSetPollingCycle

> 🔗 [https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSetPollingCycle.htm](https://help.vector.com/CANoeDEFamily/19/en/Help/Content/Topics/CAPLFunctions/XCP/Functions/CAPLfunctionXCPSetPollingCycle.htm)

**Description:**

Sets the cycle time of a parameter, if the read mode of the parameter is set to polling .


**Syntax:**

```c
long xcpSetPollingCycle(char namespace[], char variable[], long cycleTime); // form 1
```

```c
long xcpSetPollingCycle(sysvar sysvar, long cycleTime); // form 2
```


**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `namespace` | Namespace of the corresponding system variable. |
| `variable` | Name of the corresponding system variable. |
| `sysvar` | Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::" |
| `cycleTime` | Cycle time with which the parameter is measured. |


**Return Value:** 0 OK -1 System variable of the parameter was not found -2 Operation not allowed


**Example(s):**

```c
—
```
